Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code produces incorrect results for shift operations with LCC compiler
Description: The generated code might produce incorrect results under the following conditions:? The Casting mode  parameter is set to Standards Compliant .  The generated code involves shift operation.  In these conditions, if the LCC compiler is used for generating executables or for SIL(Software-In-Loop) testing, you may experience undesired outputs.?For example, consider this code:? int a = 10;  int b = a << 1ULL;   The left shift of a number should multiply it by 2. The generated code on LCC compiler wrongly assigns the variable?b ?with the value ?10  instead of 20 , which is incorrect.?

Summary: Passing a hexadecimal or binary literal to uint64 or int64 results in an incorrect answer, Conversion failed, or std::exception
Description: MATLAB might throw an exception or return an incorrect value when an int64  or uint64  function call has one of the following forms, where literal  ?is a hexadecimal or binary literal: int64(literal )  int64(-literal )  uint64(literal )  uint64(-literal )   For example: >> uint64(0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFA)Conversion failed!>> eval('uint64(0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFA)')std::exception >> uint64(0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFAu64)ans =  uint64   18446744073709551615  % Incorrect: should end with 1610>> int64(-0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFAs64)ans =  int64   -9223372036854775808  % Incorrect: should be 6
Workaround: When calling uint64 or int64 with a hexadecimal or binary literal argument, consider wrapping the argument in square brackets. This adds no runtime overhead, but the expression no longer has one of the problematic forms due to the additional syntax.>> uint64([0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFA])ans =? uint64? ?18446744073709551610

Summary: Failure in restoring saved Model Advisor exclusions
Description: When you reopen a model that has Model Advisor exclusions, the Model Advisor ignores the exclusions.

Summary: Definite integrals with parameters might be incorrect for some parameter values
Description: Definite integrals that share the same parameters in the integrands and the bounds might be incorrect for some parameter values. For example, the following result is only correct for x >= 2 : >> syms x u>> res = int(sign(u-x+2),u,[0 x])res =4 - x
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: MATLAB slows down when using echotcpip or UDP functions
Description: Due to a memory leak, MATLAB might slow down and become unresponsive when using echotcpip  or UDP functions. The issue is more prominent when you send and receive large quantities of data (e.g., data sizes of 1 GB).  This issue can occur when using the following functions and possibly other functions: echotcpip  echoudp  udpport("byte")
Workaround: Restart MATLAB to free up the leaked memory. However, the issue might reappear if you use the affected functionality again.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 5, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator does not report an error when a non-inlined S-function accesses a global parameter with storage class
Description: The code generator does not report an error when a model that contains a non-inlined S-function accesses a parameter object in the base workspace or a data dictionary. This results in generated code that might behave incorrectly. This incorrect code generation can occur when any of the following conditions are true:The parameter object has a storage class other than Auto  or Model Default  The parameter object has storage class Auto  or Model default  and the default mapping for External parameters   is a value other than Default  The default mapping for Model parameters  is a value other than Default
Workaround: If a non-inlined S-function references a parameter object in the base workspace or a data dictionary, check that all of the following conditions are satisfied:The parameter object has storage class Auto or Model DefaultThe default mapping for External parameters is DefaultThe default mapping for Model parameters is Default
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 5, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?eig might incorrectly return NaN values
Description: The eig  function uses an iterative algorithm to solve for eigenvalues and eigenvectors, and this algorithm might fail to converge in certain situations. In such situations, the function returns NaN ?values , and targets that support warnings also produce a warning. This issue is a possibility in both  MATLAB and the generated code. The implementation of the eig  function in the generated code is based on an older version of LAPACK and?might return NaN ?values when the output in MATLAB is finite. This issue can occur if both  these conditions are true:? ?You generate a static library, a dynamic library, or an executable by using MATLAB Coder or Simulink Coder ?You do not instruct the code generator to include calls to an external LAPACK library  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Result format change for Model Advisor check himl_0011
Description: The result format style for Model Advisor check himl_0011 (Check type and size of condition expressions) ?changed to the below incorrect format. This may impact report comparison of the check with baselines during validation. Warning: The following condition expressions are not logical scalars:  Block Path? ? ? Expression  Model1/EML????? (u)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when a parameter or signal is an array of buses and specifies preservation of dimensions
Description: The code generated for a model might be incorrect when the model has a signal or parameter that meets these conditions. The parameter is an array of buses. The parameter specifies preservation of dimensions. A bus in the array has an element that is an array.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b, R2023a

Summary: Configuring UDP Send to broadcast address 255.255.255.255 causes error when loading real-time application
Description: If the To IP address  parameter in the UDP Send block is set to broadcast address 255.255.255.255 , Simulink Real-Time reports an error when loading the real-time application.
Workaround: In R2021b, you can set the To IP address in the UDP send block to the subnet broadcasting address.For example, if the local IP address is 10.10.10.15 and the subnet mask is 255.255.255.0, the subnet broadcasting address is 10.10.10.255.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a

Summary: String type custom attributes are imported from IBM DOORS Next as enumerated type

Summary: Duplicate IDs when exporting SimBiology model to SBML result in an invalid model
Description: When you copy and paste model components in the SimBiology Model Builder  app, the IDs of components used for exporting the model to SBML are duplicated. Consequently, the exported SBML model is invalid because it does not satisfy the SBML standard that identifiers need to be unique.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Restoring simulation from a saved operating point might give wrong answer for certain Stateflow charts
Description: Restoring simulation of a model containing a Stateflow chart from an operating point might give wrong answer when you save an operating point, modify the operating point by changing a chart data that is written only in entry actions, and restore the simulation from the modified operating point.  The restored simulation might not reflect your change in data.
Workaround: Put a breakpoint in the chart before restoring simulation.?

Summary: invalid vector subscript error?during code generation with? a variant?root outport
Description: invalid vector subscript ?error?during code?generation when all the below conditions are true:  Signal object is attached to the root outport block i. e.   get_param(gcb,   'MustResolveToSignalObject')  returns true for the root outport block.   The storage class of this signal object is custom.  The root outport has a variant condition.  There is at least one root outport "before" this root outport that has either no variant condition or the same variant condition as this root outport.

Summary: Model Advisor check "Check reuse of subsystem code" exits with warning when?checksum information is missing
Description: Model Advisor check "Check reuse of subsystem code" ?( ID: ?mathworks.codegen.SubsysCodeReuse) ?exits with a warning when a subsystem does not provide checksum information.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 8, R2021b Update 6, R2022a Update 7, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink fails to report inconsistent initial values between Outport block and signal object specified inside referenced model
Description: Simulink fails to report inconsistent initial values between an Outport block and a signal object specified inside a referenced model, and instead simulates the model. When this issue occurs, the simulation and code generation results might not be the same, depending on which initial value is applied last.?? This issue might occur if the model has these conditions: The model contains a conditionally executed subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystem has an Outport block with an explicit value specified for the Initial output  parameter, that is, not an empty matrix ([] ). The Outport block is driven by an output port of a Model block. Inside the referenced model, the signal driving the root Outport block corresponding to the output port of the Model block has a Simulink.Signal  object associated with it. The InitialValue ?field? of the signal object is not an empty string and the initial value is not the same as the initial output value specified for the Outport block in the top model.
Workaround: When a Simulink.Signal object with an explicit initial value is applied to a signal, make sure that the destination, conditionally executed subsystem Outport block uses an empty matrix ([]) as the initial value. Also, apply this workaround when the signal in question crosses a model reference boundary.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 4, R2022b

Summary: Unexpected effect of rotational velocity on pressure and force in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block
Description: Increasing rotational velocity decreases rather than increases pressure and interface force magnitude in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.
Workaround: This workaround is equivalent to the expected behavior of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. Replace the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block with these three blocks:Single-Acting Actuator (IL)Rotating Channel (IL)Rotating Cylinder Force (IL)Make the following connections. These connections match the attached picture:Rotating Channel (IL) port A to previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port A.Rotating Channel (IL) port B, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port A, and Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port X.Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port C, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port R, and previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port R.Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port R, Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port C, and previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port C.Rotating Channel (IL) port W and Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) port W to previous connections of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) port W.Make the following parameter updates:Parameters in the Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block should correspond to parameters on the Actuator, Hard Stop, and Initial Conditions tabs of your original Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. The value of the?Piston cross-sectional area?parameter in the Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block equals the value of the annular area in-between the?Piston outer radius?and?Piston inner radius?parameters in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.Parameters in the Rotating Channel (IL) block should match parameters on the Fluid Channel tab of your original Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block. These parameters in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block map to corresponding parameters in the Rotating Channel (IL)?with a different name:Fluid entry radius maps to Port B radius of rotation.Fluid channel cross-sectional area maps to Orifice area.The Rotating Cylinder Force (IL)?block parameter Mechanical orientation?should have the opposite setting of the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL)?block. All remaining parameters in the Rotating Cylinder Force (IL) block map directly from parameters with the same names in the Rotating Single-Acting Actuator (IL) block.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Argument was not found or found more than 2 hits or Unexpected number of elements in GraphicalIndex?error from SIL/PIL simulations of AUTOSAR model
Description: ?A software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation of an AUTOSAR model fails if both of these conditions apply to the model under test: The configuration parameter?CodeInterfacePackaging  is set to?'Reusable function' . Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow chart hard codes some size information of non-scalar symbolic dimension
Description: When generating code for a 2-D matrix that is defined by symbolic dimensions,  the resulting code inserts a numeric offset in the matrix assignment based on the current value of one dimension of the matrix.?This causes an issue if you specify a different dimension post code generation since the numeric offset will not match the matrix dimension.

Summary: Exporting models with mixed datatypes in bus outputs as tool-coupling FMUs might produce incorrect simulation results
Description: When a model is exported as a tool-coupling FMU, the simulation results from the FMU might be incorrect if the following conditions are true: The model has a bus or nested bus output. The bus or nested bus has elements or sub-bus with mixed datatypes (boolean, double, int...).

Summary: MATLAB might crash for models with multiple callsites of the same function or system object analyzed by Simulink Design Verifier
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might cause MATLAB to crash in rare instances when running Simulink Design Verifier with  Test Generation or Design Error Detection being run with MCDC objective enabled.  Multiple calls to the same function or multiple instances of the same system object in the model.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 2, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect simulation results for compartments when unit conversion is enabled
Description: SimBiology might return incorrect simulation results for compartments when all of the following conditions are met: Unit conversion is enabled. The model contains multiple compartments with different units. The model contains nested compartments.  In such cases, SimBiology might use the wrong unit multiplier to convert a compartment capacity from internal units to user-specified units. Internally, the simulation proceeds with the correct units, and the reported simulation results are correct for species and parameters.
Workaround: Work around the problem by doing any of the following:Disable unit conversion.Ensure all compartments have the same units.Convert all nested compartments to top-level compartments.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Reading map data from HERE HD Live Map web service can cause error
Description: MATLAB can issue an error when you read map data from the HERE HD Live Map web service using these options: The Driving Scenario Designer app The roadNetwork  object function of the drivingScenario  object The read  object function of the hereHDLMReader  object  An example of the error when constructing hereHDLMReader object is:? The '' protocol specified in URL, 'catalogs?verbose=1&organizationType=here&layerType=versioned&q=hdlm-protobuf', is not supported. Specify the URL with the protocol 'http://' or 'https://'.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly show?Incompatible for models with arrays of complex numbers as output or input to function calls
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may incorrectly show?Incompatible  for models that meet the following conditions:The model contains arrays of complex numbers? The complex array is either the input or the output of a function call The complex array is not a simple variable?  For example, the following pattern may cause the model to be incorrectly shown as incompatible:? obj.complexArray = functionCall(obj.complexArray);  However, the following pattern will not be shown as incompatible:? complexArray = functionCall(complexArray);

Summary: Unexpected error may occur during HDL Code Generation for a large model that uses the NCO HDL Optimized block
Description: HDL code generation may error out for a model that uses the NCO HDL Optimized block with frame-based output. This error happens for bigger models like the Wireless HDL Toolbox reference application models. Before R2022a, this block was named NCO HDL Optimized, and was included in the DSP System Toolbox HDL Support library. Starting in R2022a, the block is named NCO and is in the DSP HDL Toolbox library. The error message is:? Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/addZeroProtection  Unexpected Error during HDL Code Generation.  Contact support@mathworks.com  with reproduction? steps. Assertion Failed With Debug Message:  pir_transforms/PropagateInitVal.cpp:205:getInitVal(s) == InitVal::NOT_VISITED?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MinMax block might produce incorrect results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes with NaN input and zero-crossing detection
Description: The MinMax block produces incorrect results in accelerator and rapid accelerator modes when the following conditions are true: Model solver is variable-step. MinMax block Enable zero crossing detection  is selected. MinMax block input has continuous sample time. Input to MinMax block has double data type. One of these is true: MinMax block has only one input and the first element of the input is NaN. For example, input [NaN 1] can output NaN, which is an incorrect result. MinMax block has multiple inputs and the first input has a NaN. For example, for a MinMax block with two inputs, the first input has an input of NaN and the second input has a 1, the resulting incorrect result is a NaN.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Unsatisfiable objectives for MCDC objectives where the overall condition is always false
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect Unsatisfiable (or Dead Logic)  objectives for MCDC objectives when the following conditions are met: The MCDC objective is part of a MATLAB System or MATLAB Function block. The overall expression for a || or && operation is always false.?  When the overall expression is?A && B , when this issue is experienced, all MCDC objectives are reported as Unsatisfiable : A && B with A true  A && B with A false  A && B with B true  A && B with B false?  However,  if A can be false, "A && B with A false " should be reported as Satisfiable?  if B can be false (when A can be true), "A && B with B false " should be reported as Satisfiable    On the other hand, when the overall expression is?A || B , when this issue is experienced, all MCDC objectives are reported as?Unsatisfiable : A || B with A true  A?||?B with A false  A?||?B with B true  A?||?B with B false?  However, "A || B with A false " should be reported as?Satisfiable?  "A || B with B false " should be reported as?Satisfiable
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a

Summary: HDL IP core generation using Xilinx Vivado fails as of January 1, 2022
Description: Since January 1, 2022, IP core generation fails when the synthesis tool is set to Xilinx Vivado. The following error message can be observed in the HDL Workflow Advisor Task 3.2:? Failed Task "Vivado IP Packager" unsuccessful. See log for details. Generated logfile: hdl_prj\hdlsrc\<myModel> \workflow_task_VivadoIPPackager.log  The log file at \hdl_prj\hdlsrc\<myModel>\workflow_task_VivadoIPPackager.log  reports an error message similar to the following: bad lexical cast: source type value could not be interpreted as target  ? ?while executing"rdi::set_property core_revision 2201031458 {component component_1}"  ? ?invoked from within  "set_property core_revision 2201031458 [ipx::current_core]"  ? ?(file "vivado_ip_package.tcl" line 57)
Workaround: Installation instructions  Log into an account with the appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.  Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps. On Mac systems, the folder name will have a .app extension, e.g. /Applications/MATLAB_R20XXa.app  Exit MATLAB.Locate the following file under your <MATLAB> folder and rename the file to "IPEmitterVivado_backup.p":<MATLAB>\toolbox\hdlcoder\hdlcommon\+hdlturnkey\+ip\IPEmitterVivado.pWindows Systems:?You might be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when renaming this file.  Download and unzip the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report.In the unzipped folder, navigate to the subfolder that matches the release you are using. For example, if you are using the R2021b release, open the R2021b subfolder.If you do not see a subfolder that matches the release you are using,?contact MathWorks Technical Support.In this folder, there is a newer version of the?IPEmitterVivado.p file. Copy this file and paste it in the location mentioned above.Windows Systems:?You might be prompted to provide Administrator privileges when pasting this file.Restart MATLAB.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inconsistent simulation and code generation results when input to log or log10 is negative
Description: When a Simulink model contains computation of log  or log10   and the input to this computation is negative, The result of this computation is likely NaN  in Normal mode, and The result of this computation might be -inf  in Accelerator mode, Rapid Accelerator mode, and generated code.  This computation of log  or log10 might come directly from a Math Function block, or indirectly from other logic that computes   log  or log10.     This issue affects Simulink, Simulink Coder, and Embedded Coder.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: During fast restart or runtime update diagram, Simulink reports an error when replacing a Simulink.Signal object with another signal object with the same property values
Description: When performing a fast restart simulation or an update diagram during runtime, Simulink reports an error when a Simulink.Signal  object is replaced with another one with the same property values. The reported error is similar to:The Simulink signal object 'SignalObject1' for output port 1 of block 'model/testBlock' was changed during simulation.
Workaround: If you use initialization scripts before every fast restart simulation run, consider these alternatives:Separate out the Simulink.Signal objects into a separate MAT-file and load them only once before the first simulation run.Write a wrapper around the Simulink.Signal object creation function that can check for equivalent signal objects already in the workspace and not replace them.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation result might occur when using fixed point data type in S-Function Builder
Description: Simulink might produce incorrect simulation results when you use fixed point signals in your S-Function Builder block, and the data type override is set to a value other than off .
Workaround: First, manually edit the generated source file for the S-function.Open the generated source fileIn the mdlInitializeSizes method, update the data type override flag to 0. The override flag is the last field in function ssRegisterDataTypeFxpBinaryPoint or ssRegisterDataTypeFxpSlopeBias. For example:DTypeId DataTypeId_0 = ssRegisterDataTypeFxpBinaryPoint(?S,?OUT_0_ISSIGNED,?OUT_0_WORDLENGTH,?OUT_0_FRACTIONLENGTH,?0 );Then, recompile the source file before running the simulation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022b

Summary: Connected Switch and Scope blocks produces error in XCP-based external mode simulation
Description: If a model contains a Switch block with inputs that are virtual buses and the Switch block connects to a Scope block, when you run an XCP-based external mode simulation, the simulation produces an error when Simulink tries to connect to the target application. For example: Error using coder. oneclick. TargetHook/ extModeConnect  External Mode Open Protocol SignalSelect command failed  Error in on_target_one_click   You might also observe the error if the model contains a block that uses a similar block connection?pattern internally. For example, a PID autotuner block from the Simulink Control Design library.
Workaround: Remove the Scope block and use the Simulation Data Inspector or a Dashboard library block to display the signal.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Using a Data Store Read block with a GetSet storage class that feeds into a Prelookup block or Interpolation Using Prelookup block might generate extraneous set function calls
Description: When you map a global Data Store block to a GetSet  storage class and you connect a Data Store Read block directly to a Prelookup block or Interpolation Using Prelookup block, the code generator might produce set? function calls in addition to get  function calls. The set  function calls are unnecessary for a Data Store Read block and can reduce performance.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 4, R2022a Update 1, R2022b

Summary: Performance issues when compiling a model with variants and model reference hierarchy
Description: Performance regression when compiling a Variant model with Propagate conditions outside of variant subsystem  selected for Variant Subsystems  within and contains a model reference hierarchy.

Summary: Simulation of a Simulink model with multiple MATLAB Function blocks loading similar networks might produce incorrect answers
Description: Consider a Simulink model with multiple MATLAB Function blocks that load networks differing only in weight values. Simulation of such a model might produce an incorrect answer, if the networks share the same name. For example, suppose that?net1.mat  and net2.mat  contain networks that differ by weights only and are loaded in two different MATLAB Function blocks. The two blocks contain these two MATLAB functions respectively: MATLAB Function block 1?  function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat', 'network');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);end MATLAB Function block 2 ? function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat','network'); % network name is same as in first MATLAB function blockendscores2 = predict(netB,u);end  Simulating a model that contains both these MATLAB Function blocks might produce an incorrect answer.?
Workaround: Specify unique network names for unique networks.MATLAB Function block 1?function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat', 'network1');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);endMATLAB Function block 2?function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat','network2');% network name is different from first MATLAB function blockendscores2 = predict(netB,u);end Alternatively, do not provide any network name. MATLAB and Simulink will automatically create unique network names.?MATLAB Function block 1?function scores1 = fcn(u)persistent netCif isempty(netC)    netA = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net1.mat');endscores1 = predict(netA,u);endMATLAB Function block 2?function scores2 = fcn(u) persistent netDif isempty(netD)    netB = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('net2.mat');endscores2 = predict(netB,u);end
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB can crash when calling getadjacencymatrix or when using the Model Debugger on a very large SimBiology model
Description: MATLAB can crash when calling?getadjacencymatrix  on a SimBiology model that contains hundreds of reactions using null as reactant or product. The?Model Debugger for SimBiology  also relies on getadjacencymatrix  and can crash when used with such a model.
Workaround: Replace null reactants and products with a dummy species. You can adapt the following code, which updates a SimBiology model stored in variable modelObj by replacing null with a dummy species named NULL, while ensuring the reaction rate remains the same.reactions = modelObj.Reactions;nullPattern = regexpPattern('\<null\>');for i = 1:numel(reactions)    reactionString = reactions(i).Reaction;    if contains(reactionString, nullPattern)        allSpecies = [reactions(i).Reactants; reactions(i).Products];        oneSpecies = allSpecies(1);        oneCompartment = oneSpecies.Parent;        nullSpecies = sbioselect(oneCompartment, 'Name', 'NULL');        if isempty(nullSpecies)            nullSpecies = oneCompartment.addspecies('NULL', 'Units', oneSpecies.Units);        end        nullSpeciesQualifiedName = nullSpecies.PartiallyQualifiedName;        reactionString = replace(reactionString, nullPattern, nullSpeciesQualifiedName);        rate = reactions(i).ReactionRate;        reactions(i).Reaction = reactionString;        reactions(i).ReactionRate = rate;    endend
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong answer for model with Outports using GetSet or AccessFunction storage class and a referenced model containing a Function-Call Subsystem block
Description: Code generation of a model might result in a silent wrong answer when all of these conditions are true: The model contains a Model block that is directly connected to an Outport block. The Outport block uses a GetSet  storage class or other storage class with Data Access  set to Function  and Access Mode  set to Value . The referenced model contains a Function-Call Subsystem block that is connected to the Outport block.  Code generation might also be incorrect if the output signals of the Model block in the parent model use a GetSet  storage class.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block in the parent model between the Model block and the Outports that are mapped to?GetSet or Function?storage class.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Simulink logs the value for a3  as 2 , instead of 3  as expected.

Workaround: Before simulating the model in MATLAB R2021a Update 4 or R2020b Update 6, delete the following files and foldersModel Simulation cache folder. Summary: Using VISA-related functionality on Intel-based macOS Big Sur 11.4 or 11.5.2?might crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB might crash on Intel-based macOS Big Sur 11.4 or 11.5.2 if the Instrument Control Toolbox Support Package for National Instruments VISA and ICP Interfaces is installed. Impacted MATLAB releases install NI-VISA 20.0 with this support package. The crash can occur when using any of the following VISA-related functions, apps, or Simulink blocks: visa  visadev  visadevlist  instrhwinfo  Test and Measurement Tool Query Instrument?block To Instrument?block  For example, the following line of code crashes MATLAB: instrhwinfo("visa","ni")
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps to install the NI-VISA 21.0 driver on your Intel-based macOS Big Sur 11.4 or 11.5.2 machine:Download and install NI-VISA 21.0 from NI-VISA version 21.0 for macOS.Restart your machine, as directed by the NI installer.Allow NI-VISA to load system extensions by following the instructions for "System software blocked" from the?macOS User Guide.Apply the workaround for NI bug number 1555478 mentioned in the NI documentation. Upgrading to NI-VISA 21.0 alone will not solve the issue. See https://www.ni.com/en-us/support/documentation/bugs/21/ni-visa-21-0-known-issues.html.Restart your machine, as directed by the NI installer.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Extra node when comparing Simulink models containing blocks or annotations that have the same name as a system, block, annotation, or line parameter
Description: When comparing Simulink model files, an extra node may appear in the comparison report when all  these conditions are true:? You are using the comparison tools on the MATLAB desktop or publishing the comparison report as HTML, PDF, or Word files. You added or deleted a block or annotation. The name of the added or deleted block or annotation matches the name of a system, block, annotation, or line parameter.  In the comparison report, the extra node appears on the opposite side to the block or annotation that has the same name as a parameter.
Workaround: Rename the added or deleted block or annotation. Use a name that does not match the name of any Simulink system, block, annotation, or line parameters.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Permute function is scalarized incorrectly
Description: In rare cases, incorrect code generation for the permute function may occur when all of the following conditions are met: The input matrix to the permute function has at least 3 dimensions. Exactly one dimension of the input matrix is variable-sized with an upper bound. At least one dimension?of the input matrix is fixed with the same size as the upper bound. Additional dimensions of the input matrix are fixed and are either size 1 or are the same size as the upper bound. The permute  function rearranges the fixed and variable-sized dimensions. MATLAB Coder is configured with InlineBetweenUserAndMathWorksFunctions  set to 'Always '.  Consider the following MATLAB code:     function out = foo(in,upperLimit)    assert(upperLimit>=2 && upperLimit<=3);    out = permute(in(2:1:upperLimit,:,:),[2,3,1]);    end MATLAB Coder generates:    --SNIP--     out_size[0] = 2;    out_size[1] = 1;    out_size[2] = (int)upperLimit - 1;    plast = (int)upperLimit - 1;    for (i = 0; i < plast; i++) {      out_data[2 * i] = in[3 * i + 1];      out_data[2 * i + 1] = in[3 * i + 2];    }     --SNIP-- which is incorrect when in is a 3-by-2-by-1 matrix and upperLimit is 2. This is because we assign elements 1 and 2 from in into elements 0 and 1 of out. However, the correct generated code would have assigned elements 1 and 4 from in into elements 0 and 1 of out. For example, the correct code may look like:    --SNIP--     plast = (int)upperLimit - 2;    for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) {      for (k = 0; k <= plast; k++) {        in_data[k + ((int)upperLimit - 1) * i] = in[(k + 3 * i) + 1];      }    }    out_size[0] = 2;    out_size[1] = 1;    out_size[2] = (int)upperLimit - 1;    plast = ((int)upperLimit - 1) << 1;    if (plast - 1 >= 0) {      memcpy(&out_data[0], &in_data[0], plast * sizeof(double));    }     --SNIP--  See attached example.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 3, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Code generation for messages from models that contain bus ports may omit initialization code
Description: When generating code for a model that passes messages with a referenced model, initialization code for the message port is generated in the initialize method (or constructor in the case of C++ class generation). If the referenced model has additional ports that are configured with bus data types, then its message port initialization code may be omitted during code generation.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do not configure the Model block with both message ports and bus ports. Instead, pass each bus element into the model block individually as needed.?

Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 1, R2022a

Summary: Sporadic MATLAB crashes during compilation of models with nonlinear terms and derivatives in constraint equations
Description: MATLAB might crash when you run or update a Simscape model where a constraint equation  contains a nonlinear term, which in turn contains a derivative of any variable and at least one more variable. Consider the following network equations: ??????? der(x) * u == u * u;? ? ? ? ? ? ? (1)??????? r == der(x);? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? (2)??????? y == der(r) + cos(u) * der(u);??? (3)??????? u == sin(time);? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?(4)  Equations (1), (2), and (4) are the constraint equations. When we differentiate equation (1) we get the following expression ? ? ?? der(der(x)) * u + der(x) * der(u) == 2.0 * u * der(u)   When applying derivative replacement method on this equation, der(x) * der(u)  term can cause the compilation to crash.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: DWARF parser fails to extract debugging information from ELF files compiled using Microchip's XC16 compiler

The ELF file appears as if it has been compiled without debugging information, which blocks XCP external mode workflows on Microchip 16-bit dsPIC target hardware. The workflows require the debugging information to infer memory addresses of global variables.

Description: When creating a dlnetwork object from a layer graph containing   both featureInputLayer  and image3dInputLayer  objects, the software returns a network containing only featureInputLayer  objects or only? image3dInputLayer  objects as input layers. In most cases, the network trains and makes predictions correctly. However, when interacting with the network layers, the software might throw an error such as: In 'nnet_cnn:layer:FeatureInputLayer:oneLineDisplayNoTransforms', data type supplied is incorrect for parameter {0}.
Workaround: In your code, replace featureInputLayer objects with imageInputLayer objects with input size [1 1 numFeatures], where numFeatures is the input size of the replaced featureInputLayer object. You must also reshape your input data to have have two spatial dimensions. To remove spatial dimensions in the network, you can use a flattenLayer object. For example, replace featureInputLayer(numFeatures) with imageInputLayer([1 1 numFeatures])flattenLayerand reshape the input data with format "CB" to have format "SSCB" using dlX = dlarray(dlX,"CBSS") Alternatively, in R2021a, you can remove the input layers completely and instead use the syntax dlnet = dlnetwork(layers,dlX1,...,dlXn) when creating the dlnetwork object. In this case, you must manually perform any normalization calculations done by the input layers.

Summary: Internal error thrown when using Bus Element ports during HDL code generation
Description: If Bus Element ports are used at the top-level system for HDL code generation, an internal error may be encountered. Internal Error: Could not connect the blocks in the model '<modelname>'. Look for unconnected blocks or other warnings or errors and correct them to continue.  The message indicates a block may be unconnected, but the model can still be properly connected and hit this error.
Workaround: Instead of using Bus Element ports, use explicit Bus Selector blocks and pass the desired bus element via a regular output port.

Summary: Incorrect report of data dependency violation with asynchronous function-call initiator and Rate Transition block together with loop
Description: When both of the following conditions are met, you might see an incorrect report of a data dependency violation when updating or simulating your model: Either:?  The model contains an algebraic loop, and the model configuration parameter Diagnostics > Algebraic Loop  is set to none  or warning ; or  The model contains a loop formed by a graphical connection from the state port of a block with a state port, such as an Integrator block.   The model contains both an asynchronous function-call initiator and a Rate Transition block for that asynchronous rate in the same nonvirtual hierarchy as the loop.
Workaround: Separate the loop and the blocks with asynchronous rate into different nonvirtual hierarchies. For example, group the asynchronous function-call and corresponding Rate Transition block into an atomic subsystem.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might use an outdated enum definition when simulating or generating code for a referenced model
Description: Simulink might use the previous definition of a dynamic enumerated data type when all of the following conditions are true: The enumerated type is defined by using the?Simulink.defineIntEnumType ?function. The model hierarchy contains at least one referenced model. The Rebuild ?configuration parameter of the top model is set to If any changes in known dependencies detected . The enumerated type definition has changed since the model reference simulation or code generation target was last built.
Workaround: Set the Rebuild configuration parameter of the top model to If any changes detected.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Invalid HDL code can be generated from General CRC Syndrome Detector HDL Optimized
Description: Invalid HDL code that does not compile can be generated when the General CRC Syndrome Detector HDL Optimized is used in these conditions:?? Synchronous semantics are used (from State Control block) Global reset type is set to 'synchronous' Minimize Clock Enables is on  The generated code looks like the below VHDL code which has a leading ELSE statement with no preceding IF statement.   datain_tapregister1_process : PROCESS (clk)    BEGIN  ? IF clk'EVENT AND clk = '1' THEN  ? ? IF reset = '1' OR end_valid = '1' THEN  ? ? ? datain_tapreg_1 <= to_unsigned(0, 32);  ? ? IF validIn = '1' THEN  ? ? ? ELSE   ? ? ? ? datain_tapreg_1 <= dataIn_unsigned;  ? ? ? END IF;  ? ? END IF;  ? END PROCESS datain_tapregister1_process;

Summary: pspectrum uses very low minimum threshold when plotting
Description: The pspectrum  function generates a plot with improper z -axis scaling when called without output arguments and without specifying the MinThreshold ?argument. The function chooses an unnecessarily small value for the minimum power, and as a result the power values do not occupy the entire range of the colormap. This code illustrates the bug: load batsignalpspectrum(batsignal,1/DT,'spectrogram')  The minimum spectral power displayed in the graph is lower than ¨C140 dB, as shown by the color bar.
Workaround: Use the MinThreshold?name-value argument to produce a spectrogram with a specified minimum value that does not display unwanted background content.This example uses pspectrum to plot a spectrogram with an adequate minimum threshold:https://www.mathworks.com/help/signal/ug/practical-introduction-to-time-frequency-analysis.html#TimeFrequencyAnalysisExample-2
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: TensorRT code generation for a network containing a pattern of?convolution, ReLU, and addition layers?might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Consider a network containing?convolution2dLayer ,?reluLayer , and?additionLayer ?in this pattern:  ? ? ? ? ? ? _________|_________  ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|  ? ? ? ? ?conv? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? layer? ? ?  ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|  ? ? ? ? ?relu? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? |  ? ? ? ? ? ?|_____addition______|  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ?? ?? Code generation for such a network using the TensorRT library might cause MATLAB to crash with a segmentation violation or assertion.  ?
Workaround: Update your existing MATLAB installations.Alternatively, replace?reluLayer?by an equivalent?leakyReluLayer?with zero-scaling and then proceed with code generation. For example, suppose that you have the following network architecture:?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? image_input?? ? ? ? ? ? _________|_________? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ?conv1? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?conv2? ? ?? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ?relu1? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ?|_____addition______|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|?? ? ? ? ? ? ? fully_connected?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?|? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? regression??The MATLAB code for this network is as follows:?lgraph = layerGraph( [? ? imageInputLayer([28 28 2],'Name','in','Normalization','none')? ? convolution2dLayer([3 3],2,'Padding', [1 1 1 1], 'Name','conv1')? ? reluLayer('Name', 'relu1')? ? convolution2dLayer([3 3],2,'Padding', [1 1 1 1], 'Name','conv2')? ? additionLayer(2,'Name','add')? ? fullyConnectedLayer(3,'Name','fc')? ? regressionLayer('Name', 'regOut')] );lgraph = disconnectLayers(lgraph, 'relu1', 'conv2');lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph, 'in', 'conv2');lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph, 'relu1', 'add/in2');To replace the layers, use the?replaceLayer?function.lgraph?= replaceLayer(lgraph,'relu1',leakyReluLayer(0,'Name','leakyrelu1'));In addition, if?the network?described by?lgraph?has already been trained and you would like to use it, you can reassemble it as:net = assembleNetwork(lgraph);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: If two enumeration classes have same name but reside in different packages, C++ code generation might crash or produce incorrect code
Description: If your MATLAB code uses two enumerations the have the same name but reside in different packages, C++ code generation might crash or produce incorrect code. If the code generator produces incorrect code, one of the enumeration types is replaced with the other one in the generated code. For example, consider these two MATLAB enumeration files. Both enumeration classes are named myEnum2 . One of them is placed inside the package moo . ./MyEnum2.m   classdef MyEnum2 < int16    enumeration        Value1(1)        Value2(2)    endend ./+moo/MyEnum2.m   classdef MyEnum2 < int16    enumeration        Value1(5)        Value2(6)    endend Consider the this MATLAB function: function [out, out2] = test2out = int32(MyEnum2.Value1);out2 = int32(moo.MyEnum2.Value1);end Generate C++ code for test2  by running these commands:? cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.TargetLang='C++';codegen test2 -report -c -config cfg The generated C++ code assigns the value of 1 to both out  and out2 , which is incorrect.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports decision objective for the last input index?of a Multiport switch block as Unsatisfiable.
Description: If a Simulink model contains a Multiport switch  block with following configuration:Number of data ports  set to 1 The data port is connected to the output of a?Mux ?block  then, Simulink Design Verifier always reports decision objective associated with the last input index?as Unsatisfiable  during the test generation analysis, even if it ?can potentially be covered by a simulation. Similarly, during dead logic analysis Simulink Design Verifier always reports this objective as Dead Logic .
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Error thrown from coder.mapping.internal.CodeMappingCopier.CacheRootIOCodeMappings
Description: When generating code for a subsystem, you may experience an error originating from  coder. mapping. internal. CodeMappingCopier. CacheRootIOCodeMappings.? The stack trace may appear similar to the following:  Error in coder. mapping. internal. CodeMappingCopier. CacheRootIOCodeMappings  Error in coder. internal. ss2mdl  Error in coder. internal. ss2mdl?  Error in rtwbuild - Show complete stack trace?

Summary: High-Integrity check MATLAB code with logical operators and functions does not analyze external MATLAB files at secondary and further levels
Description: The Model Advisor check? MATLAB code with logical operators and functions ? (ID:?mathworks.hism.himl_0010 ) does not analyze and flag the violations inside external MATLAB files at secondary and further levels.

Summary: ROS node generation fails for ROS messages with fields containing reserved MATLAB identifiers
Description: ROS node generation fails when a ROS message field contains reserved identifiers in MATLAB  For example, geometry_msgs/Vector3Stamped definition has a member Vector       % This represents a Vector3 with reference coordinate frame and timestamp std_msgs/Header Header  Vector3 Vector  The word Vector  is a reserved Simulink word.  A snap shot of error message when generating ROS Node from a Simulink model containing this ROS message is shown below:  <snip> ? Unable to generate code using bus object 'SL_Bus_<modelname>_geometry_msgs_Vector3Stamped' because it has an element named Vector which conflicts with a programming language reserved identifier.  </snip>?

Summary: Unable to select custom storage class when name is a partial match of built-in storage class
Description: When you create a custom storage class by using the Custom Storage Class Designer, you cannot select that storage class in the Simulink.Signal  or Simulink.Parameter  property dialog box when the name of the storage class is a partial match of a built-in storage class. For example, when you select the custom storage class Export  from the drop-down list, the resulting selected storage class is ExportedGlobal .
Workaround: Use the command-line API to set the storage class for Simulink signals and parameters.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Simulink blocks with fixed-point input might report incorrect relational boundary coverage results
Description: If your model contains a block with fixed-point inputs, you might get incorrect coverage results. For example, consider a Switch block whose control input signal is fixdt(1,3,0) , with threshold u > 3 ,  and data input signals 1  and 4 , respectively. For this case, the expected result is trigger - threshold = -4 , but the coverage report shows that +LSB  (which is +1 ) is satisfied instead:  trigger - threshold? 33%  ? ? -LSB? 0/  2  ? ? ? 0 ? 0/  2  ? ? +LSB? 2/  2
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: Error calling multiple Simulink compiler CTF files that are deployed in MATLAB production server
Description: Except for the first time you call a deployed Simulink simulation (CTF files), subsequent calls to a Simulink simulation cause MATLAB production server to?crash. The stack trace for the crash report indicates a failure in creating a Simulink.op.ModelOperatingPoint ?object.

Summary: High-Integrity check Data type and size of condition expressions results in Abnormal Exit
Description: The Model Advisor check Data type and size of condition expressions  (ID: mathworks.hism.himl_0011 ) may result in abnormal exit  on Model Advisor UI when run on a Simulink model containing test harness.

Summary: Simulink Real-Time model build generates error message license check failed
Description: During the model build, Simulink Real-Time checks for the installed QNX Neutrino tools license in the Simulink Real-Time Target Support Package. If the support package is not installed or if an issue during support package install caused an incomplete installation, model builds can fail with the build log error message:license check failed

Summary: uavLidarPointCloudGenerator object properties change when loaded from MAT file
Description: When you load a saved uavLidarPointCloudGenerator  object from a MAT file, the loaded object has all its properties reset to their default values.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using SIL/PIL Manager in?Automated Verification or Simulation Only mode
Description: MATLAB might crash when using the SIL/PIL Manager in?Automated Verification  or Simulation Only ?mode for a model hierarchy with at least three levels of hierarchy.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink model containing a Demux block connected to a Stateflow chart might generate incorrect code
Description: Simulink model containing a Demux block connected to a Stateflow chart might generate incorrect code as shown below: RootModel_P.ConstantValue[-127]  This issue is caused due to integer overflow in the matrix index.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: SimBiology project saved in R2019b cannot be opened in later releases
Description: A SimBiology project saved in R2019b with empty plot matrix plots cannot be opened in later releases.
Workaround: To workaround this bug, re-open the project in R2019b, delete all empty plot matrix plots and re-save.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB may freeze when a real-time application is stopped
Description: When a real-time application is stopped, MATLAB may freeze for a long time or crash?when any one of the following conditions is true: The ExportToBaseWorkspace  option is set before starting the Simulink Real-Time application. The external mode simulation is started by using the?Run on Target ?button in the Simulink Editor and the Signal Logging  option is checked in the Data Import/Export section of the Model Configuration Parameters.
Workaround: To avoid freezing or crashing MATLAB when stopping simulation of a real-time application use any of the following:Disable ExportToBaseWorkspace option before you start the real-time application.Disable the Signal logging?option in the Data Import/Export section of the Model Configuration Parameters when running external mode simulation. This disables all logging within the model.For release R2021a and later,?set the Record Mode option within settings of the Simulink Data Inspector to View during simulation only. The data for signals is not available after the application run in the Simulink Data Inspector or Base Workspace when using this Record Mode option.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect AUTOSAR?IncludedDataTypeSet?entries exported for Simulink enumerated types with custom storage class Exported Global
Description: Incorrect AUTOSAR IncludedDataTypeSet ?entries are exported for Simulink enumerated types that meet all of the following conditions. The enumerated types are: Configured with custom storage class Exported Global . Internal to the component model. Not present on the software component interface.  For each enumerated type, the exported AUTOSAR data type and generated Rte_Type.h  data type definition incorrectly refer to the data type of a member of the enumerated type.

Summary: remove method of SimData does not remove data
Description: For most types of queries, the remove  method does not remove any data. Instead, it returns a SimData  object with all the data present in the input object.
Workaround: Use a different SimData method to create the desired SimData object. For example, use the removebyname method to specify which data columns to be removed from SimData. Alternatively, invert the query and use the select method to specify the data to keep.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: convertToGUIDECallbackArguments function throws an error when running an app migrated from GUIDE to App Designer
Description: After migrating certain GUIDE apps to App Designer, running the app can result in the following error from within the?convertToGUIDECallbackArguments ?function:  Unrecognized method, property, or field 'Type' for class 'matlab.graphics.shape.internal.ScribePeer'   This error is most often encountered when the App Designer app contains a?UIAxes ? component.

Summary: In parallel model build where model blocks contain Speegoat fixed FPGA blocks, error occurs fl:filesystem:PathNotFound
Description: In a parallel model build where the model includes model blocks that contain Speegoat fixed FPGA blocks, an error occurs?fl:filesystem:PathNotFound .  When Speedgoat FPGA blocks are using a prebuilt FPGA bitstream, the parallel build encounters a problem finding the bitstream at the end of the model build.
Workaround: The model build completes successfully when all of these conditions apply:The parallel build feature is disabled.The slprj?folder for the build is deleted, forcing a complete rebuild.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when logical variable is initialized using if-elseif-else containing a call to error()
Description: MATLAB Coder might generate incorrect code if your MATLAB code satisfies all  of these conditions: A boolean variable is initialized using an if-elseif-else  tree. Two of the branches of the if-elseif-else  tree contain a single assignment of a literal true  or false  to the boolean variable. The other branch of the if-elseif-else  tree contains an unconditional call to error() . The boolean variable is read later in the program to determine the condition of another if  statement.  When the issue happens, the later if  statement that reads the boolean variable is incorrectly simplified assuming that the variable always takes on the value true  or false . For example, in the following program intermediate  is initialized in an if-elseif-else  tree: function out = issue_2492753(selector)    if selector == 1intermediate = false;    elseif selector == 2intermediate = true;    elseerror('Unknown selector');    end    temp = 100;    if ~intermediatetemp = 1000;    end    out = temp.^2;end When code is generated for the function issue_2492753  by running this command: codegen -config:lib -args 0?issue_2492753  In the generated code for?issue_2492753, the program is incorrectly simplified assuming intermediate always gets assigned the value false : double issue_2492753(double selector){  (void)selector;  return 10000.0;} The correct generated code is: double issue_2492753(double selector){  int a;  a = 100;  if (selector == 1.0) {    a = 1000;  }  return a * a;}
Workaround: Handle the error check and variable initialization in independent if trees. Here is a modification of the example program issue_2492753:function out = issue_2492753(selector)    if selector ~= 1 || selector ~= 2error('Unknown selector');    end    if selector == 1intermediate = false;    elseintermediate = true;    end    temp = 100;    if ~intermediatetemp = 1000;    end    out = temp.^2;endNote that the error check is performed before an if-else statement that initializes?intermediate.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Codegeneration limitations for tcpclient not documented
Description: Propertiesand methods of tcpclient  added in R2020b do not support code generation using MATLAB Coder and code generation with a MATLAB Function block. The following tcpclient  properties and methods do not support code generation: Properties  NumBytesAvailable  NumBytesWritten  ByteOrder  UserData  Terminator  BytesAvailableFcn  BytesAvailableFcnCount  BytesAvailableFcnMode  ErrorOccurredFcn    Methods  readline  readbinblock  writeline  writebinblock  writeread  flush  configureTerminator  configureCallback     Forexample, the following code does not support code generation.   functioncodegentcpclient()    t =tcpclient("127.0.0.1",4000);   t.ByteOrder= "big-endian";   end   >>codegen codegentcpclient   ??? Class'tcpclient' does not have a property with name 'ByteOrder'.   Error in==> codegentcpclient Line: 3 Column: 1   Codegeneration failed: View Error Report   Error usingcodegen (line 223)

Summary: Error that occurs during real-time application execution disables the UDP send socket
Description: When an error occurs while sending data during real-time application execution, the error disables the UDP send socket. The error description indicates the issue disabling the socket. A disabled socket cannot be re-enabled during application execution.
Workaround: Stop the application. Resolve the issue that disabled the UDP send socket. Re-start the real-time application.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier does not generate tests for relational boundary objectives when Add Tests for Missing Coverage is clicked in Simulink Test Manager
Description: When Simulink Design Verifier is invoked from Simulink Test Manager by clicking Add Tests for Missing Coverage  button, relational boundary coverage objectives are not recognized for the tests generation.

Summary: Model Advisor check?Check for optimal bus virtuality?does not display warning for subsystem boundaries
Description: Model Advisor check?Check for optimal bus virtuality  (id:?mathworks.design.OptBusVirtuality) ? does not flag virtual bus signal crossing a subsystem boundary.

Summary: Coverage simulation with a filter applied is very slow
Description: If you apply a coverage filter to a model using the Coverage filter filename  parameter or CovFilter   command line parameter and then simulate the model, the simulation is slower than normal. This performance regression is especially noticeable for very large models.
Workaround: Avoid using the CovFilter parameter. Instead, apply the coverage filter after analysis. For example, apply the filter to the cvdata object.set_param('MyModel', 'CovFilter', '');?cvd = cvsim('MyModel');?cvd.filter = 'MyFilter.cvf';?This approach is especially useful if you are attempting to collect cumulative coverage aggregated from the results of multiple simulations and only need to see filtering applied to this cumulative coverage result.? Rather than applying filtering (via the?CovFilter parameter) during every simulation, apply filtering only to the final cumulative cvdata object. This significantly reduces the number of coverage filtering operations that need to be performed.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB does not destroy MATLAB objects on the call stack after a runtime error occurs in models containing MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks
Description: When a run-time error stops the simulation of a model that contains MATLAB Function or Stateflow blocks that use MATLAB as the action language, MATLAB does not destroy temporary objects on the function call stack.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB crashes when saving a project using the "Save As" option from any of the MATLAB Compiler apps?on macOS 10.15
Description: MATLAB crashes when saving a project using the "Save As " option from any of the MATLAB Compiler apps?on macOS 10.15. This issue occurs due to a bug in the macOS "Save As " dialog.?? The crash occurs when the following conditions are met: The suggested name in the "Save As "  dialog is not altered. A file matching the suggested name already exists in the default location the "Save As " ? dialog is pointing to.? The option to overwrite the existing file is selected when prompted.?
Workaround: There are two options to workaround the crash:Alter the suggested name for the project file in the "Save As"?dialog when prompted for a name.Update the version of macOS from 10.15 to 11.0.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated using GPU Coder or MATLAB Coder for deep neural networks containing averagePooling2DLayer with PaddingValue property set to mean?produces incorrect results
Description: Deep learning code generation for averagePooling2DLayer ? supports only zero padding of the input by setting the layer 'PaddingValue'?  property to 0. If you try to pad the input with the mean of the pooling region by setting the 'PaddingValue'  property is to 'mean' , the? ?code generator proceeds with code generation without throwing any errors. This incorrect code might produce different inference results when compared with MATLAB. For example, the code generated for Network 1 produces correct results but that for Network 2 produces incorrect results. Network 1?  ? ? ? layers = [imageInputLayer([30 30 3],"Name","imageinput",'Mean',0)? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? averagePooling2dLayer(5,"Name","pool","Padding",[1 1 1 1],"Stride",[3 3])? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? regressionLayer];  ? ? ? net1 = SeriesNetwork(layers); ?? Network 2  ? ? ? layers = [imageInputLayer([30 30 3],"Name","imageinput",'Mean',0)? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? averagePooling2dLayer(5,"Name","pool","PaddingValue","mean","Padding",[1 1 1 1],"Stride",[3 3])? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? regressionLayer];  ? ? ? net2 = SeriesNetwork(layers);  This issue can also be seen when generating deep learning code using MATLAB Coder.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Parameters are evaluated incorrectly when starting with double literal followed by more than approximately 4100 whitespace characters followed by additional operations
Description: When setting a block parameter to a value consisting of a double literal followed by more than approximately 4100 characters of whitespace (depending on the length of the double literal) followed by subsequent operations, the block ignores the subsequent operations.? This issue applies to any block numerical parameter.
Workaround: Ensure that the first 4096 characters of a block parameter include code in addition to the double literal and whitespace. ?For example, either of the following produces correct results:>> set_param(gcb, 'Value', ['0 + 1.5' repmat(' ', 1, 5000) '+ 7'])>> set_param(gcb, 'Value', ['1.5 + 0' repmat(' ', 1, 5000) '+ 7'])??
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Generating coverage report takes very long time
Description: It takes a very long time to generate a coverage report, especially when attempting to generate one report for multiple cvdata  objects, such as in this example: cvhtml('covReport', cvdata1, cvdata2, cvdata3, cvdata4);?
Workaround: To generate a report showing the total cumulative coverage result accumulated from multiple simulations, do not pass multiple cvdata as input arguments to cvhtml command. Instead, sum the cvdata objects from each individual simulation together and then, pass this cumulative result as an? input argument to cvhtml.?cvdataSum = cvdata1 + cvdata2 + cvdata3 + cvdata4;cvhtml('covReport_Cumulative', cvdataSum);?
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: ARXML ImplementationDataTypes and Rte_Type.h type definitions are not generated for some AUTOSAR lookup tables
Description: Building an AUTOSAR model that contains a lookup table block does not generate the expected ARXML ImplementationDataType  descriptions and Rte_Type.h  type definitions if any of the following conditions exists. In the lookup table block dialog box: Breakpoints specification  is set to Even spacing . Breakpoints specification  is set to Explicit values ?and at least one of the breakpoint parameters is not mapped to an AUTOSAR parameter. Number of table dimensions  is greater than 3.  In the lookup table object dialog box: Breakpoints specification ?is set to Reference? and at least one of the breakpoint parameters is not mapped to an AUTOSAR parameter. Number of table dimensions ?is greater than 3.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier analysis on a subsystem fails if model parameter SolverPrmCheckMsg is disabled
Description: If SolverPrmCheckMsg  model parameter is disabled then Simulink Design Verifier analysis on any subsystem inside that model fails with the following error message: Changing property 'SolverPrmCheckMsg' is not allowed .
Workaround: This issue can be avoided by using either of the following two workarounds:Ensure that the?SolverPrmCheckMsg model parameter is enabled.Generate subsystem harness using Simulink Test and invoke Simulink Design Verifier analysis on that harness model.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Code generation for smoothdata requires a nanflag option when nonfinite support is disabled
Description: Code generation for smoothdata ?might be ?unsuccessful if both these conditions are true: Nonfinite support is turned off for code generation. Specifically, in a coder.codeConfig  or coder.EmbeddedCodeConfig  object, SupportNonFinite  is set to false . Alternatively, in the MATLAB Coder App, in the project build settings, on the Speed   tab, the Support nonfinite numbers  check box is cleared.? The?nanflag  option is not specified for the smoothdata  function.?  In such situations, if you generate code for smoothdata , you might get the following error:  Error Message:  ??? Expression could not be reduced to a constant.? Error in ==> smoothNumericArray Line: 51 Column: 23?
Workaround: Explicitly specify a nanflag option when generating code for smoothdata with non-finite support turned off.?For example:?smoothdata(data,'includenan');

Summary: MATLAB crashes when opening or loading a System Composer model that references another model
Description: MATLAB crashes when opening or loading a top-level System Composer model, when all of the following are true: The System Composer model has one or more reference components. The ports on the model referenced by one or more reference components have been renamed. All models are saved, closed, and then reopened.

Summary: Zooming on?timescope?causes signals to disappear
Description: In the timescope  window, when you zoom in on multiple signals with different sample rates, the data for the first signal disappears.

Summary: Simulink.LookupTable?property dialog boxdisplays incorrect data in tabular interface for n-D lookup table after row or column is deleted and view is switched to another 2-D slice
Description: Simulink might incorrectly display data in thetabular interface of the?Simulink.LookupTable  property dialog box for an n-D (greater than 2-D)lookup table when you:  Delete a row or column. Change the view to a 2-D slice.   The deleted row or column might redisplay.

Summary: Incorrect handling of linear term cancellation in conditional statements
Description: Inside a conditional statement, linear term cancellation is not performed, which might result in the network either being differentiated more times than necessary or remaining a high-index problem. For example, in the following equation  ? ? ?? x == if u > 0.0, y - y + z else z end;   if linear term cancellation is not performed, the solver concludes that the equation depends on x, y, and z (instead of just x and z). As a result, the solver might draw the wrong conclusion about the index of the problem, and the model might fail to initialize correctly, or the simulation might fail to converge.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur if grounded Function-Call Subsystem with IC specified on subsystem's outport is present inside model reference block
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: The Function-Call Subsystem is present inside model reference block.? Non-zero initial condition is specified on Function-Call Subsystem's outport.? The outport with initial condition is connected to model reference block's outport.? The Function-Call Subsystem is fed by Ground block.?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Results for nested suites cannot be exported under certain conditions on Windows
Description: Results for nested suites cannot be exported on Windows if all of these conditions exist: There are test nested suites. An inner nested suite has iterations. Coverage is enabled.??  This error occurs: Relationship ID already exists

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with local block outputs enabled and a Zero-Order Hold block with multiple rates inside a For Each subsystem
Description: Incorrect generated code is possible for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The? Enable local block outputs  model?configuration parameter is selected. A Zero-Order Hold block is inside a For Each subsystem  The Zero-Order Hold block's input and output ports have different sample times. The Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  model?configuration parameter is selected.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Copy block with the Exclude this block from Block Reduction optimization?parameter selected between the Zero-Order Hold block and the For Each subsystem output.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: JMAAB check for jm_0012?displays abnormal exit warning for non-default sub-id selection
Description: Model Advisor?check for?jm_0012  (Usage restrictions of events and broadcasting events ) displays abnormal exit warning  when sub-id a1  is selected. The check also inappropriately displays warnings of sub-id a1  when sub-id a2  is selected, and warnings of sub-id a3  when sub-id a2  is selected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with a function-call subsystem that has feedback loop of output going into the input
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of the following conditions:?? The output of a function-call subsystem is fed back to the input. The function-call subsystem is executed using a Function-Call Generator block.  In the generated code, one or more temporary variables might be incorrectly optimized out of the for  loop.

Summary: Stateflow charts inside Simulink based states might execute during actions on entry
Description: Stateflow charts in models using variable step solvers might experience unexpected behavior if the chart contains a Simulink based state, and that Simulink based state models a subsystem with a Stateflow chart in it.? When the Stateflow chart inside the Simulink based state contains both?entry  and during  actions, and during this step the state becomes active,  the during  actions are unexpectedly executed on entry.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect HDL code generation for a subsystem in the presence of Bus signals with fixed-point datatypes configured with 'Slope and Bias'.
Description: For a subsystem that has Bus signals with the fixed-point datatypes, the generated HDL code might be incorrect when you specify the fixed-point datatype with 'Slope and Bias'.?? HDL code generation does not supports fixed-point datatype specification with 'Slope and Bias'. For more information on the fixed-point datatypes support in HDL Coder, open the following documentation link:?? https://www.mathworks.com/help/hdlcoder/ug/supported-data-types-and-matlab-operators-and-control-flow-statements.html
Workaround: Change the specified datatype to 'Word length' and 'Fraction length' format.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur if reusable subsystem is connected to Conditional Subsystem's outport through Selector block with storage class on it's inport.
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: Model contains a reusable subsystem. The model configuration parameter Buffer for reusable subsystems  (BufferReusableBoundary ) is selected.? Reusable Subsystem's outport is connected to a Conditional Subsystem's outport through Selector block.? Number of elements present in Selector block's output signal are less than number of elements present on it's input signal.? The input signal to Selector block has storage class with initial value specified on signal object.?
Workaround: Clear the Buffer for reusable subsystems (BufferReusableBoundary) model configuration parameter.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: PLC Coder could generate incorrectly typed matrix address expressions
Description: PLC Coder may generate code that has incorrect matrix address expressions when such addresses are not constants. This may lead to errors while compiling the code in the target IDE.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Calling step function?of a persistent instance of?generalizedInverseKinematics?object fails to generate code
Description: Code generation fails when the?step ?function of? a persistent instance of the?generalizedInverseKinematics ?object? is called outside the? if isempty(gik) ?conditional block in the following pattern:  persistent robot gik if isempty(robot)  ? % Create rigidBodyTree, "robot"?  ? robot =  twoJointRigidBodyTree("row");  end  if isempty(gik)   ? % Create a?generalizedInverseKinematics?solver instance "gik"  ? gik = generalizedInverseKinematics('RigidBodyTree', robot);  end  % Obtain a solution configuration "qSol" given "q0" as an initial guess.?  qSol = gik(q0);        end  The following error message is seen:? Cannot allocate this handle object. For code generation, if a persistent variable references a handle object, you can create the handle object only once per program.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when building a model with the parameter?RollThreshold higher than the number of iterations of a For Each?Subsystem nested in a For Iterator?Subsystem
Description: Consider a model that has a For Each?Subsystem nested within a For Iterator?Subsystem. If the number of iterations of the nested For Each?Subsystem  is less than the RollThreshold  configuration setting of the model, then building such a model might result in a crash.? Note that not all models that meet the above conditions will result in a crash. The possibility of a crash is subject to the modeling semantics of the nested For Each?S ubsystem.
Workaround: Set the RollThreshold to a value less than or equal to the number of iterations of the nested For Each?Subsystem.For example, if the For Each?Subsystem is supposed to execute 4 times, then? set the RollThreshold to a value less than or equal to 4.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect lane boundary information of drivingScenario actors
Description: The laneBoundaries function of a driving.scenario.Actor object returns incorrect curvature values for the left and right lane boundaries.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might log wrong answer at root output port with automatic unit conversion
Description: Simulink automatic unit conversion might log the wrong answer at a root output port during simulation when all of the following are true: The logging SaveFormat  is set to Dataset .  The signal before the root output port is branched.  The root output port unit does not match the source signal unit and the units are convertible.  Simulink automatically converts units at the root output port.  At least one of the other branches has a different unit from this root output port.
Workaround: Manually insert a Unit Conversion block at the root output port instead of relying on automatic unit conversion.
