Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Recovered Stateflow Charts block inserted when Subsystem Reference block added to test harness
Description: When you add a Subsystem Reference block outside the component under test in a test harness, and that referenced subsystem model contains Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks,?you might see a Recovered Stateflow Charts block inserted when you reopen the harness. If you delete the corrupted charts manually and then simulate the test harness, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation result for a model with Variant blocks and Block reduction enabled
Description: When a model is composed of Variant blocks, the inactive part is removed from the block diagram during Normal mode simulation and may enable dead code path elimination to remove more blocks. In Normal mode simulation, the removal could separate a connected block group into multiple unconnected block groups. In the ERT generated code, the inactive part is not removed from the block diagram. This leads to different sorted order than the order in the generated code.

Summary: Memory leak in SUNDIALS solver can slow system performance
Description: When simulating a SimBiology model with the SUNDIALS solver, allocated computer memory is not released. This results in an increased memory consumption that can slow down computations during parameter estimation and repeated simulations.
Workaround: To release allocated memory, close and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Analysis terminates with an?Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier abstract interpretation back end message
Description: Simulink Design Verifier Test Generation analysis terminates with an Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier abstract interpretation back end  message when the following two conditions are met:The model has partial matrix references. The indices used in partial references are constant which are not used anywhere else.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model hierarchy in which one model has a single instance of a reusable library subsystem and another model has multiple instances of the same reusable library subsystem
Description: Incorrect generated code might occur for a model hierarchy containing two models that meet all of these conditions:?Model A references a reusable library subsystem once for a given specialization such as dimensions or data type. Model B references the same reusable library subsystem at least twice with the same specialization. The reusable library subsystem contains a block whose output is determined to be constant.  The generated code might have the wrong number of elements in the initialization code for the constant block outputs structure relating to the reusable library subsystems in Model B. The target compiler might not catch this incorrect code and produce an error. This code is an example of the constant block output structure for the reusable library subsystem:  typedef struct {? const int32_T Width;?????????? } ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG;  This code is an example of the constant block output structure for Model B:  /* Invariant block signals (default storage) */typedef struct {? ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG RLS1;????? ? ConstB_RLS_5QZ55YRG RLS2;????? } ConstB;   This code an example of the incorrect initialization of the model constant block output structure including the reusable library subsystem constant block output structure in Model B: const ConstB rtConstB = {? {??? 4,???????????????????????????? ??? 3????????????????????????????? ? }? ? {??? 4,????????????????????????????? ??? 3?????????????????????????????? ? }}; ?
Workaround: You can either make the reusable library subsystem a nonreusable library subsystem, or in all models that reference the reusable library subsystem, override the reusable library subsystem settings to break reuse with other models.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Code Generation Readiness tool (coder.screener) incorrectly identifies certain functions and classes as unsupported for code generation
Description: When the Code Generation Readiness tool analyzes MATLAB code, it attempts to identify toolbox functions and classes that are not supported for code generation. This tool incorrectly identifies certain MATLAB function and class names as unsupported. You can run the Code Generation Readiness tool at the command line by using the coder.screener  function, from the current folder browser by using the context menu, or in the MATLAB Coder app. This issue affects all of these workflows. Functions and classes that are misidentified for the affected releases (R2019b to R2021a) are listed below. These lists are cumulative such that all names that are misidentified in an earlier release are also misidentified in later releases. In R2019b, these names are incorrectly identified as unsupported for code generation: robotics.BinaryOccupancyGridrobotics.AimingConstraintrobotics.CartesianBoundsrobotics.JointPositionBoundsrobotics.OrientationTargetrobotics.PoseTargetrobotics.PositionTargetrobotics.PurePursuitrobotics.VectorFieldHistogramrobotics.DubinsConnectionrobotics.DubinsPathSegmentrobotics.GeneralizedInverseKinematicsfusion.AsyncMARGGPSFuserfusion.ErrorStateIMUGPSFuserfusion.MARGGPSFuserfusion.NHConstrainedIMUGPSFuserrobotics.InverseKinematicsrobotics.LidarSLAMrobotics.PRMrobotics.OccupancyGridrobotics.OdometryMotionModelcustomAntennaElementisotropicAntennaElementrobotics.PoseGraphrobotics.PoseGraph3Drobotics.Raterobotics.ReedsSheppConnectionrobotics.ReedsSheppPathSegmentrobotics.ResamplingPolicyrobotics.RigidBodyrobotics.Jointrobotics.RigidBodyTreerobotics.ParticleFilter  In R2020a, these additional names are incorrectly identified: phased.BackscatterPedestrianrobotics.LikelihoodFieldSensorModelrobotics.MonteCarloLocalization  In R2020b, these additional names are incorrectly identified: robotics.OccupancyMap3D  In R2021a, these additional names are incorrectly identified: phased.BarrageJammerphased.ConstantGammaClutterphased.gpu.ConstantGammaClutterphased.PulseCompressionLibraryphased.PulseWaveformLibraryphased.TwoRayChannelphased.WidebandTwoRayChannelrobotics.ros.Publisherrobotics.ros.Raterobotics.ros.Subscriber
Workaround: Depending on the context in which you use the Code Generation Readiness tool, do one of the following:?In the standalone Code Generation Readiness tool, ignore the misidentified function or class name.?In the Code Generation Readiness section of the MATLAB Coder App, dismiss the misidentified function or class names by clicking the Next button which advances the app to the next section.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: The value of 'ChildConfigSet' is invalid?error from rerun of parallel simulation or parallel code generation
Description: The following steps might result in an error: For a model reference hierarchy, run a parallel simulation or generate code in parallel.? Make a change to one of the models that alters the model interface without affecting the generated code. For example, change the value of an interface variable. Run the parallel simulation or generate code in parallel again. Run the parallel simulation or generate code?in parallel once more.  ?Step 4 might produce this error: The value of 'ChildConfigSet' is invalid

Summary: JMAAB check for db_0129?displays an incorrect warning for State transition tables
Description: The?Sub-ID d? of Model Advisor?check?db_0129 ( db_0129_d:?Check orientation of transition lines)?reports incorrect warning?transitions are not vertical or horizontal and/ or diagonal for flow chart loops ?for transitions in State Transition tables.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Default code mapping in a shared data dictionary may be reset to default
Description: When you save a default code mapping in a shared Simulink data dictionary, some mappings may be reset to the default values when you load the shared Embedded Coder Dictionary in another MATLAB session.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect Unsatisfiable objectives for Relational Operator blocks where vector inputs are compared to a minimum or maximum values for a particular data type
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect Unsatisfiable (or Dead Logic) objectives  when all the following conditions are met:Relational Operator block? Signal with non-scalar dimensions?? Exactly one of the inputs to the block is a constant value that is either the minimum or the maximum value for that data type, and this makes the condition always true or always false.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: TCP Client block requires parameter values for Client IP address and Client Port
Description: When using the block for TCP communications, the TCP Client block requires parameter values for the Client IP address and the Client Port. This requirement produces some operational issues: Even when these parameter values are not necessary or not known, the parameter values must be specified values for the Client IP address and the Client Port. Because the TCP Client socket remains in the TIME_WAIT state for approximately 1 minute, the real-time application cannot be restarted soon after it stops. If the model is restarted during the wait period, the target computer issues an error:? TCP Client Connect Error:?Address already in use (248)

Summary: The SimBiology model diagram can sometimes freeze, leading to a loss of the diagram layout
Description: Under some conditions, typically when a computer is low in memory, and interactions with the model diagram are done quickly, it is possible to save a project with a misconfigured block, such as a species or reaction block.? Upon loading that project, the misconfigured block produces an error that prevents the entire diagram from loading in the app. In addition, if you attempt to save the project once more while under these conditions, the diagram information is lost and cannot be retrieved.

Summary: Failed model simulations result in incorrect scaling of first and total order indices reported by sbiosobol
Description: Failed model simulations result in incorrect scaling of the first and total order indices reported by?sbiosobol . If model simulations fail during the computation of Sobol indices, the reported Sobol indices are incorrectly scaled by the factor   s = number of non-failed model simulations / number of all model simulations .? If model simulations fail while executing sbiosobol , warnings about the failed model simulations are shown in the MATLAB command window. The relative importance ranking of Sobol indices is not affected by this bug, because all first and total order Sobol indices are scaled by the same factor.
Workaround: The scaling factor,?s, can be computed from the results, sobolResults, returned from sbiosobol as follows:  s = sum(all(sobolResults.SimulationInfo.ValidSample, 2)) / height(sobolResults.ParameterSamples);To correct the Sobol indices, divide the reported Sobol indices sobolResults.SobolIndices by s.However, this workaround does not correct the values of the Sobol indices stored on the?sobolResults. In particular, plotting the Sobol indices using the plot and bar methods still reports the incorrectly scaled Sobol indices.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Using the web command to view custom documentation?in the Help browser might result in a?Page Not Found?error
Description: When the Help browser is configured to view the documentation on the web, running the web  command provided by the Help Page Location dialog box to open the custom documentation results in a Page Not Found  error in the Help browser.
Workaround: To work around this issue, update the Help preferences to use the locally installed documentation: On the Home?tab, in the Environment?section, click Preferences.Select MATLAB > Help.Set the?Documentation Location?to?Installed locally.

Summary: Error when attempting to import a DOORS 9 requirements module
Description: When you attempt to import a module from IBM DOORS 9, an error message that says?Could not recognize the date/ time format...  might appear. ?The date and time string included in the error message shows "HH:mm:ss" inserted in between the module creation or modification date and time. The error prevents the requirements module from importing to Simulink Requirements. This only happens on some installations of DOORS. One known reported case was with DOORS version 9.5.2.4, but other versions may be affected.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Code inspection fails if a model contains multiple instances of a subsystem from the same library with a referenced model inside it
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails to verify the code generated from a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains multiple instances of a subsystem from the same library. The subsystem contains a referenced model.  Simulink Code Inspector is unable to uniquely map buffers in the model for the different subsystem instances to the corresponding buffers in the generated code. Code inspection fails with a "Failed to verify" ?status.

Summary: Objects not deleted after generating PDF reports
Description: When generating PDF documents, MATLAB Report Generator fails to delete some objects that are no longer needed .

Summary: Code generation may take a long time when too many mask variables are present a model
Description: HDL Code generation may be slow when there are large number of mask variables present in the model. The mask variables could be explicitly specified by the user at the subsystem level, or they could also be from?some Simulink block implementations.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect HDL code may be generated for a trigger subsystem with non-boolean trigger value
Description: When the Design Under Test (DUT) used for HDL Code generation has a triggered subsystem and the trigger port is driven by a signal of non-boolean datatype, generated HDL code is syntactically correct but logically incorrect.
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 6, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Triggered Subsystem that has no HDL optimizations configured, generates an incorrect HDL code
Description: For a triggered subsystem inyour design that has no HDL optimization configured on it, and when applied with the following settings, ˇ®Minimize Global ResetˇŻ isturned ˇ®OnˇŻ. ˇ®No-reset registers initializationˇŻis set either to ˇ®Generate initialization inside moduleˇŻ or ˇ®Generate anexternal scriptˇŻ.  HDL Coder generates an incorrect HDL code which results in a simulation mismatchwith testbench. No warning message is generated for this problem.?
Fixed In: R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when unit mismatch exists between?Simulink.Signal object and port
Description: When you update the diagram, MATLAB might crash while propagating data types and dimensions when:? A unit is specified on a Simulink.Signal  object assigned to a signal. The unit does not match the unit specified at the source or destination port of the signal.  The crash report might contain output similar to: [? 0] 0x00007ffc28512fe3 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00143331 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00122563[? 1] 0x00007ffc2850803d sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00098365 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00077597[? 2] 0x00007ffc2851bf41 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00180033 MLTerminate_sl_units_diagnostics+00159265[? 3] 0x00007ffc2853d9d1 sl_units_diagnostics.dll+00317905 slUnitTable::reportUnitMismatch+00000577
Workaround: Change the Unit?field of the Simulink.Signal object, or the source or destination port so that the units are the same.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Generated code contains usage of dynamic memory allocation when DynamicMemoryAllocation is disabled
Description: When generating code for a MATLAB function that contains: A binary operation where one of the operands is a concatenation expression. The concatenation expression contains bounded variable-size data.  MATLAB Coder might produce generated code using dynamic memory allocation even if the DynamicMemoryAllocation  configuration option is disabled. For example when generating code for the program:? function out = issue_2462856(val,n)    boundedData1 = generateBoundedData(val,n,2);    boundedData2 = generateBoundedData(2*val,n,1);    boundedData3 = generateBoundedData(4*val,n,1);    out = boundedData1 .* [boundedData2,boundedData3];endfunction out = generateBoundedData(val,n,dim2)    coder.inline('never');    assert(n <= 255);    out = val .* (1:n)';    out = repmat(out,[1,dim2]);end by using these MATLAB commands: cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.DynamicMemoryAllocation = 'off';codegen -config cfg issue_2462856 -args {1,1} the generated code incorrectly contains usages of emxArray  APIs that use dynamic memory allocation. See the call to?emxEnsureCapacity_real_T  in the following generated code snippet:[SNIP]  b_tmp_size = tmp_size[0];  loop_ub = tmp_size[0];  i = r->size[0] * r->size[1];  r->size[0] = tmp_size[0];  r->size[1] = 1;  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(r, i);  for (i = 0; i < b_tmp_size; i++) {    r->data[i] = tmp_data[i];  }[SNIP]? See also Bug Report ID 2511529 ?
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: When a ROS service client is called multiple times using the?call?function, successive response messages get appended and increase the array size continuously.
Description: If a field in the ROS service response message is of type array, the items of that array field get appended to existing data with every call. For example, FrameName  is an array field and it is assigned every time with a frame name as shown below:?% Assigned as 1x1 cell in the service call back function>> resp.FrameName{1} = 'ABC'; Using the?call ?function to call the service client 10 times, the correct response should be a 1x1 cell {'ABC'} .  But the size of?FrameName ?field increases with each call and reaches 10x1, as illustrated below:??>> resp.FrameName {'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}{'ABC'}

Summary: Launching Model Slicer to debug or inspect a Simulink Design Verifier objective displays an error when the model has a?Referenced Configuration Set
Description: In a model with Referenced Configuration Set, setting up Model Slicer for debugging or inspecting Simulink Design Verifier?objectives (Design Error Detection, Property Proving or Enhanced MCDC) fails due to the following error:?? Error using Simulink.SimulationInput/getSetParamObj  Model is using a configuration reference. Set 'EnableConfigSetRefUpdate' to 'on' to set the parameters on the referenced configuration set.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Text between angle brackets in MATLAB Function block code is missing from Simulink Web View
Description: Simulink Web View does not display the text between angle brackets (<> ) in MATLAB Function block code. For example, suppose a MATLAB Function block contains this code: function y = fcn(u)y = sprintf('<stdio.h>'); Simulink Web View displays: function y = fcn(u)y = sprintf('');

Summary: Crash when deleting a root-level Inport or Outport block from the PostLoadFcn model callback
Description: If a model with C or AUTOSAR code mapping has a model post-load function (PostLoadFcn) which deletes a?root-level Inport or Outport block, MATLAB might crash with a stack trace that looks like the following: ?[ 10] 0x00007ffccd387fd9 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+00425945 coderdictionary::getCoderDataSectionNames+00120729  [ 11] 0x00007ffccd37f99b C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\ codemapping_c.dll+00391579 coderdictionary::getCoderDataSectionNames+00086363  [ 12] 0x00007ffccd425d58 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+01072472 coderdictionary::ModelMappingBase::getInports+00000024?  [ 13] 0x00007ffccd3b311b C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020b\bin\win64\codemapping_c.dll+00602395 coderdictionary::CoderDictionaryMapping::getValidationErrMessage+00002187?

Summary: Mismatch in HDL and simulation outputs of the RS Decoder block for back-to-back input frames
Description: When you provide back-to-back input frames to the RS Decoder block, for a set of (N, K) RS codes such as (255, 239) and (127, 121), the generated HDL code may not match the simulation output.

Summary: Uninstalling a previous version of a toolbox that includes apps fails when multiple versions of the toolbox are installed
Description: If multiple versions of a toolbox that includes apps are installed, uninstalling a previous version of the toolbox fails silently.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation might occur for Simulink model when a data dictionary hierarchy is closed between simulations
Description: Simulation might result in a silent wrong answer when the following conditions are satisified: A model links to a data dictionary hierarchy. For example, a model links to adata dictionary, A.sldd , that links to data dictionary B.sldd .? The model uses a variable that is defined in the data dictionary hierarchy, but not in a dictionary that the model is directly linked to. For example, B.sldd .  ?Under these conditions, the silent wrong answer can occur when you take these actions:  Perform an update diagram or simulate the model. Close the data dictionaries by calling the function Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll() . Modify entries defined in B.sldd  that are used by the model. Perform an update diagram or simulate the model again.
Workaround: To prevent incorrect simulation you can take either of these actions:Avoid using Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll() between model compilations or simulations.Close model after calling Simulink.data.dictionary.closeAll().??
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Watermark image covers text content in PDF documents generated by MATLAB Report Generator
Description: In PDF documents generated by MATLAB Report Generator, a watermark image covers the text content unless the image is transparent. For example, in the PDF document generated by the following code, the sine graph watermark image covers some of the "Hello World" paragraphs. plot(sin(0:0.001:4*pi))print -dpng watermark.pngd = mlreportgen.dom.Document('test','pdf')open(d)d.CurrentPageLayout.Watermark = mlreportgen.dom.Watermark('watermark.png')for i = 1:20    append(d, mlreportgen.dom.Paragraph('Hello World'))endclose(d)rptview(d)

Summary: Performance regression may occur when generating code with Code interface packaging?parameter set to C++ class
Description: If the Code interface packaging ?model configuration parameter is set to C++ class , then there might be an observed increase in the time to generate code compared to previous versions of the product.

Summary: Simulink Blockset Designer error? "Unable to find the file $bdroot" when trying to open a sublibrary containing unsupported type of blocks
Description: If you add an unsupported type of block to a sublibrary, refresh the project and try to open the sublibrary again, Simulink throws an error: Unable to find the file $bdroot
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Missing commit error when using shallow Git repositories
Description: MATLAB Git integration does not support shallow Git repositories. Using shallow Git repositories in MATLAB causes missing commit ?errors to be displayed.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Build failure might occur when using C Function or C Caller blocks in model references
Description: When you try to run or update a model, Simulink might throw a build error when all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a model reference The referenced model contains a link instance to a C Function or C Caller block in a Simulink library The Simulink library has simulation target custom code specified
Workaround: Load the Simulink library containing C Function or C Caller blocks explicitly before simulating the affected model.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: PLC code generated for Stateflow models that contain temporal conditions inside IF statements may be incorrect
Description: Consider following two possible transitions in a Stateflow chart with:Case 1:  [condition && after(5, sec)]  Case 2:  [after(5,sec) and condition]?  Following PLC code will be generated for? these cases because of the issue: Case 1:  IF condition <> 0.0 THEN? ? temporalCounter_i1(timerAction := 2, maxTime := 5000);? ? IF temporalCounter_i1.done THEN? ? ? ?result := TRUE;? ? END_IF;END_IF;  Case 2:  temporalCounter_i1(timerAction := 2, maxTime := 5000);IF temporalCounter_i1.done THEN?? ? IF condition <> 0.0 THEN? ? ? ? result := TRUE;? ? END_IF;END_IF;  The code generated in both these cases is different, however, the simulation behavior is same as case 2 code for both the Stateflow transitions.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Writing data to Azure Blob Storage fails with remote connection error
Description: Writing data to Azure Blob Storage fails and returns the error: Remote connection failed. Check the connection and/or try again later.  For example, using writetable:  >> writetable(T, 'wasbs://container@account/path_to_file/file.txt', 'WriteVariableNames', 0)Error using table/write (line 241)Unable to write to: 'wasbs://container@account/path_to_file/file.txt'Remote connection failed. Check the connection and/or try again later.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Model configuration parameter Use memcpy for vector assignment is not respected
Description: To implement copy of vector assignments,?the code generator uses a?memcpy ?call if the number of bytes to copy exceeds the?Memcpy threshold (bytes) ?model configuration parameter and the?Use memcpy for vector assignment ?model configuration parameter is enabled. In rare circumstances,?the code generator emits?memcpy ?even though Use memcpy for vector assignment ?option is disabled.?Following is the generated code to expect when the?Use memcpy for vector assignment ?option is disabled. for (i = 0; i < 15; i++) { ? ?A[i] = B[i];  }? Instead, the code generator produces the following:? memcpy(&A[0],?&B[0],?15U * (sizeof(int32))); See also?Bug Report ID?2477020 .
Workaround: Set the?MemcpyThreshold (bytes)?parameter to a very high value such as?inf or intmax('int32'). For example:set_param(modelName, 'MemcpyThreshold', intmax('int32'));
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Coverage not recorded for referenced Subsystem simulated using a test harness
Description: Coverage is not recorded for a Subsystem model referenced in a test harness. Even if you enable coverage collection, no coverage results are returned.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier Compatibility check fails with error changing property '<parameterName>' is not allowed
Description: When Compatibility check or SLDV analysis is run on a model having locked (read only) configuration parameter, the operation fails with error:Changing property '<parameterName>' is not allowed  For example if compatibility check is run on a model having configuration parameter BusObjectLabelMismatch ?set to read only, compatibility check fails with error: Changing property 'BusObjectLabelMismatch' is not allowed
Workaround: Before running Simulink Design Verifier analysis, ensure that the parameter (e.g. BusObjectLabelMismatch,?MultiTaskRateTransMsg) mentioned in the error message is not marked as read-only.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB crashes with?Access Violation when assigning to expressions with @-symbol
Description: MATLAB crashes when assigning to a variable that uses `a@b` syntax: ?>> obj@Super = 2;  or  ?>> a@b.c = 1;?  The stack trace for this crash may have "CreateAndAssignMatrixByType " or "ILplace " at the top of the trace, for example: Stack Trace (from fault):[  0] 0x00007ffc7c46dc0a  bin\win64\libmwlxeindexing.dll+00121866 MathWorks::lxe::CreateAndAssignMatrixByType<double,6>+00000202 ...many more lines or?? Stack Trace (from fault):[? 0] 0x00007ffdb43dab54? bin\win64\pgo\libmwlxeindexing.dll+00240468 MathWorks::lxe::ILplace::~ILplace+00006128?
Workaround: Expressions using the @-symbol cannot be assigned to, and variable names in MATLAB cannot contain the @-symbol (see?Variable Names in MATLAB).To prevent the crash from occurring, remove all assignments to variables using `@`.? a@b = 2;? % Remove code like this, or refactor it to a variable name that doesn't include `@`In versions R2020b and beyond this code does not crash, but instead throws an error that assigning to variables using `@`? is not supported.

Summary: power_analyze function might return incorrect value for SourceBranchNames?if the model has only one source block
Description: If you call the power_analyze  function with the 'sort'  input argument inside a model that contains only one block defined as a source, the function might return an incorrect value for the?SourceBranchNames? output field.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may error out for models containing Stateflow Charts with exported functions that contain reads or writes to global variables
Description: In some cases, Simulink Design Verifier may error out for models containing Stateflow Charts with exported functions that contain reads or writes to global variables  When this error occurs, one of the following error messages will occur: Assert Type checker is unable to infer the type of Node '@'. Please provide the type explicitly.    Exception occurred while parsing cgel file.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: Unsuccessful update of target computer from R2020b to R2021a if development computer does not have Speedgoat R2021a I/O Blockset for Simulink Real-Time installed
Description: Update of the target computer software from R2020b to R2021a is unsuccessful if the development computer does not have the Speedgoat R2021a I/O Blockset for Simulink Real-Time installed. The target computer update fails to configure the Ethernet port for communication with the development computer.
Workaround: Use any one of these workarounds to regain proper operation of your target computer.Reinstall the target computer files by using the Speedgoat target migration tool.?Log into the target as root by using the monitor and keyboard on the target computer.Install the R2021a version of the Speedgoat I/O Blockset to your R2021a version of MATLAB and Simulink Real-Time.Switch from the status monitor to the system prompt. On the target computer use the keyboard and monitor to switch virtual consoles with <ctrl><alt>2. The status monitor uses console 1. Log in as user root and password root.Run the ifconfig utility to set the IP address on the host-target Ethernet port. The Baseline machine uses wm3 while Performance machines use wm0.? These are the names known to the target computer RTOS. Contact Speedgoat if you are not sure which port to configure. For example, to?configure Ethernet port wm0 to use IP address x.y.z.w on a Performance target computer, at the target computer system prompt, type:ifconfig wm0 x.y.z.w netmask 255.255.255.0After configuring the target computer, in the MATLAB Command Window, type:tg=slrealtime('yourtargetname'); tg.update Reboot the target computer.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when a reusable subsystem input connects to a Model block input which connects to a Simulink Function block
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a nonvirtual subsystem. The nonvirtual subsystem contains a Model block.? For the nonvirtual subsystem, the?Function packaging? parameter is set to?Reusable function .? One of the inputs to the?nonvirtual subsystem?connects to the Model block's input which connects to a Simulink Function block.? In the model configuration settings of the referenced model, the?Pass fixed-size scalar root inputs by value for code generation? configuration parameter is cleared (that is,? ModelReferencePassRootInputsByReference ?is set to 'off ').
Workaround: For the subsystem containing the referenced model, in the?Subsystem Block Parameters?dialog box, on the?Code Generation?tab, set Function packaging?as?Inline?or?Nonreusable.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Invalid ARXML exported when AUTOSAR mapping specifies C type qualifiers for static or constant memory elements typed by data types of category TYPE_REFERENCE
Description: AUTOSAR code generation exports invalid ARXML for a model in which AUTOSAR mapping specifies C type qualifiers for static or constant memory elements typed by data types of category TYPE_REFERENCE. In the ARXML for the affected AUTOSAR implementation data type, the tags ADDITIONAL-NATIVE-TYPE-QUALIFIER and IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE-REF appear in the wrong order. Re-importing the exported ARXML might generate an error that starts with: Failed to validate AUTOSAR XML file "reproduction.arxml":[ line: 8869, col:111 ]: cvc-complex-type.2.4.a: Invalid content was found starting with element 'IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE-REF'. ...

Summary: The Task Manager block does not trigger the correct task when a model includes both Interrupt Channel and Memory Channel blocks.
Description: When??a model includes both Interrupt Channel and Memory Channel blocks, and  The Memory Channel event port is connected directly to the?Task Manager The Interrupt Channel output is connected to the Task Manager  The following occurs:The task tied to the event originating in the?Interrupt Channel is never executed. The task tied to the event originating in the?Memory Channel??is executed for both events originating in the Memory Channel?and?Interrupt Channel blocks.
Workaround: When your model includes both Memory Channel and?Interrupt Channel, connect the Memory Channel event port to the Interrupt Channel, and not directly to the?Task Manager block.

Summary: The MATLAB High-integrity checks need Stateflow license to appear in the Model Advisor
Description: The Model Advisor checks for MATLAB High-integrity (hism.himl_****)  do not appear in the Model Advisor if Stateflow license is missing.

Summary: Generated A2L file has prerelease banner in general release
Description: The prerelease banner "Prerelease License - for engineering feedback and testing purposes?only. Not for sale." is present in the generated A2L file in the general release.

Summary: Simulink Check certification kit test suite reports Invalid Check ID
Description: The Simulink Check test suite contains four test cases that require Simulink Design Verifier.? If Simulink Design Verifier is not installed, the following test cases report Invalid Check ID : SLCHK_HI_C035 SLCHK_HI_C036 SLCHK_HI_Q090 SLCHK_HI_Q091?
Workaround: You can use either of these options as a workaround to this issue:Install Simulink Design Verifier and rerun the Simulink Check tests in the IEC Certification Kit? ? ? ? ? ? ORRemove these test cases from the trace matrix artifact (certkitiec_slchk_tests.xls)SLCHK_HI_C035SLCHK_HI_C036SLCHK_HI_Q090SLCHK_HI_Q091

Summary: Non-ASCII output from mexPrintf might be truncated on Windows
Description: Calling mexPrintf  with arguments that produce lengthy (>1000 characters) output containing non-ASCII characters might result in a truncated output on Windows.
Workaround: If this issue is encountered, there are several possible workarounds:Call mexPrintf with shorter arguments?Convert arguments to UTF-8 before calling mexPrintf.?

Summary: Cannot create a subsystem reference from ROS Simulink blocks
Description: Cannot create a subsystem reference from ROS Simulink blocks because of errors thrown in block mask dialog.  An example of the error message thrown when creating a subsystem from a ROS Blank message block is shown below:  Error evaluating 'MaskDialog' callback of ROS Blank Message block (mask) 'untitled/ Blank Message'.  Callback string is 'ros. slros. internal. block. MessageBlockMask. dispatch('entityTypeEdit', ?gcb);'

Summary: Simulink crashes when refreshing a model block that contains a non-existent protected model
Description: Simulink will crash when all of the following conditions are satisfied: There is a hierarchy of three or more models, each included in the previous model in the hierarchy using a Model block. The lowest level model is a protected model that has parameter arguments. The lowest level model is renamed or deleted. The highest level model is refreshed.?  MATLAB crashes with a stack trace that looks like: [? 0] 0x00007ff89ac56696 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+01009302 GraphRemoveBlockTemplate::useSLM3I+00245702 [? 1] 0x00007ff89b04bdcb Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+05160395 mdlref::GetExternalLibrarySet+00002891 [? 2] 0x00007ff89b0502e2 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+05178082 mdlref::GetExternalLibrarySet+00020578 [? 3] 0x00007ff89b044d82 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+05131650 slGetGraphicalInterface+00002434 [? 4] 0x00007ff89b079d71 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+05348721 mdlref::ModelGraphicalInterface::writeGraphicalInterface+00013153 [? 5] 0x00007ff89b07a264 Z:\matlab\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+05349988 mdlref::ModelGraphicalInterfaceFactory::createGraphicalInterface+00000148

Summary: Error during ARXML import with schema notations of style 000xx for Classic AUTOSAR
Description: When importing ARXML for Classic AUTOSAR using schema notations of the modern style AUTOSAR_000xx.xsd, the import workflow shows an error message like the following: Did not recognize schema version 00045,  expected either of 4. 0,  4. 1,  4. 2,  4. 3,  4. 4, R19-11.  Schema version 00045 is equivalent to R4.3.1, which is supported for import.
Workaround: Adjust ARXML files to use the old-style schema notation (i.e. "AUTOSAR_4-y-y.xsd") instead of the modern-style notation (i.e. AUTOSAR_000xx.xsd) before importing into Simulink.

Summary: Name clash between normal MATLAB variables and enums can cause incorrect code generation with Variants
Description: When normal MATLAB variables are used as variant control expression of variant blocks it can lead to incorrect code generation when all the below conditions are met.Another Variant block uses enums as variant controls. and the elements of this enum has name clash with the variant control of the block Model is configured to generate code with ert.tlc and variant blocks have code-compile /generate pre-processor conditionals  selected.?
Workaround: When enums are used in variant expressions, ensure that elements of the enums are not used variant control for other blocks

Summary: Simulink might stop responding or give wrong answer from String Constant block when any block in model has?Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct as parameter value
Description: Simulink might stop responding or return an incorrect answer from the String Constant block when the model contains:  A block with a parameter that contains a MATLAB expression like Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct(aBusType)  A String Constant block?
Workaround: In a script or model callback function, to get the MATLAB?struct?as a variable, call the Simulink.Bus.createMATLABStruct(aBusType)?function before updating the diagram for the model.?Use the MATLAB?struct?variable as the parameter value for the block parameter.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: NaN%?reported for excluded code coverage in Simulink Test Manager coverage report
Description: For coverage that is completely excluded by coverage filters, the Test Manager coverage report indicates that coverage is NaN% , instead of showing it as "--."

Summary: EtherCAT Init block generates error for EtherCAT ENI file that defines multiple cyclic frames
Description: An EtherCAT Init block generates an error when configured to use an EtherCAT ENI file that defines multiple cyclic frames. The error occurs when the block evaluates the ENI file to populate the variable names list for the PDO blocks. Reasons for definition of multiple cyclic frames include: Separate LR and LW frames are selected, instead of selecting a single LRW frame. The number of variable names for the PDO blocks exceeds the maximum allowed frame size. To accommodate the allowed frame size, the configuration program splits the variables into multiple frames.

Summary: Generating Custom Message on MacOS BigSur fails while building custom message package
Description: When executing?ros2genmsg ?for a custom message package, the following build error is encountered on latest versions of MacOS BigSur machines(11.1 and 11.2.1).  No rule to make target '/usr/lib/libz.dylib', needed by 'libros2_msg_matlab.dylib'.?

Summary: RL agents train poorly if an observation or action?is an array of 3 or more dimensions
Description: When the observation or the action of an environment is an array of 3 or more dimensions (e.g., image width, height, color), agents incorrectly concatenate and reshape observations or actions during minibatch creation. As a consequence, the agents are not able to learn a good policy in such environments. This bug does not affect training in any environment in which actions and observations are arrays of one or two dimensions (vectors or matrices).
Workaround: Separate a 3D array observation into multiple 2D arrays in your environment. Use multiple input layers for neural networks and concatenate them along the third dimension. You must modify your environment and neural network architecture. For example, if the observation is an 80-by-80-by-3 image, you can then split the observation into three 80-by-80 images (Image1, Image2, and Image3). In this case, you can modify the 3D (80-by-80-by-3) observation into a cell array of 3 observations in step and reset functions if you use a MATLAB environment, as follows: Image1 = Observation(:,:,1);Image2 = Observation(:,:,2);Image3 = Observation(:,:,3);Observation = {Image1, Image2, Image3};Observation information for multiple image inputs can be defined as follows:ObservationInfo(1) = rlNumericSpec([80 80]);ObservationInfo(2) = rlNumericSpec([80 80]);ObservationInfo(3) = rlNumericSpec([80 80]);Then the multiple input layers can be concatenated as follows:lgraph = layerGraph();tempLayers = imageInputLayer([80 80 1],"Name","Image1",'Normalization' ,'none');lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);tempLayers = imageInputLayer([80 80 1],"Name","Image2",'Normalization' ,'none');lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);tempLayers = imageInputLayer([80 80 1],"Name","Image3",'Normalization' ,'none');lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);tempLayers = concatenationLayer(3,3,"Name","concat_1");lgraph = addLayers(lgraph,tempLayers);lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph,"Image1","concat_1/in1");lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph,"Image2","concat_1/in2");lgraph = connectLayers(lgraph,"Image3","concat_1/in3");The rest of the network is the same as the original network.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: System Composer models might have extraneous systemcomposer.arch.Component objects that corrupt the model file
Description: Working with components in System Composer might result in the creation of extraneous systemcomposer.arch.Component  objects such that the number of Component  objects associated with the model no longer matches the number of components displayed in the canvas. This issue is more likely to occur for models that include components with links to Simulink Requirements and when using these System Composer functions:? saveAsModel  linkToModel  inlineComponent   To determine whether a System Composer model has this issue and identify extraneous systemcomposer.arch.Component  objects, enter these commands in the MATLAB Command Window, replacing the value for modelName  with the name of the System Composer model: >> modelName = "mySystemComposerModel";  >> scModel = systemcomposer.openModel(modelName)  >> constraint = systemcomposer.query.PropertyValue('SimulinkHandle') == -1;  >> [invalidCompPaths, invalidComps] = find(scModel,constraint,'Recurse',true)   If either return argument for the find ?function is not empty, the model contains extraneous Component ? objects.?? If you encounter this issue,?contact MathWorks technical support .

Summary: Model build time much longer when generating A2L file
Description: The model build time is much longer when generating an A2L file.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with an error Empty character vector corresponding to an enumerated value
Description: When compatibility check is run on an Export Function model having Model Arguments  of type Simulink.Parameter , the check fails with an error?Empty character vector corresponding to enumerated value .
Workaround: Remove Model Arguments from the Export Function model.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b

Summary: Unexpected indexing error when evaluating a SimFunction object
Description: Using a?SimFunction  object to simulate a model can fail with an unexpected indexing error of the form:   Index in position 2 exceeds array bounds. Index must not exceed 4. The numbers in the error message might be different. This error is thrown if The simulation fails, and The?SimFunction ?object?has an?Observable ?object as a logged state.  This error might also occur when you use other functionality that relies on a SimFunction  object, such as fitting, global sensitivity analysis, or scanning.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following strategies to avoid simulation failures:Avoid extreme parameter values.Turn off absolute tolerance scaling.Adjust solver tolerances.The?Model Debugger for SimBiology?might also help to identify unexpected behavior during simulation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: impzest?produces wrong answers when used with multichannel input
Description: The impzest  function swaps channels when used with a multichannel?recording. For example, the output channels in this example are swapped when the input is stereo. exc = sweeptone(2,100/44100);ir1 = impzest(exc,exc);ir2 = impzest(exc,0.5*exc);stereo = [exc 0.5*exc];ir = impzest(exc,stereo);subplot(2,1,1), stem([ir1 ir2])subplot(2,1,2), stem(ir)  This bug does not affect the Impulse Response Measurer  application because the app records only one channel at a time.
Workaround: Use impzest with single-channel inputs only. Process multichannel signals one channel at a time. (See example in Description of Problem.)
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: fwrite does not write UTF-8 character data correctly
Description: Using fwrite  with character or string data results in the wrong bytes written to a file with UTF-8 encoding. For example, this code returns 255 . The correct output is [207;168] . >> fid = fopen('file.txt','w','n','UTF-8');>> fwrite(fid,char(1000));>> fclose(fid);>> fid = fopen('file.txt','r','n','UTF-8');>> bytes = fread(fid,'uint8')>> fclose(fid);bytes =   255% But bytes should equal [207;168]
Workaround: Instead of fwrite(fid,textdata) use fprint(fid,'%s',textdata).For example:>> fid = fopen('file.txt','w','n','UTF-8');>> fprintf(fid,'%s',char(1000));>> fclose(fid);>> fid = fopen('file.txt','r','n','UTF-8');>> bytes = fread(fid,'uint8')>> fclose(fid);bytes =   207   168
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Test Manager degraded performance when test case has many baseline signals
Description: If you have a test case with more than a few hundred signal criteria objects, the performance of the Test Manager might be degraded.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 5, R2021b

Summary: Calling embed with a permuted dlarray can return an incorrect result
Description: When you call the embed ?function with an input dlarray that is formatted and has had its S or U dimensions permuted, the result might not match the dimension permutation of the input. For example:? ? ? X = dlarray(randi(201,[10 20 1 8]), 'SSCB');? ? weights = rand(15,201);? ? % Permute the first two spatial dimensions in X?? ? X = permute(X,[2 1 3 4]);? ? Z = embed(X,weights);??  produces a dlarray Z of size 10x20x15x8 instead of the expected 20x10x15x8.

Summary: Model disconnects from real-time application during external mode simulation
Description: Intermittently, the connected model disconnects from the real-time application during external mode simulation. The unexpected disconnection in external mode occurs due to occasional target connection issues. Observers see the RUN ON TARGET  button transition from Stop execution ?state to Run on Target ?state. This change gives the impression that the real-time application is not running on the target. When this condition occurs, inspecting the state of the target computer confirms that the real-time application is still running.
Workaround: Stop the execution of the real-time application on the target computer by using the stop(tg) command.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect answer may be observed with reusable subsystems receiving N-D signals in the row-major mode
Description: Incorrect code may be generated under the following conditions: Model configuration parameter Array layout  is set to Row-major . Multiple subsystems have Reshape block(s) inside and the subsystem block parameter Function packaging  is set to Reusable  or Auto . The subsystems receive different N-D signals that have the same size overall. For example, [4 x 3] ,  [3 x 4] ,  [6 x 2]  have different individual dimensions but the same overall size, which is 12 in this case.
Workaround: Use one of the following:Set model configuration parameter Array layout to Column-major.Set the subsystem block parameter Function packaging to anything other than Reusable or Auto.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Custom code functions from multiple libraries not all imported into Simulink
Description: If you specify more than one custom code library in Additional build information ?in the Simulation target ?pane of the model configuration parameters, Simulink might not import custom code functions from libraries other than the first one specified.? If so, in the dialog of C Caller blocks the Function name ?parameter?lists only the functions defined in the first library, and functions defined in the other libraries cannot be called from C Caller blocks, C Function blocks, or Stateflow diagrams.
Workaround: Go to the model configuration parameters, Simulation Target?pane, and set?Undefined function handling?to Do not detect.?

Summary: MATLAB occasionally crashes when MATLAB Drive Connector is running
Description: MATLAB occasionally crashes if MATLAB Drive Connector is running on your desktop system. The generated stack trace always includes a line that mentions?agent::nanomsgtransport::AsyncMessagePoller::sendAsyncMessageRequestThreadProc  For example:?[??3] 0x00007fff27459121 agent::nanomsgtransport::AsyncMessagePoller::sendAsyncMessageRequestThreadProc at b:\ matlab\ src\ agent\ nanomsgtransport\ src\ asyncmessagepoller. cpp:141 ?

Summary: Masked subsystems containing logic that folds into to a constant value during HDL code generation causes code generation assertion
Description: MATLAB might crash during HDL code generation if both these conditions are true:?? Your model contains a masked subsystem This subsystem contains logic that might get reduced to a constant value during HDL code generation?  The stack trace for such a crash might look like this: [??0] 0x00007f946782e99f void pir::SetCompositeBlocksTagsImpl::propagateMantMul<pir::GainComp>(pir::GainComp&) at / matlab/ src/ cgir_hdl/ pir_transforms/ SetCompositeBlocksTags. cpp:140[??1] 0x00007f946782e10d pir::NetworkCompVisitor::run(pir::Network*, ?pir::CompIterator::TrType) at / matlab/ src/ cgir_hdl/ export/ include/ cgir_hdl/ dom_pir_core/ iterator. hpp:483[??2] 0x00007f946782e382 pir::SetCompositeBlocksTags::run(pir::GlobalCtx*) at / matlab/ src/ cgir_hdl/ pir_transforms/ SetCompositeBlocksTags. cpp:244 [??3] 0x00007f946733717f metadatafactory::udd::UDDMetaDataFactory_MethodInfo::invokeMethod(UDMethodSignature*, ?int*, ?void**, ?int, ?void**) at / matlab/ src/ cgir_hdl/ . . / . . / derived/ glnxa64/ src/ include/ pir_core/ udd_factory/ private/ . . / . . / . . / . . / . . / . . / . . / src/ pir_core/ export/ include/ pir_core/ udd_factory/ private/ UDDMethodCreatorPrivate. hpp:49   ?
Workaround: Inside masked subsystems, replace the logic that can be reduced to constants by equivalent constant blocks.?

Summary: Test case cleanup callback for parallel test execution runs on host MATLAB
Description: For parallel test execution using Simulink Test, the test case post-load callback run on the parallel worker MATLAB but the cleanup callback runs on the host MATLAB.

Summary: Experiment Manager displays confusion matrix with axes reversed
Description: When Experiment Manager computes the confusion matrix for a classification experiment, the true classes and the predicted classes are transposed. As a result, false positives are marked as false negatives, and the other way round.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 2, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB crashes when reading a CDF file with an attribute whose data type is unsupported
Description: MATLAB crashes when you call cdfinfo , cdflib.getAttrEntry , or cdflib.getAttrgEntry  to read an attribute entry of type CDF_TIME_TT2000  or CDF_INT8  from a CDF file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect behavior for models that contain a Rate Transition block that is not configured to ensure data integrity
Description: Incorrect behavior during accelerated simulation and incorrect code generation might occur for a model when both of the following conditions exist: The model contains a Rate Transition block that supports specification of the parameter?Initial conditions ? and the value of that parameter is non-zero. The output of the Rate Transition block is connected to the input of a second Rate Transition block that has the parameter Ensure data integrity during data transfer  set to off .
Workaround: For the second Rate Transition block, set Ensure data integrity during data transfer to on.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 4, R2021b, R2022a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing Data Store Memory and Model blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:  The model contains a Data Store Memory block and a referenced model.? One of the output signals of the referenced model connects to a Data Store Write block.? The output signal that connects to the Data Store Write block is driven by one of the following:? Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Persistent data referenced in parfor loop might be initialized incorrectly
Description: MATLAB Coder might produce code that initializes persistent data incorrectly if your MATLAB code satisfies all  of these conditions: Your MATLAB code contains a parfor  loop The body of the parfor  loop contains a function call The definition of this function uses a persistent variable  In the generated code, the data corresponding to the persistent variable is implemented by using a global variable that is an OpenMP threadprivate. To match the behavior of the MATLAB code, the data corresponding to the persistent variable must be initialized in each thread. When this issue occurs, the generated code only initializes the data in the main thread. For example, define this MATLAB function: function y = issue_2437817(n)y = 0;parfor i = 1:n    y = y + pers();endend function y = pers()persistent p;if isempty(p)    p = 2;endy = p;end Generate code for the function issue_2437817  by running this command: codegen -c -config:exe issue_2437817 -args {10}  In the generated code the persistent data?p ?is implemented by a threadprivate global variable: static double p;#pragma omp threadprivate(p)  But the function generated to initialize this data only initializes p ?in the main thread: void pers_init(void){  omp_get_max_threads();  p = 2.0;} Instead, the initialization function should have been generated as: void pers_init(void){  int ub_loop;  int i;  ub_loop = omp_get_max_threads();#pragma omp parallel for schedule(static)\ num_threads(omp_get_max_threads())  for (i = 1; i <= ub_loop; i++) {    p = 2.0;  }  p = 2.0;} This function initializes p ?for each program thread.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect result when using a string operation in a Stateflow C chart that leads to string truncation
Description: When the?String truncation checking ?parameter is set to warning  or none  in the model configuration parameters, and a Stateflow chart with C action language uses a string operation that leads to string truncation, subsequent string operations can lead to incorrect results. For example, consider the following code:? y = strcat("Hello", " world!");?  y_len = strlen(y) ;? If y  has type specified as stringtype(10) , then the expected result for y_len  is 10. However, the result for y_len  is 12, which is the length of the string "Hello world!"
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Description: While using the?Simulation 3D Vision  Detection Generator  block in a Simulink model, the pop-up  window appears and disappears when new block dialog parameters are applied, or when the  model is compiled. Users cannot react to the pop-up window since it disappears very fast.?

Summary: Host example models for Hall sensor and quadrature encoder sensor offset calibration do not display calibrated offset value for single pole pair PMSM
Description: The host models mcb_pmsm_host_offsetComputation_f28069m.slx  and mcb_pmsm_host_offsetComputation_f28379d.slx  that are part of the examples ˇ°Hall Offset Calibration for PMSM Motorˇ± and ˇ°Quadrature Encoder Offset Calibration for PMSM Motorˇ± do not display the sensor offset value after calibration completes for a single pole pair PMSM. The problem occurs due to an incorrect speed check implemented in these models that verifies whether the motor is running.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing a conditional subsystem that inherits initial conditions for output values

Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the conditional subsystem and the Unit Delay block, and select the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?property of the Signal Conversion block.

Description: Incorrect code generated from a model when all of the following conditions are present: The model contains a root Outport block with option Ensure outport is virtual  selected. The signal driving the input port of the root Outport block resolves to a Simulink.Signal  object. The StorageClass ?property of the CoderInfo  property of the Simulink.Signal  object has a value other than Auto .   In this situation the generated code should combine the memory for the root Outport block and the memory specified by the Simulink.Signal  object. However,?the root Outport block incorrectly makes an extra copy of input signal.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for?polyfit might return different results than MATLAB for badly conditioned problems
Description: If a problem is badly conditioned, the code generated for the?polyfit  function might produce different results than the polyfit  function in MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing a conditionally executed subsystem

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated using MATLAB Coder?for deep neural networks containing custom layers that uses row-major array layout and ARM Compute library might produce incorrect results
Description: Code generated using MATLAB Coder for deep neural networks that contain custom layers might produce incorrect results if both  these?conditions are true: The generated code uses row-major array layout.? The generated code takes advantage of the ARM Compute library.?  For example, consider this custom layer implementation: classdef scalingLayer < nnet.layer.Layer  %#codegen    properties        alpha    end    methods        function layer = scalingLayer(name, alpha)            layer.Name = name;            layer.alpha = alpha;        end        function Z = predict(layer, X)            Z = X.*layer.alpha;        end    endend For a deep learning network that contains this custom layer, code generated by using the configuration object cfg ?defined below might produce incorrect results.? cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.RowMajor = true;cfg.DeepLearningConfig = coder.DeepLearningConfig('arm-compute');
Workaround: Include the?coder.columnMajor?pragma in the constructor of the custom layer. This pragma instructs the code generator to produce code that uses column-major array layout just for the custom layer. The rest of the generated code still uses row-major array layout.For example, modify the previous definition of the custom layer as follows:?classdef scalingLayer < nnet.layer.Layer  %#codegen    properties        alpha    end    methods        function layer = scalingLayer(name, alpha)            coder.columnMajor;            layer.Name = name;            layer.alpha = alpha;        end        function Z = predict(layer, X)            Z = X.*layer.alpha;        end    endend

For example: s = strings(0,0);badIndex = 0; s(badIndex) = [];  Other examples of bad indices are:? 0+3*1i  inf?  nan?  pi? Summary: Models containing Drive Cycle Source blocks with?Output sample period?set to non-zero values less than?1s can take from minutes to several hours to open
Description: If you set the Drive Cycle Source block Output sample period (0 for continuous)  parameter to a non-zero values less than 1s, models with the block can take from minutes to several hours to open. The opening time depends on the drive cycle length and sample period. The model does open, however, the wait time might be prohibitive.
Workaround: To control the output sample rate, consider using a Rate Transition blocks. Specifically, use Rate Transition blocks before the velocity input and immediately after the Drive Cycle Source outputs the signals. The Rate Transition blocks allow you to specify a sample time for the model that is different from the Drive Cycle Source block.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

For example, a model that contains several Model blocks might display this performance issue.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when simulating model in the frequency-and-time simulation mode
Description: MATLAB crashes?when?the following conditions are true: Your model contains frequency variables. You simulate in frequency-and-time simulation mode. Logging to memory is enabled.
Workaround: Enable streaming to disk instead of logging to memory:?on the MATLAB Toolstrip, click?Preferences. In the left pane of the Preferences dialog box, select?Simscape, then select the?Stream data to temporary disk directory?check box.

Summary: Using context menu to open Signal Properties dialog box for a signal line in Model Explorer causes MATLAB to crash
Description: In the Model Explorer Contents  pane, MATLAB crashes when you right-click a signal line and select Properties  to open the standalone Signal Properties dialog box.
Workaround: In the Model Explorer Contents pane, select a signal line and edit the signal properties in the Model Explorer Dialog pane instead.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code may be generated in model with multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks with fixed-point Table data?data type and floating point Intermediate results data type
Description: Incorrect code may be generated if all of the following conditions are true: The model has multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks The individual n-D Lookup Table blocks have: Number of table dimensions  set to 1  or 2 . Same input and table data types. Table data  data type parameter is a fixed-point data type. Intermediate results  data type parameter is a floating point data type.  The multiple n-D Lookup Table blocks differ in their fixed-point slope values for the?Table data  data type.
Workaround: Replace each affected n-D Lookup Table block with Prelookup block(s) connected to an Interpolation Using Prelookup block.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Possible unexpected behavior for Stateflow local events sent from a transition reachable from several states
Description: A Stateflow chart might not enter the expected destination state when the following conditions are true:? A path from the currently active source state contains a transition whose action sends a local event The transition also belongs to path whose source is some other state. For example, transitions from different source states come together at a junction, and then a transition from that junction sends a local event  After the local event is resolved, Stateflow unexpectedly returns to the source state, instead of completing the transition to the destination state, and any exit actions of the source state are not executed.?

Description: In a custom code C function called by a C Caller block, when the function has both: An argument passed by pointer that is mapped to the block ports as an InputOutput ,  An accessed global variable that is mapped to the block ports as an Input ,   Simulink may throw an error during model simulation or code generation. You may see a message like the following:"c:\? matlab\? toolbox\? shared\? slcc\? src\? slcc\? src\? slccfunctionimpl.? cpp at line 1653In constructFunctionCall: Assertion failed: static_cast<int>(remainingRHS.? size()) == nGlobalOnInputSide"

Description: Simulink crashes when a Queue block is inside an export-function model.

Description: If you use polyspace-configure  to trace the build configuration of a project that you compile with the Keil ?VISION IDE, the software fails to generate a Polyspace project and does not produce any error messages.
