Summary: Priority mismatch error when Thread Trigger block or Speedgoat Interrupt Source block is connected to Model block
Description: When a Thread Trigger block or any Speedgoat Interrupt Source block is connected to a Model block, a priority mismatch error occurs. The function call signal a Thread Trigger block, or any Speedgoat Interrupt Source block does not contain the priority information that Simulink uses when connecting to a function call inport on a Model block. The lack of priority information produces a priority mismatch error.

Summary: High-integrity checks for hisl_0021,?hisl_0022, and?hisl_0102?need Stateflow license to appear in the Model Advisor
Description: The Model Advisor checks?Check for inconsistent vector indexing methods  (mathworks.hism.hisl_0021 ),?Check data types for blocks with index signals? ? (mathworks.hism.hisl_0022 )? and?Check data type of loop control variables? ? (mathworks.hism.hisl_0102 )? do not appear in the Model Advisor if Stateflow license is missing.

Summary: Error when updating or deleting tasks for common job scheduler (CJS) clusters
Description: After you use the vectorized?createTask() ?syntax to create multiple tasks at the same time on your local cluster or other common job scheduler (CJS) clusters, if you either update a task's?Function ?or?InputArguments , or delete one or more (but not all) of the tasks  then you can see a?'MATLAB:badsubscript' ?error or, in rare cases, can change a task's?InputArguments ?to incorrect values. In the latter case, the task might produce a silent wrong answer if the task runs successfully with the incorrect?InputArguments .
Workaround: As a workaround, you canAvoid updating the?Function or InputArguments?properties of tasks after you create them with the vectorized createTask() syntaxAvoid deleting some (but not all of) tasks created with the vectorized?createTask()?syntaxUse the non-vectorized createTask() syntax to create tasks in a loop
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: MATLAB may crash when using an S-function with tunable parameters that does not register run-time parameters
Description: MATLAB might occasionally crash when all of the following conditions are true: You are using an S-function with tunable parameters You do not register any run-time parameters through ssSetNumRunTimeParams , and  The parameters are n ot of type: double, single, integer, boolean, 1D char array or 2D char array?  For example, MATLAB might crash if the tunable parameters are fixed-point, struct, or char arrays of 3 or more dimensions, and no run-time parameter is registered.?
Workaround: Set the number of run-time parameters using ssSetNumRunTimeParams in your S-function with tunable parameters. Also, register the run-time parameters using?ssRegAllTunableParamsAsRunTimeParams, ssRegDlgParamAsRunTimeParam, or ssSetRunTimeParamInfo.?To detect if your Simulink model contains S-functions that could trigger this crash, set the?SfunCompatabilityCheckMsg?Configuration Parameter to?'warning'?or?'error'. Incompatible S-Functions will throw the error (or warning):"S-function "system" in "model" does not explicitly set the number of run-time parameters using?ssSetNumRunTimeParams. This compatibility diagnostic on the need for S-function upgrades can be set to?none,?warning, or?error?using the Configuration Parameters dialog".The stacktrace may contain lines similar to:[? 0] 0x00000000fe9ea270 libut.dll+00303728 ut_md5ChecksumRec::~ut_md5ChecksumRec+00000896[? 1] 0x00000000fe9eab7d libut.dll+00306045 ut_md5ChecksumRec::~ut_md5ChecksumRec+00003213[? 2] 0x00000001b825fdc7 sl_compile.dll+03210695 BusConnectionChecker::runChecks+00010407[? 3] 0x00000001b827626c sl_compile.dll+03301996 slcomp::slAddRTParamDataToChecksum+00000700[? 4] 0x00000001b8299a50 sl_compile.dll+03447376 slcomp::BdComputeChecksums+0002582??
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 3, R2021b

Summary: Simulink Check qualification test suite requires Simulink Coder to execute
Description: Simulink Coder is required to successfully execute the qualification tests for Simulink Check. If Simulink Coder is not installed, this error message occurs: Error : Unrecognized field name "Name".  Report generation stopped. Follow the test execution procedure specified in the Simulink Check Test Cases and Procedures document.  ? "chapter" contains no content and will not be inserted into document ?

Summary: Code inspection might fail with an "Out-of-bound access of array" error for a MATLAB Function block using coder.ceval
Description: Simulink Code Inspector fails verification of a MATLAB Function block that uses?coder.ceval  with an? "Out-of-bound access of array" error when all of these conditions are true:  There are two or more calls to the same function from a MATLAB Function block by using coder.ceval . The arguments at the same argument position in the two function calls differ in their size.  For example, consider the following two calls to a function foo ?that takes only one argument. coder.ceval('foo', coder.ref(u1))  coder.ceval('foo', coder.ref(u2)) ? If u1 ?and u2  have?different dimensions, the code inspection fails verification with an error message similar to the following: Out-of-bound access of array <variable name> at the following positions : 'FILE: <filename> LINE, <line number>, COLUMN, <column numbers>'  Here, <filename>  is the name of the generate C or CPP file,? <line number>  is the line number in <filename> , and? <column numbers>  are the columns numbers in line <line number> .

Summary: Invalid observable expression results in unexpected error at command line and silent error in SimBiology Model Builder
Description: If a SimBiology model contains multiple active observables and one or more of them have an invalid expression, you might see the following error while verifying or simulating the model at the command line:  All contents of the input cell array must be of the same data type. If you have such a model in the SimBiology Model Builder  app, you might experience a silent error. For example, if you click Verify , this error does not appear in the list of messages although the model verification has failed.
Workaround: To troubleshoot this issue, have only one active observable in the model at a time. If this active observable has an invalid expression, you will see a more informative error message that explains why the expression is invalid.

Summary: Simulink Check qualification test case SLCHK_SL_Q009 fails unexpectedly
Description: Simulink Check test case SLCHK_SL_Q009  in the DO Qualification Kit fails with the following error when Simulink tries to compile model DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN.slx : Error:Input data dependency violation due to function-call or action subsystems. See Subsystem Examples in the Simulink library for valid and invalid examples of function-call and action subsystems.  Error:Input ports (1) of 'DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN/ss' are involved in the loop.  Error:Input ports (FcnCall) of 'DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN/fcss2' are involved in the loop.  Error:Simulink has inserted an implicit signal conversion block after the inport blocks 'DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN/ss/In1' of subsystem 'DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN/ss' to ensure that the corresponding signals are contiguous in memory. This may cause a one-step delay for the function-call initiator block(s) 'DesignDelayedFcnCallSubsys_WARN/ss/ss/sfcn' that are observing these signals as return values from the function-call subsystem(s) they are calling. To avoid this condition, ensure that each function-call return value originates from a single block in the function-call subsystem being called.   The model compile error occurs because Model Advisor check?Check for potentially delayed function-call block return values  (mathworks.design.DelayedFcnCallSubsys ) does not execute normally. As a result, the check cannot be qualified by using the DO Qualification Kit.
Workaround: Copy the matlabroot/toolbox/qualkits/do/slchk folder and subfolders to a location where you have write access.Open Excel spreadsheet qualkitdo_slchk_tcp.xls.Find test case SLCHK_SL_Q009 (column A).Remove the row and save the spreadsheet.Open qualkitdo_slchk_run.mlx and follow the workflow to execute the test procedure.

Summary: Uncompilable C code generated due to misplaced guards in header file
Description: Uncompilable C code may be generated if all the following conditions are true: A data declaration, data type, or function should be generated to a particular C header file in the _sharedutils  folder These may be generated to the _sharedutils  folder when a data type's scope is set to Exported , a custom storage class specifies a header file, or a function is a globally scoped Simulink Function or Stateflow graphical function. There may be other instances in which this happens, but these are common scenarios.  The same header file name is specified for another entity that is generated to the model's build folder and not to the _sharedutils  folder   In this case, #ifndef  preprocessor statements in the non-shared header file may be placed incorrectly, resulting in code that cannot be compiled. Files that should be generated to the _sharedutils  are also generated in the model's build folder.
Workaround: Change the file name specification of either the item generated to the _sharedutils folder or the item generated to the model's build folder.

Summary: matlab.unittest.TestSuite.fromFolder?does not recognize test files using symbolic links
Description: When you use the?matlab.unittest.TestSuite.fromFolder ?method to create a test suite, MATLAB ignores any test files that are symbolically linked to your folder. For example, consider a test file C:\work\tests\myTest.m  on your Windows system. Create a symbolic link (or symlink) to the file by executing?this command in the command prompt: mklink "C:\work\symlink\myTest.m" "C:\work\tests\myTest.m"?  If you try to create a test suite using the symlink  folder, MATLAB ignores the test file when creating the suite. Running this command results in an empty test suite: suite = matlab.unittest.TestSuite.fromFolder('C:\work\symlink');

Summary: readtable, readmatrix, and readcell might throw an error when reading large text files with many lines that exceed 524288 bytes in length
Description: readtable , readmatrix , and readcell  might throw an error when reading large text files with many lines that exceed 524288 bytes in length. >> m = (1.23456789e123)*ones(147,65534);>> writetable(array2table(m), "numbers.txt"); >> opts = delimitedTextImportOptions("NumVariables",  65534);>> opts = setvartype(opts, "double"); >> t = readtable("numbers.txt", opts);Failed to convert character code. To hit this error condition, the text file must contain many lines whose lengths are?524288 bytes or greater.? In versions of MATLAB prior to R2020a, readtable ?does not produce an error if it is called without an Import Options object. For example, the following code does not error in R2019b: >> m = (1.23456789e123)*ones(147,65534);>> writetable(array2table(m), "numbers.txt");>> result = readtable("numbers.txt"); However, this code does error in R2020a and later releases.
Workaround: To work around this issue in R2020a and later releases, you can pass the Format name-value argument to readtable:>> m = (1.23456789e123)*ones(147,65534);>> writetable(array2table(m), "numbers.txt");>> t = readtable("numbers.txt", "Format", "auto");

Summary: Model build time much longer when generating A2L file
Description: The model build time is much longer when generating an A2L file.

Summary: Can't import some text timestamps on a system whose locale is not set to en_US
Description: On a system where MATLAB localizes to English but whose system locale setting is something other than en_US , you cannot import text timestamps that include time zone offsets specified as US localized abbreviations, such as EDT, by using the datetime  function. For example, if the system locale setting is not en_US , then this call to datetime  results in an error. >> d = datetime("26-Mar-2018 16:00:42 EDT","Format","dd-MMM-yyyy HH:mm:ss z","TimeZone","America/New_York","Locale","en_US") Error using datetime (line 649)Unable to convert '26-Mar-2018 16:00:42 EDT' to datetime using the format 'dd-MMM-yyyy HH:mm:ss z' and locale 'en_US'.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating models that contain masked Simscape blocks
Description: When you try to simulate a model containing Simscape blocks with masks put directly on them, MATLAB might crash.? In R2020a and R2020b, the crash report contains output similar to: ? ? ?0x00007fc779dc6a57 gl::sli::ModelTranslator::Impl::generateRuntimeParameterInfo(...)  In R2021a, MATLAB issues an error similar to:?? ? ? ?Error compiling Simscape network for model my_dcmotor.? ? ?Caused by:? ? ? ? ?['my_dcmotor/DC Motor/Rotor Resistance']: 'RTP_5F058808_R' is not defined in this scope.
Workaround: Using Simscape blocks as masked linked blocks is not supported. To mask a Simscape block, insert the block into a subsystem and mask that subsystem.

Summary: fplot?and related functions may be susceptible to low-precision problems
Description: When plotting symbolic expressions that?matlabFunction ?cannot handle,?fplot ,?fplot3 ,?fsurf ,?fmesh ,?fcontour ,?fimplicit , and?fimplicit3 ?perform function evaluation at low precision. In some cases, this may cause numerical instabilities, potentially including incorrect results that look reasonable. ex = str2sym("piecewise(x < 100, (pi^2-107607675/10902937)*x)");??fplot(ex)
Workaround: Avoid the low-precision evaluation by explicitly calling?subs. Note that this significantly degrades performance.syms xex = str2sym("piecewise(x < 100, (pi^2-107607675/10902937)*x)");?fplot(@(xval) subs(ex,x,xval))
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Greater than expressions that use the duration operator on the left hand side do not return true at the correct simulation step
Description: Stateflow incorrectly evaluates greater than expressions when all of the following conditions are true: The Stateflow chart uses a fixed sample time The expression includes a duration  operator on the left hand side The right side of the expression evaluates to a value that is not a whole multiple of the chart's sample time  When these conditions are met, Stateflow evaluates the expression as?true  one time step later than expected.
Workaround: For charts that include the duration operator on the left side of a greater than expression, ensure that the right side of the expression specifies time that matches sample time of the chart.Alternatively, you can change the sample time of the chart to accommodate all values of time that are used in the chart.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Unable to perform assignment error when opening block dialog of State Reader or State Writer block
Description: Simulink might throw the following error when you open the block dialog for a State Reader or State Writer block: Unable to perform assignment because the left and right sides have a different number of elements   The error occurs if the model contains a Stateflow chart that contains a Simulink Function.

Summary: Wireless Waveform Generator app might transmit a distorted signal constellation from lab instruments and result in incorrect EVM
Description: The Wireless Waveform Generator app can transmit generated waveforms using lab test instruments supported by the?rfsiggen  function (Instrument Control Toolbox, see https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/details/2413350 ). While using the app in the Transmitter mode, the signal constellation transmitted by an instrument might be distorted, leading to larger-than-expected EVM values. This occurs when provided IQ data values are greater than 1. The operation of the app in the Generator mode is not affected. This issue affects the output of RF instruments from all supported vendors, including: Rohde & Schwarz Keysight

Summary: A Simulink model that contains ROS Read Data block and a ROS Publish or ROS Subscribe block fails to simulate
Description: A Simulink model that contains ROS Read Data block and a ROS Publish or a ROS Subscribe block fails to run. An example of the error thrown in diagnostic viewer is shown below: An error occurred while running the simulation and the simulation was terminatedCaused by:For 'Output Port 2' of 'modelname/ Read Data/ SourceBlock', ?the method 'stepImpl' of the System object 'ros. slros. internal. block. ReadData' returns a bus type whose number of fields does not match the number of fields defined in the method 'getOutputDataTypeImpl'.

Summary: Override SIL/PIL mode setting might be incorrect in the test result report
Description: If the model used in a test file is not on the path, the value of Override SIL/PIL mode ?set in the Test Manager might show an incorrect value in the test result report.

Summary: SLRT Overload Options block provides incomplete operation in referenced model or explicitly partitioned model
Description: The SLRT Overload Options block provides incomplete operation in a referenced model or in an explicitly partitioned model. When used in these types of models, the operations include: The block provides the expected control of the selected task thread. For example, the maximum overload number cannot be overwritten for the selected thread. The block cannot output the correct overload count.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when performing a cut and paste of a stereotyped component after launching the Analysis?Viewer
Description: MATLAB might crash if the Analysis Viewer is opened on an architecture model and a component with stereotypes applied is cut and pasted onto any layer of that architecture model. If MATLAB does not crash immediately, it might crash if the Analysis Viewer is relaunched.

Summary: Target computer might crash if?Default parameter behavior?is?Tunable and?Run on Target button starts real-time application
Description: If you set the?Default parameter behavior ?to?Tunable  and you start the real-time application by using the?Run on Target  button in the Simulink Editor (external mode operation), the target computer might crash.

Summary: Folders are created when opening a Simulink model?in the current directory
Description: Opening a Simulink model creates a folder named dig  in the current working directory. This folder contains folders such as blob_storage , cache , and Code Cache .

Summary: Test file cleanup callback runs incorrectly when using MATLAB Unit Test Runner
Description: If you run a Simulink Test file using the MATLAB Unit Test Runner, the test file cleanup callback runs multiple times instead of only once.

Summary: Editing MATLAB code that is also open in a MATLAB Coder project might produce an UnsupportedOperationException error
Description: If you edit MATLAB code that is part of an open MATLAB Coder project and add calls to MathWorks functions that do not support code generation, the stack trace from an?UnsupportedOperationException  error might appear in the MATLAB command window. If one such error appears, subsequent edits to your MATLAB code might trigger successive, identical errors. This error is purely cosmetic and does not affect either your MATLAB Coder project or your ability to progress through the MATLAB Coder app.
Workaround: When this error starts appearing, close and reopen your MATLAB Coder project.

Summary: Aerospace Blockset Orbit Propagator block incorrectly calculates transformation between fixed-frame coordinate systems and inertial coordinate system (ICRF) for some parameter configurations
Description: The Aerospace Blockset Orbit Propagator  block incorrectly calculates the transformation between fixed-frame coordinate systems and the inertial coordinate system (ICRF) when one of the following criteria is met, causing inaccurate propagation results: Propagation method  is set to  Numerical (high precision) , Central body   is set to a value other than Earth , and Gravitational potential model   is set to Oblate spheroid (J2)  or Spherical harmonics . Propagation method  ?is set to  Numerical (high precision) , ? Input external accelerations   is set to on , and?External acceleration coordinate frame   is set to Fixed-frame . Units  ?is set to English (kts) .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation when a Concatenate block is inside an Iterator Subsystem
Description: Incorrect code might be generated in some cases when an Assignment block feeds to a Concatenate block that is inside an Iterator Subsystem. This occurs while generating code from a Simulink model.

Summary: Simulation error in Condenser Evaporator blocks above the critical pressure
Description: When simulating the Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) block or the Condenser Evaporator (TL-2P) block above the critical pressure of the refrigerant, you may receive the following error: ??In divide, the denominator must be nonzero.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a root Outport block that has a fixed-point data type with nonzero bias and an initial condition specified on its input signal
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: The Remove root level I/O zero initialization  configuration parameter is selected (that is,? ZeroExternalMemoryAtStartup ?is set to 'off '). The root Outport block has a fixed-point?data type with nonzero bias.? The root Outport block input signal connects to a subsystem Outport block with an initial condition.? The sum of the nonzero bias on the fixed-point signal and the initial condition on the subsystem Outport block is equal to zero.?
Workaround: Clear the Remove root level I/O zero initialization configuration parameter.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: On launching Model Slicer, an incompatibility message incorrectly indicates that a Model block inside a For Each subsystem is configured in normal mode
Description: Launching the Model Slicer on a model with a For Each subsystem containing a Model block throws an error of this form. The parameter 'Simulation mode' of the Model block <..> is set to 'Normal'. However, the Model block is inside subsystem <..>, which contains a For Each block. Simulink currently does not support this scenario. Consider changing? the parameter 'Simulation mode' of Model block <...> to 'Accelerator'.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Normal mode simulation and code generation results might not match if the model is composed of an atomic subsystem containing only a Bus Selector block
Description: When a model is composed of an atomic subsystem that contains only a Bus Selector block, block reduction might remove the buffer synthesized in that subsystem incorrectly in code generation. This may lead to a simulation result that is different from the normal mode simulation.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation results might be incorrect in the Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator mode for reusable subsystem outport connected to constant sample time signal
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all of the following conditions are met: Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator simulation mode is chosen. Reusable subsystem's outport is connect to block with constant (inf ) sample time? Configuration parameter BufferReusableBoundary  is checked
Workaround: Disable BufferReusableBoundary configuration parameter.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: JIT MEX generated from MATLAB function that accepts four or more input arguments might produce incorrect answers if at least one argument is of half type
Description: The generated MEX function might produce incorrect answers if all  these conditions are true: In the coder.MexCodeConfig  object, the EnableJIT  property is set to true. Alternatively, in MATLAB Coder app, in the Generate  step, the Use JIT compilation  check box is selected. This setting instructs the code generator to generate the MEX function by using the just-in-time (JIT) compilation technology. Your MATLAB code contains a function that accepts four or more input arguments. At least one of these arguments is passed data of half type.

Summary: Previously generated Model Testing results are not visible in the Model Testing Dashboard
Description: When you collect results in the Model Testing Dashboard and close the dashboard, you may see one of the following when you open the dashboard again for the project: The dashboard displays no results and is unable to generate new results. MATLAB crashes.  This occurs if the Model Testing Dashboard does not correctly save the results that you collected in the previous session. The results file, located at <project root>/derved/results.dmr , may be corrupted.
Workaround: Manually clear the persistent results.Close the Model Testing Dashboard.Close the current project.Delete the <project root>/derived/results.dmr file. If the file is locked, the project is still open in MATLAB. Close MATLAB completely and delete the file by using the operating system.Open the project.Open the Model Testing DashboardAfter you perform these steps, the Model Testing Dashboard can trace the project artifacts and collect new results. However, this is a temporary fix, and the problem may return after you close and reload the project.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C/C++ code generated for MATLAB code that uses handle classes and inheritance might be incorrect
Description: Code generation might produce incorrect C/C++ code if your MATLAB code satisfies?all  the following conditions:? Your MATLAB code creates a handle class B. The constructor of handle class B instantiates another handle class C. The handle class B has a base class A, whose constructor also instantiates handle class C. Both allocations of C are stored in a cell array or structure, which are then stored as properties of A and B, respectively.?  Example MATLAB code that satisfies all these conditions: classdef A < handle    properties        property1    end    methods        function this = A            this.property1 = {C(1)};        end    endendclassdef B < A    properties        property2    end    methods        function this = B            this.property2 = {C(2)};        end    endendclassdef C < handle    properties        property    end    methods        function this = C(property)            this.property = property;        end    endendfunction [a, b] = mainx = B;a = x.property1{1}.property;b = x.property2{1}.property; In such situations, the generated code might use the same memory for both instances of C, instead of allocating separate memory for the two instances. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB Functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Add a dummy field to the nested type. (In the above description, the nested type is class C.) Make sure that the derived and base constructors assign different types to this?dummy field.For example, modify the code in the previous section by adding the field dummy?to the implementation of class C. Notice that the constructor of class A is modified to assign a character type to dummy, while?the constructor of class B is modified to assign a double type to?dummy.classdef A < handle    properties        property1    end    methods        function this = A            this.property1 = {C('a', 1)};        end    endendclassdef B < A    properties        property2    end    methods        function this = B            this.property2 = {C(1, 2)};        end    endendclassdef C < handle    properties        dummy        property    end    methods        function this = C(dummy, property)            this.dummy = dummy;            this.property = property;        end    endendfunction [a, b] = mainx = B;a = x.property1{1}.property;b = x.property2{1}.property;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for deep neural networks containing custom layers with an array layout different from that of the entry-point function may produce incorrect results
Description: Code generated for deep neural networks containing custom layers produces incorrect results if the following conditions are met:? Custom layer definition has non-scalar?properties. Custom layer definition has the?coder.rowMajor  pragma in the constructor indicating that the class properties are to be stored in row-major layout but code is generated by specifying column-major layout.  (or) Custom layer definition has the?coder.columnMajor  pragma in the constructor indicating that class properties are to be stored in column-major layout but code is generated by specifying?row-major layout.?   For example, consider the following custom layer implementation:?? classdef multiplyLayer < nnet.layer.Layer  ? ? %#codegen  ? ? properties?? ? ? ? alpha? ? end??  ? ? methods  ? ? ? ? function layer = multiplyLayer(name, size)??? ? ? ? ? ?coder.rowMajor;???? ? ? ? ? layer.Name = name;? ? ? ? ? ? ?layer.alpha = rand(size, 'double');? ? ? ? end? ? ? ??  ? ? ? ? function Z = predict(layer, X)  ? ? ? ? ? ? numBatches = size(X, 4);? ? ? ? ? ? numChannels = size(X, 3);? ? ? ? ??? ? ? ? ? ? Z = ones([size(X, 1), size(layer.alpha, 2), size(X, 3), size(X, 4)], 'like', X);  ? ? ? ? ? ? for iBatch = 1:numBatches?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?for iChannel = 1:numChannels? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? tmp = X(:, :, iChannel, iBatch) * layer.alpha;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Z(:, :, iChannel, iBatch) = tmp;? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? end? ? ? ? ? ? end  ? ? ? ? end??? ? end  end  For this example, the generated code produces incorrect results if code is generated with column-major layout.?
Workaround: When generating code, specify the layout?at the command line, with code generation configuration properties, or by using the code generation?app?instead of specifying the layout for a custom layer in its constructor.For example, generate code with row-major layout by setting the RowMajor?property of the coder configuration object and use this configuration object when invoking codegen. Remove the?coder.rowMajor pragma from the multiplyLayer?class constructor.cfg = coder.config('mex');cfg.RowMajor = true;?

Summary: SLRT Overload Options block may cause a build failure in some model configurations
Description: In some model configurations, the Overload Options may fail the build process by throwing an error containing the following string:"StartCallbackAPI. h:14:15: note:???no known conversion for argument 1"
Workaround: On the block mask, choose 'Input Port' as the source for the overload options.

Summary: Uninstalling a previous version of a toolbox fails when the toolbox includes apps and multiple versions are installed
Description: If a toolbox includes apps and multiple versions of that toolbox are installed, uninstalling a previous version of the toolbox fails.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Writing a very large dataset to an HDF5 file using h5write might error out on Linux
Description: Using h5write  to write a very large dataset to an HDF5 file might error out on a Linux platform. For example: >> data = randn(5475,80000);>> h5create(filename,'/a',size(data));>> h5write('test.h5','/a',data);Error using hdf5lib2Unable to open or create 'test.h5' due to internal error.
Workaround: If the dataset size cannot be reduced, create and write the HDF5 file using the HDF5 low-level Interface by first setting the?sec2?driver.For example:fapl = H5P.create('H5P_FILE_ACCESS');% Use the default sec2 driver using low-level API to work around the bug.H5P.set_fapl_sec2(fapl);fid = H5F.create("test.h5",'H5F_ACC_TRUNC','H5P_DEFAULT', fapl);space_id = H5S.create_simple(2, fliplr([5475,80000]), []));lcpl = H5P.create('H5P_LINK_CREATE');dcpl = H5P.create('H5P_DATASET_CREATE');dset_id = H5D.create(fid,"/a",'H5T_NATIVE_DOUBLE',space_id,lcpl,dcpl,'H5P_DEFAULT');H5D.close(dset_id);H5P.close(dcpl);H5P.close(lcpl);H5S.close(space_id);H5F.close(fid);% Write the data using 'fapl' created above.fid = H5F.open("test.h5",'H5F_ACC_RDWR',fapl);dataset_id = H5D.open(fid,"/a",'H5P_DEFAULT');space_id = H5D.get_space(dataset_id);data = randn(5475,80000);memspace_id = H5S.create_simple(2,fliplr([5475,80000]),[]);H5D.write(dataset_id,'H5ML_DEFAULT',memspace_id,space_id,'H5P_DEFAULT',data);H5S.close(space_id);H5D.close(dataset_id);H5F.close(fid);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated for Width block when output signal with a constant value is configured with default storage class
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a Width block when all of these conditions exist: The Width block has a constant value output. For example, a Width block with a fixed-dimension input signal that generates the signal dimension as output. The output signal is configured with the Model default  storage class through either individual mapping or a resolved signal object. The default storage class for internal data is Default.   In this case, the signal variable is not initialized in the generated code.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: RF instruments might generate distorted signal constellation and incorrect EVM
Description: The signal constellation generated by an RF signal generator instrument might be distorted, leading to larger than expected EVM values. This occurs when provided IQ data values are greater than 1. This affects the following functions and apps: rfsiggen  (Instrument Control Toolbox) Wireless Waveform Generator app (Communications Toolbox, since R2019a), see https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/details/2421208   This affects the output of RF instruments from all supported vendors, including: Rohde & Schwarz Keysight
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: imwarp can produce incorrect results for certain 2-D projective transformations
Description: The imwarp  function can produce incorrect results for certain geometric transformations specified as projective2d  objects. An incorrect result appears as a uniform image in which all pixel values have the default fill value or the fill value specified using the 'FillValues' ?name-value argument .
Workaround: You can prevent an incorrect result by using the following code before calling the?imwarp?function:iptsetpref('UseIPPL',false);Note that this will globally change the hardware optimization preferences for all Image Processing Toolbox functions and will result in slower behavior. To return the preference to the factory default state, specify the preference as?true.

Summary: The Extended Kalman Filter and Unscented Kalman Filter blocks produce incorrect state estimates when noise covariance inputs are time varying
Description: The software outputs incorrect values for states and state-error covariance when the user supplies time-varying noise covariances through the input ports in the Extended Kalman Filter and Unscented Kalman Filter Simulink blocks.

Summary: For patchMicrostrip antenna with dielectric, directivity is not oriented even after changing the direction by enabling the tilt for the antenna. However, pattern orients correctly with antenna in case of substrate set to Air.
Description: Analysis of the pattern for patchMicrostrip with and without tilt : Pattern for the antenna is oriented correctly along with direction of the antenna with no tilt for both air and other dielectrics. Pattern for the antenna is oriented correctly by enabling the tilt for the antenna with substrate as air, but the orientation is not in accordance with the antenna for other dielectrics. The issue is due to orientation? not accounted on the mesh of the antenna after it is generated, which in turn causes directivity to remain untilted.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for deep learning networks targeting MKL-DNN library might produce wrong output for certain dimensions of input to yolov2TransformLayer
Description: Code generated for deep learningnetworks that take advantage of the?Intel Math Kernel Library for Deep Neural Networks (MKL-DNN)?might produce incorrect output if both ?these conditions are true: The network contains a yolov2TransformLayer . The number of anchor boxes the?yolov2TransformLayer ?uses satisfies one  of these conditions:  For AVX2 machines, the number of anchor boxes is not a multiple of 4. For AVX512 machines, the number of anchor boxes is not a multiple of 8.

Summary: Unexpected error because of unresolved component names when simulating a?SimBiology.Scenarios?object using?SimFunction
Description: When simulating a?SimBiology.Scenarios ?object using?SimFunction , the simulation fails unexpectedly because component names are not resolved to model components in the SimBiology model. This happens when the names contain unnecessary square brackets. Examples of unnecessary brackets:  A species DrugTarget  is specified as [DrugTarget] . A parameter k ?is specified as?[k] . In a single compartment model, a species Drug 1 ?is specified as [Drug 1] .
Workaround: Remove the unnecessary brackets from quantity component names in the SimBiology.Scenarios object by using the?updateEntry?function to change their names.For example:Create a SimBiology.Scenarios object referencing species DrugTarget and Drug 1 with brackets:sObj = SimBiology.Scenarios("[DrugTarget]", [0.1, 0.3, 0.5, 0.7]);?add(sObj, "cartesian", "[Drug 1]", [2.4, 2.5, 2.6, 2.7, 2.8, 2.9]);Change the names of the first and second entries in the SimBiology.Scenarios object:updateEntry(sObj, 1, "Name", "DrugTarget");??updateEntry(sObj, 2, "Name", "Drug 1");
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for custom layers with column-major layout and non-scalar double properties of size less than 11 produces incorrect results
Description: Code generated for deep neural networks containing custom layers produces incorrect results if the following conditions are met:? Custom layer has properties of type double. Number of elements of the property is lesser than or equal to 10.? Code generation uses column-major layout.  For example, consider the following custom layer implementation:?? classdef multiplyLayer < nnet.layer.Layer  ? ? %#codegen? ?  ? ? properties  ? ? ? ? alpha  ? ? end  ? ? methods?  ? ? ? ? function layer = multiplyLayer(name, size)  ? ? ? ? ? ? layer.Name = name;  ? ? ? ? ? ? layer.alpha = rand(size, 'double');  ? ? ? ? end  ? ? ? ? function Z = predict(layer, X)  ? ? ? ? ? ? numBatches = size(X, 4);  ? ? ? ? ? ? numChannels = size(X, 3);  ? ? ? ? ? ? Z = ones([size(X, 1), size(layer.alpha, 2), size(X, 3), size(X, 4)], 'like', X);  ? ? ? ? ? ? for iBatch = 1:numBatches  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? for iChannel = 1:numChannels  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? tmp = X(:, :, iChannel, iBatch) * layer.alpha;  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Z(:, :, iChannel, iBatch) = tmp;  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? end  ? ? ? ? ? ? end  ? ? ? ? end  ? ? end  end  For this example, the generated code produces incorrect results if the hyperparameter alpha  is initialized with a size of [2 1 3] (number of elements is 6). However, the generated code produces the correct answer if the size is [2 2 3] (number of elements is more than 10).
Workaround: Generate code in the row-major layout by setting the RowMajor?property of the coder configuration object and use this configuration object when invoking codegen. For example,cfg = coder.config('mex'); cfg.RowMajor = true;?

Summary: Inconsistent results between Model Advisor check detailed and summary reports
Description: For a given Model Advisor check, when one of these options for defining the error severity of the check is true: Input parameter Check result when issues are flagged ?is set to Fail  The " severity" ? attribute in the Model Advisor check configuration file (.json ) is defined as? "severity":required   The results in the Model Advisor detailed and summary reports are inconsistent.? The detailed report correctly labels the severity of the flagged results as error .? However, the summary report incorrectly labels the severity as?warning .?

Summary: MATLAB Job Scheduler passwords are cleared when switching client MATLAB versions
Description: When you use a MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster with security level 2 or higher, you must login with a username and password (credentials). When you are prompted for your password, you can select an option "Forever on this computer" to remember your credentials. If you switch the client MATLAB version between R2020a (7.2) or earlier and R2020b (7.3), you will be prompted for your password again after previously selecting the "Forever on this computer" option. If you do not login to the MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster, your credentials will still be remembered in the previously used version of MATLAB. For example, if you switch the client version from MATLAB R2020a (7.2) to R2020b (7.3) and revert back to R2020a (7.2), you will not be prompted for your password if you previously selected the "Forever on this computer" option in version R2020a on the client.

Summary: Executing the compiler.package.installer function on macOS more than once ignores any new or changed content after the first function call
Description: When using the compiler.package.installer  function on macOS, repeated uses of the function do not change the application to be installed by the installer. For example, if you call?compiler.package.installer ?with "AdditionalFiles"  set to a text file named "fileA.txt" , and immediately call it again, but with "AdditionalFiles"  set to a text file named?"fileB.txt",  then when you run the most recent installer, the?"fileA.txt" ?text file is installed instead of the?"fileB.txt" ?text file.

Summary: Exporting test-cases from Simulink Design Verifier to Simulink Test causes?Export operation aborted error
Description: Exporting test-cases from Simulink Design Verifier to Simulink Test could fail and display an?Export operation aborted  error dialog box under the following conditions:A Signal Builder harness is chosen for exporting the test-cases Simulink Design Verifier analysis is configured with parameter constraints
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Root Inport Mapper crashes MATLAB when trying to link data of type Simulink.SimulationData.Parameter
Description: MATLAB stops responding when: Simulation results containing Simulink.SimulationData.Parameter ?are logged from an internal harness. Root Input Mapper links these results from a MAT-file or base workspace.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: C Caller block generates incorrect results or crashes during simulation when using global variables of struct type as input or output ports
Description: If you select?Enable custom code globals as function interface ,?located on the?Simulation Target ?pane of the model configuration parameters dialog: If a custom code global variable of struct  type is configured as a C Caller block port, the block might generate wrong results during simulation. If the struct  type contains bit fields, MATLAB might crash during simulation.

Summary: Invalid Model version value on model save
Description: Incorrect appended Model version  value is seen in the history section when Prompt to update model history ?is set to When saving model . The Model version  value is displayed as double value.Example: Incorrect Model version observed: <username> -- Tue Feb 0217:09:19 2021 -- Version 2.100000e+00  <username> -- Tue Feb 02 17:08:00 2021-- Version 2  Expected Model version: <username>-- Tue Feb 02 17:09:19 2021 -- Version 1.2

Summary: Erroneous parameter validation errors when simulating the Battery (Table-Based) block with Current directionality parameter set to Enabled
Description: In the Battery (Table-Based) block, if you set the Current directionality  parameter to Enabled , MATLAB might throw one of these errors when you run the model: Length of Terminal resistance, R0(SOC) must be equal to length of Vector of state-of-charge values, SOC.? In ee.sources.table_battery_base. Size of Terminal resistance, R0(SOC,T) must be equal to length of Vector of state-of-charge values, SOC by length of Vector of temperatures, T. In ee.sources.table_battery_base.  The Length of Terminal resistance, R0(SOC)  and Size of Terminal resistance, R0(SOC,T)  parameters referred to in the error messages are hidden and unused.

Summary: Assertion error in?CG::SimpleConstructionFacade::structMemberRefE when generating code for Simscape models
Description: When generating code for a Simscape model, MATLAB crashes. The crash report contains output similar to: 0x00007ffb9504be43 CG::SimpleConstructionFacade::structMemberRefE at b:\matlab\src\cg_ir\facade\simpleconstructionfacade.cpp:1386 (in \\matlab\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll)0x00007ffba1ac0303 exec::cg::ExternalFunctions::translate at b:\matlab\toolbox\physmod\common\exec\cg\src\emitter\externalfunctions.cpp:393 (in \\matlab\bin\win64\physmod_common_exec_cg_emitter.dll)

Summary: Missing equations in the exported report for Simulink notes
Description: Using the?slreportgen.report.Report  class to publish an HTML, PDF, or Word report for a specified notes or model file might result in missing equation content in the report. For a large model, the notes printer might time out prematurely with this warning:? Warning: Host system error.  Timed out printing Notes content.

Summary: JMAAB and Edit-Time checks for db_0132 display inconsistent warnings for feedback loops
Description: The?Sub-ID b? of Model Advisor?check?db_0132 ( db_0132_b: Check for transition orientation in Flow charts) displays?warnings for transitions using Graphical, Simulink and MATLAB functions, but edit-time check does not display any warnings.?Similarly, the edit-time check displays warnings for transitions in flowcharts with states in the chart, but Model Advisor check does not display any warnings.

Summary: 'IncludePath',?'DefinedMacros', and?'UndefinedMacros' values ignored for files specified with 'SupportingSourceFiles' argument
Description: When you call clibgen.generateLibraryDefinition  or clibgen.buildInteface  with the 'SupportingSourceFiles'  name-value pair argument, MATLAB ignores the values specified by the 'IncludePath' , 'DefinedMacros' , and 'UndefinedMacros'  name-value pair arguments for the files provided under 'SupportingSourceFiles ' option.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when loading or updating a model that contains a model reference hierarchy
Description: MATLAB might crash when you load or update a model that contains a model reference hierarchy.

Summary: Simulink Test execution might error if parallel builds is enabled
Description: If the Parallel Builds option is enabled for the system under test of a test case, then the execution of this test case might fail. The failure is caused by? the model being compiled before the test case Post-Load callback is executed.

Summary: Icons on push buttons do not appear in web apps?
Description: When a web app deployed to MATLAB Web App Server has multiple push buttons with icons, some of the icons do not render. This issue occurs only on servers running on Linux systems.
Workaround: Refreshing the app page one or more times corrects the problem.

Summary: Uncompilable C++ code generated due to assignment of const pointer to non-const local pointer in model reference step function
Description: Simulink Coder may generate code similar to the snippet below when a bus signal is used at the input and the model is built for a model reference coder target: void step(const bus_T* input) { bus_T* local; local = input; ... } This code might fail to compile using a C++ compiler because there is an assignment from a const pointer to a non-const local pointer.?

Summary: JMAAB check for?db_0146?displays an incorrect warning for?Trigger?block
Description: The?Sub-ID a? of Model Advisor?check?Check position of conditional blocks and iterator blocks ?(db_0146_a:?Block layout in conditional subsystem)? displays the following incorrect warning for?Trigger block when placed at the top of the subsystem. Warning :? The following conditional blocks are not located at the top of the subsystem diagram.

Summary: Coordinate reference system (CRS) properties of raster reference objects are not saved
Description: When you save a MapCellsReference , MapPostingsReference , GeographicCellsReference , or GeographicPostingsReference  object, the ProjectedCRS  or GeographicCRS  property is not saved. As a result, when you load the object, the ProjectedCRS  or GeographicCRS  property is empty.
Workaround: To save the value of the ProjectedCRS?or?GeographicCRS property, assign its value to a separate workspace variable. Then, save the variable and the raster reference object. Reassign the property value after loading.

Summary: Test for Model Component?wizard in Test Manager incorrectly adds new test case to existing test file
Description: If you have a test file open in the Test Manager and you use the Test for Model Component wizard to autogenerate test cases, the new test cases are always added to that test file even if you specify a new test file location.
Workaround: Before using the Test for Model Component wizard to create test cases and save them to a new test file, close all open test files in Test Manager.?

Summary: Signal Editor block silently fails the execution of some set_param commands
Description: For the Signal Editor block, the execution of the set_param ?function with? the parameters 'ActiveScenario'  or 'ActiveSignal' ?might silently fail if set to invalid entries. For example, if the specified scenario or signal does not exist, then the function appears to succeed but actually fails without error and the parameter is set to the first alphabetical scenario or signal in the MAT-file.

Summary: Incorrect storage class access function is used for custom TLC code
Description: Embedded Coder might use the value function of a storage class, even though the user authored TLC code requests the address.  For example, LibBlockInputSignalAddress ?returns the value function with a C address operator, if the custom block has a signal with a storage class that returns access function as its input.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 5, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: The "Color" property of the systemcomposer.profile.Stereotype class is not accessible
Description: Scripts or functions that modify stereotype definitions error out because the "Color" property on the systemcomposer.profile.Stereotype class is not available.
Workaround: Rename the "Color" property to "ComponentHeaderColor" in your script or function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for Mod block with AUTOSAR 4.0 code replacement library
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains at least one Mod block whose two input types are of the same type. The input types are one of the following: int8  uint8  int16  uint16  int32  uint32   The AUTOSAR 4. 0 code replacement library is used to generate code. The? Mfx_Mod  function? is present in the generated code.  Because the block behavior is different than the AUTOSAR standard, numerical results from code generation will be incorrect when mod(x1, x2)  meets one of the following conditions:? ?x1 < 0  ?x2 = 0
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a, R2021b

Summary: Model build does not generate A2L file when State Flow chart and If?block appear in?For Each block
Description: During model build, the code generator does not generate an A2L file if the model contains a State Flow chart and an?If ?block appears within a?For Each ?block.

Summary: Setting the parent of a Tree object?removes the tree nodes
Description: Changing the parent? of a Tree   object created by uitree  results in the treeappearing blank. This might happen when setting the Parent ?property of the Tree ? object or setting the Parent   property of a container like a panel? that contains the tree.
Workaround: Recreate the tree by calling the uitree?function and specify the new parent.

Summary: Accelerator mode build time increased from R2020a to R2020b
Description: Accelerator mode build time might have increased in R2020b as compared to R2020a for a model with a large number of Constant blocks.

Summary: Simulink Coverage throws errors after a harness model simulation fails
Description: If you run a coverage simulation on a Simulink Test Harness model which fails due to a compile-time error, the coverage tool might throw error messages during subsequent actions. For example, the following error message might be shown when attempting to close the model under test: Error in parameter number 3.
Workaround: Execute cvexit to clear the coverage environment before taking other actions.

Summary: Jobs submitted using generic scheduler interface?in nonshared mode can appear as failed after you restart MATLAB
Description: You can use the generic scheduler interface and?parallel.cluster.RemoteClusterAccess ?to submit jobs to a cluster that uses a third-party scheduler and MATLAB Parallel Server. When you submit jobs from a client which does not share a file system with the cluster (nonshared mode), jobs that have not yet completed appear as failed after you restart MATLAB.

Summary: Xcode 12 compiler error when generating C code on macOS for a flexible beam block with run-time length parameter
Description: When simulating in Rapid Accelerator mode or generating C code on macOS using Xcode 12, a Simulink model containing a flexible beam block with its Length  parameter configured to Run-time  will cause the following compiler errors: error: implicit declaration of function 'sm_core_compiler_sortIndices' is invalid in C99 [-Werror,-Wimplicit-function-declaration]error: implicit declaration of function 'sm_core_compiler_uniqueTol' is invalid in C99 [-Werror,-Wimplicit-function-declaration]error: implicit declaration of function 'sm_core_compiler_subdividePartition' is invalid in C99 [-Werror,-Wimplicit-function-declaration]   These errors do not occur with previous versions of Xcode.

Summary: Poorly formatted Iteration names and Excel tab names when generated test cases are exported to Simulink Test
Description: Iteration names and tab names in the Excel file corresponding to the iterations are generated in the form Scenarios_1 , Scenarios_1_1 , Scenarios_1_1_1  instead of Scenarios_1 , Scenarios_2 , Scenarios_3  if test-cases generated by Simulink Design Verifier are exported to Simulink Test when:Using the Export test cases to Simulink Test  link in the Simulink Design Verifier Results Summary  Windows and selecting Excel  from the Test Data Format  drop-down selection in the Export Design Verifier Test Cases  dialog Using the Create Test for Component Wizard ?in Simulink Test Manager, select the Use Design Verifier to generate test input scenarios ?option in the Test Inputs ?page, and select EXCEL ?in the Specify the file format ?drop-down selection of the Generated Test ?page.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a

Summary: Indexing into an object stored in a constant property returns incorrect result
Description: Indexing into certain types of objects that are stored in a constant property of another class can fail.?For example, the class MyClass  has a constant property ConstantProperty  that stores a MATLAB string array. classdef MyClass     properties (Constant)         ConstantProperty = ["This", "is", "a", "constant", "property"];     end end The following indexing expression returns the incorrect value when invoked from within a script; in particular,?val ?has the value?"This" ?instead of the expected value?"constant" . val = MyClass.ConstantProperty(1,4)val =     "This"
Workaround: The problem can be avoided by splitting the indexing expression into two parts. For example,constantProperty = MyClass.ConstantProperty; val = constantProperty(1,4)val =     "constant"Then, val has the value "constant", as expected.

Summary: gpuDevice returns incorrect error for GPU devices supported via forward compatibility, e.g., Ampere devices
Description: Calling gpuDevice  with no input arguments results in an incorrect error on a machine with one or more GPU devices that are supported via forward compatibility and no other supported GPU devices. The following incorrect error is displayed: Error using gpuDevice (line 26)  Unable to find a supported GPU device. For more information on GPU support, see GPU Support by Release.?  For example, this incorrect error occurs when you call gpuDevice  on a machine that contains only GPU devices with Ampere architecture.? The correct error message states that the device can be used by enabling forward compatibility for GPU devices.? Error using gpuDevice (line 26)  GPU device is not supported because it has a higher compute capability (8.0) than is natively supported by the CUDA libraries. Use parallel.gpu.enableCUDAForwardCompatibility(true) to enable forward compatibility for GPU devices. If you enable forward compatibility, you might see unexpected behavior in GPU computations. Learn more.?
Workaround: To see the correct error message regarding forward compatibility for GPU devices, call gpuDevice with the index of the device you are interested in:gpuDevice(1)?

Summary: Links to imported requirements are missing in Model Testing Dashboard
Description: Links to requirements that are imported from third-party applications, such as Microsoft Word, are not displayed or analyzed in the Model Testing Dashboard. As a result, tests and requirements that link to the imported requirements appear in the Untraced  folder on the Artifacts  pane.

Summary: Override units not applied to signals plotted in the Simulation Data Inspector when plots update at the start of a new simulation
Description: When plots automatically update to display data from a new simulation, override units specified in the Simulation Data Inspector preferences are not applied for plotted signals.

Summary: SIL/PIL simulation fails for custom storage classes with Default memory sections
Description: Consider a top model that contains a Model block where: Simulation mode  is either Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) .  A data object of the referenced model, for example, an mpt.Parameter  object, specifies Global  storage class and Default  memory section.  When you run a top model simulation, a build failure occurs, producing an error message. For example: myWorkingFolder/slprj/ert/mySubsystem/sil/xil_interface.c:47:17: error: expected '=', ',', ';', 'asm' or '__attribute__' before 'customParam'  Default uint8_T customParam;

Summary: Searching a Simulink model using the Finder might throw warnings and errors
Description: When searching a Simulink model using the Finder, edit-time and compile-time warnings and errors might be thrown. These warnings and errors do not affect the search. For example, searching a model that contains a Bus Selector block that does not connect to a bus-capable block might throw a warning. In this case, the warning looks like: Warning: 'Bus Selector1' must be connected to a Bus Creator,?Bus Selector or a bus capable block.?A possible cause of this error is the use of a bus-capable block (such as Merge or Unit Delay) that in this current situation is unable to propagate the bus downstream to the block reported in this error.?Please see Simulink documentation for further information on composite (i.e.?bus) signals and their proper usage.

Summary: After obtaining a pslinkoptions object from a model, updating its VerificationMode property does not change model validation behavior
Description: When using Polyspace R2020b with a compatible earlier version of MATLAB and Simulink (e.g., R2018b and R2019a), you can extract Polyspace options from a model programmatically by calling the function pslinkoptions . Updating the VerificationMode  property of this object fails to change the model verification behavior. For instance:? psopts = pslinkoptions(model) % Verification mode is set to BugFinder in the modelpsopts.VerificationMode = 'CodeProver' % Bug: Cannot change verification mode to Code Prover

Summary: Signal selection in 'BaselineCriteria' table of test case not applied correctly
Description: If you specify a bus signal in an external baseline file, the bus and its leaf signals are expanded in the BaselineCriteria table of the Test Manager. Bus leaf signals that you do not select in the table are incorrectly included in the baseline comparison test.

Summary: Incorrect markers for residuals plot in SimBiology Model Analyzer
Description: In the SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, if you try to plot residuals for fit results generated using a mixed effects fitting algorithm, the plot markers for IWRES  and CWRES  are swapped from those indicated in the legend.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Non-reentrant code error not reported when AUTOSAR model has concurrent server runnable inside subsystem
Description: Simulink fails to detect and report non-reentrant code in a concurrent AUTOSAR runnable when the following conditions are true: In an AUTOSAR model, a server runnable has CanBeInvokedConcurrently  set to true . The server runnable is inside a subsystem, as can occur when creating an AUTOSAR model by using?createComponentAsModel . The server runnable contains modeling constructs that generate non-reentrant code (for example, blocks with state).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using FOR/WHILE loop index variable outside the loop could result in test bench verification failures for Multiprog and PCWorx IDEs
Description: When the FOR/WHILE loop, loop index variable is not of int16 data type, using the loop index variable outside the loop could result in testbench verification failures for the generated code. This affects Multiprog and PCWorx target IDEs.

Summary: Legacy Serial or Legacy Serial F blocks error when selecting count+16 bit uint?or count+32 bit uint?transmit data type
Description: Selecting the count+16 bit uint  or count+32 bit uint ?transmit data type for Legacy Serial or Legacy Serial F blocks or opening a model from a previous release that uses these data types for these blocks causes an error. The binary data stream receive cannot handle bytes with the value 0. Choosing count+16 bit uint ?in the upper-subsystem block causes an error because pushing that value to the Legacy Serial Write block does not match the list items in the lower-level block. The binary data receive does not work correctly because the link between the low-level-read block and the FIFO write inside both subsystems is not one of the count+ versions.? Null bytes terminate the data instead of being handled correctly.
Workaround: Install R2020b update 4.

Summary: "Unrecognized function or variable" error when validating or building AUTOSAR Classic Platform model that specifies symbolic values for root port Min and Max
Description: AUTOSAR model validation and code generation throw an error if both of the following conditions exist: Symbolic Min  and Max  values are specified for a Simulink root port in an AUTOSAR Classic Platform model. A non-zero InitValue  is specified in the AUTOSAR Communication attributes  for the same port.  Validation and code generation display an error similar to the following: "Unrecognized function or variable 'my_Var'". ?Caused by: Variable 'my_Var' has been deleted from base workspace.
Workaround: Specify non-symbolic values for the Min and Max?properties on root ports.

Summary: Simulation errors when using the Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator block with specific scenes, including US highway and Virtual Mcity
Description: If you use the Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator block with specific scenes, the simulation can result in this error: An error occurred while running the simulation and the simulation was terminated   You can encounter the error if you configure the Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block with these scenes: Parking lot  Open surface  US city block  US highway  Virtual Mcity  Large parking lot

Summary: The SimBiology Model Builder app errors if a species is copied from a nested compartment and then pasted without having a compartment selected
Description: When copying a species from a nested compartment and then attempting to paste without having a destination compartment selected in the Diagram tab or the Browser pane, the SimBiology Model Builder app shows the error: ?Compartment NAME is being used by another SimBiology model component object. Specify a different NAME. ? A nested compartment is a compartment that is contained within another compartment.
Workaround: To avoid this issue:Select the destination compartment you want to paste the species into. Right-click the destination compartment and select Paste in the context menu, or use keyboard shortcuts.

Summary: Viewing logged simulation results by selecting?Simscape > View simulation data?from?the block?context menu throws an error
Description: If there are multiple simlog  variables in the MATLAB workspace and some of the corresponding models are closed, then using a block context menu and selecting?Simscape > View simulation data ?to view the logged simulation results generates an error: Model is not loaded. If the second argument to the function is either a Simulink block handle or a Simulink block path, model must be loaded before using the function.
Workaround: Whenever you close a model, clear the corresponding?simlog variable from the MATLAB workspace. After that, you can use the?View simulation data?option successfully.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Referenced model might not propagate constant value to a parent model in generated code
Description: Generated code produces a different result than simulation when all of these conditions apply: The model hierarchy contains at least three levels of models. Configuration parameter Default Parameter Behavior ?is set to Inlined  for all models. A referenced model contains a triggered subsystem where the trigger port is unconnected or driven by a Ground block. The output port of the triggered subsystem has a nonzero initial condition. The triggered subsystem passes its output to a root output port in its parent model. The root output port of the parent model has a constant sample time. The signal is passed up through at least two levels of the model reference hierarchy.  The generated code for the signal in the higher level model does not use the initial condition value from the triggered subsystem output port.

Summary: MATLAB might crash with Simulink.findVars when Merge blocks receive?inactive signals from variant blocks
Description: MATLAB might crash with? Simulink.findVars ?on a model with Merge  block having one or more inactive input ports,?while the Merge  block is active.Merge block input port can be inactive for below cases when signal is driven by Variant blocks with Update Diagram ?activation type and the condition evaluates to FALSE Variant blocks with any activation type but propagated condition turns out to be FALSE ?Example : Two cascaded Variant blocks with A==1 and A==2, will implicitly set a condition A==1&&A==2, which evaluates to FALSE.    Example of the stack trace: [? 0] 0x00007ffe913f2080 L:\R2020a_install\bin\win64\sl_graphical_classes.dll+02564224 SlSigHierInfo::getNumChildren+00000000  [? 1] 0x00007ffe6bafeff3 L:\R2020a_install\bin\win64\slbus.dll+00520179 SlSigHierPropAPI::CacheCompiledSigHierInfo+00000083  [? 2] 0x00007ffe6bea7ed0 L:\R2020a_install\bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+02064080 SlBusInsBlkAPI::CopyAndCacheBusInfoFromPort+00000192  [? 3] 0x00007ffe733da594 L:\R2020a_install\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+16885140 sluConnectPortsWithSegmentAndNotifyTransaction+00161060  [? 4] 0x00007ffe6bf69176 L:\R2020a_install\bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+02855286 RTWCGIndex::RTWCG_Index::setSystemIndex+00003606  [? 5] 0x00007ffe6c01c4c4 L:\R2020a_install\bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+03589316 VarHiddenConnectionInfo::setNIForRemoval+00013316  [? 6] 0x00007ffe6bfa1c64 L:\R2020a_install\bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+03087460 slcomp::data_transfer::DataTransferSpec::resolveSettingThruType+00012052  [? 7] 0x00007ffe6bf90e7c L:\R2020a_install\bin\win64\sl_compile.dll+03018364 SLCompDiagramImpl+00000700?

Summary: HDL code generation for DUT, containing a multiplier of complex datatype that has unconnected output, might cause crash
Description: If the DUT contains a multiplier of complex datatype that has an unconnected output port, dead-logic elimination might not remove the complex multiplier. This behavior might cause MATLAB to crash during HDL code generation.
Workaround: Manually remove the multiplier of complex datatype that has unconnected output port from the DUT.

Summary: Without DSP System Toolbox license, HDL code generation from Simscape model fails at model generation
Description: HDL code generation from Simscape models fails at the model generation stage of the workflow when sharing or RAMs are requested and DSP System Toolbox is not installed.

Summary: Simulink models that contain S-Function Builder blocks might fail to build in Accelerator / Rapid Accelerator modes
Description: When building in Accelerator / Rapid Accelerator modes, you might get an error related to undefined reference to sfunName_wrapper  functions. These functions are defined in the?sfunName_wrapper.c  (or .cpp ) files that are generated when generating code for S-Function with S-Function Builder. This might cause building in Accelerator / Rapid Accelerator modes to fail when mixed languages are involved (for example, S-Function in C and Simulink code generation target language set to C++, or vice versa.) On macOS platform with XCODE12, the build might fail with errors about implicit declaration of these functions, which are in generated code when building in Accelerator / Rapid Accelerator modes.
Workaround: Use a wrapper file that calls the C++ code from the S-Function Builder block.For an example that shows this approach, see the attached ZIP file. The attached ZIP file contains these files:?exbus.slx?- Example model filesampcall.h??- Wrapper header for calling the C++ code with a C wrapper functionsampcall.cpp??- CPP file that calls C++ code with a C interfacesfbbusex_bus.h??- Generated bus header from the S-Function BuilderGenerate the object file sampcall.obj?before building the model in Accelerator / Rapid Acelerator mode. To generate this file, run this command:?mex -c sampcall.cpp?After generating the obj file, make sure that the file is included in the libraries tab of the S-Function Builder UI.?After you run this commnad, building in Rapid Accelerator or Accelerator mode generates and builds the S-Function without producing linkage errors.??

Summary: ROS Toolbox functions rosinit or rosgenmsg may fail with older versions of Python 2.7.
Description: The ROS Toolbox functions rosinit  or rosgenmsg  may fail with some older versions of Python, such as 2.7.11rc, with the following error message:? Error using ros. internal. createOrGetLocalPython (line 221)Could not install the required Python packages.  System error message: Ignoring indexes: https:/ / pypi. python. org/ simple Collecting catkin-pkgCollecting empyCollecting docutilsCollecting pyparsingCollecting python-dateutilCollecting pyyamlCollecting rosdepCollecting rosdistroCollecting rosinstallCollecting rosinstall-generatorCollecting rospkgRequirement already satisfied (use --upgrade to upgrade): setuptools in c:\ python2711rc1\ lib\ site-packagesCollecting sixCollecting vcstools    Complete output from command python setup. py egg_info:    error in vcstools setup command: 'extras_require' must be a dictionary whose values are strings or lists of strings    containing valid project/ version requirement specifiers.     ----------------------------------------Command "python setup. py egg_info" failed with error code 1 in c:\ users\ appdata\ local\ temp\ pip-build-ddh4ap\ vcstools.  Use pyenv function to set the path to the Python executable and retry the command.
Workaround: Use Python version 2.7.15 for ROS Toolbox.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Normal?and Accelerator mode simulations may yield incorrect results for complex fixed-point types with word length greater than 64 bits
Description: Simulation results for?Normal ?and?Accelerator ?mode?may be inaccurate in models containing Simulink blocks with a complex parameter of fixed-point type which has word length greater than 64 bits and the block output datatype does not match the parameter type. For example, in a model containing a Constant block attached to an Outport block, if you specify the Constant value  as fi(1.5+2.5i,1,67,1) ?and specify the Output data type ?as fixdt(1,77,1) , an incorrect output is generated during simulation.? Observed in but not limited to the following blocks:  Constant block Gain block Unit Delay block Delay block
Workaround: The key condition which triggers this bug is that the block parameter undergoes data type conversion from its original fixed-point type to a different fixed-point type.Use alternative designs that avoid implicit data type conversions within the blocks for complex values which use fixed-point datatypes and have word length greater than 64 bits.This can be achieved by either one of these:Maintain data type consistency within the block by setting?Output data type?to?Inherit.Use a Data Type Conversion block when data type conversions are needed.Perform any data type conversions needed within the parameter or expression declaration in the block. For example, fi(fi(1.5+2.5i,1,67,1),1,77,1).
Fixed In: R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a Update 1, R2021b

Summary: Fixed-Point Tool might not display histogram visualization for accumulator element of the Sum block
Description: When using the Iterative Fixed-Point Conversion ?or Range Collection  workflow in the Fixed-Point Tool , if your model contains a Sum block in the system under design and you select?Simulation with derived ranges  as the range collection mode to use, the tool does not display the histogram visualization for the accumulator element of the Sum block. Other blocks with internal elements may be similarly impacted.?

Summary: ROS 2 node generated from Simulink model containing ROS 2 Subscribe block may crash at run-time
Description: A ROS 2 node generated from a Simulink model, that contains the ROS 2 Subscribe  block, might crash due to a segmentation fault. The crash is more likely to occur when the topic is published at a higher rate than ROS 2 Subscribe  block sample rate.?

Summary: Save All action might update unmodified requirement set and link set files
Description: The?Save All  action is available in the Requirements Editor and in the Requirements Manager App in Simulink. It is meant to save all requirement set and link set files loaded in the MATLAB session with a single action. This action updates all file timestamps for loaded requirement set and link set files including files that are not modified. The correct behavior is to save the file and update its timestamp only when the file content is modified.
Workaround: Use the Save action instead by selecting requirement or link set file one by one.

Summary: MATLAB appears to hang after processing test results using matlab.unittest.TestRunner
Description: After using?matlab.unittest.TestRunner  to?r un a script that processes and reports the results for a given set of tests, MATLAB appears to hang, remaining in a busy state for an extended time before returning to the command prompt. The time it takes for MATLAB to return to the command prompt increases as the number of sets of tests increases.?

Summary: Error generated while compiling code that calls readMemberData function
Description: If a function being compiled with MATLAB Compiler includes a call to the readMemberData  function, the following error is generated during compilation: Unable to resolve the name predmaint.internal.pmdata.var.opt.FrameOption.FramePathPrefix.

Summary: Editing stereotype property values in an architecture model for out-of-date profiles causes MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash if you attempt to edit an architecture model element's stereotype property value if the profile is out-of-date and you do not resolve the inconsistencies on loading the model. When such a model is loaded, a warning is displayed that the profile is out-of-date and a shortcut to fix the inconsistency is shown. This situation may arise if the profile was edited when the model was closed. New properties added, or properties removed are thus not present in the model using the profile, causing this inconsistency.
Workaround: Upon loading an out-of-date model, fix the inconsistencies by clicking on the Fix-It action in the warning message.

Summary: Opening a file from a UNC path might hang if the network location is unresponsive
Description: When trying to open a file from a network location either by using the open  command or by clicking on the file in the Current Folder browser, MATLAB might become unresponsive.

Summary: HDL Code Advisor check for 'unsupported storage class for signal objects' may abnormally exit in some circumstances
Description: HDL Code Advisor check for 'unsupported storage class for signal objects' may abnormally exit when model does not have any storage classes.

Summary: Client running macOS might not be able to connect to a cluster running on another machine
Description: You can use Parallel Computing Toolbox functionality to connect to clusters from a client running macOS. When the client tries to connect to a cluster on another machine, for example, to start a parallel pool on a cluster, you sometimes see an error message similar to the following. Starting parallel pool (parpool) using the 'MyCluster' profile ...  Error using parpool (line 149)  Could not connect to any MATLAB Job Scheduler clusters called "ClusterName" running on "clusterhost.domain.com". Your version of MATLAB is R2020b. Check that the?MATLAB Job Scheduler you are trying to connect to supports this version of MATLAB.  The following MATLAB Job Scheduler clusters were found but failed the communication tests:  Name:? ClusterName, Host:? clusterhost.domain.com, Error: The following exception occurred within Parallel Computing Toolbox or MATLAB Parallel Server code:  java.rmi.UnknownHostException: Unknown host: ClientHostName; nested exception is:  java.net.UnknownHostException: ClientHostName  ClientHostName  is a name that is different to the computer's hostname . To find your computer name, run !hostname  from the MATLAB Command Prompt. If ClientHostName  is the same as your computer name, check that your DNS is correctly configured to allow communication between the client and cluster.

Summary: Model Advisor Exclusion Editor displays?unidentified block ID error and renders the Exclusion Editor non-editable
Description: When a model with existing exclusions is opened in Simulink and immediately saved as a new file (File->Save As),?an error message?Unidentified block ID  is displayed. The same behavior is seen when a block with exclusion is deleted.?

Summary: Inserting block from Simulink library into a Synchronous Subsystem might throw error
