For example: s = uavScenario("UpdateRate", 5, "StopTime", 2); p = uavPlatform("UAV", s, "Trajectory", waypointTrajectory([0 0 0; 1 0 0], "TimeOfArrival", [0 1]));  gps = uavSensor("GPS", p, gpsSensor("SampleRate", 2));? % GPS update interval is 0.5 second, which is not a multiple of the scenario update interval 0.2 second. setup(s);  isRunning = true; ?for idx = 1:5  isRunning =  advance(s);  ? ? ?  updateSensors(s);  ? ? ? [isUpdated,  ?t,  ?lla] =  read(gps)? % lla output could be NaN in certain steps.  end?
Workaround: To avoid this problem, change your scenario or sensor update rate so that the sensor update interval is a multiple of scenario update interval:?s = uavScenario("UpdateRate", 10,?"StopTime",?2);p = uavPlatform("UAV",?s,?"Trajectory",?waypointTrajectory([0 0 0; 1 0 0],?"TimeOfArrival",?[0 1]));gps = uavSensor("GPS",?p,?gpsSensor("SampleRate",?2));?

Summary: Model advisor check Usable characters for Inport blocks and Outport block?erroneously flags bus elements
Description: The Model Advisor check?Usable characters for Inport blocks and Outport block  (mathworks.jmaab.jc_0211 ) flags the bus elements if it is a bus. For example, if Bus1 and Bus2 are two Simulink bus signals and var1 and var2 are elements of Bus1, and Bus1 is element of Bus2. Then one of the port is "Bus2.Bus1.var1" which is flagged by the check mathworks.jmaab.jc0211 .

Summary: Displaying cvdatagroup object causes unrecognized property error
Description: An error occurs when displaying a cv.cvdatagroup  object at the MATLAB command-line. For example, the cvsim  command below performs a coverage analysis on a model reference hierarchy (with a top model named "mTopModel") and returns a cv.cvdatagroup  object to capture the results.? However, displaying the object causes an error. >> cvdg = cvsim('mTopModel');>> cvdgcvdg = ... cv.cvdatagroup? mTopmodelUnrecognized method, property, or field 'isObserver' for class 'cvdata'.Error in cv.cvdatagroup/display (line 27) ? ? if(this.get(names{ii}).isObserver)  This bug only affects displaying the cv.cvdatagroup  object at the command-line, and does not affect the validity of the results. The object can still be used with any of the relevant coverage functions such as cvhtml  to generate a report or?cvmodelview  to highlight results.
Workaround: If you wish to see what cvdata objects are contained within the cv.cvdatagroup object, you can use cv.cvdatagroup.getAll instead.

Summary: NR Polar Encoder block output is incorrect for the first simulation run when Configuration source is From property and the parameters are defined by workspace variables
Description: If the NR Polar Encoder block has?Configuration source  set to From property  and you use a workspace variable to define either of the parameters, then the internal configuration does not update correctly when the workspace variable is changed. The first simulation run after changing the variable returns the wrong answer. A second run with no changes returns correct results.
Workaround: Set the Configuration source?parameter to From port and use Constant blocks to define the K and E values on the ports.

Summary: Potential crash during Update Diagram with System Object block
Description: Simulink can potentially crash during Update Diagram if the model contains System Object block where the System Object definition has been changed since the last Update Diagram.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code that uses row-major array layout and contains BLAS calls might return incorrect answers for certain functions
Description: Code generated for certain MATLAB functions might return incorrect answers if all  of these conditions are true: You generate a static library, a dynamically linked library, or an executable. You generate code that uses the row-major array layout. You instruct the code generator to include calls to an external BLAS library in the generated code.  Functions for which the generated code might return incorrect answers include?cov ,  nancov ,  pca ,  sgolay , and? sgolayfilt .

Summary: MATLAB might sporadically crash if the user exits MATLAB while debugging MATLAB code
Description: MATLAB might sporadically crash if the user exits MATLAB while debugging MATLAB code

Summary: NCO HDL Optimized block errors out when phase increment is zero and you are using frame-based output
Description: The NCO HDL Optimized block returns this simulation error message when you apply a phase increment of zero, and the Samples per frame  parameter is greater than 1.? Subscripted assignment dimension mismatch: [1][N ] ~= [1][0].?  N  is the value of the?Samples to frame  parameter.?? The same problem occurs with the dsp.HDLNCO System object(TM).?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Size of variable-size data incorrectly modified prior to generated code that reads and writes the same data
Description: For the following MATLAB coding pattern An assignment statement completely writes a variable-size matrix The right-hand-side of the assignment statement contains an access of a single element of the same variable-size matrix using a subscript  MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code for this statement that modifies the size of the variable-size data prior to the code that reads the variable-size data. This is incorrect when the read of the variable-size data also uses the modified size to compute a subscript. This generated code might result in reading uninitialized memory and/or incorrect numeric results. The following MATLAB function exemplifies this issue: function out = issue_2329962(x,y,z)    assert(isa(x,'double'));    assert(size(x,1) <= inf);    assert(size(x,2) <= inf);    assert(isa(y,'double'));    assert(size(y,1) <= inf);    assert(size(y,2) <= inf);    assert(isa(z,'double'));    assert(size(z,1) <= inf);    assert(size(z,2) <= inf);    y = x(2,3) .* y(4,4) + z;    out = max(42,y(end));end When code is generated for this function using the command:?? ?codegen -c -config:exe issue_2329962 ? The generated code contains   loop_ub = z->size[0] * z->size[1];  i = y->size[0] * y->size[1];  y->size[0] = z->size[0];  y->size[1] = z->size[1];  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(y, i);  b_x = x->data[x->size[0] * 2 + 1] * y->data[y->size[0] * 3 + 3];  for (i = 0; i < loop_ub; i++) {    y->data[i] = b_x + z->data[i];  } The MATLAB statement?y = x(2,3) .* y(4,4) + z  corresponds to the coding pattern described above where y is the variable-size data both written and read. The expression in the generated code?x->data[x->size[0] * 2 + 1] * y->data[y->size[0] * 3 + 3]  corresponds to the MATLAB expression?x(2,3) .* y(4,4) . Note that the size of y  has been incorrectly modified prior to this expression. Instead code should have been generated such that the size of y  is updated after the multiply expression:   loop_ub = z->size[0] * z->size[1];  i = y->size[0] * y->size[1];  b_x = x->data[x->size[0] * 2 + 1] * y->data[y->size[0] * 3 + 3];  y->size[0] = z->size[0];  y->size[1] = z->size[1];  emxEnsureCapacity_real_T(y, i);  for (i = 0; i < loop_ub; i++) {    y->data[i] = b_x + z->data[i];  }

Summary: Table UI component duplicated from a grid layout manager in App Designer is not visible
Description: When you duplicate a table UI component from a grid layout manager in App Designer using the "right-click-drag" gesture, the table is not visible and cannot be deleted.
Workaround: Save and reload the app. You will then be able to delete the duplicated table.

Summary: Requirements authored in Excel, imported to Simulink Requirements, and exported as ReqIF file might contain undefined XML namespace
Description: When you author requirements in Microsoft Excel, import those requirements into Simulink Requirements, and then export the requirements as a ReqIF file, the exported file might contain undefined XML namespace. This happens when cells in your Excel document contain formatting such as centered or bold text. Some third-party requirements management tools use an XML validator to validate a ReqIF before importing it. A ReqIF with undefined namespace will fail the validation.?

Summary: Code generation fails with error "Unable to find Interface within the ModelReferenceInterfaces scope"
Description: Code generation might fail for a referenced model that only has event based rates with the following error message:"Unable to find Interface within the ModelReferenceInterfaces scope"

Summary: Incorrect parameter override values applied during test execution
Description: If a test case has a parameter override, and uses inputs from a Simulink Design Verifier MAT file that specifies the same parameter, then during simulation the MAT-file parameter value is applied, but the test case result shows that the parameter override is applied.

Summary: Production and target properties of a coder.HardwareImplementation object synchronize with each other on modification
Description: The behavior of the coder.HardwareImplementation ?object changes in R2020b. This change in behavior might cause incorrect code generation for your target hardware if you execute any code generation script that relies on the pre-R2020b behavior of the?coder.HardwareImplementation ?object . Prior to R2020b, modifying a production hardware property of the?coder.HardwareImplementation ?object did not affect the corresponding target hardware property and vice-versa. For example, modifying the ProdHWDeviceType  property had no effect on the TargetHWDeviceType  property. In R2020b, if the ProdEqTarget   property is set to?true , the target and production hardware properties synchronize with each other when either one is modified: Modifying a production hardware property automatically modifies the corresponding target hardware property Modifying a target hardware property automatically modifies the corresponding production hardware property  For example, consider this MATLAB code: hw = coder.HardwareImplementation;hw.ProdHWDeviceType = 'Generic->8-bit Embedded Processor';disp(hw.TargetHWDeviceType); Prior to R2020b, this code displays 'Generic->MATLAB Host Computer' .  In R2020b, this code displays?'Generic->8-bit Embedded Processor '.  This behavior change causes a backwards incompatibility which might affect your legacy code that manipulates coder.HardwareImplementation  objects. Also, in R2020b, when you convert a MATLAB Coder project to an equivalent script of MATLAB commands, the generated script might be incorrect if any one  of these conditions is true: Your project uses a custom?coder.HardwareImplementation ?object in which?ProdEqTarget ? is set to?false  Equivalently, in the MATLAB Coder or GPU Coder app, in the All Settings  section of the More Settings  dialog,? Test hardware is the same as production hardware ?is set to No .   Also, in R2020b, a script generated from a MATLAB Coder project might behave differently compared to previous releases if both  these conditions are true: You generated this script by using a previous release In this script, the?ProdEqTarget ?property is set to?false ?

Summary: Compiling a model with an observer reference might crash MATLAB when the observer model has an asynchronous sample time
Description: When you compile an observer model that has an asynchronous sample time, MATLAB might crash instead of Simulink showing an error that the sample time is unsupported.

Summary: The edfread function and edfinfo object throw hard errors when an EDF+ file has signal data but has no actual signal-specific annotations other than record times
Description: When an EDF+ file has signal data and data record start times as annotations, but no other signal annotations, the?edfread ?function and?edfinfo ?object throw this error: Error using timetable (line 470)  RowTimes property must contain one time for each row in the timetable.

Summary: Lidar 3-D Object Detection Using PointPillars Deep Learning example has a dependency on Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox
Description: augmentData  is a helper function in the Data Augmentation section of the example. It uses a function called randsample , which requires Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox. As a result, you will not be able to run the Train Model section of the example without installing Statistics and Machine Learning Toolbox.
Workaround: In the helper function augmentData.m, replace the following code (line 45) :? if randsample([true,false],1) with this code:?if randi([0,1])

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for MATLAB code in which the loop bounds are unknown at compile time and the lower bound is greater than the upper bound
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for MATLAB code in which the loop bounds are unknown at compile time and the lower bound is greater than the upper bound. In the incorrect code, SIMD optimized code incorrectly computes the scalar loop bounds. ?Consider this MATLAB function: ?function out = dynamicBounds(a, b, start, stop)  ??? assert(isa(start, 'int32'));   ??? assert(isa(stop, 'int32'));   ??? out = zeros(size(a), 'like', a);  ??? for i = start:stop  ??????? out(i) = a(i) * a(i) + b(i) * b(i);  ??? end  ?end  When start > stop +1, the loop should execute zero iterations, but in the incorrect generated code, SIMD optimizations might result in one or more iterations executing at run time.
Workaround: To obtain correct behavior without disabling SIMD optimizations, you can modify the bounds of loops exhibiting this issue. For the preceding example, review the problematic case and alter the loop bounds to ensure that there is zero iteration.?if start > stop+1? ? start = int32(1); ? ? stop = int32(0); ?end ?for i = start:stop ? ? ... ?endThis modification ensures zero iterations of the loop without encountering the error condition start > stop+1.

Summary: MATLAB might hang or crash when using a deep learning network inside a resettable subsystem
Description: Triggering the reset signal to a subsystem that contains a deep learning network can cause MATLAB to hang or crash. The crash report may contain the following assertion error:Assertion `it == m_output.end()' failed

Summary: MATLAB crashes when changing a non-string stereotype property value on an architecture model after the model has been instantiated via the Analysis Viewer
Description: MATLAB crash occurs in the below scenario:Find or create an architecture model containing stereotypes and stereotype properties on model elements. Launch Analysis Model to open the Instantiate Architecture Model dialog. Select all stereotypes and click Instantiate . The Analysis Viewer opens up.? On the architecture model, change the value of a non-string stereotype property using the Property Inspector.

Summary: Plot index specified in Test Manager Simulation Output Logged Signal Set not applied correctly
Description: When you use table iterations in the Test Manager to iterate over Logged Signal sets and you specify plot index values in the Logged Signal Set table, no signals are plotted automatically?upon running the test case.
Workaround: Add this script to the preload or postload callback section of the test case.iterations = sltest_testCase.getIterations;if ~isempty(iterations)? % find the iteration? it_indx = contains({iterations.Name}, sltest_iterationName);    ? % get the logged signal set used in the iteration? if any(it_indx) && ~isempty(iterations(it_indx).TestParams{5}{2})? ? lgSet = tc.getLoggedSignalSets;? ? lg_indx = contains({lgSet.Name}, iterations(it_indx).TestParams{5}{2});? ? lgSet(lg_indx).Active = true;? endend

Summary: Import from IBM DOORS Next not working when configuration management is enabled for project
Description: When attempting to import an IBM DOORS Next module from Jazz server version 6.0.6.1 or later, and when Configuration Management is enabled for the DOORS Next project, the? Importing Requirements dialog in Simulink Requirements fails to display the list of modules. After selecting the project and selecting?Full module hierarchy ?in the Importing Requirements dialog box, the UI takes a while to refresh the DNG Module ?selector, but no entries appear in the drop-down list.

Summary: Issues with modifying session file location and renaming session file in Flight Log Analyzer app
Description: In the Flight Log Analyzer app, if you modify the location ofthe previously saved session file and load the session file with the app tosave it again, the app tries to save the session file in the previously savedlocation.  Also, if you rename the session file and load the session file with the app to save it again, the app tries to save the session file with the previouslysaved session name.

Summary: Unable to set data type for lookup table object in model workspace to alias data type in base workspace
Description: When you have a lookup table object in a model workspace and an alias data type in the base workspace, the data type for the lookup table object breakpoint cannot be set to the alias data type.

Summary: Enum file is missing from automatically generated project for protected model
Description: An enum file that is required by the protected model, but is not used by the root inputs or outputs of the model, is not included in the automatically generated project archive (.mlproj ) that is created for the protected model and its dependencies.
Workaround: The creator of the protected model can:Unpack the automatically generated project archive (.mlproj).Add the missing enum file to the project.Create a project archive from the project that contains the enum file.

Summary: Example model for FOC Autotuner Block on real-time systems uses obsolete bitstream file to configure Speedgoat hardware
Description: The model  mcb_pmsm_foc_autotuner_speedgoat.slx , provided as part of the example  Tune PI Controllers Using Field Oriented Control Autotuner Block on Real-Time Systems , uses an older version of bitstream file to configure FPGAs on Speedgoat hardware. As a result, the inverter in the Speedgoat electric motor control kit might not turn on.

Summary: ROS Master is not reachable in case of Wi-Fi or other slower connections.
Description: The default timeout for checking if a ROS master is available is 2 sec. This default is not enough for some Wi-Fi connections or other slower connections.

Summary: Incorrect update of target critic representation when training DQN agent
Description: Training a DQN agent can produce a bad policy due to incorrect updates of the target critic representation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: For an RNN whose sequence input layer?accepts 3-D image sequences, code generated for the cuDNN target might produce different output size than MATLAB
Description: In R2020b, deep learning code generation for?SequenceInputLayer  ?using the ?cuDNN library supports only vector and 2-D image sequence inputs. However, the code generator does not throw an error for 3-D image sequence inputs and proceeds with code generation. Suppose that you try to generate code that takes advantage of the cuDNN library for a recurrent neural network (RNN). If the sequence input layer?of the network accepts 3-D image sequences, the?generated code might produce a different output size compared to MATLAB.

Summary: Analysis may terminate with an?Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end?error message.
Description: Analysis might terminate without producing the results due to floating-point computations in the model that produce very large values. When you run the analysis with a user interface, it displays the following error message: Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end.  When you run the analysis in the?Test Generation ?mode, the error message displayed at the MATLAB Command Window is: Test generation produced errors  In releases beginning in R2018a, the below stack trace is produced: src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:475 err=data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double    src/data_graph/graph_rat_prop-new.c:110 err=data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double    src/tempo/tempo_safe_induction.c:1343 tempo_error=tempo_err_unknown  When this issue is hit, the stack trace will contain data_graph_err_rat_val_too_big_for_double
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 5, R2021a

Summary: Block LoadFcn callbacks do not execute in test harnesses
Description: When blocks inside an internal test harness contain a LoadFcn callback, they might not execute when a test harness is opened or loaded.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using a context menu in Simulink or Stateflow on macOS Big Sur
Description: On macOS Big Sur (11.0), using a context menu in any Simulink or Stateflow window might cause MATLAB to crash. For example, control +clicking a block in the Simulink Editor, an element in the Model Explorer, or a parameter in a block dialog box might cause the crash.

Summary: Code compiled with MATLAB Compiler SDK or executed with MATLAB Job Scheduler on Linux can crash due to an assertion failure in?dl-open.c
Description: Some code compiled with MATLAB Compiler SDK or executed with MATLAB Job Scheduler on Linux can sporadically crash with the following assertion failure:? Inconsistency detected by ld.so: dl-open.c: 265: dl_open_worker: Assertion `_dl_debug_initialize (0,?args->nsid)->r_state == RT_CONSISTENT' failed! For MATLAB Compiler SDK, the failure can occur with multi-threaded applications. For MATLAB Job Scheduler, the failure causes worker MATLAB processes to occasionally hang. For example, when running code that starts a large parallel pool, the formation of the pool can fail and one or more workers in the MJS cluster become unusable.
Workaround: You can work around the issue by setting the KMP_INIT_AT_FORK environment variable false before running your code.On all machines using MATLAB Compiler SDK, set the environment variable before starting MATLAB or running the compiled application:  KMP_INIT_AT_FORK=falseFor the MATLAB Job Scheduler, set the environment variable in the mjs_def.sh file on all nodes in your cluster:Edit the mjs_def.sh file.Add the following on a new line at the end of the file:export KMP_INIT_AT_FORK=falseRestart the MJS service on each node in the cluster.

Summary: Incorrect adjacency matrix for some SimBiology models using reversible reactions
Description: The adjacency matrix is incorrectly constructed when calling getadjacencymatrix  on a SimBiology model containing reversible reactions that have the same species on both the left-hand and right-hand sides of the reaction, for example "null <-> a + null " or "x + y <-> x + z ".
Workaround: Replace each reversible reaction that has the same model species on both sides of the reaction with two equivalent irreversible reactions. For example, replace "x + y <-> x + z" with reactions "x + y -> x + z" and "x + z -> x + y".

Summary: Model reference simulation in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode ignores updated default model argument
Description: After a model reference simulation, if you change the default value of a model argument at the point of definition, during subsequent simulations that use Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode, a top model that uses the default value ignores updates to that value .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the Sqrt block with fixed point input may mismatch simulation behavior
Description: Generating HDL Code from a subsystem with a Sqrt block results in incorrect code? under the following conditions: 1) The input to the block is fixed-point type?? 2) The Intermediate results  in the Datatype pane of block properties is set to a value other than Inherit:inherit via internal rule.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Code generated for the Signed Sqrt block with double data type may be incorrect
Description: Generating HDL Code from a subsystem with a Sqrt block results in incorrect code when following conditions are met Input is double data type. Floating Point IP Library?is set to ?Native Floating Point   Under these circumstances, the generated VHDL has compilation errors while the generated Verilog has simulation mismatches during simulation with an HDL Simulator.

Summary: CPU utilization in code execution profiling report is 0%
Description: If you run a processor-in-the-loop (PIL)?or XCP external mode simulation with execution profiling enabled and TimerTicksPerSecond ?set to a?uint64  value , the generated profiling report can show CPU utilization?values that are fixed at 0%.

Summary: Labeler apps render a blank dialog box when you add a new scene label definition.
Description: The Define new scene label ? dialog renders a blank dialog box when you try to add a new scene label definition in the Ground Truth Labeler , Image Labeler , Video Labeler,  or the Lidar Labeler . The problem occurs after loading a saved labeler session that contains 60 or more scene label definitions. The?Label Name ,?Color, Group , and?Description? fields are missing from the dialog box, and only the?OK? and?Cancel? buttons are shown. When you click?OK  , the labeler returns the following error: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.    Error in vision.internal.labeler.tool.FrameLabelDefinitionDialog/onOK (line 89)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? isValidName = this.SessionLabelSet.validateLabelName(newLabelName) ...    Error in vision.internal.uitools.OkCancelDlg>@(varargin)this.onOK(varargin{:}) (line 39)  ? ? ? ? ? ? this.OkButton = uicontrol('Parent', this.Dlg, 'Callback', @this.onOK,...  ?  Error while evaluating UIControl Callback.

Summary: Performance problems when running experiment trials with large data in parallel
Description: When your experiment uses large training or validation data sets, training in parallel takes longer than expected. This issue is caused by unnecessary transfer of data between the workers and the client. For example, with imagedatastores larger than 1 GB, the data transfer can take many minutes.

Summary: MATLAB running in a terminal window may crash when the terminal window is resized
Description: If you launch MATLAB within the terminal emulator on Mac or Linux using one of these commands: matlab -nojvm?  matlab -nodesktop?  then resizing the terminal window can crash MATLAB.??

Summary: Persistence: mps.cache.DataCache put?function fails silently when attempting to write objects larger than 512 MB to Redis cache
Description: Redis cache does not support writing objects larger then 512 MB. The?mps. cache. DataCache ?put ?function fails silently when you configure MATLAB to use Redis for data persistence and use the?put ?function to write to the Redis?cache an object larger than 512 MB. The large object is not written to the Redis cache, and MATLAB does not throw an error to indicate so. This issue does not affect MATLAB variables and objects smaller than 512 MB, which the?put ?function can successfully write to the cache.

Summary: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of floating-point expressions in Stateflow charts does not check ~= and != operators
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of floating-point expressions in Stateflow charts? (ID: mathworks.maab.jc_0481) does not check ~=  and !=  operations as expected by jc_0481 MAB guideline. The check is meant to analyze equality and inequality operations (==, ~=, !=) on floating-point data types as per the MAB guideline jc_0481.?

Summary: Tooltips in Model Testing Dashboard are incorrect for Japanese locale
Description: When you hover over a widget in the Model Testing Dashboard, the tooltip does not display correctly. For example, placeholders like?[[‚Ž]] appear in the tooltip.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier ignores certain justification rules in filter files
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may ignore certain justification rules for individual objective outcomes while running Test Generation or Dead Logic Detection.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 2, R2021a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when linked library blocks are opened and closed repeatedly without clearing global data objects
Description: MATLAB might crash under these conditions:Masked blocks from custom library are used in model. After compilation, if the model is closed and opened again without clearing global data objects such as LookupTable objects. Masks are opened.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model that contains a Model block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets all of these conditions:The model contains a referenced model. An output signal of the referenced model connects to a block that has an update method, such as a Unit Delay block.

Summary: Model advisor S-function checks might incorrectly flag the S-functions generated by the Legacy Code Tool
Description: Model Advisor S-function checks incorrectly flag S-functions generated from the Legacy Code Tool, even if the the S-function does not have state data or multiple sample times. The checks can also incorrectly flag user-defined S-functions where SS_OPTION_CAN_BE_CALLED_CONDITIONALLY  is true.

Summary: S-Function Builder displays an error when building an S-Function with non-double parameters
Description: When building an S-Function with the S-Function Builder block that contains a parameter with a data type other than double, the block might display an error similar to the following: Error reported by S-function 'system' in 'model/S-Function Builder': The data type and or complexity of parameter 1 does not match the information specified in the S-function Builder dialog. For non-double parameters you will need to cast them using int8, int16, int32, uint8, uint16, uint32 or boolean.
Workaround: Use the following steps to avoid the issue:Double-click the S-Function Builder block to open the parameters dialog. Manually edit the parameter values that are not double type, to cast them to the appropriate type.Click OK.For example, for an int8 parameter with a value of 2, change the parameter value to int8(2) and click OK.? Then update the diagram or simulate the model to build the S-Function.

Summary: Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator block does not detect some actors within Unreal Engine environment.
Description: The Unreal Engine environment?reports incorrect velocities for some actors. Depending on how the environment is configured, these incorrect velocities can be extremely large, prompting the Simulation 3D Vision Detection Generator block to filter detections from those actors out based on the Maximum detectable object speed ?parameter.

Summary: The WindXYZ inport of the Vehicle Body 3DOF Longitudinal?block can become wrongly connected internally to the external forces port when External suspension is selected
Description: When the External suspension  option of the block is selected, the WindXYZ inport can be incorrectly connected internally to the external forces port Fext, causing a units propagation error to occur.
Workaround: Check the External forces checkbox, then uncheck it. This will fix the miswiring issue.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: Real-time test cases in Test Manager for models with Stateflow chart using verify keyword errors
Description: When you run real-time test cases in Test Manager and the?models or harnesses in the system-under-test contains Stateflow charts that use the verify  keyword, then this error occurs when processing data from the target: Not enough input arguments  The test case fails and no verify signals are shown in the results.

Summary: Serialization fails for objects that are serialized as structs
Description: It is possible for class definitions that overload the loadobj ? method, which generates the serialized representation of objects of that class, to represent the object as a different data type in the serialized form.? Before the final serialization (to JSON or Protobuf) takes place, the object is converted to MATLAB data array representation.? A bug in that conversion results in an exception being thrown for these types of objects.

Summary: Requirements Traceability Report for MATLAB code file mentions obsolete file type
Description: When you generate a Requirements Traceability Report for a .m ?file, the section title includes an incorrect reference to an obsolete file type. The section title is? "Requirements from PATH_TO_FILE.req" ?but should be? "Requirements from PATH_TO_FILE.slmx."  Similarly, when you generate a report for a .m  file without traceability links, the displayed message says "PATH_TO_FILE.req does not exist or has no links"  instead of "PATH_TO_FILE.slmx does not exist or has no links."

Summary: When running a web app, slider UI components do not show up
Description: When running a web app, slider UI components in the app do not show up. The rest of the app appears as expected.
Workaround: A workaround is to reload the web page hosting the web app.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector might fail to verify the initial value of outports of an enabled subsystem in a referenced model
Description: Code inspection fails to verify the initial value of the outports of an enabled subsystem in a referenced model under all of these conditions: The outports are of Simulink.Bus  datatype. The initial value of the outports is set to a Simulink.Parameter ?that uses the?storage class Auto .

Summary: solve returns an error?for some problems containing?an empty OptimizationExpression
Description: solve  can return an error when both of the following conditions are met: The OptimizationProblem  calls diff , which then returns an empty OptimizationExpression . The?OptimizationProblem ?objective or constraint derivatives are calculated using automatic differentiation. See?Automatic Differentiation in Optimization Toolbox .  The fault is in the gradient calculation for diff  in reverse mode automatic differentiation. This calculation is not robust to empty inputs.

Summary: Sporadic crash might occur when running Simulink model with Sequence Viewer block and Model block in Accelerator mode
Description: Sporadic crash might occur when running a Simulink model that contains Sequence Viewer blocks and Model blocks?in Accelerator  mode.
Workaround: Run the model with Sequence Viewer block and Model block in normal mode.

Summary: Custom template makefile on Windows does not fully honor?'MAKEFILE_FILESEP = /'?macro
Description: On Windows, the file separator is by default the  \ ? (backslash) character. If you use a custom template makefile that defines the file separator with this macro:  'MAKEFILE_FILESEP = /'??   then, the source-to-object rules in the generated makefile can contain paths with mixed file separators. For example:?  C:\MATLAB\R2019a\toolbox\coder\rtiostream\src\utils/rtiostream_utils.c   Only the last file separator is set to the? /  (forward slash) character, which results in compilation errors: . ../llvm-9.0.1.1/WIN64/bin/clang -c -D__VXWORKS__ ....... -O2 C:\MATLAB\R2019a\toolbox\coder\rtiostream\src\utils/rtiostream_utils.c  clang: error: no such file or directory: 'C:MATLABR2019atoolboxcoderrtiostreamsrcutils/rtiostream_utils.c'  clang: error: no input files  make.exe: *** [rtiostream_utils.o] Error 1
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 3, R2021a

Summary: Load of real-time application fails for non-English locale
Description: The load of the real-time application fails for non-English locale due to incorrect timestamp on the real-time application MLDATX file.

Summary: Unable to create rosmessage for the service request and service response for custom services
Description: When custom messages are generated for services, service request and service response are not shown in rosmsg list . They are not available in the custom message registry.

Summary: Cannot create a service client for a custom ROS service
Description: When calling the rossvcclient  function for a service that uses custom messages , you will see this error: ? ? ? ?Failed to create a service client.  This happens when the ROS master needs to connect with real hardware.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB code with System objects stored in cell arrays generates HDL code with only one instance for all objects in the cell array
Description: When System objects are stored in cell arrays and step method is called by dynamically selecting one element from the cell array, the generated HDL code does not create instances for all System objects. In generated HDL code, one instance gets reused for all the elements in the cell array. For the following example MATLAB code only one RAM is created. function y = fcn(u, c)    persistent objs  if isempty(objs)  ? ? objs = cell(3,1);  ? ? for ii=1:3  ? ? ? ? objs{ii} = hdl.RAM;  ? ? end  end    y = step(objs{c}, u, uint8(1), true);
Workaround: Store System objects in individual variables and not in cell arrays. Do not assign to System object variables conditionally.function y = fcn_cell_workaround(u, c)persistent obj1 obj2 obj3if isempty(obj1)? ? obj1 = hdl.RAM;? ? obj2 = hdl.RAM;? ? obj3 = hdl.RAM;endy = coder.nullcopy(uint8(0));if c == 1? ? y = step(obj1, u, uint8(1), true);endif c == 2? ? y = step(obj2, u, uint8(1), true);endif c == 3? ? y = step(obj3, u, uint8(1), true);end
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020b Update 4, R2021a

Summary: Upload might fail when destination project uses custom Quality Objectives definition
Description: If you apply a custom?Quality Objectives  definition to a Polyspace Access project, uploads to that project might fail. The issue occurs when the custom?Quality Objectives  definition uses non-default SQO thresholds for any of these?Run-time Checks > Global Criteria for RTE checks  entries: No systematic run-time errors (red checks) No? unreachable branches (gray checks) No non-terminating?function calls and loops  To view the Quality Objectives  definition, go to the?Configuration ?tab of the?Quality Objectives Settings ?window.

Summary: Generated custom messages with empty nested messages errors out in ROS Simulink Blocks
Description: When you add a Blank Message block and set the message type to your own generated custom messages, Simulink gives the following error: Error evaluating 'MaskDialog' callback of ROS Blank Message block (mask) 'BasicModel/ Blank Message'. Callback string is 'ros. slros. internal. block. MessageBlockMask. dispatch('entityTypeEdit', ?gcb);'?   This happens when a custom message definition has empty nested messages.?

Summary: Compiling a model containing a subsystem reference block with physical ports and unsaved changes may crash Simulink
Description: Compilation of a model containing a subsystem reference block may crash if the following conditions are met  The subsystem reference block contains physical port(s) as part of its interface? There are unsaved changes inside the subsystem reference block?

Summary: ROS 2 Publish block publishes messages at a slower rate
Description: The ROS 2 Publish block sometimes publishes messages at a slower rate than the sample time of the block. When using messages with multiple nested messages in the fields, the rate of publishing is greatly reduced.

Summary: Deep Network Designer errors when exporting layers with function handle properties
Description: Deep Network Designer errors when exporting a layer or network containing a property specified using a function handle. This error also appears when analyzing or training the network in Deep Network Designer. For example, construct a layer with a function handle initializing the weights and import the layer into Deep Network Designer. layer = convolution2dLayer(5,3,'WeightsInitializer',@rand);?  deepNetworkDesigner(layer)?  Try to export the layer by clicking?Export . Deep Network Designer displays the error: Expected input to match one of these values:?  'narrow-normal', 'glorot', 'he', 'zeros', 'ones'?  The input '@rand', did not match any of the valid values.

Summary: Closed data dictionary might cause unnecessary rebuild of model reference simulation or code generation target
Description: Simulating, generating code, or updating the diagram of a top model in a model hierarchy might cause an unnecessary rebuild of a model reference simulation or code generation target when all of the following conditions are true: The referenced model is configured to simulate in accelerator mode. The referenced model is?not loaded. The referenced model uses a data dictionary that is not loaded. The referenced model contains a MATLAB Function block or a Stateflow block that uses a data type defined in the data dictionary. The first build of the model hierarchy successfully completed.  The rebuild message looks like: ### Model reference simulation target (aModelRef_msf.mexa64) for model aModelRef is out of date because a MATLAB file used by a Stateflow block or a MATLAB Function block has changed.
Workaround: Load the referenced model or the linked data dictionary.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 2, R2021a

Summary: Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar does not use?Detection range parameter to detect objects
Description: The Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar does not use the?Detection range ?parameter value to detect objects.??Instead, if you specify a Detection Range  parameter value, the block incorrectly returns detections within the default 1 m to 150 m range.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model produces incorrect answer when one or more reusable subsystems receive 1-D input and N-D input in row-major layout
Description: Incorrect code is generated under these conditions:? Model has reusable subsystem that has Function packaging set to Auto or Reusable function. Model has multiple copies of the reusable subsystem. One or more of these subsystems receive 1-D signals. One or more of these subsystems receive N-D signals configured in row-major array layout.

Summary: writePort?function errors with negative values on AXI4 Slave interface
Description: When writing negative values to an AXI4 Slave interface on the?fpga ?hardware object using?writePort , the following error message may appear: Error using matlabshared.libiio.aximm.base/validateData (line 202)  Expected input to be an array with all of the values <= 2.14748e+09.  If you have a model that has signed ports mapped to an AXI4 or AXI4-Lite interface and enable the option "Generate MATLAB software interface script " during IP Core generation, the generated script may have this issue. Summary: Coverage might return incorrect information when run on a Stateflow chart that contains an enumeration of a custom base type
Description: Coverage results might be incorrect for Stateflow charts for which all of the following conditions are true:? The chart contains a graphical, MATLAB, or Simulink function At least one input argument to the function is defined as an enumeration The chart passes an integer or other base type when calling the function

Summary: The command window password prompt for Matlab Job Scheduler does not correctly wait for input
Description: When using a Matlab Job Scheduler configured with security level greater than zero, certain operations require the user to interact with a password prompt to confirm their identity. If the MATLAB client is running in nodisplay mode, this prompt switches from an interactive window to a running in the command window. For some operations, this command window prompt does not correctly wait for the user input. This means the prompt cannot be correctly completed, and instead the user input is executed as a command in the MATLAB console.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: writePort?function of?fpga hardware object errors with vector data on AXI4 Slave interface
Description: When writing vector data to an AXI4 Slave interface on the fpga  hardware object using writePort , the following error message may appear: Error using hdlshared.internal.VectorAddressUtils.validateStartAddrForBlock (line 125)  Address for vector port must start from the boundary of an address block (integer multiple of a power of two number), for example: 'x"FFFFC"'. Summary: Code generation assertion when using matrix multiply operation in MATLAB Function Block with variable sized matrices
Description: Code generation asserts when the model has:MATLAB Function Block with variable sized matrix of structs passed as arguments. MATLAB Function block uses matrix multiply operator on the function arguments which are created as variable sized matrices.
Workaround: MATLAB Function Block with functions accepting matrix arguments, should use for-loop to apply matrix multiply operation on individual elements of the matrix.

Summary: ?rosgenmsg or ros2genmsg?function throws confusing error when message type definition is missing.?
Description: The?rosgenmsg ?or?ros2genmsg ?function?throws a confusing error when generating a custom message definition with: 1.? A misspelled message name in the .msg file. 2.? ?Missing .msg files.  ?The error returned is: ? ? Specified message package does not exist. Check the message package specified.?

Summary: Code generation might crash when accessing property of a class if that class is assigned as initial value of another class's property
Description: Code generation might crash if all   of these conditions are true:? In your MATLAB function, you create an instance of a class A?  A property of class  A ?is assigned an initial value that is an instance of another class  B  Class  B ?has a property that is not? assigned anywhere except in the classdef  declaration Your MATLAB function accesses this property of class  B   For example, this issue occurs when you try to generate code for the MATLAB function xMcosInitVal :? function result = xMcosInitValobj = McosInitVal;result = obj.property.property;  % obj.property has an initial value of "SimpleClass"end classdef McosInitVal    properties        property = SimpleClass    endend classdef SimpleClass    properties        property = 42;    endend This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function Blocks or MATLAB System Blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: find_mdlrefs function with @Simulink.match.codeCompileVariants filter returns incomplete results for expression or sim/codegen switching mode
Description: For model reference hierarchy containing variant subsystem with variant control mode in expression or sim codegen switching, invoking find_mdlrefs  with MatchFilter as @Simulink.match.codeCompileVariants  may return incomplete list of referenced models.? Consider a top model containing a hierarchy of referenced models, where one of the referenced models has a variant subsystem with model block choices. After the top model has been compiled for codegen using feval(topModel, [],[],[], 'compileForCodegen') , the post-compile variants match filters can be used with find_mdlrefs   or find_system  functions as below.  find_mdlrefs(topModel, 'MatchFilter', @Simulink.match.codeCompileVariants)  find_system(topModel, ..., 'MatchFilter', @Simulink.match.codeCompileVariants, 'BlockType', 'ModelReference')   These above calls can return an incomplete set of referenced models or blocks for the above two calls respectively.?

Summary: The cumulative distribution function (CDF) calibration curves are incorrect in the example "Physical Layer Abstraction for System-Level Simulation"
Description: The large-scale path loss calculation is incorrect for the generated plots "Calibration: CDF of SNIR per?subcarrier" and "Calibration: CDF of effective SINR per reception," causing the calibration curves to not match their references.

Summary: Help browser is unresponsive after search
Description: When a search is performed in the Help browser for a term that does not exist in the documentation, the Help browser becomes unresponsive.
Workaround: Close and reopen the Help browser.

Summary: Non-English characters in ASAP2 (A2L) file are mangled after address replacement
Description: If you specify an address replacement for an ASAP2 (A2L) file using the rtw.asap2SetAddres  function with ASAP2Fil e and InfoFile  as inputs, the non-English characters are mangled and incorrectly displayed in the A2l file as a result.?

Description: A compatible model containing a MATLAB Function block fails code inspection if the MATLAB Function block accesses a nested bus.

Description: Simulating a model may fail and return an error like the following: Data dictionaries (.sldd files) 'one.sldd' and 'two.sldd' contain different Embedded Coder dictionaries and are in the same data dictionary hierarchy. A data dictionary hierarchy can contain only one coder dictionary.?
Workaround: Use the coder.dictionary.move?function to move a coder dictionary from one data dictionary to the other, or use the coder.dictionary.remove function to delete a coder dictionary from one of the data dictionaries.

Summary: Code inspection fails for a model containing a partially assigned nested bus that passes to an if-else decision logic or enabled subsystem
Description: A model containing a partially assigned nested bus fails code inspection if the bus passes to an if-else decision logic or an enabled subsystem.

Summary: MATLAB?does not recognize the normalization insensitivity of MacOS11 (Big Sur) file system
Description: The macOS file system type Id attribute has updated to anew value in MacOS11 (Big Sur) which can cause incorrect behavior in MATLAB whenused with file names containing canonically equivalent characters, such as the character ¨ą , which is canonically equivalent to the sequence u  andˇ§ . MATLAB treats the MacOS11 (Big Sur) volume as Normalized Decompose, that is, characters are decomposed by canonical equivalence.

Description: After generating and building code for a referenced model, if you set the configuration parameters Rebuild  to Never  and Never rebuild diagnostic  to Error if rebuild required and i f the configuration parameter System target file  is set to?ert.tlc  or slrt.tlc , then the next simulation produces this error: The model '<model name> ' has the Rebuild parameter on the Model Referencing pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box set to Never. However, the following referenced models are out-of-date for the following reasons. Consider resetting the Rebuild parameter to a value other than Never.  Caused by:  Model reference code generation target (<referenced model >.c) for model <referenced model >?must be recompiled because either <referenced model >.c has changed since the last build, or it has never been compiled.

Summary: C++ coding rules checking stop at linking phase with message The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11
Description: When the C++ coding rules are computed with the option -misra-cpp , -jsf-coding-rules , or -autosar-cpp14 , in some cases, analysis might stop with the message: Global compilation phase...Linking phase for C++ coding standards...Error: The process "ps_cxx_fe" received the signal 11.Received signal 'SIGSEGV'. Exiting... The issue occurs because of a bug in the C++ linker used internally for linking translation units.?
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the option -no-linkage-cpp-codingrules, but be aware that in this case some coding rules are lost. The computation of these coding rules involve more than one translation unit. For instance, MISRA C++ rule 3-2-3 suggests that types used in multiple translation units shall be declared only once (in a header file preferrably). The checker for this rule looks for multiple declarations in separate translation units. You lose this and similar checkers involving multiple translation units when using the option??-no-linkage-cpp-codingrules.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Direct Lookup Table(n-D) block with complex floating point table data might generate incorrect HDL code
Description: The generated HDL code for a Simulink model might be incorrect if all  these conditions are true: The model contains a Direct Lookup Table(n-D) block The values in the?Table data ?block parameter?are complex floating point types. Examples of floating point types include single, double, and half-precision types.   During verification of the generated code, the incorrect HDL code might cause simulation failure in ModelSim.

Summary: Code generation may error with a 'Unrecognized method, property, or field 'Identifier' for class 'RTW.DataImplementation' message
Description: For a model containing a variant block and a message block that connects to a Root Inport or Root Outport block, code generation might error with this message: 'Unrecognized method,  property,  or field 'Identifier' for class 'RTW. DataImplementation'?

Summary: NCO HDL Optimized HDL simulation doesn't match Simulink simulation when reset is applied at reset input port
Description: This problem occurs with an NCO HDL Optimized block configured to have a reset input port and a phase increment input port.?? When you simulate the generated HDL code, the behavior when the reset signal is applied in the HDL simulation doesn't match the Simulink simulation.? The same problem also occurs with the dsp.HDLNCO System object when it is configured to use a reset input argument and a phase increment input argument.??

Summary: Incorrect simulation results when referencing a function-call model in accelerator mode
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect when all of these conditions apply: The model hierarchy contains at least three levels of models. The top model of the model hierarchy simulates in normal mode. A referenced model contains a Model block that has a function-call or trigger port and a triggered sample time type. That Model block's?Simulation mode  is Accelerator . The number of sample times varies among the models in that Model block's path. For example, the top model might have three discrete sample times, and the model that references that Model block might have two discrete sample times.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 7, R2020b Update 6, R2021a

Summary: Code generation report creation fails when using rtwreport function
Description: When you use the rtwreport  function to create a code generation report for a model with?Simulink?Report Generator, the report generation fails and displays this error: Error running reportCannot find an exact (case-sensitive) match for 'Path'.

Summary: Training reinforcement learning agents using parallel computing might crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB can crash while training a reinforcement learning agent using parallel computing. The crash is due to a communication race condition between the client and worker processes. The crash is more frequent for larger parallel pools or if the environment has a short simulation time.? On Windows, the report for this crash can contain a line similar to the following: [? 2] 0x00007ffa47bd675b? bin\win64\pgo\libmwlxeindexing.dll+00026459 MathWorks::lxe::IIndexingContext::HasSubsrefForParen+00000075?   On Linux, the crash report can contain a line similar to the following: [? 1] 0x00007fd688d8160c /usr/local/MATLAB/R2020b/bin/glnxa64/libmwlxeindexing.so+00972300 _ZNK9MathWorks3lxe16IIndexingContext18HasSubsrefForParenEPK11mxArray_tag+00000060?
Workaround: To bypass the communication race condition for PG, DQN, DDPG, TD3, SAC, and PPO agents, use synchronous parallel training and configure the workers to wait until the end of the episode before sending data to the host. To do so, configure your?rlTrainingOptions?object as shown in the following code:trainOptions = rlTrainingOptions;trainOptions.UseParallel = true;trainOptions.ParallelizationOptions.Mode = "sync";trainOptions.ParallelizationOptions.StepsUntilDataIsSent = -1;Using?StepsUntilDataIsSent = -1?is not supported for AC agents. To avoid a communication race condition for these agents, consider using a PPO agent with experience-based parallel training or a PG agent with gradient-based parallel training.
