Summary: In unit-by-unit mode Polyspace annotations are ignored on header files
Description: If you run Polyspace Code Prover with the option -unit-by-unit  or Verify files independently , results on header files appear unjustified despite Polyspace annotations justifying the results. The reason is that the header files are included in two or more .cpp  files. In unit by unit mode: During verification of the first unit with the shared header, the header file content is verified and the Polyspace annotations are then applied.? During verification of the second and later units, the results in the header file are drawn from the previous verification, but the Polyspace annotations are no longer applied.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover Server, Polyspace Code Prover

Summary: Cannot create a service server for a service rosapi/GetParam
Description: When creating messages and services which contain the "C++" keyword, the following error is returned: Unable to create a service server.

Summary: SimFunction errors on a SimBiology.Scenarios object that contains doses with empty dose tables
Description: The simulation of a?SimBiology.Scenarios ?object using?SimFunction ?errors unexpectedly if the Scenarios  object contains doses with empty dose tables. Empty dose tables can occur if all properties of the dose are parameterized.

Summary: Test execution failure with parameter overrides and SIL or PIL simulation mode
Description: If you configure iterations of a test case to override the simulation mode to SIL or PIL and if the test case also has parameter overrides, then execution of this test case errors.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: dsp.Channelizer and Channelizer Block will generate incorrect channel data if the 'OversamplingRatio' property is greater than 2
Description: dsp.Channelizer and Channelizer Block will generate incorrect channel data if the 'OversamplingRatio' property is greater than 2.

Summary: When calling multiple packages that are created by MATLAB Compiler SDK in Python or C#, MATLAB Runtime crashes while terminating the packages.
Description: When calling multiple packages that are created by MATLAB Compiler SDK in Python or C#, MATLAB Runtime crashes while terminating the packages.  For example, ?if we have MATLAB functions that call Simulink Compiler's sim command ,? such as myFunc1 and myFunc2, [myFunc1. m]function out = myFunc1()? ??  model = 'model1'; ? ? ?simInp = Simulink.SimulationInput(model);   ? ? ?simInp = simulink.compiler.configureForDeployment(simInp);   ? ? ?out = sim(simInp);   end [myFunc2. m]function out = myFunc2()? ?? model = 'model2'; ? ? ?simInp = Simulink.SimulationInput(model);   ? ? ?simInp = simulink.compiler.configureForDeployment(simInp);   ? ? ?out = sim(simInp);   end after compiling these m files into separate Python packages?myFunc1 and?myFunc2, ?we can call multiple packages in Python. import myFunc1import myFunc2obj1 = myFunc1. initialize()obj1. myFunc1()obj2 = myFunc2. initialize()obj2. myFunc2() MATLAB Runtime crashes while terminating, and?end with "Abnormal termination:std::terminate()" error.?The issue is caused by each package unloading sl_proxy_deployed?microservice module and the sl_proxy_deployed?module doesn't handle multiple unload properly.
Workaround: Users can package multiple functions into one module to avoid crash during module termination.For example,?if we have MATLAB functions myFunc1 and myFunc2,[myFunc1.m]function out = myFunc1()? ?? model = 'model1';? ? ?simInp = Simulink.SimulationInput(model);? ? ?simInp = simulink.compiler.configureForDeployment(simInp);? ? ?out = sim(simInp);end[myFunc2.m]function out = myFunc2()? ?? model = 'model2';? ? ?simInp = Simulink.SimulationInput(model);? ? ?simInp = simulink.compiler.configureForDeployment(simInp);? ? ?out = sim(simInp);endWe can compile both m files into a single Python package myFunc12,?we can call both functions in the package in Python.import myFunc12obj = myFunc12.initialize()obj.myFunc1()obj2.myFunc2()The package will run and terminate successfully.

Summary: Using Java Builder or similar code to print a variable can crash MATLAB
Description: Running on a non-interpreter thread and using Java Builder or similar code to print a MATLAB variable can cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Analysis failure with Polyspace Server products when using the -batch option
Description: When a Polyspace analysis is sent to a remote server, in some circumstances, the analysis options can be altered.? This can lead to errors in the remote analysis, while the same analysis runs to completion when performed locally.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier might fail to complete normally when performing Out of bound array access detection on models containing matrix-typed data with an InitialValue
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might fail to complete normally when all of the following conditions are true: Design Error Detection is performed with?Out of bound array access   enabled A data object of matrix type has a non-empty InitialValue   specification (such as through the Model Workspace).  In some cases, analysis might fail with an "Error detection produced errors" message. In all cases, the return status of sldvrun will be 0 when this issue is applicable.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Viewing PowerPoint presentation more than once using rptview or pptview results in error
Description: Viewing a PowerPoint presentation more than once using rptview  or pptview  results in this error: Error using mlreportgen.utils.internal/executeRPC (line 60)  Retry calling function "@()showNETObj(hNETObj)".    Error in mlreportgen.utils.PPTPres/show (line 54)  ? ? ? ? ? ? mlreportgen.utils.internal.executeRPC(@()showNETObj(hNETObj));    Error in mlreportgen.utils.internal/rptview (line 33)  ? ? ? ? ? ? show(viewer);    Error in rptview (line 65)  mlreportgen.utils.internal.rptview(fileName, varargin{:})    Error in test1 (line 9)  rptview(ppt);    Caused by:  ? ? Error using mlreportgen.utils.PPTPres>showNETObj (line 237)  ? ? Message: Presentation (unknown member) : Object does not exist.  ? ? Source: Microsoft PowerPoint  ? ? HelpLink:

Summary: Use of nargout in function with arguments block might return incorrect value
Description: Calls to nargout ?with empty parentheses "nargout() " ?in functions that contain arguments blocks might incorrectly return the value of nargin .? For example, define the MATLAB function foo :? function [a,b] = foo(x)arguments    xendnargout()end Call the function foo ?as follows :? >> foo(1); MATLAB might incorrectly return the value of nargout  as 1. ?T he correct nargout  value for this call is?0 .??
Workaround: Remove the empty parentheses from the call to?nargout:?function [a,b] = foo(x)arguments    xendnargoutendIf you make the same call to foo as before,??>> foo(1);MATLAB returns the correct value 0 of nargout.??

Summary: Connections inside a Subsystem Reference block appears broken when a model is loaded
Description: In certain situations, when you load a model that contains a Subsystem Reference block, the blocks inside the Subsystem Reference block might not get rendered correctly. Also, the connections inside the Subsystem Reference block might be broken. This issue might happen if you try loading a model with a Subsystem Reference block immediately after performing?any  of these actions: Copy and paste to the Subsystem Reference block in which the paste operation fails and throws an error? Converting a Subsystem block to a Subsystem Reference block in which the conversion fails and throws an error
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code from a Simulink model containing a Function Caller block
Description: Generating code from a Simulink model with one or more Function Caller blocks may cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Renaming the root node in the Blockset Tree panel in Simulink Blockset Designer causes an error
Description: Changing the name of the root node in the Blockset Tree panel in Simulink Blockset Designer produces the error "Unrecognized method, property, or field 'BLOCKSET_LIBRARY' for class'Simulink.BlocksetDesigner.internal.MetaDataManager'".

Summary: MATLAB warns about being disconnected from the license server after connection has been restored
Description: If you are using MATLAB with a network license and you have enabled idle timeout, you might encounter this issue. When the MATLAB connection to the license server times out and is idle a long time, MATLAB continues to issue warnings about not being connected even after the connection is restored. This commonly happens when a VPN connection is stopped and MATLAB is left idle overnight. Then, in the morning after the VPN connection is restored, MATLAB issues these warnings.
Workaround: To clear the messages, restart MATLAB.

Summary: Error when linking MATLAB Function block code to selection in IBM DOORS Next
Description: When you link a requirement from IBM DOORS Next to code in a MATLAB Function block by right-clicking the code selection and selecting Link to Selected Item(s) in DOORS Next , this error occurs:  Selection linking failed: index exceeds number of array elements (0) .

Summary: Requirements report fails to generate if links in the report have a custom link type
Description: Simulink Requirements link objects have a Type  property. The Type  property can be one of the built-in link types or a custom link type. When you generate a requirements report, you can choose to include the links to or from the requirements in the report. If any of these links have a custom link type, then the report fails to generate.

Summary: Simulink Requirements link is not copied from library block to referenced instance of library block
Description: When you add a masked library block or a?Subsystem?block from a Library to a Simulink model, a referenced instance of the library block is created. If the library block contains a link to a requirement in Simulink Requirements, the referenced instance of the library block also contains the requirements link. You can edit the referenced instance of the library block by disabling the link to the library block. Then, you can re-establish the library link by pushing the changes back to the library block. However, the requirements link might not be copied into the referenced instance of the library block in some cases. This can happen when you are working with a referenced instance of a library block that is nested inside of another referenced instance of a library block. In this case, if you click Disable this link ?from the warning message on the Simulink canvas, then the requirements link will not be copied into the referenced instance of the library block. Instead, if you click Disable Links ?in the Subsystem ?tab, or right-click the subsystem and select Library Links  > Disable Link , then the requirements link is copied into the referenced instance of the library block as expected.
Workaround: If you click?Disable Links?in the?Subsystem?tab, or right-click the subsystem and select?Library Links >?Disable Link, then the requirements link is copied as expected.
Products: Simulink Check, Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible when a Constant block in a referenced model drives conditionally executed subsystem output
Description: Code generation might produce incorrect results when all of these conditions apply:The Underspecified initialization detection  configuration parameter is set to simplified ?for the model. The model contains a Model block inside a conditionally executed subsystem, such as an enabled, triggered, or function-call subsystem. The output of the Model block connects directly to an Outport block in the conditionally executed subsystem. The output of the Model block is driven by a Constant block connected to a root Outport block in the referenced model. The Sample time  of the Constant block is set to inf .
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Model block output port and the Outport block in the conditionally executed subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur if reusable subchart contains exported graphical function with return value
Description: Incorrect code might occur for a model that meets the following conditions:The Stateflow chart contains a graphical function from custom Simulink library. This exported graphical function has output data. This graphical function is called from different subcharts present within the same model.
Workaround: Set the graphical function's Function Inline Option?property to Inline.

Summary: Polyspace fails to report some results, with the error: Database::connect: failed to open the interprocess mutex
Description: In some cases, Polyspace might fail to access the database where it stores findings, and as a consequence some findings might not be reported. When this occurs, the Polyspace log contains the following entry, possibly several times: ? Database::connect: failed to open the interprocess mutex.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: The ssim, multissim, and multissim3 functions?return higher quality scores than expected.
Description: An incorrect rounding mode in the implementation of these algorithms lead to inflated quality scores. Reported SSIM values are higher than expected.

Summary: Project does not automatically rename usages of data dictionaries inside internal test harnesses
Description: Project automatic renaming does not update data dictionary names  inside internal test harnesses saved in a model. When renaming a data dictionary, in the Rename  dialog box, Project offers to automatically update all links to the data dictionary. However, the automatic renaming does not complete the expected actions :?  When the user expands the impacted model and double-clicks the Data Dictionary  line in the Rename  dialog box, Project highlights the data dictionary of the containing model instead. When the user confirms renaming the data dictionary in the Rename  dialog box, Project does not update the link in the internal test harness.
Workaround: Manually update the data dictionary name in the internal test harness.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: MATLAB throws an out of memory error or crashes when calling h5disp or h5info on an HDF5 file containing a scalar string
Description: When returning information from an HDF5 file containing HDF5 scalar strings, h5disp  and  h5info  fail with an out of memory error or crash MATLAB.

Summary: Delay balancing might fail during HDL code generation in the presence of From and Goto blocks implementing a feedback loop in large complex models
Description: In large and complex models, delay balancing might fail when the design contains feedback loops implemented using From and Goto block pairs. The error message typically states: ?Delay balancing unsuccessful because Delay introduced in feedback loop cannot be path balanced.  ?Offending Block: <path-to-block>  The error message also points to the block whose output connects to the offending Goto block of the From and Goto block pair.
Workaround: Replace the offending From and Goto?block pairs with actual signal connections in the design.

Summary: 3-D convolutions on a GPU can cause MATLAB to crash when running Windows 10.
Description: On Microsoft Windows 10 platforms, 3-D convolutions on a GPU can cause MATLAB to crash. This includes: Training a network containing a convolution3DLayer  object using the trainNetwork  function Training a dlnetwork  object containing a convolution3DLayer  object using a custom training loop. Training a network defined as a model function that uses the dlconv  function on data with three spatial dimensions. Making predictions with SeriesNetwork or DAGNetwork objects containing     a convolution3DLayer  object using the classify, predict, and activations functions.           Making predictions with a dlnetwork object containing             a convolution3DLayer  object using the predict and forward functions.                 Making predictions with a network defined as a model function that uses the                 dlconv  function on data with three spatial dimensions.

Summary: Requirements Editor might not display images in descriptionfor requirements imported with ReqIF
Description: When you import aReqIF file into Simulink Requirements, if the imported requirements containimages, the images might not display in the Requirements Editor.

Summary: Synchronous Machine block displays incorrect unsaturated characteristic
Description: For the Synchronous Machine block,?if you select the Electrical > Plot Open-Circuit Saturation (pu)  option on the block context menu, the displayed unsaturated characteristic is incorrect.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: SIMD code generation results in incorrect answers for min/max operations operating on NaN inputs
Description: When you enable SIMD code generation through an Intel SSE/AVX  or ARM NEON CRL  table, the optimized loops for min/max operations may produce incorrect answers for NaN  inputs. The SIMD optimized implementations of min/max operations do not preserve the expected behavior of a non-NaN  input being returned when only one input of min/max operations is a NaN . The precise behavior depends on the particular SIMD instruction set being targeted, but neither of the supported vendor's SIMD instruction sets matches simulation behavior for min/max operations.

Summary: The Define New ROI Label?dialog appears blank when you try to add a new ROI label definition in any of the Labeler apps
Description: The Define New ROI Label ? dialog renders a blank dialog box when you try to add a new ROI label definition. The problem occurs after loading a saved labeler session that contains 60 or more label definitions into a Labeler app.  The Label Name , Color, Group , and Description  fields are missing from the dialog box, and only the OK  and Cancel  buttons are shown. When you click OK  , the labeler returns the following error: Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type. Error in vision.internal.labeler.tool.ROILabelDefinitionDialog/onOK (line 139)  ? ? ? ? ? ? isValid = this.SessionLabelSet.validateLabelName(newLabelName)...  Error in vision.internal.uitools.OkCancelDlg>@(varargin)this.onOK(varargin{:}) (line 39)  ? ? ? ? ? ? this.OkButton = uicontrol('Parent', this.Dlg, 'Callback', @this.onOK,...?  Error while evaluating UIControl Callback.?
Workaround: If you wish to add additional label definitions to a loaded session with 60 or more label definitions, then perform the following steps:In a new labeler session, recreate the label definitions and additional new "spare" label definitions to save for future modification.In a later session, you can load the previously saved definitions and modify the labels.The additional labels created in step 1 were created as placeholders for future sessions.

Summary: Unable to import an SMBL file containing reaction modifiers
Description: When importing an SBML file with sbmlimport , SimBiology incorrectly handles reaction modifiers if the model contains multiple compartments or the name of the reaction modifier is not a valid MATLAB variable name. In some cases, the file will import without error, but the model incorrectly import species and reactions. In other cases, the import will fail with an error message such as the following: Invalid Reaction value. Spaces are required before and after species names and stoichiometric values, for example, 2 A + B -> 2 C. Stoichiometric values must be greater than zero. Type 'help SimBiology.Model.addreaction' for more information.Error in privatesbmlio
Workaround: Replace each modifier in the SBML file with a corresponding reactant and product with the same stoichiometric coefficient. For example, consider an SBML file containing the following definition for reactants, products, and modifiers in a reaction:<listOfReactants>  <speciesReference species="reactant" stoichiometry="2" constant="true"/></listOfReactants><listOfProducts>  <speciesReference species="product" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/></listOfProducts><listOfModifiers>  <modifierSpeciesReference species="modifier"/></listOfModifiers>Replace the above SBML with the following:<listOfReactants>  <speciesReference species="reactant" stoichiometry="2" constant="true"/>  <speciesReference species="modifier" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/></listOfReactants><listOfProducts>  <speciesReference species="product" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/>  <speciesReference species="modifier" stoichiometry="1" constant="true"/></listOfProducts>

Summary: SVN cleanup removes unversioned files, ignored files, and stale transactions and locks
Description: In releases prior to R2020a Update 5, SVN cleanup removes stale locks,                    unfinished transactions, and unversioned or ignored files.  Carefully use this functionality as it might delete unversioned files that you need for your design.

Summary: Security Issue: MATLAB Compiler may include your SMTP server login information when packaging applications
Description: If you send email from within MATLAB using the sendmail  function after setting up your SMTP server information using the setpref  function, MATLAB Compiler includes your SMTP server username and password when packaging applications.
Workaround: To prevent this from happening, replace your SMTP server username and password in your MATLAB preferences with blank values. You can do this by typing the following commands at the MATLAB command prompt:setpref('Internet','SMTP_Username','');setpref('Internet','SMTP_Password','');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for certain sparse assignment operations might produce incorrect answers
Description: Code generated for the statement?S(:,X) = R ?in your MATLAB code might produce?incorrect answers if all ?of these conditions are true: ? S is a sparse row vector R is a row vector (either sparse or full) X is a valid column index for S

Summary: On Windows 10 May 2020 Update, a computer restart can occur when model simulation ends
Description: On Windows 10 May 2020 Update   and later operating systems, a computer restart can occur when Simulink Desktop Real-Time model simulation in external mode ends. The computer restart occurs more often when the model uses a large value for the ExtModeTrigDuration  parameter. This parameter specifies the size of external mode signal buffers.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for?a model containing a?Data Store Write block inside a reusable subsystem that interacts with another subsystem that has an initialization function
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: ?The model contains a subsystem that has an initialization function. ?The output of this subsystem connects to the input of another subsystem.? ?For the latter subsystem, in the Subsystem block parameters dialog box,? the?Function packaging ?parameter is set to? Reusable function  The latter subsystem has a Data Store Write block that directly connects to its subsystem input.

Summary: Calling the imaqhwinfo function for hamamatsu and qimaging adaptors might return a misleading error message
Description: When calling imaqhwinfo("hamamatsu")  or imaqhwinfo("qimaging") , MATLAB might return an error message containing a link to the Add-On Explorer to install the support packages for these adaptors. However, the link is broken since native support for Hamamtsu and Qimaging have been removed from MATLAB in R2020a. For example, the old error message is as follows. >> imaqhwinfo("qimaging")   Error using imaqhwinfo (line 131)   To use the 'qimaging' adaptor, install the QImaging support?package via Add-On Explorer.     The updated errors no longer link to the Add-On Explorer and have new messages. For example, the new error message is as follows.? >> imaqhwinfo("qimaging")   Error using imaqhwinfo (line 143)   QImaging is no longer natively supported in MATLAB. For more information, see QImaging Camera Support from Image?Acquisition Toolbox.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: err output port of NR Polar Decoder gives wrong answer
Description: Simulating NR Polar Decoder block in accelerator or rapid accelerator modes produces a wrong answer for the?err  output port.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the cast?function might return a real matrix instead of a complex matrix with zero imaginary part
Description: If you use the 'like' ?argument of the?cast ?function in the generated code , the result might have incorrect complexity if all ?these conditions are true: The first input A  (the input array that is to be cast) is sparse, real, and numeric (not logical) The last input p  (the prototype) is complex
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: MISRA C 2012 Rule 13.2 not detected for volatile field of a struct variable
Description: MISRA C 2012 Rule 13.2 states that "the value of an expression and its persistent side effects shall be the same under all permitted evaluation orders." A violation is not detected when both these conditions are true: Both operands of a binary expression are volatile. One of the operands uses both array access operation (operator [] ) and structure field access operation (operators .  or -> ), such as g.b[0] .  For example, the following code indicates rule detection: extern volatile unsigned char a[10];typedef struct {  volatile unsigned char b[10];} stf;extern stf g;void funcA(unsigned char *x, unsigned char *y){  *x = a[0] + a[1]; //      MISRA C 2012 Rule 13.2 can be detected  *y = g.b[0] + g.b[1]; //  MISRA C 2012 Rule 13.2 cannot be detected}
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Autorename fails for a Simulink bus object containing multiple bus elements.
Description: The autorename fails for a Simulink bus object that meets the following conditions: The bus object contains two or more bus elements. One of the bus elements is based on the type that is being renamed.  MATLAB stack trace: Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BaseWorkspaceFileHandler/modifyObject  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.BusObjectHandler/refactor  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Buses.Refactoring.VariableRefactoringHandler/refactor  Error in dependencies.internal.action.refactor  Error in dependencies.internal.action.refactor  Error in Simulink.ModelManagement.Project.Dependency.refactor?
Workaround: Manually modify the remaining instances of the bus usages.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Polyspace on Windows crashes in the C to intermediate language translation phase
Description: When running Polyspace Code Prover, you might see an error message in the?C to intermediate language translation   phase. The message refers to a?ps_c_me.exe ?executable. For instance: System error Invalid argument (inval: Invalid argument)?Error: unable to execute C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\ps_c_me.exe (errno=22).? The issue occurs because the internal?ps_c_me.exe  executable crashes on Windows if the full command is too long (32k characters limit). This might happen if you use a very large number of options when running Polyspace Code Prover.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover Server, Polyspace Code Prover

Summary: Imported assessment results are empty in a new MATLAB session
Description: If you export a Simulink Test results file and then imported it into a new MATLAB session, the assessment results in the result set are empty and invalid.

Summary: MATLAB Web App Server on Linux produces an error when using the?uigetfile?function in a web app
Description: When? you use the uigetfile  function in a web app that is running on the MATLAB Web App Server in Linux, the server produces the following error in the session log:  "Permission denied"

Summary: Failure to login to IBM DOORS Next when performing oslc.configure() procedure
Description: When logging in to IBM DOORS Next (previously known as IBM DOORS Next Generation or DNG), after the correct server name, user name, and password are provided by user, MATLAB prompts for the password again, and again, and then prompts to confirm server name and/or user name, followed by more password prompts, but login never succeeds after entering all correct information.

Summary: Simulink might crash when multiple models simulate simultaneously, but the models finishing last contain string signals
Description: Simulink allows multiple unrelated models to simulate at the same time. Simulink might crash if one model finishes simulation and the remaining models contain string signals.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: MAAB check?Stateflow transition appearance incorrectly flags transitions crossing junctions
Description: The Model Advisor check?Stateflow transition appearance ? (Check ID:mathworks.maab.db_0129) incorrectly flags?transitions crossing junctions when transitions and junctions are grouped into sub-charts within the states.

Summary: Code View hangs after post-processing generated code
Description: When you customize the generated code after it is written to the disk, the Code View might hang when you try to view the modified code files.?

Summary: Simulink models with calls to functions utilizing sparse matrices may produce an unexpected error during simulation and C++ code generation
Description: Simulation and generating code for models containing calls to functions utilizing sparse matrices (e.g, filtfilt and sparse) will result in an unexpected error under the following conditions: ?The function is called from a MATLAB Function Block or Stateflow-Based Block (e.g, Truth Table) ?The model Configuration has C++ set as its Simulation Target Language   The error will contain text "undefined reference to", for example: Copy_of_mg2282394_20a_sfun.mexa64 @Copy_of_mg2282394_20a_sfun.mol????? c1_Copy_of_mg2282394_20a.o: In function `c1_chartstep_c1_Copy_of_mg2282394_20a(SFc1_Copy_of_mg2282394_20aInstanceStruct*)':c1_Copy_of_mg2282394_20a.cpp:(.text+0x17a5): undefined reference to `cs_di_sqr(int, cs_di_sparse const*, int)'c1_Copy_of_mg2282394_20a.cpp:(.text+0x17bf): undefined reference to `cs_di_lu(cs_di_sparse const*, cs_di_symbolic const*, double)'

Summary: The spectrum plot is displayed in linear units.
Description: The spectrum plot of radarWaveformAnalyzer?is displayed in linear units but should be display in dB to agree with the y-axis label.?
Fixed In: R2019b Update 9, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Streaming modes of Multiply-Accumulate block might fail to generate HDL test bench
Description: When the Multiply-Accumulate block is used in a larger model that contains blocks such as HDL FIFO, and the?Operation mode  block parameter is set to Streaming - using Start and End Ports  or Streaming -using Number of Samples , then it might?cause the generated model to fail to compile, and hence fail to generate HDL testbench.
Workaround: Move the Multiply-Accumulate block into its own Subsystem when the?Operation mode?block parameter is set to?Streaming - using Start and End Ports?or?Streaming -using Number of Samples, and then set the?FlattenHierarchy parameter to off on the Subsystem block.

Summary: Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar sensor mounted under wheel of vehicle causes vehicle to tilt
Description: If you mount a Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar sensor block under the one of the wheels of a Simulation 3D Vehicle with Ground Following block, then the vehicle becomes tilted. The tilt of the vehicle is relative to the height of the sensor above the ground.

Summary: Persistent variable access inside an if condition preceded by a for loop can generate an assertion during HDL code generation
Description: Persistent variable inside an if condition right after for-loop can cause assertion. Here is an example snippet code that can generate the following assertion during code generation. Failed Assertion failed: b:\ matlab\ src\ cgir_hdl\ pir_transforms\ cgfcnconverter. cpp:845:false?  Example MATLAB Code:?  function y = access_buffer(u, EnableOut)  persistent buffer  if isempty(buffer)  ? ? buffer = zeros(1, 100, 'int16');  end  index = uint16(0);  y = int16(0);  if EnableOut  ? ? for i=1:20  ? ? ? ? index = index + uint16(i);  ? ? end  ? ? y = buffer(index); % read state  end  buffer(1) = u; % Write state  end

Summary: A timer TasksToExecute property can affect a running TimerFcn
Description: Changing the TasksToExecute  property while a multirate timerFcn  is running should cause the timer to react.  This assumes that the TasksToExecute  property gets reset to a new value before the timer finishes scheduling timerFcn , based on the previous value of the TasksToExecute  property.? Otherwise, a race condition can occur. For example:   import matlab.unittest.TestCase;  testCase = TestCase.forInteractiveUse;  changeTasksToExecuteMidCallTo = 20;  timer1 = timer('Period',1,'ExecutionMode','FixedRate','TimerFcn','disp(''Running'')','TasksToExecute',40);  start(timer1);  pause(1);  timer1.TasksToExecute = changeTasksToExecuteMidCallTo;   wait(timer1); % wait for the timer to stop Note : If the timer has already scheduled 40 tasks before execution of timer1.TasksToExecute = changeTasksToExecuteMidCallTo; , the timer will not be affected. Use this kind of workflow sparingly, as it has race condition issues. Ensure that the timer ran 20 times, changing the TaskstoExecute  affected the number of times that the timer ran. Assuming no race condition:  testCase.verifyEqual(timer1.TasksExecuted, changeTasksToExecuteMidCallTo);

Summary: The function factormay return wrong results for polynomial expressions when called with the option'FactorMode', 'Full'
Description: The function factor might return a wrong factorization of polynomial expressions when called withthe option 'FactorMode', 'Full' . The product of all factors and the expressionmay differ by a constant factor.?

Summary: Code generation for the?resample?function errors out for constant inputs when variable sizing is enabled and dynamic memory allocation is turned "Off"
Description: Standalone code (static library/dynamically linked library/Simulation-in-the-Loop (SIL)/Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL)) generated for the?resample ? function returns an error when the input arguments are constants and the configuration settings satisfy these conditions: Variable Sizing is enabled Dynamic Memory Allocation is turned "Off"   For example, define a function?foo ?that calls?resample : function y = foo(x) %#codegenx = rand(100,1);y = resample(x,4,5);end Generate a standalone C static library for the function?foo ?using the following commands: >> cfg = coder.config('lib');>> cfg.EnableVariableSizing = true;>> cfg.DynamicMemoryAllocation = "Off";?>> codegen foo -config cfg The commands result in this error message: ??? Computed maximum size of the output of function 'resample' is not bounded.  Static memory allocation requires all sizes to be bounded.  The computed size is [:? x 1].  Please consider enabling dynamic memory allocation to allow unbounded sizes.  Error in ==> foo Line: 3 Column: 5  Code generation failed: View Error Report  Error using codegen (line 210)?

Summary: Pasting large amounts of text into a Stateflow object is very slow with syntax highlighting enabled
Description: Simulink becomes visibly slow when more than a thousand characters of text are added to a Stateflow state or transition if you have syntax highlighting enabled. When you paste an order of magnitude more text than this, the editor stops responding as it completes the highlighting operation.?
Workaround: Use MATLAB Function blocks wherever possible to capture large amounts of code instead of putting it directly on a state or transition label.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: sum(x,dim) might return incorrect answers for?single?and?half?inputs?in generated code that uses row-major layout
Description: Code generated for the function  sum(x,dim) , where?dim ?is the dimension to operate along,?might return incorrect answers if all  the following conditions are true: class(x)  is 'single ' or 'half ' The generated code stores  x ?in row-major order x  is not a vector x  is not empty The dim  argument is supplied size(x,dim)  is greater than 1024  size(x,k)~=1 ?for some?k < dim   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: fmincon?code generated with?MATLAB Coder can return an incorrect result
Description: Because of an error in the code generation version of the fmincon  'sqp'  algorithm, the internal line search can sometimes cause the algorithm to converge to an infeasible point or a point that is not a local minimum. You can observe when the internal line search operates in the Step Length column of iterative display (Display  option = 'iter' ). When any reported step length is less than 1, the line search operates, and the code generation version of fmincon  can sometimes give an incorrect result.
