Summary: Nonlinear index reduction may not work correctly for models involving linear cancellation of variables
Description: In models that have a high differential index and equations involving linear cancellation of some variables (for example, y == x-x ), the nonlinear index reduction?algorithm may either fail to reduce the differential index, or may?produce a suboptimal index-reduced system.

Summary: Incorrect simulation results when a Constant block connects to a Model block with a faster rate inside the referenced model
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect when all of the following conditions apply: The Treat each discrete rate as a separate task ?configuration parameter is selected. The model contains a Constant block that directly drives an input port of a Model block. The Constant block has explicit discrete sample time specification. In the referenced model, a signal from the root Inport block transitions to a faster rate. For example, a Rate Transition block, subsystem Inport or Outport block, or Signal Specification block changes the rate.  During the first time step, the Constant block should execute after the blocks with faster rates. The faster block should receive a zero initial value from the Constant block. However, the Constant block incorrectly initializes with the value specified for its?Constant value ?parameter.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Conversion block at the output port of the Constant block, and select the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter of the Signal Conversion block.

Summary: Incorrect analysis results on certain models containing Sqrt blocks with fixed-point input signal
Description: Simulink Design Verifier might report incorrect derived ranges and analysis results under the following conditions: The model contains a?Sqrt? block with function?parameter set to sqrt  or  rSqrt  The input signal type of the Sqrt  block is?fixed-point with more than 32 bits wordlength and/or non-zero fractionlength

Summary: Using the?memcpy function in a Stateflow chart throws assertion failure errors during simulation
Description: When you run a model that uses memcpy ?as a custom code C function in a Stateflow chart and the "Import Custom Code" configuration parameter is enabled, Simulink throws an error similar to: failed assertion in b:\matlab\toolbox\shared\cgxe\src\cgxe\engine\jitengineimpl.cpp at line 883:?In sanityCheckFunctionCalls: VM Internal Error: Found a function 'memcpy' with an unknown input type

Summary: Running the Similarity-Based Remaining Useful Life Estimation example causes an error
Description: After you manually download and unzip the data from the NASA PHM data repository, then run the first step of the example script, the Similarity-Based Remaining Useful Life Estimation example generates the following error message:  ?Error using helperLoadData (line 21)  The VariableNames property must contain one name for each variable in the table.
Workaround: Use the following steps to work around this error:Open the helperLoadData.m file:>> edit helperLoadData Comment Line 11, so that it looks like this:% t(:,end) = []; %Discard last column of loaded data as all are empty ''

Summary: Navigation broken from imported DOORS Next item to the original item in DOORS Next project
Description: An error may be displayed when Configuration Management is enabled for the DOORS Next project, and you perform the below actions in sequence: An imported IBM DOORS Next (also referred to as DNG) item is selected in the Simulink Requirements Editor. In the right pane, click Show in document  to navigate from the imported reference to the original item in IBM DOORS Next.  Depending on MATLAB session configuration, there may be an Unknown configuration  error on the MATLAB side, or the web browser may display a page that states: Unknown or deleted configuration .

Summary: Requirements imported directly from IBM DOORS Next have incomplete contents
Description: When you directly import requirements from IBM DOORS Next (also known as IBM DOORS Next Generation or DNG), the Description ?property for the? imported referenced requirements does not contain the full requirement text that is listed in IBM DOORS Next.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when deleting or modifying a file that is part of a class
Description: MATLAB can crash when using a class and you delete or modify a file that either contains the class definition or implements a method of the class.

Summary: Channelizer HDL Optimized block does not display the correct latency on the block
Description: The latency displayed on the Channelizer HDL Optimized block does not match the actual latency in simulation. The latency on the block is one cycle less than the actual value for all configurations except one case. The exception is, when the "Output vector size" parameter is set to "Same as number of frequency bands" and the input vector size is the same. In this case, the latency displayed on the block is 3 cycles less than the actual value.

Summary: nufftn can cause MATLAB??to crash when operating on a single sample point or query point
Description: Calling the nufftn  function can result in a crash when the inputs are:  A single sample point and multiple, non-uniform, non-gridded query points Multiple sample points and a single query point
Workaround: ?Several workarounds are possible depending on the inputs to the function.When there is a single query point f, a "dummy" second query point can be used to avoid the crash.? For example:x = randn(100);tx = 1:100;ty = 1:100;?[Tx,Ty] = ndgrid(tx,ty);f = [0 0];?y = nufftn(x, [Tx(:) Ty(:)], [f; f]);When there is a single sample point t, a "dummy" second sample point and input value can be used to avoid the crash.? For example:x = randn;?t = [0 0];fx = 1:100;fy = 1:100;[Fx,Fy] = ndgrid(fx,fy);y = nufftn([x; 0], [t; t], [Fx(:) Fy(:)]);In cases where the non-uniform points (whether sample points or query points) can be separated into grid vectors, the crash can be avoided by instead specifying the non-uniformly spaced grid through the grid vector syntax.? For example:% Replacing y = nufftn(x, [Tx(:) Ty(:)], f);y?= nufftn(x, {tx, ty}, f);or% Replacing y = nufftn(x, t, [Fx(:) Fy(:)]);y = nufftn(x, t, {fx, fy});?

Summary: Import from DOORS Next module or query does not work with port number 443
Description: If you click Import in the Simulink Requirements menu, and select "IBM DOORS Next Generation" for the document type, Simulink Requirements will prompt you for server login information, and will allow you to select a Project to import from, but the rest will not work.  For example, if you select the option to import a specified Module, Simulink Requirements will try to populate the Module selector combo-box with the list of available modules, but the list remains empty. This happens when DOORS Next server is configured to use the default HTTPS port number 443. The malfunction does not happen with any other port. For example, if server is configured to use IBM's default port number 9443, import works as expected.

Summary: MATLAB web browser might not work behind a proxy server
Description: The MATLAB web browser might fail to load external web pages when a proxy server is being used. For example, the following command might fail to load the mathworks.com page when a proxy server is in use. web('http://mathworks.com')

Summary: removedose?causes MATLAB to crash
Description: When you call?removedose ?on a dose object that is not on a model, MATLAB crashes. To check whether the dose you are trying to remove is on a model, use getdose .

Summary: Generating a coverage report in the Test Manager for subsystem harnesses at different levels in a library causes an assertion failure
Description: Generating a coverage report in the Test Manager for subsystem harnesses at different levels in a library causes an assertion failure in cvi.TopModelCov.moveHarnessTest: failed assertion in b:\matlab\?toolbox\?slcoverage\src\?slvnv\?utils\?object.?cpp at line 2472: id>0 && id<pool_capacity && object[id]!=NULL[??0] 0x00007ffe7a7a775f Q:\?matlab\?bin\?win64\?slvnv.dll+00423775 modelcov_root_relatives_and_consistency+00000239[??1] 0x00007ffe7a7a6ec1 Q:\?matlab\?bin\win64\?slvnv.?dll+00421569 modelcov_merge_data+00000385..?.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 7, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: polyspace-access command in Linux crashes or fails to upload or download results
Description: On Linux, if you use the?polyspace-access  command and do not have write access to the temporary folder used to zip and unzip files, one or more of the following issues might occur: Download commands fail with the message: Cannot open zip file . Upload commands fail with the message: No such file or directory .  If you do not have read access to this folder, the command might crash after completion. This issue typically happens if you do not have a temporary folder defined in your environment variables. The??polyspace-access ?command then uses a default temporary folder, /tmp/ps-center-results . You can confirm this situation by checking if the zip file path in the command output starts with /tmp/ps-center-results .
Workaround: To avoid the issue, use one of these workarounds:Set one of the environment variables?TMP,?TMPDIR,?TEMP, or?TEMPDIR, to a?temporary folder for which you have read and write access.Use the -tmp-dir option with polyspace-access to specify a temporary folder with read and write access.Enable read and write access on?/tmp/ps-center-results. Each time the folder is deleted, you might have to apply this workaround again.

Summary: Simulating a model with ToFile block inside a subsystem reference with parsim throws an error
Description: Running a model with ToFile  block inside a subsystem reference with parsim  throws an error. The ErrorMessage  property on the Simulink.SimulationOutput  object returned from parsim  shows the following error message: Subsystem reference block 'mf14WithSSRef/Subsystem Reference' has unapplied changes. ?

Summary: Simulation Data Inspector archive run limit does not limit the size of data logged in temp directory
Description: The Archive Size Limit ?setting in the Simulation Data Inspector and the?Simulink.sdi.setArchiveRunLimit ?function are intended to allow users to limit the size of the data logged to the temp directory by specifying the number of simulation runs for which to retain data. However, the archive run limit for the Simulation Data Inspector does not limit the size of the data logged to the temp directory.
Workaround: When you see the size of the logged data in the temp directory growing too large, call the Simulink.sdi.getArchiveRunLimit?function. This function returns the configured run limit and removes runs that are no longer shown in the Simulation Data Inspector archive. You can call this function after each simulation to prevent further growth of the data in the temp directory.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes for models with reference blocks on second model compilation
Description: Simulink crashes on second model compile when the below conditions are true: The top-model is referencing one or more model reference blocks. The sub-models are being closed using close_system  from any of the top model's callback.? The top-model is re-updated.  Crash snippet:-?? 0x000000013f367fc7 BlockDiagramLockManager::unlockLoadedModels() at BlockDiagramLockManager.cpp:42  0x000000013f36e4ee LibraryUpdateHelper::UpdateLibraryLinks() at LibraryUpdateHelper.cpp:500  0x000000013f36ed55 UpdateLibraryLinksAndEvalInit(SLRootBD*, SLCompBD*, bool) at LibraryUpdateHelper.cpp:0?  0x000000013e1179ab SLCompLibrariesMdlRefAndVariants(SLCompBD*) at SLCompLibrariesMdlRefAndVariants.cpp:519

Summary: Branched lines might be disconnected while automatically arranging system
Description: Automatically arranging a system block diagram might disconnect a line that is branched on one end and not connected on the other end.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening AUTOSAR model in which blocks are linked to a library
Description: MATLAB might crash when opening an AUTOSAR model if both of these conditions exist in the model: Simulink states, signals, or data stores are mapped to AUTOSAR variables. The mapped states, signals, or data stores are associated with blocks that are linked to a library.

Summary: Overwritten Embedded Coder Dictionary in Simulink data dictionary
Description: When you open the Embedded Coder app or the Embedded Coder Dictionary dialog for a model, if the model uses a configuration set that you store in a Simulink data dictionary, the Embedded Coder Dictionary in the Simulink data dictionary might be overwritten.

Summary: ASAP2 files describe lookup table blocks as VAL_BLK instead of STD_AXIS?when multiple lookup table blocks?share the same lookup table object or parameters
Description: When multiple lookup table blocks share the same lookup table object or parameter, the generated A2L file describes the blocks as?VAL_BLK  instead of STD_AXIS .

Summary: Unconnected Stateflow message input port without an internal receiving queue may crash MATLAB when you generate code
Description: MATLAB might crash when you generate code for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains a Stateflow chart with a message input For message input Use internal queue ?check box is not selected The message input port is unconnected

Summary: Crash or assertion when using certain mappings in atomic subcharts
Description: Generating code from an atomic subchart with certain mappings and nested function calls might result in an error message in the Diagnostic Viewer:? ?CGIR assertion 'In flags: This code should be unreachable!'  The same issue might cause MATLAB to crash. Both situations produce a stack similar to:? [ 0] 0x000000028ef9e52c bin\win64\stateflow.dll+00910636 mwboost::serialization::singleton_module::unlock+00050156   [ 1] 0x000000028ef9e0cb bin\win64\stateflow.dll+00909515 mwboost::serialization::singleton_module::unlock+00049035?  [ 2] 0x000000028f6c8b36 bin\win64\stateflow.dll+08424246 sl_hmi::InstrumentedSignals::getCount+00474662?
Workaround: Only use mappings of matching scope:Map subchart inputs to main chart inputs.Map subchart outputs to main chart outputs.Map subchart data store memory to main chart data store memory or local data.Map subchart parameters to main chart parameters

Summary: intlinprog returns an incorrect solution when the problem contains identical integer variables
Description: intlinprog  can return an infeasible solution, a suboptimal solution, or an incorrect objective function value under thefollowing circumstances. There is a set of indices of integervariables S, with S having at least two elements, satisfying all of the followingconditions. (For example, S = [1,3].) All elements of theobjective vector f(S) are equal. (For example, f(1) = f(3).)? All elements of theinequality matrix A(:,S) are equal also. (For example, A(1,1) = A(1,3) = A(2,1) = A(2,3) = ... for all rows of A.) All elements of theequality constraints Aeq(:,S) are equal also. (For example, Aeq(1,1) = Aeq(1,3) = Aeq(2,1) = Aeq(2,3) = ... for all rows of Aeq.) The Heuristics  optionis set to 'basic'  (default), 'intermediate' , or 'advanced' .  The? LPPreprocess  option is set to? 'basic'  (default).   The error is due to an inconsistency between LP preprocessing and the 1-opt heuristic. The 1-opt heuristic runs for? 'basic' ,?'intermediate' , or?'advanced' ?heuristics.
Workaround: There are two workarounds:Set the Heuristics option to 'rss', which has similar behavior to 'basic', but does not call 1-opt heuristic. options = optimoptions('intlinprog','Heuristics','rss');Set the LPPreprocess option to 'none'. options = optimoptions('intlinprog','LPPreprocess','none');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when adding an enum with an integer if the?CastingMode parameter is set to 'Standards'
Description: Incorrect code might be generated from MATLAB code in which an addition operation occurs for an enum and integer, one of them being a positive value and the other being a negative value, and if the CastingMode  parameter is set to 'Standards' .?  In the below example, an addition operation occurs for an enum and integer.? Sample MATLAB Code: ? classdef SampleEnumClass < uint16  ? ? enumeration  ? ? ? ? val(43165)  ? ? end  ? ? methods (Static)  ? ? ? ? function retVal = addClassNameToEnumNames  ? ? ? ? ? ? retVal = true;  ? ? ? ? end  ? ? end  end  function out1 = func_swa(in1)  % in1 - int32  % SampleEnumClass is an Enumeration whose superclass is uint16  ? ? out1 = in1 + (cast(SampleEnumClass.val, 'int32'));? %?SampleEnumClass.v5 and in1 are not both positive or negative values  end  In the generated C code, an 'unsigned int ' cast is incorrectly added, which gives unexpected results when value of? enum or integer is negative and the other is not:? Generated C code:?  int func_swa(int in1)  {  ? int out1;  ? if ((in1 < 0) && (SampleEnumClass_val < (unsigned int)(int)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? (MIN_int32_T - in1))) { // incorrect unsigned int cast around (MIN_int32_T - in1)  ? ? out1 = MIN_int32_T;  ? } else if ((in1 > 0) && (SampleEnumClass_val > (unsigned int)(int)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? (MAX_int32_T - in1))) { // incorrect unsigned int cast around (MAX_int32_T - in1)  ? ? out1 = MAX_int32_T;  ? } else {  ? ? out1 = (int)(in1 + (int)SampleEnumClass_val);  ? }  ? return out1;  }?

Summary: dlnetwork objects might have nonpositive TrainedVariance state values for batch normalization layers
Description: Converting small numbers to single precision can result in positive numbers being converted to nonpositive numbers with single precision. This can result in nonpositive values for batch normalization variance state when training or assembling deep learning networks. For non-gpuArray ?input, the?predict ?function for?dlnetwork ?objects throws an error when a batch normalization layer has a nonpositive value in the?TrainedVariance ?state parameter.?For example: net = resnet50;lgraph = layerGraph(net);lgraph = removeLayers(lgraph,'ClassificationLayer_fc1000');dlnet = dlnetwork(lgraph);trainedVariance = zeros([1 1 64]) + 1e-64;dlnet.State{2,3} = {single(trainedVariance)};dlX = dlarray(rand(224,224,3,'single'),'SSCB');dlYPred = predict(dlnet,dlX);Error using nnet.internal.cnn.dlnetwork/predict (line 258)Layer 'bn_conv1': Invalid input data. The value of 'Variance' is invalid. Expected input to be positive.
Workaround: For each batch normalization layer in the dlnetwork object, set the TrainedVariance state to eps('single') using the dlupdate function. For example:idx = dlnet.State.Parameter == "TrainedVariance";boundAwayFromZero = @(X) max(X, eps('single'));dlnet.State(idx,:) = dlupdate(boundAwayFromZero, dlnet.State(idx,:));

Summary: Misleading error when opening a project that uses a new format in older MATLAB releases
Description: Loading a project in releases prior to R2020b fails if all  of these conditions are true:The project is saved in R2020b The project uses the default project format  In such situations, MATLAB issues a misleading error:No project found in root folder.   The actual cause of the error is that MATLAB releases prior to R2020b do not recognize the new default project format.

Summary: VHDLLibraryName customization is ignored during HDL test bench generation
Description: During test bench generation, the parameter VHDLLibraryName  is ignored and the test bench continues to use library work  for the generated package file. Compiling this test bench can lead to compilation errors in the HDL Simulator.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Direct Lookup Table block might produceincorrect results in simulation and generated code when table is providedas block input with underspecified data type
Description: The Direct Lookup Table block might produceincorrect results in simulation and generated code when all the followingconditions are true: Make Table aninput  check box is  selected and the table is provided as a block input. The table provided as a block input has an underspecified data type (or is specified via Inherit via back propagation ). The output signal of the block has an underspecified data type.

Summary: Video Labeler app returns an error when you export data from a loaded custom source.
Description: The Video Labeler  app returns an error when you export labeled data from a custom data source.  The app returns the following error: Error using groundTruthDataSource/parseAndPopulateInputs (line 490)   Input folders or files contain non-standard file extensions.   Use FileExtensions Name-Value pair to include the non-standard file extensions.   Warning: Error occurred while evaluating listener callback.?

Summary: mlreportgen.utils.html2dom.prepHTMLFile incorrectly prepares HTML file with special characters
Description: If you use mlreportgen.utils.html2dom.prepHTMLFile ?with? an HTML file that contains special characters, such as? < ,?> ,? or?&,  and then pass the prepared HTML to? mlreportgen.dom.HTML ?or? mlreportgen.dom.HTMLFile , an error occurs with this message: >> s = mlreportgen.utils.html2dom.prepHTMLFile('example.html'); >> html = mlreportgen.dom.HTML(s); Error using mlreportgen.dom.HTML  HTML error: expected entity name for reference

Summary: Labeler custom reader returns an error while readingdata source.
Description: The custom reader used to read custom files and other datasources for labeler applications, returns an error related to incorrecttimestamps. The reader incorrectly sets the start time to zero producing an error similar to, or starting with:?  Error using groundTruthDataSource/ parseAndPopulateInputs

Summary: Unable to plot data from imported datasets with columns whose names only differ by case in the SimBiology Model Analyzer app
Description: When you?plot data from imported datasets with columns whose names only differ by case in the?SimBiology Model Analyzer ?app, the app?shows the following error message: "Plot Error: Unable to perform assignment because the left and right sides have a different number of elements."

Summary: Example model that implements six-step commutation to control BLDC generates unexpected spikes in motor phase currents
Description: The model?mcb_bldc_sixstep_f28379d.slx , mentioned in the example "Six-Step Commutation of BLDC Motor Using Sensor Feedback", generates? unexpected spikes in motor phase currents because of the current sampling that occurs at PWM transition.?

Summary: Empty file and segmentation fault message when generating RTF documents on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 or CentOS 7.8
Description: If you generate an RTF document on a Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7 or CentOS 7.8 platform, the size of the resulting RTF document is 0 bytes and a segmentation fault occurs.

Summary: Compilation error when simulating model containing certain Simscape blocks
Description: When the underlying equations?for a Simscape block use the prod  function applied to a single-element vector or matrix, simulating a model containing such block may generate a compilation error similar to the following: Error compiling Simscape network for model m1.  Caused by:  ? ? Passing empty expression container where non-empty container is expected.

Summary: Lookup Table blocks unable to perform numerical perturbation when using Cubic spline or Akima spline interpolation method
Description: 1-D, 2-D, and n-D Lookup Table blocks are unable to perform numerical perturbation when the Interpolation Method  parameter is set to Cubic spline  or Akima spline .

Summary: Compilation error occurs if For Each Subsystem is present within a graphical function of a resusable Stateflow chart
Description: Compilation error might occur for a model that meets the following conditions: Model contains a reusable Stateflow chart with graphical function. Graphical function contains For Each Subsystem.

Summary: Saving a symfun object and loading it in a different MATLAB version can result in the loaded object having class sym
Description: Saving a symfun  object in R2019b or earlier and loading the mat file in R2020a can result in the loaded object having class sym  instead of symfun . No warning is printed.
Workaround: Convert the loaded object into a symfun:load data.matf(x) = f;?orload data.matf = symfun(f,x);

Summary: An error can occur when Model Advisor generates a report in PDF format
Description: When using the Model Advisor to generate a report in PDF format, an error can occur with a message similar to the following: Error using mlreportgen. dom. HTMLHTML error: attribute name expected

Summary: Simulink Test Manager might crash when running tests that collect coverage
Description: If you add coverage after running multiple test cases, Simulink Test Manager might crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB functions that recursively call themselves might produce incorrect code
Description: C/C++ code generation from MATLAB might produce incorrect code if all  of these conditions are true: The source MATLAB code contains a function that (directly or indirectly) calls itself? The first call to the recursive function (or the function that starts the recursion) passes a constant input to that function During recursion, the subsequent calls to that function pass different values for that input parameter?  For example, define a MATLAB function simple2 ?that calls the recursive function? recursive_fcn ? with a constant input?5 : function out = simple2 %#codegenout = recursive_fcn(5);end The function recursive_fcn ? recursively calls itself with a different input value for each call:function out = recursive_fcn(n)if n > 0    m = n - 1;    out = 0;    out = recursive_fcn(m);    out = out + do_something(n);else    out = 0;endend function out = do_something(n)out = cumsum(1,n);end The generated C code contains an infinite recursion and does not terminate. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: signalDatastore errors out when reading files from a remote location.
Description: signalDatastore  is unable to read data from a remote location, such as cloud storage in Amazon S3? (Simple Storage Service), Microsoft? Azure? Storage Blob, or Hadoop? Distributed File System (HDFS?).

Summary: Simulink toolstrip appears blank when proxy information set in MATLAB preferences
Description: If you set proxy information in MATLAB preferences, the Simulink toolstrip might load blank.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, open Simulink from the MATLAB Command Window. Do not initialize Simulink by using MATLAB startup options, such as?matlab -r start_simulink.

Summary: Drive Cycle Source block produces an incompatible file error
Description: The Drive Cycle Source block produces an incompatible file error dialogue message when importing files with non-uniformly sampled time data.
Workaround: Click?OK?to accept the error. You will then see the correct error message regarding the issue with non-uniformly sampled data.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation might ignore AUTOSAR per-instance properties of mapped signal, state, or data store
Description: AUTOSAR code generation might ignore the per-instance properties of a mapped signal, state, or Data Store Memory block after the corresponding block is resolved to a library. For example, in the generated C code, the ShortName ?value specified for a signal mapped to AUTOSAR ArTypedPerInstanceMemory  might be absent.
Workaround: After a block corresponding to a mapped signal, state, or data store resolves to a library, and before generating code, re-specify the per-instance properties in the AUTOSAR mapping.?Repeat each time the model is re-opened.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Check trigger signal names?flags Simulink functions nested in Stateflow charts
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check trigger signal names ?(Check ID:?mathworks.jmaab.jc_0281 ) incorrectly flags the Simulink functions nested in the Stateflow charts.

Summary: Running MATLAB on Windows platforms using matlab -batch might?sporadically produce a warning message
Description: On Windows platforms, when you start MATLAB R2020b noninteractively?to run scripts, functions, or statements (using the?matlab ?command with the?-batch ?option), this warning message might appear in the command prompt or the console output log: IMAGE_REL_AMD64_ADDR32NB relocation requires anordered section layout.  The message appears sporadically and originates from a third-party library that is used by the MATLAB execution engine. You can ignore the warning because it does not affect the execution of your code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor run might run incorrectly when filter input setting used for Simulink to physical signal converter blocks
Description: If the Simscape model contains Simulink-PS Converter blocks, in the Block Parameters dialog box for the blocks, on the Input Handling  tab, the Filtering and derivatives ?parameter must be set to Zero derivatives (piecewise constant)  or Provide Signals  and the Provided signals  parameter must be set to Input only. Using a different setting can cause the converter blocks in the HDL implementation model generated by running the Simscape to HDL Workflow Advisor to not preserve the filtering behavior. This can result in a numeric mismatch between the Simscape model and the implementation model. In R2018b and R2019a these filtering options are not caught by the Simscape to HDL Workflow advisor and the generated model does not have the filtering behavior that is set on the Simulink-PS Converter. In? R2019b_update6, the error in the Simscape to HDL advisor will be: Unrecognized property 'SPSFilterBlocks' for class  ????????????'ssccodegenworkflow. SwitchedLinearWorkflow  In R2020a_update4 onward the error will read:  Only the ''Provide signals'' and ''Input only'' settings on S-SP conveters are supported with the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor.
Workaround: Add a Discrete Filter block before the Simulink-PS Converter block to capture the filter behavior and change the Block Parameters of the Simulink-PS Converter block to use Provide Signals and Input only.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Multiple CAN FD  channel connections cannot run on the same MathWorks virtual channel
Description: You cannot simultaneously run more than one CAN FD channel connected to the same MathWorks virtual device channel.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Bus signal connecting Unit delay block to MATLAB function block may generate wrong code
Description: Incorrect PLC Code might be generated if all of the following conditions are true in the Simulink subsystem for which code is being generated:A signal connects a Unit-delay block to a MATLAB function block The 'auto ' option is set in Block Parameters (Subsystem)  -> Code Generation  pane.
Workaround: Change the Function packaging option to 'Reusable function' instead of 'Auto' in Block Parameters (Subsystem) -> Code Generation pane of the MATLAB function block in question.

Summary: MATLAB Production Server Compiler App missing icon resulting in blank image
Description: On Linux, the image icon of the MATLAB Production Server Compiler application is missing in the app gallery. This is solely a graphical issue that does not affect the functionality of the MATLAB Production Server Compiler.?

Summary: When using Signal Editor interface,?duplicating or using copy and paste on signal plotted inside dataset might cause non-stop spinner
Description: When?using the Signal Editor interface, a non-stop spinner might appear when the Signal Editor interface contains a signal plotted inside a dataset/scenario. If you then duplicate or copy and past the signal, a persistent non-stop spinner might appear.?

Summary: Using the?Classes option of classificationLayer results incorrectly normalized loss for networks with sequence or 3-D image input.
Description: When using the trainNetwork  function to train a classification networks with a?sequenceInputLayer ?or an?image3dInputLayer ?object, using the 'Classes'  option of classificationLayer  results incorrectly normalized loss and can prevent the network from converging.
Workaround: There are two ways to avoid the issue:If the training targets for trainNetwork are a categorical vector or cell array with the correct categories, then do not use the Classes option of classificationLayer. In this case, the trainNetwork function automatically determines the correct categories from the training targets.If the training targets for trainNetwork are a categorical vector or cell array with incorrect categories, then rename the categories of the training targets using the the renamecats function before calling the trainNetwork function and do not use the Classes option of classificationLayer

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Complex to Magnitude-Angle block returns incorrect value at angle output port when input is NaN
Description: The Complex to Magnitude-Angle block returns incorrect results from the simulation and generated code at the angle output port when all the following conditions are true: The parameter Output  is set to either Angle   or Magnitude and Angle . The input is a real signal with a value NaN .  The result from the simulation and the generated code is pi , but should be NaN .

Summary: Generating tests based on existing coverage data may fail to complete normally for models with Logical Operator blocks having unsatisfiable objectives
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may fail to complete normally when all of the following conditions are met: 1) Test Generation performed based on existing coverage data. 2) At least one logical operator block has all of the following properties:?? a) At least one objective covered in the existing coverage data?? b) At least one Unsatisfiable objective?? c) No vector inputs

Summary: tabularTextDatastore errors when it encounters prefixed hex and binary literals
Description: An error is encountered when constructing a tabularTextDatastore for data which has variables that are prefixed hex literals such as 0xabc943 or prefixed binary literals such as 0b10101111. For example:  ?>>?ttds = tabularTextDatastore(filename);  ?Error using datastore (line 175)  Output argument "formatString" (and maybe others) not assigned during call to  "matlab.io.internal.text.determineFormatString>convertDatatypeToFormatString".?
Workaround: Supply the appropriate hex / binary textscan format specifier via the 'TextscanFormats' name-value pair of TabularTextDatastore. For example, if the third variable has prefixed hex values such as?0xabc943?and the fifth variable has prefixed binary values such as?0b10101111 use:?>> ttds = tabularTextDatastore(filename,'TextscanFormats',{'%q','%f','%x','%f','%b'});?

Summary: Code generation assertion for AUTOSAR model with corrupt signal, state, or data store mapping
Description: AUTOSAR code generation might fail with an assertion if the model has a corrupt signal, state, or data store mapping. While this corruption can happen for multiple reasons, it usually happens when an AUTOSAR model contains a Stateflow chart with atomic subcharts. The build displays an error message containing this text: In getIndividualDSMGroupPropertyFromMapping: Assertion failed: aBlk != nullptr

Summary: Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valve might incorrectly calculate the Pressure Compensator valve and Variable Orifice areas
Description: The Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valve incorrectly calculates the Variable Orifice opening fornonzero Initial opening  values and might use an incorrect value for the Pressure Compensator maximum passage area.
Workaround: If the block has a nonzero value for Initial Opening, you can remedy this with the following steps:Set the?InitialOpening?parameter to 0 m.Add a PS Add block and connect its output port to the Pressure-Compensated Flow Control Valveblock port?C.?Connect a PS Constant block to one of the PS Add block input ports. Set the Constant parameter equal to the desired?Initial Opening?value.?Connect the control signal originally connected to port?C?to the other PS Add block input port.Additionally, if the Valve parameterization setting is?By area vs. opening table, set theOrifice maximum area?equal to the lastelement of Tabulated orifice area. Whilethe?Orifice maximum area?is not used in the?By area vs. opening table setting, the parameter is used to set the maximum passage area of the Pressure Compensator valve.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect output from IC block when using Parameter Writer?block?to set IC block's initial value parameter
Description: When an IC block is placed inside a Model block, and the IC block's initial value  parameter uses an instance parameter of the Model block, Simulink improperly allows this instance parameter to be set by a Parameter Writer block outside the Model block.? In this situation, the IC block's output might be incorrect, causing incorrect results in simulation and generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Discrepancy between results for normal vs. SIL/PIL simulation for export-function Model block with Initialize port
Description: Software-in-the-loop (SIL) or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation results differ from normal simulation results for a model when all of the following conditions are met:  The top model or test harness contains a Model block with?Show model initialize port ?selected.  In the same Model block, Simulation mode  is set as Software-in-the-loop (SIL)  or Processor-in-the-loop (PIL).  In the referenced model, the configuration parameter?Code Generation ?>  Optimization?> ?Remove internal data zero initialization ?is selected. The initial value for any internal data in the referenced model is zero.  The difference occurs when the Initialize port is called a second time during simulation. The normal mode simulation reinitializes the internal data of the referenced model to zero, while the SIL/PIL simulation does not.

Summary: Example model mcb_pmsm_foc_sim.slx depends on Fixed-Point Designer product license for single datatype
Description: The example model, mcb_pmsm_foc_sim.slx (which is explained in the Code Verification and Profiling Using Processor-In-the-Loop Testing example), errors when datatype is changed to single if Fixed-Point Designer product is not present.

Summary: Some stereotypes and stereotype properties are missing from the dialog for creating a view
Description: There are stereotypes and stereotype properties missing in the filter builder section of the create view dialog. This can occur when profiles are linked to referenced models and not to the model that the view is being created for.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink.CoderInfo?object that has Identifier property set causes missing initial values in generated PLC code
Description: Simulink.CoderInfo ?object has an Identifier  property that can be used to change the name of the generated variable. This can result in the variable tr  having?a different name than that of the original object.  ? For example,  >> K1 = Simulink.Parameter?  >> K1.CoderInfo.StorageClass = 'ExportedGlobal'  >> K1.CoderInfo.Identifier = 'gainTune'  The generated code in the above case contains a variable named gainTune  instead of K1 . The initial value for this variable might be missing in the generated PLC code.?

Summary: Profile does not have the correct name in System Composer after the profile file is renamed in the file system
Description: If a profile file is renamed through the MATLAB File Browser or in the OS native file system, then when the profile is loaded in System Composer, the name of the profile is incorrect and shows the previous name.?

Summary: dlgradient can return incorrect results for models using the crosschannelnorm function with even channel window size and the execution occurs?on a GPU
Description: There is a bug in the NVIDIA library function cudnnLRNCrossChannelBackward  used by the crosschannelnorm  function to compute the cross-channel normalization backward function on a GPU.Because of this,  dlgradient can return incorrect results for models using the crosschannelnorm function with even channel window size     and  the execution occurs?on a GPU.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, gather data from the GPU before calling the crosschannelnorm function.For example, replace instances of the code dlY = crosschannelnorm(dlX,windowSize);with?dlX = gather(dlX);?dlY = crosschannelnorm(dlX,windowSize);?After the operation, do not cast the data to the GPU as this can cause the issue to persist.

Summary: "Unrecognized function getTypeFromPackageInfoUrl" error displays in the Command Window on startup.
Description: When MATLAB starts up, if add-ons are installed that have updates available, the error "Unrecognized function getTypeFromPackageInfoUrl" displays in the Command Window. The error also displays when logging into MATLAB Online. After MATLAB starts up, all add-ons updates fail to install.
Workaround: No workaround available.

Summary: Using VideoReader to read WebM files crashes MATLAB
Description: On Windows platforms, reading a WebM video file using VideoReader  crashes MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Reciprocal block produces wrong answer for zero-valued fixed-point inputs
Description: When the following conditions are true, the simulation behavior and generated code behavior for the HDL Reciprocal block do not match: The input value is 0 . The input data type is signed fixed-point with negative fraction length. Number of iterations ?is set to 1 .   The generated code produces the expected result while the simulation does not.?

Summary: Spectrogram plot in radarWaveformAnalyzer app reverted to 3D from 2D
Description: The Spectrogram plot in radarWaveformAnalyzer app is shown as a 3D plot instead of a 2D plot

Summary: MATLAB might crash when deleting Stateflow events, data, or messages
Description: MATLAB might crash due to an interaction between the Symbols window and deleting objects in the Stateflow chart.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model with a MATLAB Function block with a nontunable parameter bus
Description: The generated C/C++ code might be incorrect for a model that meets these conditions:  The model contains a MATLAB Function block. The MATLAB Function block has a nontunable parameter of bus type that is a bus array. Each element of the bus array contains an array of numeric values. Some of the nested arrays of numeric values contain only real values while others contain values with a non-zero imaginary part.  For a model that meets the above conditions, the generated code might contain incorrect numeric values for the nontunable parameter. Specifically, the imaginary part of some parameter values (that is, those that should be equal to 0) are set to wrong values. This issue manifests itself as a subtle change in the generated code from run to run. One way to detect the presence of this issue is to re-generate C/C++ from a model two times in a row, on different computers or after rebooting the computer. If the model is affected by this issue, you will likely see different numeric values in the generated code in those two builds.?

Summary: Diagnostic Service Component internal error during simulation of AUTOSAR Basic Software service calls
Description: Simulating Diagnostic Event Manager (Dem) caller blocks that invoke AUTOSAR services implemented in a Diagnostic Service Component block can generate an error that references Diagnostic Service Component block internals.? For example: A definition for the function 'Dem_GetIUMPRDenCondition' called by Function Caller block'TestHarness/Diagnostic Service Component/RTE Service Connector/Port_GetIUMPRDenCondition/Dem_GetIUMPRDenCondition(caller)',could not be found.

Summary: HDL code generation error when Generated Model check box is disabled and optimizations such as Hierarchical Distributed Pipelining is enabled
Description: HDL code generation for a Simulink model results in an error when the following conditions are met: An HDL optimization such as Hierarchical Distributed Pipelining  and Generate optimization report  settings are enabled The Generated Model  check box is disabled.  ?In this case, the error occurs when the optimization is successful on the model.
Workaround: When using HDL optimizations on the model, enable model generation by selecting the?Generated Model?check box, and then generate code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Distributed Pipelining adds invalid registers when a model contains non-constant source blocks
Description: When applying Distributed Pipelining to a design with non-constant source blocks, such as a counter, invalid registers can be moved to the output of the block. The invalid registers break functional equivalence to the original model.  Using optimizations like Sharing or Streaming in conjunction with Clock Rate Pipelining will also introduce counters in the design which could be susceptible to this issue.  Checking simulation of the Validation Model can expose whether or not a design experiences this bug.

Summary: readtable errors if the SpreadsheetImportOptions property VariableNamesRange is specified as a positive scalar integer
Description: readtable ?errors if the?SpreadsheetImportOptions ?property?VariableNamesRange ?is specified as a positive scalar integer, which is a valid VariableNamesRange  value. >> opts = spreadsheetImportOptions("VariableNamesRange", 1);?>> readtable('patients.xls', opts)Error using matlab.io.ImportOptions/readtable (line 499)Comparison between double and string is not supported.
Workaround: To prevent readtable from throwing an error, specify the VariableNamesRange?property as a character vector that represents the starting cell for the data.>> opts = spreadsheetImportOptions("VariableNamesRange", "A1");?>> readtable('patients.xls', opts);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Prelookup block might output wrong results in normal mode simulation with in-range index linear search method and mixed double, single, and built-in integer types
Description: The Prelookup block might output wrong results in normal mode simulation when these conditions are all true: Index search method ?is set Linear search .  The input value to the Prelookup?block is within the range of the specified breakpoints. The input, breakpoint, and fraction have double or single precision data type. The index has a built-in integer type.?  Code generation and simulation targets like accelerator and rapid accelerator modes output correct results.?

Summary: Invalid data detected at the host when using an FTDI JTAG connection for the AXI Master or FPGA Data Capture features
Description: When using a micro-USB cable for an FTDI JTAG connection with a development board, data transfers are corrupted for capture depths larger than 4096 samples and data is mismatched for memory transfers larger than 16MB. This issue impacts the usage of FTDI JTAG connections with supported and custom boards.  The following are the list of impacted supported boards: MiniZed Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC ZCU104 Evaluation Kit Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC ZCU106 Evaluation Kit Zynq UltraScale+ RFSoC ZCU111 Evaluation Kit  Data transfers for smaller capture depths and smaller memory may succeed. However, the exact reason for corruption is unknown and the issue is sporadic.
Workaround: Use a non-FTDI based connection between the host machine and the board. The following boards are tested to work with the Digilent JTAG-HS3 programming cable:Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC ZCU104 Evaluation KitZynq UltraScale+ MPSoC ZCU106 Evaluation KitZynq UltraScale+ RFSoC ZCU111 Evaluation Kit

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using wordcloud in live script or function
Description: If you call wordcloud ?in an.mlx  file and then run the file, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: Access violation during calls to get_param caused by unavailable sl_proxy services
Description: sl_proxy  microservices are used to support get_param  and?set_param  calls in Simulink. If the sl_proxy ?microservice is not installed, loaded or initialized properly, get_param/set_param  calls cause access violations.

Summary: MATLAB might crash while opening/closing model in Simulink Onramp
Description: MATLAB might crash while opening/closing model in Simulink Onramp. Sample of the stack trace is as below:? [0] 0x0000000056ade134 MW_QT::QObject::isWindowType+00000004 at matlab\bin\win64\Qt5CoreMW.dll+2351412 (no debugging symbols found)  [1] 0x0000000056303878 MW_QT::QApplication::notify+00000136 at matlab\bin\win64\Qt5WidgetsMW.dll+79992 (no debugging symbols found)  [2] 0x00007ffb639e786e GuiApplication::notify at b:\matlab\src\glee\gl_qtgui\guiapplication.cpp:2064 (in matlab\bin\win64\glee.dll)  [3] 0x0000000056a4c668 MW_QT::QCoreApplication::notifyInternal+00000120 at matlab\bin\win64\Qt5CoreMW.dll+1754728 (no debugging symbols found)  [4] 0x0000000056307670 MW_QT::QApplicationPrivate::sendMouseEvent+00000896?matlab\bin\win64\Qt5WidgetsMW.dll+95856

Summary: Performance degradation when simulating nesting export-function models with run time check
Description: When simulating a multitasking model referencing nested export-function models that have multiple exported functions invoked in different sample times, there is a performance degradation when model parameter Enable strict scheduling checks for referenced models  is selected.
Workaround: Go to Model Settings (Configuration Parameters) > Model Referencing and uncheck Enable strict scheduling checks for referenced models.

Summary: MATLAB stalls during test execution
Description: During test execution using the Test Manager, MATLAB might stall.
Workaround: Force MATLAB to close and restart it. Then, open the Test Manager and rerun the test.?

Summary: Sharing report is incorrect when clock-rate pipelining is enabled on blocks inside a feedback loop with specified oversampling factor
Description: The Streaming and Sharing report might show that sharing failed even though it is actually successful when the following conditions are met: ?Resource sharing is applied on blocks that are inside a feedback loop in the Simulink model ?In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Clock-rate pipelining  and Generate optimization report  check boxes are selected, and?a ?value greater than 1 ?is specified for the Oversampling factor.   ?In this case, the report shows the incorrect status of the sharing optimization.?

Summary: The input function might block callback processing in compiled applications and libraries on Windows
Description: Compiled libraries and applications that call input  might not process graphics or timer callbacks until user input is received. This issue is encountered only  on Windows.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code from a Simulink model with a Function Caller block in an inactive variant
Description: Generating code from a Simulink model causes MATLAB to crash when all the following conditions are true. The Function Caller block is in an inactive variant. The Function Caller block invokes a Simulink Function block that is defined in a referenced model. The Function visibility ?parameter of the Simulink Function block is set to scoped , and the C/C++ Function Interface of the block is configured.?

Summary: Error occurs during Simulink Design Verifier test case generation when used from the Test Manager if you have multiple subsystem harnesses for different subsystems
Description: If the Test Manager has two test cases:  Test Case 1  (which is a testcase for the subsystem harness for Model/?  Subsystem1)  Test Case 2  (which is a testcase for the subsystem harness for Model/?  Subsystem2).  An error occurs when you:?  Run?Test Case 1 , go to Results and Artifacts ,?   select the result obtained by this simulation, and run Add tests for missing coverage ?  . Run?Test Case 2 , go to Results and Artifacts ,?   select the result obtained by this simulation, and run Add tests for missing coverage ?  .  Simulink  throws an " input coverage data is not consistent " error.
Workaround: Close and reopen the model between test cases.

Summary: MATLAB crashes after deleting a dose from a SimBiology model
Description: SimBiology dose objects maintain references to targeted species as well as parameters in parameterized dose properties. Those references become invalid if you delete the dose object using the delete  function, or if you move a target species from one compartment to another in the SimBiology app. MATLAB crashes if you: Rename a species or a parameter referenced by the deleted dose object. Save a project containing invalid references in the SimBiology app.
Workaround: In the MATLAB command lineUse the method removedose?to safely remove doses from the model. For more information on how to use this method, type:help SimBiology.Model.removedose The dose object returned by removedose can be deleted with the delete function.For example, to delete a dose object?dObj with the name 'doseName' from a SimBiology model?mObj, enter:delete(removedose(mObj,'doseName'));? % instead of: delete(getdose(mObj,'doseName'));?ordelete(removedose(mObj,dObj));? % instead of: delete(dObj);??In the SimBiology appClear the TargetName property of the dose object before deleting a dose, or before moving a species that is targeted by a dose from one compartment to another.To clear the TargetName?property, right-click the dose and select Properties. Delete the TargetName property in the Block Property Editor.

Summary: Code spacing is larger than 1 LSB for unimpaired Binary Weighted DAC
Description: The output of a unipolar DAC should range over [0,2^N-1]  LSB.The output of a bipolar DAC? should range over [-2^(N-1),2^(N-1)-1]  LSB. In both cases, 1 LSB = Vref/2^N .  But the Binary Weighted DAC ranges over [0,2^N]  LSB for a unipolar DAC and [-2^(N-1),2^(N-1)]  LSB for a bipolar DAC. However, the DAC does not have the extra code such ranges imply. This results in a code spacing slightly larger than 1  LSB for unimpaired converters.

Summary: MATLAB might crash with errors when running GPU codegen?
Description: MATLAB might crash while generating GPU code with the stack trace containing the following errors:?  Assertion at region/PstImpl.cpp line 775: In computeEnclosingRegions: Pushing null enclosing Region Stack Trace (captured): ... Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: movmax and movmin?can return partially incorrect results for some inputs
Description: The movmax  and movmin  functions can return partially incorrect results when both of these conditions are present: No sample points are supplied, or the supplied sample points are uniformly spaced The input is longer than twice the window length  In cases where incorrect output is generated, if the total window size is k , then the incorrect values only occur in the final (k+2)  elements of the output.
Workaround: A workaround for this issue is to provide non-uniformly spaced sample points that do not change the output.For example, consider a call to movmax with a window size of [kb kf], where?kb?and?kf?are integers:y = movmax(x,[kb kf]);You can replace this call to movmax?with the commands:n = length(x);y = movmax(x,[kb kf],'SamplePoints',[1.1 2:n]);The modified call to movmax adds non-uniform sample points that do not change the output. Any window that should include the sample point 1 will also include the sample point 1.1, while any window that should not include the sample point will similarly not include 1.1.

Summary: MATLAB crash during code generation if?VariantConditionMismatch?parameter is set to warning or error
Description: Model with variant block and if VariantConditionMismatch  parameter is set to warning or error then it may crash during code generation when all the below conditions are met:   Model block with one output block  Reference block connected to bus creator block?    Snippet from the stack trace:??
