For example, the following code with two EdgeCount  measurement channels, returns data from only only the higher: s = daq.createSession("ni");ch0 = addAnalogInputChannel(s, "Dev1", "ai0", "Voltage");ch2 = addCounterInputChannel(s, "Dev1", "ctr2", "EdgeCount");ch3 = addCounterInputChannel(s, "Dev1", "ctr3", "EdgeCount");inputSingleScan(s)ans =   -0.1495         0  125.0000 In this case, the EdgeCount  value for the channel ctr2  returns a false value of 0 .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unexpected result or error when using a tabulated data parameterization of the Pressure Relief Valve
Description: In the Pressure Relief Valve block, when the Opening area parameterization  is set to Tabulated data - Area vs. pressure , and the pressure drop between port A  and port B  exceeds the last element of the?Pressure differential vector , one of the following may occur: The orifice area may incorrectly change to an unexpected value. The block may erroneously generate the following message: "Nonlinear solver: failed to converge, ?residual norm too large."
Workaround: To avoid these errors, either:Replace the Pressure Relief Valve with a Pressure Compensator block.Set the?Opening area parameterization?to?Tabulated data - Area vs. pressure.Connect the pressure sensor port X to port B. Connect the sensor port Y to port A.Reverse the order of the elements in the?Opening area vector. For example, if the existing vector is [0.25, 0.8, 1.3], adjust it to [1.3, 0.8, 0.25]. This can be achieved by setting it to?fliplr([0.25, 0.8, 1.3]).Reverse?the order of the elements in the Pressure differential vector and flip the element signs. For example, if the existing vector is [-10, 5, 13], adjust it to [-13, -5, 10]. This can be achieved by setting it to?-fliplr([-10, 5, 13]).All other parameters and port connections should match the original Pressure Relief Valve block settings.?In the Pressure Relief Valve block, use a linear parameterization by setting the?Opening area parameterization?to Linear area-pressure relationship.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: Signal Editor prematurely indicates that a save operation is complete
Description: When the Signal Editor is saving data, the indicator that the save is occurring does not appear.? You might notice a delay when saving large data files.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for deep learning networks using TensorRT might produce incorrect answer if the design file calls?predict and activations for?the same network
Description: Code generated for deep learning networks using TensorRT library might produce an incorrect answer if all of the following conditions are met: Design file contains calls to?predict  and activations? for the same network. The size of the input passed to?predict  and activations  is the same .   ?An example design file is as follows:? function [out1, out2] = predict_activations(in)  %#codegen  persistent mynet;  if isempty(mynet)?  ?mynet = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('network.mat');  end  out1 = predict(mynet, in);  out2 = activations(mynet, in, 'layername');  end?
Workaround: Replace the call to the?predict?function with the?activations function by specifying the output layer name of the network.For example, if the name of the output layer is 'classification_output', then the example design file can be rewritten as:?function [out1, out2] = predict_activations(in)%#codegenpersistent mynet;if isempty(mynet)??mynet = coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('network.mat');end% Replace 'predict(mynet,in)' with an activation call?out1 = activations(mynet, in, 'classification_output');out2 = activations(mynet, in, 'layername');end?

Summary: Simscape model compilation fails when using legacy random number generator
Description: When you are using a legacy random number generator (for which RandStream.getGlobalStream.Type  returns 'legacy' ), running or compiling a Simscape model results in the following error:   Conversion to double from cell is not possible.
Workaround: Upgrade your code to use a nonlegacy random number generator, as described in https://www.mathworks.com/help/matlab/math/updating-your-random-number-generator-syntax.html, then recompile the Simscape model.

Summary: HDL code generation error when sending an array of bus signal into both an Assignment block and a For Each Subsystem block
Description: If an array of bus signal drives both a For Each Subsystem and an Assignment block, HDL code generation error can occur: Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/invokeBackEnd  Assertion failed:  base_core/portmgr.cpp:340:Error: Invalid port index 6 requested when 5 is the max port index for comp <Component  CtxName="arrayOfBusForEach" RefNum="c16" UserName="Subsystem" RTTIClass ="N3pir15NetworkInstCompE" Network="n0"/>?
Workaround: Wrapping the Assignment block in a new, non-flattened Subsystem can work around this issue.

Summary: Setting gain on graphicEQ object after closing parameterTuner returns an error
Description: The following steps return an error:? Create a graphicEQ  object: h = graphicEQ ; Open parameterTuner  on the object:? parameterTuner(h)  Close the parameterTuner  UI. Set graphicEQ  gain property programmatically: h.Gains(1) = 1 ;  The error message looks similar to:     Invalid or deleted object.    Error in graphicEQ/createParameterTunerWidgets (line 763)                        f.Position(3) = 80 * 11;    Error in graphicEQ/updatePlot (line 717)                        createParameterTunerWidgets(obj);    Error in graphicEQ/set.pGains (line 361)                updatePlot(obj);    Error in graphicEQ/set.Gains (line 353)                obj.pGains = gainVec;
Workaround: To avoid this issue, set graphicEQ gains either programmatically or with the parameterTuner user interface. Avoid using both with the same object.To continue working after encountering the issue, reopen the parameterTuner UI by calling parameterTuner on your graphicEQ object and keep it open. While the parameterTuner UI is open, you can set gain values programmatically or using the UI.

Summary: Model Advisor check Check usage of Merge block flags Initialize Function block
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check usage of Merge block ?(ID: mathworks.jmaab.jc_0659  and mathworks.hism.hisl_0015 ) wrongly flags the usage of Initialize Function blocks with Merge blocks.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Index outputs of union, setdiff, and setxor might refer to the last instances of repeated elements instead of the first instances
Description: The generated code for setxor  and union  might return the indices corresponding to the last occurrence of repeated elements in its second and third outputs rather than the first occurrences. For example, with?[y,ia,ib] = x_union([2,2],1) ,  we should have?ia = 1 , but code generation returns?ia = 2 .?  Similarly, the generated code for setdiff ?might return the last occurrence of repeated elements in its second output rather than the first occurrence. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when System Composer functions have invalid or missing stereotypes as input
Description: When creating a System Composer architecture model, if the following functions are called with an invalid or missing stereotype, it can cause the model to get corrupted and might lead to a MATLAB crash: addComponent  addPort  connect   When the corrupted model is saved or loaded and edited, the Simulink Diagnostic Viewer might display an error that indicates the System Composer model is inconsistent, and then result in a MATLAB crash.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Streaming and sharing of resources that are clock-rate pipelined can cause HDL mismatch
Description: If streaming and sharing are both requested when Clock Rate Pipelining  is enabled, the generated HDL code might have a numeric mismatch when compared to the original model. The mismatch occurs because the scheduling logic inside the clock-rate pipelined region does not correctly handle resources participating in multiple optimizations that require scheduling such as streaming and sharing. This can happen if streaming is requested on a subsystem which contains another subsystem requesting sharing.
Workaround: Remove the sharing request from the lower subsystem, or avoid using Clock Rate Pipelining when requesting the streaming and sharing optimizations on the same resources.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Simulink crashes while saving a model containing a Subsystem Reference block
Description: Simulink may crash while saving, for a model containing a Subsystem Reference  block with the following stack:[ 0]slPrmValue::isStringPrmValue at b:\ matlab\ src\ simulinkblock\ blk_obj\ slprmvalue. cpp:47?[ 1]SLRootBlock::getGrParamUValue at b:\ matlab\ src\ simulinkblock\ blk_core\ slrootblock. cpp:982?[ 2]slsr::util::referenceModelParamValueIsEmptyOrDefault at b:\ matlab\ src\ sl_lang_blocks\ subsystem_reference\ subsystemreferencehelper. cpp:55 [ 3]slsr::util::getSRComponentBD at b:\ matlab\ src\ sl_lang_blocks\ subsystem_reference\ subsystemreferencehelper. cpp:372[ 4]Simulink::CornerLineDrawer::paint at b:\ matlab\ toolbox\ simulink\ src\ sl_editor\ glyph\ slsubsystemreferencenodedetails. cpp:60?

Summary: S-Function Builder may fail to build when specifying non-ASCII include paths
Description: S-Function Builder wrote C and C++ wrapper files in UTF-8 encoding instead of the Simulink system encoding. When using non-ASCII characters in the source file, compilation might fail with an error similar to: test_wrapper.cC:\temp\test_wrapper.c: warning C4819: The file contains a character that cannot be represented in the current code page (932). Save the file in Unicode format to prevent data lossC:\temp\test_wrapper.c(17): fatal error C1083: Cannot open include file: '.\ĄĆĄąĄČ\testheader.h': No such file or directory

Summary: Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block only loads scenes in the Unreal Engine??project?/Game/Maps folder
Description: In the Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block, if you set Scene source  to Unreal Executable ,? the block only loads scenes that are in the?Unreal Engine??project?/Game/Maps ?folder. If you set Scene  to a scene path that is not in /Game/Maps , the block does not load the scene.

Summary: Error in Code view after running SIL/PIL simulation
Description: The Code view displays the error message "index exceed number of array elements" after you generate the code for a model and run a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation with the verification configuration parameter Measure task execution time ?selected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for multiple persistent variable initializations across multiple if(isempty(p)) regions might be incorrect
Description: Suppose that your MATLAB code contains multiple instances of this code pattern: persistent p;?  x = <constant_value>; if(isempty(p))? ? ?p = ... x ...end?  If all of the following conditions are true, the code generator might produce incorrect code for your MATLAB code: Two instances of this code pattern (that declare and initialize two different persistent variables) use the same variable x .  The values that? x ?is assigned to are constant values  The assignment of the constant values to x happens outside the? if(isempty(p)) ?regions.  The value of  x ?changes between the? if(isempty(p)) ?regions.   Taking these conditions into account, the MATLAB code pattern that might produce incorrect code is: persistent p1, p2;x?= <constant_value>;??...?if(isempty(p))? p1 = ... x ...end ?  x = <different_constant_value>;?...if(isempty(p))??p2 = ... x ...end  This bug might not affect all situations that use this code pattern. One situation in which the bug might occur is when x is assigned to a structure constant.? Here is an example MATLAB function that causes this bug to occur in the generated code. Although this example uses coder.ceval, this bug might also occur in code that does not use coder.ceval. function out = foo()  persistent p;persistent p2;  X = coder.const(struct('prop', 1));if(~coder.target('MATLAB'))?? ? coder.ceval('externalFcn', X);end  if(isempty(p))?? ? p = X;end  X = coder.const(struct('prop', 2));if(~coder.target('MATLAB'))?? ? coder.ceval('externalFcn', X);end?  if(isempty(p2))? ???p2 = X;end  out = p.prop + p2.prop;end   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: Directly assign the persistent variables to the constant values inside the if(isempty(p)) region. That is, change the code pattern in the previous section to:persistent p1, p2;...? if(isempty(p))  ? ?p1 = ... <constant_value> ...end...? if(isempty(p))  ? ?p2 = ... <different_constant_value>...end

Summary: readtable might misclassify char data as metadata
Description: In text files where the datatype of all columns is char , the presence of a numeric data entry causes detectImportOptions  to classify all previous rows as metadata. As a result, calling readtable  on the text file with default import options skips the initial rows of data. For example:     >> students = ["Sally"; "Brian"; "Andrew"; "Ashley"; "Norman"; "Ellie"; "Linda"];    >> grades = ["A-"; "B"; "91"; "C+"; "87"; "A+"; "B-"];    >> gradesTable = table(students, grades);    >> writetable(gradesTable, "studentGrades.csv");     >> readtable("studentGrades.csv")    ans =      5ˇÁ2 table         students     grades        __________    ______        {'Andrew'}    {'91'}        {'Ashley'}    {'C+'}        {'Norman'}    {'87'}        {'Ellie' }    {'A+'}        {'Linda' }    {'B-'} When you pass the file to detectImportOptions , the DataLines  property of the ImportOptions  object excludes the first two lines of data:     >> ops = detectImportOptions("studentGrades.csv");    >> ops.DataLines    ans =         4   Inf
Workaround: To work around this issue, compute the ImportOptions object using detectImportOptions and set the DataLines property to the desired data range.?For example:    >> ops = detectImportOptions("studentGrades.csv");    >> ops.DataLines = [2 Inf];    >> readtable("studentGrades.csv", ops)    ans =      7ˇÁ2 table         students     grades        __________    ______        {'Sally' }    {'A-'}        {'Brian' }    {'B' }        {'Andrew'}    {'91'}        {'Ashley'}    {'C+'}        {'Norman'}    {'87'}        {'Ellie' }    {'A+'}        {'Linda' }    {'B-'}

Summary: The Pressure Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve may default to an incorrect value for the maximum orifice area.
Description: In the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve block, when?Orifice parameterization ?is set to By orifice area vs. orifice opening table , and the sum of the signal at port C  and the Orifice initial opening  ?exceeds a certain threshold, the orifice area may equal a value that is unrelated to the values in?Tabulated orifice area .   In R2018b and earlier, this threshold is the value of the?Orifice maximum opening , which is visible when the?Orifice parameterization  is By maximum opening and area .?In R2019a and later, this threshold is 0.005 m, the default value of?Orifice maximum opening .
Workaround: To avoid these errors, either:Replace the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve with a Variable Orifice block and Pressure Relief Valve block.Connect the Variable Orifice S port to the node that was previously connected to the original block C port.Connect the Variable Orifice port A and Pressure Relief Valve A to the node that was previously connected to the original block A port.Connect the Variable Orifice port B to the node that was previously connected to the original block B port.Connected the Pressure Relief Valve B to the node that was previously connected to the original block R port.Set the Variable Orifice?Model parameterization?to?By area vs. opening table.Leave the Variable Orifice Orifice orientation?as?Opens in positive direction.Set all other Variable Orifice parameters to match the parameters on the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve?block Variable orifice tab.Leave the Pressure Relief Valve Opening area parameterization??as Linear area-pressure relationship.?Set all other?Pressure Relief Valve parameters to match the parameters on the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve?block Pressure compensator tab.?In the Pressure-Compensated 3-Way Flow Control Valve block, use a linear parameterization by setting the?Orifice parameterization?to?By maximum opening and area.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB crashes when dragging ports on?an Enabled Synchronous Subsystem block
Description: Dragging a port on an Enabled Synchronous Subsystem block with a mouse causes MATLAB to crash.Snippet from the MATLAB crash:  [??0] 0x00007ff9a135e410???C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ mf0. dll+00451600 mf::zero::ModelElement::Impl::isProxy+00000000  [??1] 0x00007ff9a11fac01 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ mm_interface. dll+00568321 sl::mfzero::interfaceModel::Port::Impl::setIndexOne+00000049  [??2] 0x00007ff9866ef822 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_lang_blocks. dll+07796770 PortInterface::CopyHandler::willUseCopyInfoForAdd+00088242  [??3] 0x00007ff98671a71c C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_lang_blocks. dll+07972636 sl::interfaceModel::modify::transferMastershipTo+00142156  [??4] 0x00007ff9866f78bf C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_lang_blocks. dll+07829695 sl::interfaceModel::modify::renumberPort+00000063  [??5] 0x00007ff987f34e92 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ libmwsimulink. dll+11554450 SLGlue::renumberPort+00000082  [??6] 0x00007ff9775dec13 C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_editor. dll+08186899 Simulink::FlexiblePortPlacement::doesSimulinkSupportFlexiblePortPlacement+00206307  [??7] 0x00007ff9775c11ad C:\ Program Files\ MATLAB\ R2020a\ bin\ win64\ sl_editor. dll+08065453 Simulink::FlexiblePortPlacement::doesSimulinkSupportFlexiblePortPlacement+00084861

Summary: Audio Test Bench hangs if MATLAB Debugger is stopped during a test bench simulation.
Description: Audio Test Bench enters an unusable state when the following actions occur:? You add a break point in the source code of the Object Under Test used in Audio Test Bench. You start a simulation in Audio Test Bench.? The Audio Test Bench begins initializing, and the break point is hit in MATLAB Editor / Debugger.? If you select "Quit Debugging", Audio Test Bench will get stuck in an initializing state.? All buttons in Audio Test Bench are disabled in this state, including the ability to close the app.  The only way to recover the app when these actions occur is to restart your MATLAB session.
Workaround: Do not stop debugging in MATLAB debugger while Audio Test Bench is running. Instead, stop the test bench simulation.

Summary: Signal Analyzer copy display functionality does not work
Description: Users of the Signal Analyzer  app are not able to copy one or more displays to the clipboard and paste them into another application.

Summary: Cannot override logging for data store defined in data dictionary using Test Manager.
Description: When you create a logged signal set in the Test Manager Simulation Outputs section, a data store defined in?data dictionary does not appear in the signal selection dialog,

Summary: Simulate with Embedded Types button remains disabled in the Fixed-Point Tool after applying data types
Description: When using the Iterative Fixed-Point Conversion  workflow in the Fixed-Point Tool, if a subset of data type proposals are deselected in the Accept ?column of the Results  spreadsheet and then Apply Data Types ?is selected, then the Simulate with Embedded Types ?button remains disabled.

Summary: Operations are performed on the wrong columns when editing timetables with non-ASCII variable names in the Variables editor
Description: In the Variables editor, if you try to perform an operation on one or more selected columns in a timetable with non-ASCII variable names, the Variables editor might perform these operations on the wrong columns. Examples of operations include deleting or sorting columns using the context menu or toolstrip, or plotting using the?Plots? tab.
Workaround: To workaround the problem, you can either:Change the variable names to be ASCII variable names.Perform the operations in the Command Window using the appropriate numeric indexing.?

Summary: Traceability link from Requirement to Simulink Test Case appears unresolved
Description: A traceability link from a requirement to a Simulink Test Case may appear as unresolved (yellow triangle warning icon) in the Requirements Editor even if the linked Test Case is opened in the Simulink Test Manager. This may?happen immediately after creating a link. The warning icon appears in the Requirements Editor in the?Label? column or in the right pane under?Properties  when, in the?View ?drop-down menu,?Links ?is selected. Navigation to the Test Case from the Requirements Editor might work if the .mldatx? file is on MATLAB path, but does not work if the .mldatx  file is not on MATLAB path, even if the Test File is already loaded in the Simulink Test Manager.

Summary: Generated 802.11ax HE waveforms have high peak-to-average-power-ratio (PAPR)?under some parameter combinations
Description: The?post-forward-error-correction?(post-FEC) padding bits used by?wlanWaveformGenerator?to generate 802.11ax HE SU, HE TB, and HE MU waveforms result in a high PAPR when the transmission configuration requires many?padding?bits.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when using Boolean vector or matrix with C Function block
Description: Using a Boolean vector or matrix with a C Function block and indexing into it could cause MATLAB to crash when you simulate your model.? You may see a message like the following: CGIR assertion 'In moveToParentAndMapScriptInputs: Assertion failed: isCGTypeMatched(matrixBase,  pointerBase)' failed in 'matlab\ toolbox\ shared\ slcc\ src\ slcc_blocks\ src\ cfunctionblockrtwcg. cpp'[??0] 0x00007ffdb80ca932 libmwslcc_blocks. dll+00567602 SLCC::SymbolLocation::setLength+00354386?

Summary: The h5writeatt function errors when setting the parameter TextEncoding to 'system'
Description: Using the HDF5 high level function h5writeatt  to write an attribute to the H5 file with the parameter TextEncoding  set to 'system' , the function fails with an error.? For example: h5create('myfile.h5','/g1/g2/myDataset',[100 200 300]);date = datestr(now);h5writeatt('myfile.h5','/','creation_date', 'junk', 'TextEncoding', 'system'); Unrecognized function or variable 'attvalue_size'.Error in h5writeatt>createDatatypeId (line 186)        elseif attvalue_size > 0Error in h5writeatt (line 86)datatypeId  = createDatatypeId(Attvalue, useUtf8);

Summary: emd function throws unexpected error and gaussdesign?function throws incorrect error
Description: The emd  function throws an error when the maximum number of intrinsic mode functions specified with 'MaxNumIMF'  is reached and 'Display'  is set to true . The error can also occur if 'MaxNumIMF'  is not specified and emd  uses the default maximum value. The error message is:Incorrect number of parameters supplied for 'shared_signalwavelet:emd:general:MaxNumIMFHit'.?Expected 0 but found 1.   The gaussdesign  function throws an incorrect error when the filter order is odd. The filter order is specified as the product of the?number of symbols and the number of samples per symbol period. The error message is: Incorrect number of parameters supplied for 'signal:rcosdesign:OddFilterOrder'. Expected 0 but?found 2.

Summary: Multi-testbench signal group time range check may cause multi-testbench code generation workflow to error
Description: Generating multi-testbench code from a model throws a conformance check error if: ????? * the model contains one or more Signal Builder blocks, and ????? * the Signal Builder blocks create signal groups with different time ranges

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Mismatch in value of parameter Scalarize vector ports?between protected model and model in which it is used generates incorrect HDL code
Description: If the value of the Configuration parameter Scalarize vector ports  does not match between the model underneath a protected subsystem and the model in which the protected subsystem is being used, then HDL Coder can generate incorrect code. If the design contains a protected subsystem for HDL code generation, then all Configuration Parameters must match between the top model and the protected model as mentioned in this HDL Coder documentation page:?Protected Model Restrictions for HDL Code Generation .
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Error when generating VHDL test bench for model reference as DUT when?ScalarizePorts?value mismatches between top and referenced model and referenced model contains Single Port RAM
Description: Generating test bench from the DUT might display an error when the following conditions are met: The DUT is used as a model reference block TargetLanguage  is specified as VHDL  There is a mismatch in the?ScalarizePorts ?parameter value between the top-level model and the referenced model The referenced model contains a Single Port RAM block.  The error message looks similar to this:  Undefined function 'Name' for input arguments of type 'hdlcoder.signal'.  Error in slhdlcoder.HDLTestbench/createInputOutputGeneration>createDataSourceOrSink (line 568)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? [], hSigOut.Name, dataRdEnb, local_tb_enb_delay);    Error in slhdlcoder.HDLTestbench/createInputOutputGeneration (line 66)  ? ? [addrSig, enbSig, dataIsConst] = createDataSourceOrSink(this, hN, topN,...    Error in slhdlcoder.HDLTestbench/makehdltbpir (line 107)  ? ? [snkCompareSigs, snkEnables] = this.createInputOutputGeneration(hD, tbpir,...    Error in slhdlcoder.HDLTestbench/makehdltb (line 185)  ? ? ? ? this.makehdltbpir;    Error in slhdlcoder.HDLCoder/makehdltb (line 105)  ? ? this.TestBenchFilesList = hdlTBGen.makehdltb;    Error in privmakehdltb (line 22)  hc.makehdltb(params);    Error in makehdltb (line 78)  ? privmakehdltb(varargin{:})

Summary: MATLAB is not blocked when writing data to a?serialport?object
Description: When writing data to a serialport  object, MATLAB is not blocked until all the data has been transmitted over the serial port, although it should be. This applies to the following serialport  methods: write  writeline  writeread  writebinblock?   Because of this, there might be unexpected errors caused by attempting to access data that has not been transmitted to the serial port yet. For example, attempting to flush data when the previous write operation has not yet been completed returns an error.s = serialport("COM6",38400)?  % write returns immediately, even though data is still being transmitted   write(s,ones(1,1e6),"uint8")   % flush returns an error since the previous write operation is still running   flush(s)  Error using asyncio.MessageHandler/onError (line 31)  The I/O operation has been aborted because of either a thread exit or an application?request

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with a Unit Delay block inside a For Iterator or While Iterator subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a For Iterator Subsystem or While Iterator Subsystem block. There is a Unit Delay block inside the For Iterator Subsystem or While Iterator Subsystem block. The Unit Delay block connects to the Outport block of the For Iterator Subsystem or While Iterator Subsystem block. The Initial condition  parameter of the Unit Delay block and the Outport block have different values.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Numerical mismatch between normal and acceleratormode simulation for variable dimension inputs when the configuration parameter?UseRowMajorAlgorithm is selected
Description: There is a numericalmismatch between a normal and accelerator mode simulation under all of these conditions:  A Sum of Elements block or Product of     Elements block is configured for variable dimension inputs. The model configurationparameter Use algorithms optimized for row-major array layout  (UseRowMajorAlgorithm ) is selected.

Summary: Unable to add a folder to a project in compiler apps such as Application Compiler
Description: In compiler apps such as Application Compiler, in the "Files required for your application to run " section, when you try to add a folder to your project, you get the following error message:  "The following MATLAB files were not added because they could not be read:? <folder location>"
Workaround: In the toolstrip for compiler apps such as Application Compiler, click "Settings".In the text box for "Additional parameters passed to mcc", specify the folder you want to include by typing:??-a?<full folder location>Save and package the project.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier throws a nonintuitive error message on certain models containing MATLAB Function blocks
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may throw the error message 'Internal data used by this model has unsupported data type pointer. Simulink Design Verifier?can not analyze this model'  under the following conditions: The model contains a MATLAB Function block with?Support variable-size arrays ?option checked The MATLAB Function block invokes the?functions? all  or any ?on multiple arrays of different sizes
Workaround: Before invoking Simulink Design Verifier, follow these steps:In the MATLAB Function Block Editor, select Edit Data.Clear the?Support variable-size arrays?check box.

Summary: Error when saturation is enabled in Multi-Winding Transformer block
Description: If you enable saturation in the "Simscape / Electrical / Specialized Power Systems / Fundamental Blocks / Elements / Multi-Winding Transformer" block and, in the?Advanced ?tab,?you select a discrete robust solver (Trapezoidal robust or Backward Euler robust), MATLAB throws this error when you run the model:Unrecognized function or variable 'y'. Caused by:Variable 'y' does not exist.

Summary: Table?UI?component is not visible when you assign a context menu?to it during design time
Description: Table  UI?component visibility is affected when you assign a context menu?to it in an app during design time. If you save and reload the app, then the Table  UI component is no longer visible.

Summary: Parallel building of referenced models opens parallel pool even though?Automatically create a parallel pool is disabled
Description: If you select the Enable parallel model reference builds  check box for the top model of a model reference hierarchy, then building the model automatically opens a parallel pool of MATLAB workers even if the Parallel Computing Toolbox preference Automatically create a parallel pool  is not selected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for PC Worx 6.0?target when using models with?Shift Arithmetic?blocks
Description: Behaviour of?Shift Arithmetic ?instructions on target?PC Worx 6.0? is different from that in Simulink. This could lead to incorrect code generation for Out-Of-Range bit shift operations.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Slow comparison and merge for Simulink models that contain a large number of Stateflow charts and states.
Description: When comparing and merging Simulink models that contain thousands of Stateflow charts and states, the comparison can take a long time to complete.

Summary: When running simulation for a Simulink model containing a deep learning network in either a MATLAB function block or a Deep Learning block, if the user does not have the correct support package, the thrown error is incorrect.
Description: Performing simulation or code generation for a deep learning model without the requisite MATLAB Coder Interface or GPU Coder Interface for Deep Learning Libraries support package returns an incorrect error.? In such situations, you may observe the following error messages in the Diagnostic Viewer: Simulink does not have enough information to determine output sizes for this block. If you think the errors below are inaccurate, try specifying types for the block inputs and/or sizes for the block outputs.  Component:MATLAB Function | Category:Coder error  Undefined function 'coder.loadMatObj' for input arguments of type 'char'.    Function 'MATLAB Function' (#25.56.96), line 3, column 13:  "coder.loadDeepLearningNetwork('alexnet')"  Launch diagnostic report.

Summary: Unable to complete an HTTP request from MATLAB in an environment with Kerberos proxy
Description: The HTTP Interface will cause an authentication failure when authenticating with a Kerberos proxy.? You will not be able to successfully complete the HTTP request.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, disable the Kerberos proxy.

Summary: Logic Analyzer displays incorrect data during simulation when display radix is hexadecimal
Description: The Logic Analyzer displays incorrect waveform data values during the simulation when:  Display radix  is? Hexadecimal .  Simulation is run and paused at least once.  Use the cursor to view data values during the simulation.  Data values are corrected after the simulation ends or if you zoom in or out.

Summary: Failure to display differences in configuration objects stored in Simulink data dictionaries
Description: When you compare two Simulink data dictionaries that contain configuration objects, MATLAB may fail to display the differences. When you click Change Summary  and navigate to elements within the configuration objects, the summary window shows that a component of the configuration objects is invalid or deleted.
Workaround: Save each configuration object in a MAT file, and then compare the two MAT files.

Summary: MATLAB Coder app might ignore the Dynamic memory allocation interface setting when generating C++ code
Description: Suppose that, in the MATLAB Coder app, you set these parameters:? Language ?to C++  Dynamic memory allocation interface ?to Use C style EmxArray ?  In such situation, the code generator might ignore the?Dynamic memory allocation interface ?setting and continue to generate code that uses the?coder::array?template  .  Also, running scripts that you create by using the?Convert to script ?option in the app might have this issue.
Workaround: In the MATLAB Coder app, use the?Convert to script?option to?convert your project to a script.?Open the generated script in MATLAB.In the script, move the entire line that sets the?TargetLang?parameter so that it immediately follows the line in the script that calls?coder.config.Proceed with code generation by running the modified script instead of using the Generate button in the app.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code does not initialize instance-specific parameters for models that specify dynamic allocation
Description: Generated code does not initialize instance-specific parameters for models that specify dynamic allocation: For a GRT-based model: Code interface packaging  is set to Reusable function  For an ERT-based model:?Code interface packaging ?is set to?Reusable function ?and Use dynamic memory allocation for model initialization  is selected  In this case, the instance-specific parameter values are initialized to zero instead of the nominal values specified in the model.

Summary: Missing cast may cause warning when building generated code
Description: When casting a multi-dimensional array to single dimension pointer variable, MATLAB Coder drops an essential cast which may cause warning(s) when building the generated code. The following code snippet generated by MATLAB Coder shows an example of this issue: ? ? int multi_dim_array[2][4];? ??  ? ? int (*single_dim_pointer)[8];? ??  ? ? single_dim_pointer = &multi_dim_array[0][0];  Compiler issues warning message "assignment to ˇ®int (*)[8]ˇŻ from incompatible pointer type ˇ®int *ˇŻ "? when building this code.  To avoid this warning, MATLAB Coder needs to insert a cast to the right-hand side of the assignment: ? ? single_dim_pointer = (int (*)[8]) &multi_dim_array;

Summary: Missing example files in documentation topic "Access Data Through Functions by Using Storage Classes in Embedded Coder Dictionary"
Description: The example files, used in documentation topic "Access Data Through Functions by Using Storage Classes in Embedded Coder Dictionary", are not shipped with Embedded Coder and the example file links are broken. These are the missing example files: rtwdemo_roll_Value.h  rtwdemo_roll_Value.c  rtwdemo_roll_Pointer.h  rtwdemo_roll_Pointer.c   Access the documentation topic by using this command in the MATLAB Command Window: web(fullfile(docroot, 'ecoder/ug/access-data-through-functions-with-coder-dictionary-defaults.html'))
Workaround: Download and unzip the attachment in your current working directory where you want to run the example.

Summary: When opening a text file with UTF-16 or UTF-32 encoding using the Import Tool, a warning message is shown, and if you proceed, the data may not be?imported correctly.
Description: If you try to open a text file with UTF-16 or UTF-32 encoding, the Import Tool displays a warning message stating that the encoding is not supported.? If you continue to load the file anyways, it is opened with UTF-8 encoding, and the file may not be displayed or imported as expected.
Workaround: Use command line functions like readtable instead.

Summary: Potential HDL test bench simulation error for models containing Selector blocks with one-based indexing and index ports
Description: For Selector blocks in one-based indexing mode, to avoid simulation errors due to zero initial values arriving at the input port, HDL Coder inserts zero-protection switches before the index ports in the generated model.? HDL Coder might not insert this zero protection switch before the index port for dimension 2 of the input signal if the following conditions are met: The input signal is an unoriented vector The Index Option  array for the first dimension is either Select all  or a dialog option The Number of input dimensions  parameter equals 2 The indexing mode is one-based   As a result, simulation of the generated model might fail if zero values reach this input port.
Workaround: A zero-protection switch must be manually inserted before the indexing port on the Selector block.? This switch carries over into the generated model and prevents a simulation failure.

Summary: Wrong sample time inferred in HDL implementation model when model base rate is faster than Simscape solver sample time
Description: After running the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor, certain source blocks in the HDL implementation model can have a Sample time  of -1 . This can cause a wrong sample time to be inferred when the model base rate is faster than the Simscape Solver sample time.
Workaround: Change the Sample time on these blocks to a value equal to (Simscape Solver sample time)/(Number of solver iterations).

Summary: Spotlight view fails to open or opens with no connections and all components stacked on top of each other
Description: Creating a spotlight in the composition editor or from an existing spotlight might fail. If this occurs, you will see one of the two outcomes: The progress indicator continually spins and the spotlight is never rendered. The spotlight renders, but all of the components are stacked on top of one another and there are no connections shown.

Summary: Code generation products add the prefix?b_? to a field name if the field name matches the name of a struct or bus type that is part of the same data structure
Description: MATLAB Coder, Simulink Coder, and PLC Coder add the prefix?b_ ?to a field name if the field name matches the name of a struct  type that is part of the same data structure.For example,? if there are two bus types myStruct  and myStruct2 , and there is an element in myStruct2  named myStruct , the name of this field matches the name of the other bus type. Simulink Coder adds the prefix b_, ?so that the names are not the same as follows: typedef struct {double f1;} myStruct;?typedef struct {myStruct b_myStruct;? ?// field name prefixed with b_} myStruct2;

Summary: The docview and rptview commands can error when updating a Microsoft Word document
Description: When?docview  or?rptview ?updates a Microsoft Word document, the following error can occur: Error using mlreportgen.utils.WordDocMessage: Call was rejected by callee. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x80010001 (RPC_E_CALL_REJECTED))Source: Microsoft.Office.Interop.WordHelpLink:Error in mlreportgen.utils.WordDoc/updateImplError in mlreportgen.utils.WordDoc>@(varargin)this.updateImpl(varargin{:})Error in mlreportgen.utils.internal/OfficeDoc/executeWithRetryError in mlreportgen.utils.WordDoc/updateError in mlreportgen.utils.internal/rptviewError in rptview (line 65)mlreportgen.utils.internal.rptview(fileName, varargin{:})

Summary: Subsystem Reference changes cannot be saved from the top model, if it is inside a Model Reference
Description: After editing a Subsystem Reference  block, which is inside a Model Reference  block, user cannot save the changes from the top model. Also the Save All  button doesn't get enabled.

Summary: Code generation fails when indexing into a table or a timetable using logical row indices that have a variable size
Description: Calling codegen  on a function that indexes into a table or timetable and that specifies the table rows using variable-size logical arrays might return this error: ??? Loop unrolling failed because the number of iterations is unknown. The error occurs if the table or timetable contains at least one variable that is a cell array.

Summary: Training DDPG or TD3 agents that use an actor or critic configured to run on a GPU generates an error
Description: Training DDPG or TD3 agents that use an actor or critic configured to run on a GPU generates an error. An actor or critic representation uses a GPU for computation when the UseDevice  property of the associated rlRepresentationOptions  object is set to "GPU" . The generated error is similar to: Error using rl.env.AbstractEnv/simWithPolicy (line 70)An error occurred while simulating "myEnvironment" with the agent "myAgent"....Caused by:    Error using rl.env.SimulinkEnvWithAgent>localHandleSimoutErrors (line 689)    Invalid input argument type or size such as observation, reward, isdone or loggedSignals.        Error using rl.env.SimulinkEnvWithAgent>localHandleSimoutErrors (line 689)        Unable to compute gradient from representation.            Error using rl.env.SimulinkEnvWithAgent>localHandleSimoutErrors (line 689)            Unable to evaluate the loss function. Check the loss function and ensure it runs successfully.                Error using rl.env.SimulinkEnvWithAgent>localHandleSimoutErrors (line 689)                Input data dimensions must match the dimensions specified in the corresponding observation and action info specifications.
Workaround: Use an actor or critic configured to run on a CPU to train DDPG or TD3 agents

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Models containing blocks configured to use the CORDIC approximation algorithm might instead get a precise result in the generated code
Description: The generated C code might not match simulation results for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains at least one of the following blocks which is configured to use the CORDIC approximation algorithm instead of the corresponding precise algorithm. Magnitude-Angle to Complex Trigonometric Function  The input to the block above is a constant value or the input becomes a constant value due to optimizations that occur during code generation.  The generated C code might contain an incorrect constant valuebecause the precise algorithm acts on the constant input value instead of the specifiedCORDIC approximation algorithm acting on the constant input value.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for a function-call, triggered, or enabled and triggered subsystem that is configured for reusable function packaging
Description: When a model contains a function-call, triggered, or enabled and triggered subsystem with the parameter?Function Packaging? set to?Reusable function, ?generated code might be incorrectly placed in the? <model>_step0 ?function instead of another?<model>_step ?function. This incorrect code generation occurs when the trigger port for the subsystem connects to a block that has a sample time different from the fundamental sample time of the model.

Summary: ETL service does not start after upgrading to Polyspace Access
Description: After you upgrade Polyspace Access to version R2020a and click START ALL in the cluster operator web interface, the ETL service does not start. This might happen if the migration of data to the upgraded database fails. When you check the ETL log by using this command: docker logs -f polyspace-etl  You see error messages similar to these messages: Cannot execute: CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS runidx_3002 ON "Result"."CounterExampleInfo" USING BTREE ("RefCodeInformation")?  PSQLException: ERROR: relation "Result.CounterExampleInfo" does not exist  ...?  ?-!> [date at time] *.datasource.etl.PvfLogTracer.log(...) at 18| Cannot do migration (2019b_to_2020a.txt)?  --> [date at time] *.datasource.etl.StorageScanner.doPrsMigration(...) at 1408| Data migration failed (exiting)  You might also see this error message: Cannot do migration (2019a_to_2020a.txt)

Summary: MATLAB might crashwhen an entry-point function contains a mix of in-place GPU array and non-GPUarray variables
Description: Consider the following example where?a? and b? are inputs as well as outputs to the?inplaceGPU  entry-point function. function [b, a] = inplaceGPU(a, b) a = a + 10;? b = b + 10;? end  When generating?CUDA??code for the entry-point function, MATLAB might crash if either a ? or?b ? is a GPU array input. For example, codegen -config coder.gpuConfig -args {gpuArray(rand(10,10)), rand(10,10)} inplaceGPU

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL coder might generate incorrect code for 1-bit HDL Counter with step value of -1
Description: The generated code from the HDL Counter might be incorrect when the?step value  is -1  and the difference between the?Word length  and Fraction length  is 1 .
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 7, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling discretize with repeated bin edges and the 'IncludedEdge' option set to 'right' might produce incorrect answer
Description: In the generated code, calling  discretize  might produce incorrect answer if all  these conditions are true:?  The bin edges provided to discretize   include repeated values, implying that at least one bin has right and left edges that are the same.  The 'IncludedEdge'  option is set to 'right'  and the bin with repeated edges is not the first (i.e. left-most) bin. This indicates that the bin with repeated edges must be empty because the conditions for an element to be placed in it contradict each other.   MATLAB correctly leaves such a bin empty, but the generated code might place elements in it.? Here is an example that illustrates this issue: discretize([0 1 2],[0 0 1 1 2],'IncludedEdge','right')?  MATLAB execution produces this result:?   [1? ? ?2? ? ?4]?  Generated code produces this result:? [1? ? ?3? ? ?4]?  Note that if the call to discretize ?does not use the ?'IncludedEdge' ?option or if the?'IncludedEdge' ?option is set to?'left' , the generated code ?is not affected by this issue.? This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Constant global variable passed to external C/C++ function by using coder.ceval might not be initialized correctly in the generated code
Description: This issue affects code generation if all of these conditions are true: Your MATLAB code passes a global variable to an external C/C++ function by using coder.ceval  The global variable is passed by reference by using?coder.rref  During code generation, you initialize the global variable to a constant value by using coder.Constant?    In such cases, the global variable might not be initialized correctly in the generated code.?
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, assign the global variable to a temporary local variable. Pass the temporary variable to the external C/C++ function by using coder.ceval.For example, suppose that your MATLAB code looks like this:function usingConstantGlobalglobal G;coder.ceval("DoSomething",coder.rref(G));end To work around this issue, change the code to: function usingConstantGlobalglobal G;tmp = G;coder.ceval("DoSomething",coder.rref(tmp));end
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating PLC code for a model that uses Simulink.Signal
Description: MATLAB might crash during PLC Code generation if all of the following conditions are met: PLC code generation target is MultiProg 5.0. Model uses Simulink.Signal Model contains user defined types which contain very long fieldnames?  The stack trace may look like this: [? 0] 0x00007fff0194897b C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\cgir_plc.dll+01411451 CG::plc_rtw_interface+00042731  [? 1] 0x00007fff0194890f C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\cgir_plc.dll+01411343 CG::plc_rtw_interface+00042623  [? 2] 0x00007fff01949f82 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\cgir_plc.dll+01417090 CG::plc_rtw_interface+00048370  [? 3] 0x00007fff019513f6 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\cgir_plc.dll+01446902 CG::plc_rtw_interface+00078182

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Logging Queue block's output might cause issues when simulating using rapid accelerator mode, simulating referenced models in accelerator mode, or running generated code
Description: If a Queue block is not connected to a Message Receive block, logging the Queue block's output using Scope block, To Workspace block, or Simulink Data Inspector might not provide any data when: Simulating using rapid accelerator mode Simulating referenced models in accelerator mode Running the generated code when MAT-file logging?is enabled

Summary: Incorrect carrier damping loss calculation in Limited Slip Differential, Open Differential, and Active Differential blocks
Description: The carrier damping loss calculation, set using the parameter?Carrier damping, bd , is incorrect by a factor of one quarter of the carrier to driveshaft ratio, Ndiff/4. The affected blocks are Limited Slip Differential, Open Differential, and Active Differential. For typically small values of?Carrier damping, bd ?or in applications with a?Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff? of approximately 4, there might not be any noticeable effect.
Workaround: Apply a quarter factor of the?Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff?to the nominal value used for the parameter?Carrier damping, bd.For example, if?Carrier damping, bd?is nominally 1e-3 and the?Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff?is 3.7, then the corrected entry for the parameter?Carrier damping, bd?is 1e-3*3.7/4.

Summary: MATLAB may crash when using the getDataInterfaces function of Code Descriptor API
Description: If you call the getDataInterfaces  method on a coder.codedescriptor.CodeDescriptor  object with the 'ExternalParameterObjects ' argument, MATLAB may crash if the model has an unused parameter.
Workaround: Call getDataInterfaces with the 'Parameters' argument, or remove the unused parameter from the model.

Summary: Wrong diagnostic, mentioning Inf or NaN values, appears in?error message when model fails to initialize
Description: When Simscape initialization fails, the error message may incorrectly mention Inf  or NaN  values, even if there are no Inf  or NaN  values in the model: Detected nonfinite (Inf or NaN) value s while evaluating equations or their partial derivatives at the start of initialization. Such equations may cause the initialization solve to fail. In order to fix this problem, you can either modify some equations or set different prestart values for some of the variables involved. The equations are listed below, as well as their prestart values in computational units.  If you find the prestart values and the variable names listed under?this message do not match, it is safe to?ignore the message.

Summary: Error when generating VHDL test bench for model reference used as DUT with mismatch in value of ScalarizePorts property between the top model and referenced model
Description: For DUT used as a model reference block, generating VHDL test bench from the DUT might display the following error when there is a mismatch in the value of the parameter ScalarizePorts  between the top-level model and the referenced model: Undefined function or variable 'PirInputSignals'.  Error in slhdlcoder.HDLTestbench/createInputOutputGeneration>createDataSourceOrSink (line 590)  ? ? ? ? ? ? outScalars = muxComp.PirInputSignals;  Error in slhdlcoder.HDLTestbench/createInputOutputGeneration (line 66)  ? ? [addrSig, enbSig, dataIsConst] = createDataSourceOrSink(this, hN, topN,...  Error in slhdlcoder.HDLTestbench/makehdltbpir (line 121)  ? ? [snkCompareSigs, snkEnables] = this.createInputOutputGeneration(hD, tbpir,...  Error in slhdlcoder.HDLTestbench/makehdltb (line 185)  ? ? ? ? this.makehdltbpir;  Error in slhdlcoder.HDLCoder/makehdltb (line 106)  ? ? this.TestBenchFilesList = hdlTBGen.makehdltb;  Error in privmakehdltb (line 25)  hc.makehdltb(params);  Error in makehdltb (line 78)  ? privmakehdltb(varargin{:})

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Potentially incorrect VHDL test bench code generated for multirate design with a vector port named phase?at DUT interface and ScalarizePorts parameter set to on
Description: The generated VHDL test bench code from HDL coder might fail to compile and generates an error message similar to: # ** Error: s_tb.vhd(160): No feasible entries for infix operator "=".? # ** Error: s_tb.vhd(160): Type error resolving infix expression "=" as type std.STANDARD.BOOLEAN.?  # ** Error: s_tb.vhd(474): VHDL Compiler exiting?  This error occurs when the following conditions are met:  HDL test bench is generated with VHDL  as the TargetLanguage  The design has multiple rates with a vector port named phase  at DUT interface ScalarizePorts  property is set to on.
Workaround: Either set ScalarizePorts to off or use a different name other than phase for the vector port.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Statement incorrectly generated outside of for loop containing nested parfor loop
Description: For a MATLAB coding pattern involving? A for  loop with a child parfor  loop A statement at the parent for  loop level accesses a variable that is only elsewhere accessed in the child parfor  loop The variables are in an a = foo(a)  style I/O variables of the parent function  MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code with the statement moved outside of the for -loop. See the following MATLAB function example_2214470 function [out1,io1] = example_2214470(in1,io1)    out1 = zeros(10,1);    for k1 = 1:10intermediary = in1(1) + io1(1);parfor k2 = 1:10    io1(k2) = k1.*k2;endout1(k1) = intermediary .* in1(k1);    endend Note that the statement intermediary = in1(1) + io1(1);  contains an a = foo(a)  variable io1  which is only accessed within the child parfor  loop. When generating code using the command? codegen -c -config:exe example_2214470 -args {zeros(10,1),zeros(10,1)} the issue is observed in the generated code: void example_2214470(const double in1[10], double io1[10], double out1[10]){  double intermediary;  int k1;  int b_k1;  int k2;  if (isInitialized_example_2214470 == false) {    example_2214470_initialize();  }  intermediary = in1[0] + io1[0];  for (k1 = 0; k1 < 10; k1++) {    b_k1 = k1 + 1;#pragma omp parallel for \ num_threads(omp_get_max_threads())    for (k2 = 0; k2 < 10; k2++) {      io1[k2] = b_k1 * (k2 + 1);    }    out1[b_k1 - 1] = intermediary * in1[b_k1 - 1];  }} Note that the statement?intermediary = in1[0] + io1[0];  is incorrectly generated before the for loop. Instead the following generated code should have been produced  void example_2214470(const double in1[10], double io1[10], double out1[10]){  double intermediary;  int b_k1;  int k1;  int k2;  if (!isInitialized_example_2214470) {    example_2214470_initialize();  }  for (k1 = 0; k1 < 10; k1++) {    b_k1 = k1 + 1;    intermediary = in1[0] + io1[0];#pragma omp parallel for \ num_threads(omp_get_max_threads())    for (k2 = 0; k2 < 10; k2++) {      io1[k2] = b_k1 * (k2 + 1);    }    out1[b_k1 - 1] = intermediary * in1[b_k1 - 1];  }}

Summary: C Function block local variables defined as extern are visible in generated code
Description: C Function block allows declaration of variables as extern   for use in the block for code generation. The?extern   is preserved when block code is generated instead of being eliminated.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a model that contains subsystems
Description: MATLAB might crash while generating code with the following stack trace for a model that contains subsystems: [??0] 0x00007ffc68371f4c SLCG::CompiledDataInputPortWrapper::populateRecurseOnInput at b:\ matlab\ src\ slcg_impl\ compiledmodel\ compileddatainputportwrapper. cpp:210 (in \ \ mathworks\ devel\ bat\ archive\ R2020a\ perfect\ matlab\ bin\ win64\ slcg_impl. dll)[??1] 0x00007ffc68371508 SLCG::CompiledDataInputPortWrapper::populateAllAttributes at b:\ matlab\ src\ slcg_impl\ compiledmodel\ compileddatainputportwrapper. cpp:273 (in \ \ mathworks\ devel\ bat\ archive\ R2020a\ perfect\ matlab\ bin\ win64\ slcg_impl. dll) [??2] 0x00007ffc6836ace4 SLCG::BlockWrapper::populateCompiledDataInputPorts at b:\ matlab\ src\ slcg_impl\ compiledmodel\ blockwrapper. cpp:60 (in \ \ mathworks\ devel\ bat\ archive\ R2020a\ perfect\ matlab\ bin\ win64\ slcg_impl. dll)  One possible scenario under which the crash might occur is that the output signals from a subsystem connect to an S-function block which has enabled expression folding.

Summary: interpolateHRTF might compute wrong output when using 'VBAP' algorithm
Description: Certain combinations of HRTF databases and desired source positions might cause a numerical warning that is indicative of a wrong output. For example, using the ARI HRTF database: ARIDataset = load('ReferenceARI.mat');hrtfData = permute(ARIDataset.hM,[2,3,1]);sourcePosition = ARIDataset.meta.pos(:,[1,2]);interpolateHRTF(hrtfData,sourcePosition, [99.5 -2],'Algorithm','VBAP');Warning: Matrix is close to singular or badly scaled. Results may be inaccurate. RCOND =  4.151500e-17. > In audio.internal/HRTFInterpolation/performVBAPInterpolation (line 368)  In audio.internal/HRTFInterpolation/performInterpolation (line 79)  In interpolateHRTF (line 84)
Workaround: Use the default 'bilinear' method, or revert to the?'bilinear'?method when the?'VBAP'?method produces a numerical warning (ID 'MATLAB:nearlySingularMatrix').

Summary: Modifications to HDL Block Properties of blocks inside Subsystem Reference are not saved on to the model
Description: Changes made to any of the HDL Block Properties of blocks inside a Subsystem Reference do not dirty the model. Hence, saving these changes by clicking Apply  in the HDL Block Properties dialog box do not apply the properties onto the model.
Workaround: After changing HDL block properties make some trivial changes to the model structure and force dirty flag on the model. For example moving the position of the block in question should trigger Dirty flag. Click save model to force HDL block property changes registered to the disk.

Summary: Example for running PMSM in different modes does not display import and export parameters as it uses Powertrain Blockset
Description: In the Motor Control Blockset example model mcb_pmsm_operating_mode_f28379d , the Surface Mount PMSM block uses Powertrain Blockset license. ?If you use Powertrain Blockset license, the Surface Mount PMSM block in the example does not display the options to export or import the motor parameters (Load parameters  panel is not displayed). However, if Powertrain Blockset license is not available, the example uses the Motor Control Blockset license and the Surface Mount PMSM block displays the options to import and export motor parameters, as intended.?

Summary: Incorrect override of parameters in Simulink Test
Description: If a model has a configuration reference variable of data type char  and you override the value of the variable using Simulink Test Manager, the value is not set correctly. The incorrect value might cause test failures.

Summary: web function sometimes returns an error when called from a compiled application
Description: If a compiled application is created with the MATLAB>Help>Documentation Location  preference?set to Web, on mathworks.com , an error occurs when the deployed application is run.
Workaround: Before authoring a compiled application, set the?MATLAB>Help>Documentation Location?preference to Installed locally.

Summary: Conditionally executed array subscript?is incorrectly executed unconditionally in generated code
Description: Conditionally executed array subscript in MATLAB Function blocks and MATLAB System blocks might result in generated code that executes array subscript unconditionally, when the MATLAB code contains the the following coding patterns: 1) If-else statement where each branch assigns to the same variable and multiple uses of the same array subscript See md1.slx in ReproductionModel.zip for an example.? The MATLAB code in the MATLAB Function block contains the following statement:? ??  ? if index ~= 0 ? ? ? Alpha = ot(index) > 0; ? else ? ? ? Alpha = false; ? end ? if index ~= 0? ? ? ? Alpha = ot(index) < 10; ? end         In this example array subscript ot(index)  should be executed only when index  is not zero.?The code generated from this model contains:  ? Alpha_tmp = md1_U.In1[md1_U.index - 1]; ? md1_Y.Alpha = ((md1_U.index != 0) && (Alpha_tmp > 0.0)); ? if (md1_U.index != 0) { ? ? md1_Y.Alpha = (Alpha_tmp < 10.0); ? }  Note that t he array subscript?    md1_U.In1[md1_U.index - 1] ?is hoisted out and executed unconditionally in the generated code.  2) Assignment statement involving short-circuiting operators and multiple uses of the same array subscriptSee md2.slx in ReproductionModel.zip for an example.? ?The MATLAB code in the MATLAB Function block contains the following statement:  ? Alpha = index ~= 0 && ot(index) > 0 && ot(index) < 10;  In this example array subscript?ot(index) ?should be executed only when?index ?is not zero.?The code generated from this model contains the following:  ? Alpha_tmp = md2_U.In1[md2_U.index - 1]; ? md2_Y.Alpha = ((md2_U.index != 0) && ((Alpha_tmp > 0.0) && (Alpha_tmp < 10.0)));  Note that t he array subscript?   md2_U.In1[md2_U.index - 1] ?is hoisted out and executed unconditionally in the generated code.

Summary: Accelerator mode model block simulation fails if referenced model uses?storage classes on root-level ports
Description: If you create storage classes in an Embedded Coder Dictionary and apply them to the root input or output ports of a model, when you reference the model using a model block and simulate the block in accelerator mode, simulation fails and displays this error: Referenced model '[model name]' uses '[storage class name]' as the default storage class for either Inports or Outports. ?However, ?'[storage class name]' is a user-defined storage class in an Embedded Coder Dictionary, ?so a referenced model cannot use it for this purpose.

Summary: Polyspace analysis fails with error about anonymous union members
Description: When you run a Polyspace analysis configured with a TI compiler and arm target, and you use a designator for an anonymous union member, the analysis might fail during the compilation phase with an error message similar to this: C:\TICompiler\src\test.c, line 30: error: a designator for an anonymous union member can only appear within braces corresponding to that anonymous union|    Test t2 = { 2, .y = y  };
Workaround: To avoid this issue, add braces around anonymous union members. For example,Test t2 = { 2, { .?y = y } };
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Calling readtable or readtimetable on a file with no data returns a cell array
Description: Calling readtable  or readtimetable  on a file with no data returns a cell array. The expected data type is double. This can affect type conversion on the read result. For example, the following code could generate an error:T = readtable('myfile.csv')T.Var1 = int32(T.Var1)
Workaround: To work around this issue, use either of the following techniques:Re-create an empty table without the use of readtable.T = readtable('myfile.csv')T = table('VariableNames',T.Properties.VariableNames,'VariableTypes',{'double','uint8'},'Size',[0,width(T)])Replace the entire variable with a type other than cell.T = readtable('myfile.csv') T.Var1 = NaN(height(T),1);

Summary: Math displays containing an expression inside a square root show incompletely
Description: When a square root encloses an expression with multiple child subexpressions, the renderer displays only the first child subexpression. Consider the valid MathML encoding? <math>  ? <msqrt>  ? ? <mi>a</mi>  ? ? <mo>+</mo>  ? ? <mi>b</mi>  ? </msqrt>  </math>  The expression 'a' is the first child subexpression, '+' and 'b' are two more children. When you paste this encoding into the equation editor in a Live Script using the context menu entry "Paste as MathML" or use the expression as a Simulink block equation label, the expression renders as sqrt(a)?instead of sqrt(a + b).
Workaround: Wrap the <msqrt> arguments in an explicit mrow as in this example code.<math>? <msqrt>? ? <mrow>? ? ? <mi>a</mi>? ? ? <mo>+</mo>? ? ? <mi>b</mi>? ? </mrow>? </msqrt></math>There is just one child expression of the square root, the <mrow> expression. This workaround causes the equation to render correctly.?

Summary: 3rd-Party requirements tool does not accept ReqIF exported by Simulink Requirements
Description: When a requirement file containing links is edited in Simulink Requirements, exported as a ReqIF file, and then imported into a 3rd-party requirements tool (such as Jama), the user might see an error which says "Unable to parse ReqIF content" . This error might occur for files that are created in Simulink Requirements, or those that are created in a 3rd-party requirements tool and then imported into Simulink Requirements.

Summary: SimBiology apps error if a reaction has an empty name
Description: As of R2019b, all reaction objects should have unique and non-empty names. All reactions are created with names, but due to a missing error check the name can subsequently be cleared (that is, the Name  property set to the empty character vector). If a reaction is updated to have an empty name, the SimBiology Model Builder  app and SimBiology Model Analyzer  app might behave incorrectly.

Summary: rtw.asap2SetAddress fails with the DWARF file when used to set the addresses in the ASAP2(a2l) file
Description: When you use the rtw.asap2SetAddress  function? with the DWARF file to set the addresses in the ASAP2(a2l) file, a failure occurs with the following error message: ?"Index exceeds the number of array elements (0)".

Summary: Internal assertion generated during HDL code generation for floating-point operators inside model reference in Native Floating Point mode
Description: An internal assertion is generated during HDL code generation when following conditions are met:  Subsystem contains model reference that uses floating-point operators OptimizationReport property is enabled on the model? Floating Point IP Library  is set to Native Floating Point
Workaround: Disable the OptimizationReport property on the model and generate HDL code.

Summary: Unrecgonized date-time format error when importing DOORS module
Description: In R2020a, when you import a DOORS module on a non-English locale machine and the DOORS module was created by extracting from an archive (.dma or .dpa) or by importing an RTF file, the import might fail with an error that says?"Could not recognize the date-time format ˇ­" . The?error might display a string like "23-October-2018" which?cannot be parsed into a DateTime  object on non-English installation.

Summary: The Dependency Analyzer incorrectly analyzes function argument and class property validation functions that accept inputs.
Description: The Dependency Analyzer incorrectly displays a dependency on a file named "(" when analyzing function argument or class property validation functions that accept an input.

Summary: Test suite and test file cleanup callbacks are executed before all test cases are complete
Description: If one of the test cases in a test suite or test file fails, the associated cleanup callback is executed before all tests have completed executing.?

Summary: AUTOSAR Diagnostic Event Manager event failure or success not flagged if event ID counter exceeds rather than meets threshold
Description: When simulating Diagnostic Event Manager (Dem) events using the AUTOSAR Diagnostic Service Component block, you set Dem event ID counter thresholds that represent event failure or success. The software should flag event failure or success when a counter update meets or exceeds a threshold. But exceeding a threshold without meeting it exactly fails to trigger event failure or success. For more information about Dem event ID counter thresholds, see the Diagnostic Service Component block reference page.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when printing in System Composer or AUTOSAR
Description: Attempting to print a System Composer or AUTOSAR diagram containing ports on the system boundary will crash MATLAB.

Summary: Simulink Toolstrip is blank when username contains non-ASCII characters
Description: When a username contains non-ASCII characters, the Simulink Toolstrip is blank.
Workaround: Access actions from the Simulink Toolstrip in the Simulink Editor canvas by using the Ctrl?+?. (period)?keyboard shortcut.

Summary: blfread does not recognize CAN FD Rx messages
Description: When reading CAN FD messages from a BLF-file using blfread , messages denoted as "Rx" direction are not imported as expected. This issue also occurs when parsing metadata with blfinfo . Messages with "Tx" direction are properly imported. Messages with "TxRq" are ignored by design.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for inference functions with unsupported values for the?'SequenceLength' argument produces incorrect results
Description: Deep learning inference functions such as predict , predictAndUpdateState , and classify  support code generation for the 'SequenceLength'  name-value pair argument. Only the 'longest' and 'shortest' option of this name-value pair is supported for code generation. When any other sequence length value is passed to the inference function , the software issues the following warning and proceeds with code generation by using the default sequence length value. Warning:? 'SequenceLength' value passed to the inference method is invalid. The only supported 'SequenceLength' values for code generation are 'longest' and 'shortest'. The default value 'longest' will be used instead.   However, the generated code does not produce the results expected when using 'longest'  as the sequence length value.
Workaround: To generate correct code for an inference function taking 'longest' as the value for 'SequenceLength', pass 'longest'?as the value to 'SequenceLength' or do not pass any value for 'SequenceLength'. The resulting generated code is correct in this case. The generated code is wrong only when a value other than 'longest' or 'shortest' is passed to the inference function.

Summary: Using the getPCIInfo(tg)?function for a 20 core Speedgoat Target Machine causes a reboot of the target computer
Description: When using the getPCIInfo(tg)  function on a 20-core Speedgoat Target Machine, the target might crash, resulting in an automatic reboot.

Summary: Incorrect objective status reported when a model is analyzed in Accelerator simulation mode
Description: Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports the objectives as Undecided due to runtime error  when a model is analyzed in Accelerator  simulation mode. In earlier releases, Simulink Design Verifier reported statuses as?Satisfied - needs simulation .

Summary: Code generation error with global Data Store Memory and Export-Function model
Description: You may see the below error if you generate code for a model that contains a global Data Store Memory in an Export-Function model: Internal error while creating code interface description file: codeInfo.mat. Aborting code generation.
Workaround: Do not use a global Data Store Memory with an Export-Function Model.

Summary: MEX compilation fails to find clang/clang++ for compilation on macOS Catalina
Description: This issue arises in some Xcode versions (e.g. Xcode v11.4) if macosx SDK has a version number including a patch number. MEX option file uses the full version number including the patch number to find the SDK path and fails to do so since the patch number is not part of the macosx SDK path. As an example, the following error is displayed when compiling a MEX file: Building with 'Xcode with Clang'.  Error using mex  xcodebuild: error: SDK "macosx10.15.4" cannot be located.  xcrun: error: sh -c '/Applications/Xcode11.4.app/Contents/Developer/usr/bin/xcodebuild -sdk  macosx10.15.4 -find clang 2> /dev/null' failed with exit code 16384: (null) (errno=No such  file or directory)  xcrun: error: unable to find utility "clang", not a developer tool or in PATH
Workaround: >> cd(prefdir)?For compiling .c files:?>> mex -setup??For compiling .cpp files:??>> mex -setup C++??Replace the following lines in the mex_C_maci64.xml and/or mex_C++_maci64.xml file:? ? ?<SDKVER>? ? ? ? <cmdReturns name="xcrun -sdk macosx --show-sdk-version"/>? ? </SDKVER>with:?? ? <SDKVER>? ? ? ? <cmdReturns name="xcrun -sdk macosx --show-sdk-version | awk 'BEGIN {FS=&quot;.&quot;} ; {print $1&quot;.&quot;$2}'"/>? ? </SDKVER>?

Summary: Region zoom behaves incorrectly for App Designer apps and UI figures
Description: Zooming into a rectangular region of a 2-D plot with z -values or a 3-D plot in a 2-D view may result in incorrect z -axis limits if the plots are in an App Designer app or a figure created with the uifigure  function.
Workaround: If you explicitly call the uiaxes or uifigure functions, then call the axes or figure functions instead.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for the TIA Portal: Double Precision target IDE could experience inconsistent behavior when type casting a floating-point data type? to an integer data type.
Description: ?When type casting a floating-point data type to an integer data-type Simulink PLC Coder uses REAL_TO_DINT. This is different from the results in TIA Portal: Double Precision due to the different behavior of the REAL_TO_DINT typecast in the IDE. Desired behavior is to use the TRUN function where the decimal portion of the floating-point data type is removed.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect generated code for model with DQ Limiter block and Inverse Park Transform block
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a DQ Limiter block and an Inverse Park Transform block from the Motor Control Blockset. DQ Limiter block's output Saturated d-axis voltage connects to the Inverse Park Transform block's input d-Axis component. DQ Limiter block's output Saturated q-axis voltage is connected to?Inverse Park Transform block's?input q-Axis component. The inputs to the blocks are fixed point data types with non-zero fractional length.  An example model that demonstrates this issue is in the attachment. In the generated code, the Inverse Park Transform block's output?Alpha-axis component is incorrectly reused to compute the Beta-axis component. Below is an example of the incorrect generated C code: md_basic_blocks_v3_Y.Alpha = (md_basic_blocks_v3_Y.Alpha << 2) - rtb_Switch1_idx_1 * 3;  md_basic_blocks_v3_Y.Beta = (rtb_Switch1_idx_1 << 2) + md_basic_blocks_v3_Y.Alpha * 3;  Here?md_basic_blocks_v3_Y.Alpha?is the Inverse Park Transform block's output?Alpha-axis component, which is first updated then used to calculate Beta-axis component incorrectly. The correct code should use a temporary when calculating the the Beta-axis component: md_basic_blocks_v3_Y.Alpha = (rtb_Switch1_idx_0 << 2) - rtb_Switch1_idx_1 * 3;   md_basic_blocks_v3_Y.Beta = (rtb_Switch1_idx_1 << 2) + rtb_Switch1_idx_0 * 3;

Summary: Comment lines can cause detectImportOptions to return the wrong import options
Description: If all lines in a file prior to the VariableNames  line are commented, detectImportOptions  incorrectly detects the table's VariableNames  and the number of header lines in the file, even if  the CommentStyle  name-value pair is specified. For example, given the following file:    #Line 1  #Line 2   LastName,Gender  Smith,Male  Johnson,Male  Williams,Female  Jones,Female  # Brown,Female  Wilson,Male  Moorem,Male  Taylor,Female calling detectImportOptions  with CommentStyle,{'#'}  returns import options with unexpected values for VariableNames  and DataLines : >> opts = detectImportOptions(file, "CommentStyle",{'#'});  >> disp(opts.VariableNames)    {'x_Line1'}    {'Var2'}>> disp(opts.DataLines)     2   Inf
Workaround: To avoid this issue, specifiy the NumHeaderLines name-value pair. For example:>> opts = detectImportOptions(file, "CommentStyle",{'#'}, "NumHeaderLines",2);  >> disp(opts.VariableNames)    {'LastName'}    {'Gender'}>> disp(opts.DataLines)     4   Inf

Summary: Hall sensor example occasionally fails to spin the motor in controlled speed
Description: The two example models, mcb_pmsm_foc_hall_f28379d.slx and mcb_pmsm_foc_hall_f28069m.slx (which are explained in the Field-Oriented Control of PMSM by Using Hall Sensor example), do not spin the motors at a controlled speed. This behavior is because of spurious initial speed value, and the issue is found to be sporadic.

Summary: Use of nargin in anonymous and nested functions of functions with arguments blocks returns incorrect value
Description: In functions with Arguments Blocks, if nargin is used in a nested function or an anonymous function, it returns the parent function's nargin value. For example, function foo(x)    arguments       x     end    nest() % No input arguments    function nest       disp("Nargin is: " + eval('nargin'));     endend>> foo(1)Nargin is: 1 The value of nargin in this case should be zero, but due to a bug in the handling of arguments blocks, the parent's value of nargin is returned. This only impacts nested functions and anonymous functions in the body of a function with an arguments block. Local functions return the proper value.?
