Workaround: Refactor the code to use local functions instead of nested functions when the value of nargin is required.Before:function foo(x)    arguments       x     end    nest() % No input arguments    function nest       disp("Nargin is: " + eval('nargin'));     endend>> foo(1)Nargin is: 1After:function foo(x)    arguments       x     end    local() % No input argumentsendfunction local   disp("Nargin is: " + eval('nargin')); end>> foo(1)Nargin is: 0

Summary: MATLAB might crash when the Property Inspector updates
Description: When the Property Inspector is open, selecting elements in the Simulink Editor canvas might cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Internal assertion during HDL code generation for certain operators with mix of single and double types in Native Floating Point mode for MATLAB Function block
Description: For certain operators with a mix of double and single-precision data types, HDL code generation for MATLAB Function blocks in Native Floating Point mode might generate an assertion: Native floating-point code generation failed with the following error message: Assertion failed.  This error might occur when a binary operator such as add, multiply, or divide has a double-precision non-constant input and a single-precision constant input. The output of this operation has a single precision type, which generates an error during code generation because Native Floating Point  does not support mixed typing (double input to single output). function y = fcn(u)  temp = u + single(1);? ? ? ? ? ??% u is double typed, whereas temp is single typed, which results in the assertion. ? y = double(temp);  end
Workaround: Make the intermediate casting explicit in the MATLAB operations if mixed precision is used.function y = fcn(u)temp = single(u) + single(1);y = double(temp);end

Summary: Using keyboard shortcuts while editing a signal name might result in unexpected behavior
Description: Unexpected behavior might occur when using keyboard shortcuts to edit signals in the signal hierarchy. For example, using Ctrl+Z  to revert an active name edit might undo the last signal tree operation, such as a signal insertion, instead of reverting the name edit.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit Delay block with non-virtual bus as input generates incorrect HDL code
Description: Generating HDL code from a Unit Delay block with a non-virtual bus as input and a non-zero initial condition results in a simulation mismatch.
Workaround: Consider using Unit Delay or Integer Delay with zero initial value for code generation.

Summary: For asynchronous tasks, model initialization code incorrectly placed after thread creation code
Description: A task function running before the data structures used by a block in the?associated function-call subsystem may lead to undefined behavior in the generated code. You may see this issue when a task block (such as Linux or VxWork Task in the Embedded Coder Support Package for Xilinx Zynq Platform) is connected to a function-call subsystem that contains certain blocks (UDP, TCP/IP from the same support package).

Summary: MATLAB displays warnings when gathering code coverage information as part of a continuous integration system
Description: When gathering code coverage information as part of a continuous integration system, MATLAB displays a number of extraneous and unhelpful warnings. For example, MATLAB displays this warning: Warning: MATLAB Connector is not running.  > In message. unsubscribe  ??In matlab. internal. profileviewer/ MessageService/ delete

Summary: 'conflicting types' error observed while building code using Simscape in a custom target environment
Description: Under certain specific custom hardware implementations, some functions in the generated C code have type mismatches that result in build failures. This bug can occur if the hardware distinguishes between 'int ' and 'int32_t ' or if you have changed default types in your custom TLC.

Summary: Requirements report displays unrelated text with requirement description
Description: When you generate a Requirements Report for a Simulink model with the "Include details from linked documents"?option enabled?in Report Settings,?the generated page will?display HTML style noise like this: "p, li { white-space: pre-wrap; }" for every reported link destination in a Simulink Requirements Set (links that point to entries?in?.slreqx files).
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Lookup table might produce incorrect answers for fixed-point Breakpoints?with non-trivial slope and Table Data of single data type
Description: Lookup table blocks might produce mismatched results between simulation and generated code when all of the following conditions are true: Breakpoints ?is of a fixed-point data type. The fixed-point data type has a slope value not equal to 1. Data type of both Table data  and output  is single. Interpolation method is?Linear point-slope  or Linear Lagrange .  These blocks are affected: n-D Lookup Table 1-D Lookup Table 2-D Lookup Table  You are more likely to encounter this problem when?it requires double precision to represent the?SlopeAdjustmentFactor  of the fixed point type of the Breakpoints.

Summary: Shared library conflicts cause standalone applications to fail in some Windows based deployment environments
Description: Shared library conflicts cause standalone applications to fail in some Windows based deployment environments. When a standalone application created by MATLAB Compiler is running on a Windows system, the default Windows library search order is followed. This causes conflict in some deployment environments where DLLs shipped with MATLAB Runtime take lower precedence over the same DLLs present in other directories such as the Windows System  directory. As a result, standalone applications fail in certain environments. The conflict manifests itself as follows: The standalone application might report DLL library related error message. The standalone application runs fine on most machines but only fails on certain machines. The standalone application can run successfully when copied to <MATLAB Runtime Root>\bin\win64  and be launched from there in an environment where it fails.

Summary: Workspace variables used in System object? masks are not resolved during?Refresh Init
Description: The issue is encountered when you use a base or model workspace variable in the mask of a System object??inside a PassThrough block (Custom workflow using PassThrough block ).??When you click on the?Refresh Init ?button, the workspace variable should be resolved and its value should be placed in the instantiation section of the Init code. Instead, the?variable name is placed in the instantiation section, which does not resolve to a value. As a result, an error occurs when the Simulink model? is run.
Workaround: Do not use base or model workspace variables in System object? masks.? Instead, use the value desired.

Summary: Divide block with ShiftAdd architecture generates internal error for vector dividend and scalar divisor
Description: HDL code generation for a Divide block with Architecture   set to ShiftAdd ?throws an error that contains: ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?'pir_core:pirudd:assertionFailed' ? when the dividend is a fixed-point vector data and the divisor is a fixed-point scalar data.

Summary: MATLAB crashes while updating requirement from IBM DOORS Next server
Description: If you open a requirement set created in IBM DOORS Next (previously known as IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation, sometimes referred to as DNG) that was imported with a query based import and try to update a requirement, the MATLAB session will crash.

Summary: Error generated when accessing a credit scorecard created in a prior release
Description: If you load a creditscrorecard  object that was saved in an earlier MATLAB version, an error occurs when you access that object with any of the following functions: probdefault   validatemodel   compact   displaypoints   fillmissing   predictorinfo  score

Summary: Help link error for Interior PMSM and Surface Mount PMSM blocks
Description: Clicking the Help  button for library blocks Interior PMSM and Surface Mount PMSM generates an error.
Workaround: Open these web pages to see the help information for the blocks on the MathWorks documentation:https://www.mathworks.com/help/mcb/ref/interiorpmsm.htmlhttps://www.mathworks.com/help/mcb/ref/surfacemountpmsm.html

Summary: Generated Verilog code for Moore charts due to mix of blocking (=) and non-blocking (<=) assignments in the same process
Description: When generating Verilog code from a Moore  chart using HDL Coder, the generated code contains a mix of blocking (=)?and non-blocking (<=) assignment statements?in the same always process block. This can result in a STARC violation as the STARC coding style recommends that an always process block contains either blocking or nonblocking assignments.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation assertion or incorrect code generated for model containing error-status ports for two elements of the same AUTOSAR receiver port
Description: Generating code for an AUTOSAR model that meets all of the following conditions fails or results in incorrect code: The model contains two bus element inports mapped to different elements of the same AUTOSAR receiver port. For each bus element inport, the model contains a signal inport configured for AUTOSAR data access mode ErrorStatus . One of the following conditions exists: One bus element inport is configured for data access mode ImplicitReceive  and the other for ExplicitReceive . Both bus element inports are configured for ExplicitReceive .   If one bus element inport is configured for data access mode ImplicitReceive  and the other for ExplicitReceive , code generation fails with the following assertion: CGIR assertion 'In apply: Assertion failed: var2 != nullptr' failed in 'code_gen/AutosarHiddenBufferTransform.cpp:581' If both bus element inports are configured for ExplicitReceive , incorrect code is generated. The error status ports use the value of the data element rather than the error status of the RTE call.

Summary: Traceability report in HDL Coder leads to cryptic error message during code generation.
Description: When the traceability report is enabled and the generated model is disabled, HDL Coder generates a cryptic error during code generation. ### Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.  Error in slhdlcoder.HDLTraceabilityDriver/generateRecommendationsSerialization>getHighlightingFilename  Generating the traceability report while model generation is disabled is not supported.?
Workaround: Either enable model generation or disable the traceability report generation.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Signal object InitialValue ignored on root inputs of referenced models when storage class is 'Model default'
Description: If a signal object with InitialValue  is resolved for a root input and the model does not have external input data specified, the signal object's InitialValue  will be used for the root input value throughout simulation. However, if the signal object is resolved for a root input of a referenced model and its storage class is 'Model default ', the signal object is not propagated from the referenced model up to the calling model and the InitialValue  is ignored.
Workaround: Use any one of these workarounds:Specify external input data for the top model so that root input values are explicitly specified for all simulation time steps.Specify a storage class other than 'Model default'.

Summary: Incorrect carrier damping loss calculation in Limited Slip Differential and Open Differential blocks
Description: The carrier damping loss calculation, set using the parameter Carrier damping, bd , is incorrect by a factor of one quarter of the carrier to driveshaft ratio, Ndiff/4. The affected blocks are Limited Slip Differential and Open Differential.  For typically small values of Carrier damping, bd ?or in applications with a?Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff? of approximately 4, there might not be any noticeable effect.
Workaround: Apply a quarter factor of the Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff to the nominal value used for the parameter Carrier damping, bd.For example, if Carrier damping, bd is nominally 1e-3 and the Carrier to drive shaft ratio, Ndiff is 3.7, then the corrected entry for the parameter Carrier damping, bd is 1e-3*3.7/4.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Undefined simulation and code generation behavior might occur when signal that drives two Outport blocks resolves to a Simulink.Signal object
Description: Undefined simulation and code generation behavior might occur for the following modeling pattern:  A signal resolves to a  Simulink.Signal  object with a non-Auto storage class and a non-empty initial value.?  The signal branches and feeds into two or more Outport blocks. This bug can still occur if one or more virtual blocks are present between the driving signal and any of the Outport blocks.  No error is reported for this modeling pattern during model compilation.
Workaround: The issue might be avoided by inserting non-virtual blocks between the driving signal and each of the destination Outport blocks.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during?rtwbuild?for a model with reference arguments and mask parameter callbacks
Description: MATLAB might crash when performing?rtwbuild  on a Simulink model that contains the following: A model reference with a model argument. The model argument is defined in the model workspace as a struct. The referenced model contains a masked block within a subsystem The masked block has a promoted parameter. The promoted parameter values are set based on the corresponding mask parameter callback.  An example crash snippet is as below: 0x00007ffedb646b79 SlVariableMaskWS::getWorkspace at b:\matlab\src\sl_prm_engine\utility\slvariable.cpp:1245  0x00007ffedb645f5f SlVariable::getMxArrayRef at b:\matlab\src\sl_prm_engine\utility\slvariable.cpp:300  0x00007ffedb6462db SlVariable::getSlArray at b:\matlab\src\sl_prm_engine\utility\slvariable.cpp:799  0x00007ffed020254b GetStructRefValueWithoutIndices at b:\matlab\src\sl_compile\rtwgen\astsup.cpp:1634  0x00007ffed020144e ASTExprHasNonDoubleMath at b:\matlab\src\sl_compile\rtwgen\astsup.cpp:1747  0x00007ffed0202085 GenerateASTForPrmExprFromBlkOrWks at b:\matlab\src\sl_compile\rtwgen\astsup.cpp:2919

Summary: Labeler apps error out while?attempting to load?files with?single-character or single-digit names
Description: When you use any Labeler apps, like the Image  La beler  app to?load files with single-digit or single-character names like 1.jpg or a. jpg, the app returns the following error: Error: vision.? internal.? labeler.? tool.? Layout/? hideTabXCloseButton Java exception occurred: java.? lang.? ClassCastException: java.? lang.? Character cannot be cast to java.? lang.? String at com.? mathworks.? widgets.? desk.? DTOccupant.? getName(DTOccupant.? java:373) at com.? mathworks.? widgets.? desk.? Desktop.? clientMatches(Desktop.? java:6443) at com.? mathworks.? widgets.? desk.? Desktop.? getDTClient(Desktop.? java:6380) at com.? mathworks.? widgets.? desk.? Desktop.? getClient(Desktop.? java:815)??
Workaround: The file names must have at least two characters or two digits. For example, if the file name is 1.jpg then?rename it to 01.jpg.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Runtime error not reported when switch expression is non-finite valued in MATLAB function
Description: Simulating a MATLAB function containing a switch statement requires that the value of the switch expression is representable by the C type?int . This is enforced through a runtime error if this condition is violated. When simulating a MATLAB function with a program containing: A double or single precision switch expression The expression is multiplied by a literal 0  Simulink might omit reporting the necessary runtime error. This can lead to incorrect runtime behavior when the switch expression is non-finite valued. A simple example can be seen in model_example_2204299.slx  within the attached file example_2204299.zip . Simulating this model?should result in a runtime error: Expected a value representable in the C type 'int'.  Found -nan instead.Error in example_2204299 (line 2)   switch(x*0) When this issue occurs, a runtime error is not thrown. Instead the simulation behaves as if the switch expression is valued as 0. This issue can affect models containing MATLAB Function Block, MATLAB Function in Stateflow, or MATLAB System Block.? See also Bug Report ID 2172393 ?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when capturing a baseline to a spreadsheet
Description: MATLAB might crash when you click Capture  in the Test Manager Baseline Criteria  section or when you use? sltest.testmanager.captureBaselineCriteria .

Summary: Feature tables that are exported by the app and returned by app-generated code do not have the same column order
Description: Feature tables that are exported by the app and returned by app-generated code do not have the same column order. For example, generated code puts the condition-variable columns first in the feature table, while the app export process puts the condition-variable columns last.

Summary: Read Point Cloud block throws errors when running the simulation
Description: The Read Point Cloud  block?errors out during simulation for certain types of Point Cloud ROS Messages. When the data-type of the Intensity  field in a?sensor_msgs/PointCloud2?ROS message?is not uint8, the Read Point Cloud  block fails with following message: Unable to perform assignment because the size of the left side is N-by-1 and the size of the right side is (N*2)-by-1.  In the error message, the value, N , depends on the Maximum point cloud size  block parameter.

Summary: Saving JDBC data sources causes the Database Toolbox license to be checked out on MATLAB startup
Description: Configuring a JDBC data source using one of the following methods will cause the Database Toolbox license to be checked out on start-up the next time MATLAB is opened (even if no Database Toolbox features are used): Saving a data source through the?JDBC Data Source Configuration ?dialog in Database Explorer Executing the?saveAsJDBCDataSource ?method of a?JDBCConnectionOptions ?object

Summary: Feature tables that have been exported to the workspace or Classification Learner might incorrectly match feature names with feature values
Description: When a feature table contains at least one feature that the user imported into the app, the app exports that table with a mismatch between feature names and feature values. In the exported table, the imported feature names come before the derived feature names but the derived feature values come before the imported feature values.

Summary: Certain changes made to the properties of a coder.Type object by using the Coder Type Editor might not be committed
Description: If you use the Coder Type Editor to change the properties of a coder.Type  object, certain changes might not be reflected in the auto-generated MATLAB code for the type or in the coder.Type  object that you subsequently save in the base workspace. This issue might affect your workflow if you perform this sequence of actions in the Coder Type Editor:? For a coder.Type  object, edit any property that uses a text field. For example, class, size, or type name. For the same object, immediately change another property that belongs to one of these two categories: The property takes a boolean value. For example, for a double  type, the Sparse and Complex?properties.  In the Coder Type Editor, you can modify the value of the property by using a spinner (up/down arrows). For example, for an embedded.fi  type, the WordLength  and FractionLength  properties.  Observe that, in the MATLAB Code pane of the type editor, the change made in step 2 is not reflected in the auto-generated MATLAB code for the type. Save the coder.Type  object.? Observe that the coder.Type  object that you saved in the base workspace does not reflect the change made in step 2.?
Workaround: For every change you make to a property that uses text fields (for example, size, class, or type name), commit the change by clicking away from the text field or by pressing the enter key. Only after you commit the change in this way, make changes to any other property.

Summary: Uncompilable generated code might occur for MATLAB code containing a loop that operates on variables of different data types with SIMD enabled
Description: The generated code might be uncompilable for MATLAB code that contains a loop that operates on variables of different data types when an Intel SSE or Intel AVX code replacement library is specified.In the uncompilable code, the loops vectorize incorrectly, so that the generated codecontains pseudo-SIMD instructions that are not valid for the target. These pseudo-instructions appear in the generatedcode as??vload  and vstore  functions.
Workaround: Do not specify an Intel SSE or Intel AVX code replacement library.

Summary: The trainSSDObjectDetector function produces inaccurate results or throws an error for rectangular input images
Description: Training an SSD object detection network on images of rectangular size by using the?trainSSDObjectDetector ?function, Produces inaccurate results if the input size of the image input layer of the network has the same values for the height and width dimensions.? Generates an error if the input size of the image input layer of the network has different values for the height and width dimensions.

Summary: Simulation mode set to Normal mode, once the analysis is finished
Description: If simulation mode of a model is set to a value other than Normal   mode and Simulink Design Verifier analysis is performed on that model, then simulation mode changes to Normal   once the analysis finishes.

Summary: Fast Restart mode gets disabled after Simulink Design Verifier analysis
Description: If Fast Restart ?is enabled for a model and Simulink Design Verifier analysis is performed on that model, then Fast Restart  gets disabled once the analysis has finished.

Summary: Added missing support for stretch matrix in the Simulation 3D Fisheye Camera block.
Description: The Simulation 3D Fisheye Camera block was missing stretch matrix parameter and therefore the Scaramuzza camera model was not fully supported. In some cases, a camera calibrated as described in Fisheye Calibration Basics , could not be fully represented in the Unreal Game Engine simulation environment.

Summary: Error when opening live script with Unstack Table Variables or Stack Table Variables live editor task
Description: When opening a live script that contains either the Unstack Table Variables or Stack Table Variables live editor task, you might see the error: "Dimensions of array being concatenated are not consistent"  in place of the live editor task.
Workaround: Load table or timetable that had been selected in the live editor task as a workspace variable. Then close and reopen the live script.

Summary: Using?ncdisp?or?ncinfo?on certain OPeNDAP endpoints generates an error
Description: On macOS platforms, displaying contents and accessing information?from certain OPeNDAP endpoints?using ncdisp  or ncinfo ?returns an error in MATLAB. For example, the following code ? ncdisp('https://oceanwatch.pifsc.noaa.gov/erddap/griddap/OceanWatch_aqua_chla_monthly');  returns this error: Error using netcdflib  The NetCDF library encountered an error during execution of 'open' function - 'Access failure (-77)'.  Error in netcdf.open (line 67)  [varargout{:}] = netcdflib ( 'open', filename, varargin{1} );  Error in internal.matlab.imagesci.nc/openToRead (line 1278)  this.ncRootid = netcdf.open(this.Filename,'NOWRITE');  Error in internal.matlab.imagesci.nc (line 121)  this.openToRead();  Error in ncdisp (line 62)  ncObj = internal.matlab.imagesci.nc(ncFile);
Workaround: To avoid this issue, add the file .dodsrc in your home directory (~/.dodsrc) containing the following text:  HTTP.SSL.VALIDATE=1Then restart MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Function block using MATLAB Datapath architecture might result in mismatch with nested user function calls
Description: For repeated function calls to the same function that are nested, such as?subfunc(a, ?b, ?subfunc(c, ?d, ?e)),? the validation model might show a mismatch or code generation might error out unexpectedly. This issue can occur when floating-point signals are present at the MATLAB Function block interface, or when fixed-point signals are present at the block interface with MATLAB Datapath  specified as the HDL architecture.
Workaround: For the nested function call, store the result in an intermediate variable and use that as an input to the outer function call instead.

Summary: Performance regression during code generation for deep learning object detectors with a large number of classes
Description: Code generation for object detectors such as yolov2ObjectDetector  or ssdObjectDetector  with a large number of classes (100 or more) could take several minutes or hours.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated PLC code might produce incorrect results due to automated type conversion from unsigned to signed integer
Description: PLC coder automatically converts unsigned 32 bit integers types to signed equivalent for some targets. This conversion might occur for certain target PLC IDEs that do not support unsigned types. Operations like SHL (shift left) might cause the output to hit the bit boundary producing incorrect results. For example: >> bitshift(uint32(67026944),  16,  'uint32') % original code with uintans =  uint32   3221225472   and ?  ?>> bitshift(int32(67026944),  16,  'int32')  % converted to int as target does not support uintans =  int32   -1073741824  produce completely different results even though their Boolean representation is the same,  >> dec2bin(bitshift(int32(67026944),  16,  'int32'))ans =    '11000000000000000000000000000000'>> dec2bin(bitshift(uint32(67026944),  16,  'uint32'))ans =    '11000000000000000000000000000000'
Workaround: If the target IDE does not support unsigned integers, do not use unsigned integer types in your Simulink model. Also, consider not using "large-valued" signed 32-bit integers, as these might produce intermediate unsigned 32-bit integers.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when simulating models with non-complementary motion actuation and at least one implicit 6-DOF tree joint
Description: If a Simscape Multibody model has non-complementary motion actuation and at least one implicit 6-DOF tree joint, MATLAB might crash during a normal simulation. The crash occurs when an insufficient computed torque error occurs, and the joint with the largest magnitude error is an implicit 6-DOF joint.  When generating code for such a model (rapid accelerator, etc), MATLAB will always crash.
Workaround: Simscape Statistics Viewer displays the number of implicit 6-DOF joints in a model; try to eliminate all implicit 6-DOF tree joints by adding explicit joint blocks, such as the 6-DOF Joint block, from the Simscape Multibody library.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Running steps in Test Sequence are not highlighted in the animation during simulation
Description: Some steps deep in the step hierarchy of a Test Sequence block are not highlighted correctly in the animation during simulation, even though the steps are actually running.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: The Cartridge Valve Insert block may implement an incorrect value for the poppet stroke.
Description: In the Cartridge Valve Insert block, when Orifice specification  is set to either?Area vs. opening table  or Pressure-flow characteristic , the poppet stroke remains at its default value of 0.005 m, irrespective of the?Tabulated orifice opening   parameter value.
Workaround: To avoid this error, replace the Cartridge Valve Insert block with a Hydraulic Cartridge Valve Actuator block and Variable Orifice block as shown on the block?Documentation page:Connect the Hydraulic Cartridge Valve Actuator port A to the Variable Orifice port A.Connect the Hydraulic Cartridge Valve Actuator port B to the Variable Orifice port B.In the Cartridge Valve Actuator block,?Set the?Poppet stroke equal to the last element of the Tabulated orifice opening, minus the first element of the?Tabulated orifice opening, from the Cartridge Valve Insert block.Set the?Poppet-seat Initial gap?equal to the Cartridge Valve Insert Initial opening setting.Ensure that the Actuator Orientation?remains at its default setting of Acts in positive direction.All other parameters should match the parameter values in the Cartridge Valve Insert block.In the Variable Orifice block,?Ensure that the Orifice?Orientation?remains at its default setting of Opens?in positive direction.Ensure that the Initial opening?parameter remains at its default value of 0 m.All other parameters should match the parameter values in the Cartridge Valve Insert block. The Cartridge Valve Insert block parameters correspond to the following Variable Orifice block parameters:Orifice specification to?Model parameterizationTabulated orifice opening to?Orifice opening vectorTabulated orifice area to Orifice area vectorTabulated pressure differential to?Pressure differential vectorTabulated flow rate to?Volumetric flow rate table
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: C Function block may use code specified in the section were MATLAB_MEX_FILE was supposed to be defined when simulated in model reference accelerated mode
Description: C Function block defines MATLAB_MEX_FILE for simulation. When simulated in model reference accelerated mode, the block may not define the MATLAB_MEX_FILE for simulation.This could result in block using the code where MATLAB_MEX_FILE was supposed to be defined when simulated in model reference accelerated mode.

Summary: Cloud cluster wizard shows wrong dialog title
Description: When using the Create a Cloud Cluster wizard, and after signing in, the title of dialog is incorrect. The dialog title remains Sign In when the dialog displays pages describing the cloud workflow. You can safely ignore the incorrect dialog title.

Summary: Comparison Tool is slow when comparing large live code files or when it is being resized
Description: The Comparison Tool is slow when comparing large live code files. It is also slow when comparing live code files and the size of the window is changed or when merging two live code files.

Summary: MATLAB might sporadically crash while executing the update method of ChartContainer subclasses
Description: The update method in a chart that is a subclass of? matlab.graphics.chartcontainer.ChartContainer ?might crash in the following cases: If the update  method throws an error?? If you run the update   method in the MATLAB debugger If you Interrupt the update  execution method with Ctrl+C

Summary: Import from IBM DOORS Next Generation broken for non-default server instance
Description: IBM DOORS Next (previously known as IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation or IBM DOORS Next Generation, sometimes referred to as DNG) is typically hosted under /rm/ section of the Jazz server, but the platform does support multiple requirements servers on same Jazz host, with server suffix like "rm1", "rm2", etc. The Import feature in Simulink Requirements cannot work with IBM DOORS Next projects that are hosted on servers with non-default server instance suffix, only the default configuration ("rm") works.? For non-default server configuration, there is a repeated password prompt from the Import UI after selecting "IBM DOORS Next Generation".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink.Parameter object with 64-bit integer value not checked against Min and Max properties
Description: When the data type of a Simulink.Parameter  object is 64-bit integer, the value is not checked against the specified Min  and Max  properties. This can result in a silent wrong answer.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Improper sample time propagation to nonvirtual blocks outside of Initialize, Reset and Terminate subsystems
Description: Sample time used in Initialize, Reset and Terminate subsystems may propagate to nonvirtual blocks outside the subsystem. This may cause incorrect generated code or a crash during code generation.
Workaround: Insert a Signal Copy block in front of the nonvirtual block to stop the sample time propagation.

Summary: Annotation comments with color leads to collapsed HDL code generation check report
Description: HDL code generation check report gets collapsed when the model contains annotation comments with non-plain text, that is, comments having color or different font or size.

Summary: Incompatibility error during code generation for models with Simulink Function and Function Caller blocks with inconsistent C/C++ identifier names
Description: When both of the following conditions are true, Simulink generates an incompatibility error during code generation. The model contains a Simulink Function block with the?Function visibility  block?parameter set to? global , and one or more corresponding Function Caller blocks in another model in the hierarchy. The C/C++ Function Interface of the blocks is configured with inconsistent?C/C++ identifier names.  In this scenario, the incompatibility error that is thrown is similar to the following error.? *****?  Function caller prototype '$N(u1 rt$I$N$M,u2 rt$I$N$M,u3 rt$I$N$M)' is not compatible with function definition '$N(u1, u2, u3)'?  ******
Workaround: Configure the Simulink Function block and Function Caller block to have the same C/C++ identifier names.

Summary: Simulating a model hierarchy with model arguments that span multiple levels might produce incorrect results
Description: Simulation results for a model hierarchy will be incorrect when all of these conditions apply: A model argument is promoted up through multiple levels of the model hierarchy. The referenced model that defines the model argument in the model workspace is a function-call model or has a virtual bus that crosses the model boundary. A Model block that receives the model argument from a lower level specifies a value for it.  Simulation will use a model argument value that is specified at a lower level of the model hierarchy instead of the value specified at the highest level Model block that receives the model argument.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink might not rebuild rapid accelerator target when?accelerator target is up to date
Description: Simulink might not rebuild the rapid accelerator target for the top model of a model hierarchy when all of the following conditions apply: You have a model hierarchy with at least one referenced model.  You make a change to a model in the hierarchy that requires a rebuild of the model reference simulation target during simulation in accelerator mode. You simulate the top model of the hierarchy in accelerator mode. You simulate the top model of the hierarchy in rapid accelerator mode.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Simulink.io.SignalBuilderSpreadsheet export method on signal or dataset containing signal with duplicate points might error
Description: If a set of signals passed to the export  method contains duplicate time values for one or more signals, Simulink might return an error because? interpolation methods do not support duplicate time values.

Summary: Code generation error when subsystem contains Stateflow chart and execution time profiling is enabled
Description: Suppose your model has a subsystem that contains a Stateflow chart and function profiling is enabled through the?Measure function execution times  configuration parameter. When you generate code through a right-click subsystem build, you might see an error. For example:   Error at element 4 of first input to get_param:Invalid Simulink object name: <SID>

Summary: ARXML import for AUTOSAR adaptive software component validates existing enumerations against header file requirements for AUTOSAR Classic Platform instead of AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform
Description: When importing ARXML for an AUTOSAR adaptive software component, imported enumeration data types could match those that already exist in the model's scope (base workspace) and can be reused. These existing enumerated data types are validated against the header file name requirements for the AUTOSAR Classic Platform ('Rte_Type.h') instead of the requirements for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform ('impl_type_<symbol>.h'). A false error similar to the following is thrown:? Error using arxml.importer/createComponentAsModel     You are defining enumerated type ENUMNAME.    However, a corresponding enumeration class already exists. The header file    for the class is not set to Rte_Type.h. Either implement the static method    getHeaderFile and configure it to return Rte_Type.h, or remove the class from the path.?
Workaround: Removeexisting enumerations from the path and workspace to avoid this error.

Summary: Simulink Diagram and ElementDiagram reporters error for models with custom background color
Description: When generating a snapshot of a model that has a custom background color, t he slreportgen.report.Diagram  and slreportgen.report.ElementDiagram  reporters throw an error with the following message: Error using double Conversion to double from cell is not possible.?
Workaround: Before using the Diagram or ElementDiagram reporters, change the background color of the model to a named color ('white', 'red', etc.)mdl = "vdp"; open_system(mdl);? set_param(mdl, "ScreenColor", "[0.5, 0.25, 0.25]") rpt = slreportgen.report.Report("output"); diag = slreportgen.report.Diagram(mdl);oldColor = get_param(mdl,"ScreenColor");set_param(mdl, "ScreenColor","white"); add(rpt, diag); set_param(mdl, "ScreenColor",oldColor); close(rpt); rptview(rpt)

Summary: Extract Equations step in Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor does not terminate when Stop time of a model is Inf
Description: The Extract Equations  step of the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor simulates the original model is extract the differential algebraic equations. If the Stop time  of the model is set to Inf , the model simulation doesn't terminate and the Extract Equations  step doesn't get completed. Change the Stop time  of the model to capture the relevant simulation behavior in a short amount of time.

Summary: Launching an analysis from MATLAB generates the error: Product required for 'pslinkrunImpl' not installed
Description: Launching a Polyspace analysis from MATLAB on a model reference with a configuration set reference with a command like this:   pslinkrun(modelName,? psCfg,? true); generates the following error: Error using pslinkrunImplProduct required for 'pslinkrunImpl' not installedError in pslinkrun (line 34)[resultDir,? sysResultDir] = pslinkrunImpl(varargin{:});Error in myfunc (line 90)    [~,? resDir] = pslinkrun(modelName,? psCfg,? true);
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder Server, Polyspace Bug Finder

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using?polarscatter?or polarplot with markers
Description: When using the?polarscatter ?or?polarplot ?function with markers, MATLAB might crash and generate a stack trace that includes the term 'createSizes' .
Workaround: There is a workaround for polarlscatter, but not for polarplot.If you are using the polarscatter function, specify the size data for the markers.? If you want the markers to be the same size, specify one of the marker sizes to be a slightly different size than the others.For example, this code crashes MATLAB:?polarscatter(rand(1,20000), rand(1,20000))The default marker size is 36 for polarscatter. You can avoid the crash by setting one of the marker sizes to 36.001, which is a visually imperceptible difference:s = 36*ones(1, 20000);s(1) = 36.001;polarscatter(rand(1, 20000), rand(1, 20000), s)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Absolute-time temporal logic operators before and after may result in incorrect behavior when used with constant thresholds and with milliseconds and microseconds as time units
Description: Absolute temporal logic operators before(n,time_unit)  and after(n,time_unit)  may result in an incorrect behavior during simulation and code generation if all of the following conditions are true: The chart executes with a fixed sample time step of size sample_time . n  is a numeric literal constant (e.g, 1.2). time_unit  is msec  or usec . round(n/sample_time)  is equal to 2^k - 1 for some integer k>0. n  is slightly larger than an integer multiple of sample_time . More precisely, this condition is satisfied if: time_unit ?is?msec ?and? ceil(n/(sample_time*1000) - (sample_time*1e-8)) != ceil(n/(sample_time*1000) - (sample_time* 1e-5))  time_unit ?is?usec ?and? ceil(n/  (sample_time*1000000) - (sample_time*1e-8) ) != ceil(n/(sample_time*1000000) -  ( sample_time* 1e-2) )    ?????????? where the arithmetic operations are performed according to IEEE 754 standard.  For example, when the sample time is 1 second,  the expression after(1000002.5, usec) will never be true. the expression before(1000002.5,?usec)?will be true at all time steps.
Workaround: Either:Change the constant threshold to an integer multiple of?sample_timeUse sec as the?time_unit

Summary: Incorrect execution coverage for referenced export-function model
Description: When an?export-function model ?is referenced from another model, such as in a model reference hierarchy or via a block diagram test harness, coverage analysis may report that the corresponding model block is missing execution coverage even if the referenced model has been executed. This might cause the Model block in the parent or harness model to be reported as having 0% execution coverage, even though the referenced export-function model shows greater than 0% coverage.?
Products: Simulink Check, Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage

Summary: Model Advisor check for an AUTOSAR adaptive model with message root I/O crashes MATLAB
Description: Model Advisor check for an AUTOSAR adaptive model crashes MATLAB if all of these conditions are true: The model contains a root Inport or Outport connected to a Message Receive or Message Send block. The root Inport or Outport is mapped to an event. Model advisor check  Check integer word length ?is enabled.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink produces incorrect result from atomic subsystem that outputs same signal multiple times with different initial values
Description: A Simulink model might produce a silent wrong answer when all the following conditions are true: The model has an atomic subsystem with multiple output ports. Two or more of the output ports have the same source but different initial value settings.? A Bus Creator block groups the signals from the two output ports into a virtual bus. Then, a Bus Selector block selects the two signals and feeds them to different destinations.
Workaround: Add a Signal Conversion block before one of the Outport blocks in the atomic subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unit Conversion block returns incorrect generated code for conversions with offsets and fixed-point data types with bias
Description: The Unit Conversion block might return incorrect answers for accelerator mode, rapid accelerator mode simulation, or code generation when the following conditions are true:The Output data type  parameter is set to Inherit: Inherit via back propagation , The input or output data type is a fixed-point data type with a bias, The unit conversion has an offset, for example, ˇăC  to ˇăF  or ˇăC  to K .
Workaround: Set the Output data type parameter of the Unit Conversion block to Inherit: Inherit via internal rule and insert a Data Type Conversion block after the Unit Conversion block.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Output of set operations with the 'rows' option might not be in sortrows order when NaNs are present
Description: The?union , intersect ,?setdiff , and?setxor ?operations with the?'rows' ?option might produce outputs that are not sorted in sortrows ?order when NaNs  are present.? This issue is likely to occur when all the following conditions are true: The two rows being compared both contain NaNs  in the same column. If the first condition is met, the result is a "tie" condition that is broken by comparing the values in the adjacent columns to determine which of the two rows should be placed above the other.  Such rows may be swapped in the output.? This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Products: Simulink Coder, MATLAB Coder, Embedded Coder

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing variant condition for data initialization in reusable subsystem
Description: Variant conditions associated with data could be missing when all the following conditions are met :Data has a initial condition associated with it, for example the initial condition of a delay block. The data is present inside a reusable subsystem. The data has a propagated preprocessor variant condition from Variant Source/Sink block.

Summary: Check for root Inports with missing properties incorrectly flags inherited sample time
Description: The Model Advisor check?Check for root Inports with missing properties  (Check ID:mathworks.hism.hisl_0024) incorrectly flags?inherited sample time? when? the only entity that makes the? model an export-function model is Simulink Function block.

Summary: Adding certain cDAQ modules in a particular order can result in an error
Description: Adding certain cDAQ modules to a Session or DataAcquisition in a particular order might result in an error when both these conditions are true: One of the devices does not support on-demand operations and others do. The device that supports on-demand operations is added after a  device that does not support on-demand operations.  For example, using the following modules:  NI cDAQ1Mod2 National Instruments NI 9223 (BNC)NI cDAQ1Mod4 National Instruments NI 9239 (BNC)NI cDAQ1Mod5 National Instruments NI 9244NI cDAQ1Mod6 National Instruments NI 9227 the following code can generate an error:  d = daq.getDevices();s = daq.createSession('ni');addAnalogInputChannel(s,'cDAQ1Mod2',[0,1,2,3],'Voltage');addAnalogInputChannel(s,'cDAQ1Mod4',[0,1,2,3],'Voltage');addAnalogInputChannel(s,'cDAQ1Mod5','ai0','Voltage');addAnalogInputChannel(s,'cDAQ1Mod6','ai0','Current'); %Error is on this line because this device supports on-demand acquisition .
Workaround: To avoid this issue, add the device that supports on-demand operations first. For example: addAnalogInputChannel(s, 'cDAQ1Mod6','ai0','Current');addAnalogInputChannel(s, 'cDAQ1Mod2',[0,1,2,3],'Voltage');addAnalogInputChannel(s, 'cDAQ1Mod4',[0,1,2,3],'Voltage');addAnalogInputChannel(s, 'cDAQ1Mod5','ai0','Voltage');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect Rte read function call generated for AUTOSAR model with bus ports and ErrorStatus inport
Description: An incorrect Rte  read function call is generated for anAUTOSAR model in which all  of the following conditions exist: A bus inport and signal inport are mapped to the sameAUTOSAR data element. The bus inport is mapped to AUTOSAR data access modeExplicitReceive . The signal inport is mapped to AUTOSAR data access modeErrorStatus .  The generated code stores the error status and the received data in the same variable, rather than different variables.

Summary: Incorrect error message with variants and mask workspace variables during Fast Restart mode
Description: Incorrect error message is shown when all the following conditions are true:The model uses Variant Subsystem and masked workspace variables in the variant choices. The active choice of the Variant Subsystem is switched in between two simulations. The second simulation uses Fast Restart mode.?  The error message Invalid Simulink Object name  is displayed for content in the previously active variant choice.
Workaround: Close and reopen the model in between simulations.

Summary: Ignored values for model diagnostic parameters?NonCurrentReleaseOperatingPointMsg and?OperatingPointInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg
Description: Setting the values for the model diagnostic parameters NonCurrentReleaseOperatingPointMsg  and?OperatingPointInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg ?has no effect on the behavior controlled by these parameters.
Workaround: Use the alternative command-line names for these parameters to change the model settings:For NonCurrentReleaseOperatingPointMsg?use SimStateOlderReleaseMsg.For OperatingPointInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg use SimStateInterfaceChecksumMismatchMsg.

Summary: polyspace-configure could not open options file
Description: Running?polyspace-configure ?on a build command results in this message after the build command is complete: ERROR: Could not open options file optionsFile   This error indicates that during compilation, a compiler options file was accessed using a relative path.? polyspace-configure ?fails to locate options files with a relative path. Failure to locate and read the options file can lead to the generated project being incomplete. For instance, some options or source files might be missing.
Workaround: If possible, in your build system files, change the options file path to an absolute one. This workaround is not always possible because the options files are often temporary files generated by the build tools.

Summary: ARXML import of enumeration data type for AUTOSAR adaptive software component does not create Simulink enumeration data type
Description: When importing ARXML that contains enumeration data types for an AUTOSAR adaptive software component, import does not create a Simulink enumeration data type. Instead, import creates data type objects based on the enumeration base type (for example, uint8).?
Workaround: Manually create enumerationdata types in Simulink. Identify enumerations in the ARXML and createthe corresponding enumeration types in Simulink.?

Summary: Reading string data in MDF-files might generate the error: Result buffer too small
Description: Although the ASAM MDF standard states that strings stored in MDF-files should be zero-terminated, in practice some tools generate files that do not comply.?When attempting to read strings that are not zero-terminated from MDF-files, the following error might occur: ? Result buffer too small

Summary: Generated VHDL code fails to compile when HDL RAM System blocks have initial value of data type?fixdt(0,1, 0)
Description: When the design has HDL RAM System block of fi(0, 1, 0) input data type and initial value specified, the generated VHDL code fails to compile. This erroneous behavior is specific to VHDL target language for HDL RAM's that support initial value.

Summary: Simulink Compiler does not support configuration references
Description: Simulink compiler cannot create standalone applications from models that use configuration references. Attempting to do so will produce an error: Warning: The following error was caught while executing 'onCleanup' class destructor:Error using buildRacTarget>locCleanupPostParameter update is not supported for a configuration reference. Update parameter in the configuration set that it references.?

Summary: Models exported from SerDes Designer app toSimulink? results in a hard crash for some combinations of Symbol Time andSamples per Symbol
Description: In the SerDes Desinger  app, if you set Symbol Time  to 32.25  ps and Samples per Symbol  to greater than 8 , then export the modelto Simulink?, the exported model experiences a hard crash for Simulink?.This is due to the improper storage of a parameter in the serdes.ChannelImpulse ?mask . The improper storage of the parameter leads to an operation that requestsa matrix of size much larger than the computer is physically able to handle andmakes the system crash.
Workaround: SetSamples per Symbol to 8 while designing the model in the SerDes Designer app.Once you export the model to Simulink?, you can change the Samples per Symbol parameter toyour desired value.

Summary: Generating code for Bus Creator with bus inputs can lead to assertion indicating Empty records are present
Description: When using buses in a Simulink model, an internal assertion might be generated if a bus composed of other buses is used. This is typically modeled by using a Bus Creator block with bus inputs. Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/createCGIR  Assertion failed:    matlab\src\cgir_hdl\dom_pir_core\recordlowercomponent.cpp:142:Empty records are present

Summary: Images from exportgraphics and copygraphics might be incorrect
Description: The graphics captured by the exportgraphics  or copygraphics  functions might be shifted to the upper-left corner of the image, or it might be clipped in when any of these conditions are true:  The Renderer  property of the figure is set to 'painters'   You call the exportgraphics  or copygraphics  function inside a parfor loop You start your MATLAB session with the -nodisplay  option?

Summary: The check for JMAAB?Check Stateflow transition appearance (mathworks.jmaab.db_0129)?displays an incorrect warning
Description: Model Advisor JMAAB check for  db_0129:?Check Stateflow transition appearance displays an incorrect warning ?? Following transition labels are not visually associated to the corresponding transition .?  For example, Case1: For a vertical transition, the transition label is long transition_label = variable1+variable2 +variable3 + variable4 +variable5 +variable6;  Case2: For a horizontal transition, the transition label is long transition_label = variable1+...  variable2 +...  variable3 +...   variable4 +....  variable5 +....  variable6;
Workaround: 1. Change the transition label(condition/action) variables to shorter names.action1 = a1+a2+a3+a4+a5+a6;2.Case 1: Split the action into multiple linestransition_label = variable1+...variable2 +...variable3 +...variable4 +....variable5 +....variable6;Case 2: Merge into a single line:transition_label = variable1+variable2 +variable3 + variable4 +variable5 +variable6;?

Summary: ARXML import for AUTOSAR adaptive software component ignores data types specified as STD-CPP-IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE of category TYPE_REFERENCE
Description: When importing ARXML foran AUTOSAR adaptive software component, import ignores data types specified asSTD-CPP-IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE  of category TYPE_REFERENCE . As a result, you might see errors when updating the diagram, validating AUTOSAR properties, or generating code due to the missing data types.

Summary: Error using rapid accelerator for referenced model using parameter with Identifier specified
Description: Code generation fails for a referenced model in rapid accelerator mode when the model uses a Simulink.Parameter  object that specifies a value for the Identifier  property.
Workaround: Simulate the model with normal or accelerator mode, or temporarily clear the Identifier property.

Summary: Lookup Table Optimizer will issue error "Undefined function 'minmax'" for FPGA target if Deep Learning Toolbox is not installed.?
Description: When using the Lookup Table Optimizer on a Simulink model with the target hardware set to ASIC/FPGA, if Deep Learning Toolbox is not installed, then the error "Undefined function 'minmax'" will occur. This error will occur when clicking the Next button to move from the Setup panel to the Create panel. This error will be experienced on your model only if all of the following are true: The commandget_param(bdroot,'ProdHWDeviceType') returns'ASIC/FPGA->ASIC/FPGA'  And the command?which -all minmax returns'minmax' not found
Workaround: Create a file named minmax.m that contains the following:function y = minmax(u)??? % minmax temporary workaround for Bug 2194166 with Lookup Table Optimizer??? %??? y = [min(u(:)), max(u(:))];end Place the directory containing the file?minmax.m?on your MATLAB path.For example, ????? addpath('C:\myLocalTools\bugWorkArounds\R2020aAndEarlier') Verify that the file is on your path by running this command:? ? ? which -all minmaxThe output should point to the minmax.m file that you just created. For the example location given above, the output would be????? C:\myLocalTools\bugWorkArounds\R2020aAndEarlier\minmax.m Test the function by calling ??? y = minmax(1:128)Verify that the output isy =???? 1?? 128?

Summary: Results export or import fails when custom criteria diagnostic contains a null character
Description: In the Custom Criteria section of a test case, if a diagnostic contains a null character (e.g., MATLAB char(0)), the following error occurs when importing or exporting the test results: DMR client's dictionary function threw std::exception '<unspecified file>(2): unexpected end of data'
Workaround: Do not use null characters in custom criteria diagnostics.

Summary: Error occurs when AliasType used in a C Function block with no external typedef is provided
Description: A parsing error occurs when an AliasType defined on either the: C Function block Symbol Specification table incoming input signal   without any external typedef defined in the header files of the C Function block custom code or in the?Configuration Parameters  > Simulation Target  > Header file  of the model.
Workaround: Specify the typedef in the custom block header file or include a file that has the typedef for the AliasType in the Configuration Parameters > Simulation Target > Header file.

Summary: AUTOSAR XML import fails with Unrecognized method, property, or field 'IsApplication' for class 'M3I.Object'
Description: AUTOSAR import fails when reading ARXML that contains fixed-axis BaseType s that use float  or single  primitive types.? The importer generates the following message: Error using arxml.importer/createComponentAsModel (line 115)Unrecognized method, property, or field 'IsApplication' for class 'M3I.Object'.

Summary: Simulink Code Inspector fails code inspection when alookup table object is passed as an argument to a referenced model
Description: WhenSimulink Code Inspector inspects a referenced model, code inspection fails if alookup table object is passed as an argumenttothe referenced model.

Summary: Running FPGA-in-the-Loop for ZCU111 board using HDL Workflow Advisor may result in an error
Description: In the step 1.1 of HDL Workflow Advisor, if you select "FPGA-in-the-Loop" as target workflow and "Xilinx Zynq UltraScale+ RFSoC ZCU111 Evaluation kit" as target platform, the following error occurs:? Error The FPGA device used in Xilinx Zynq Ultrascale+ RFSoC FPGA ZCU111 evaluation kit is Zynq Ultrascale+/ xczu28dr-ffvg1517-2-e/ / . ?This FPGA device is not supported by current synthesis tool Xilinx Vivado 2019. 2 ˇ­
Workaround: Perform the following steps:Close HDL Workflow AdvisorStart fpgaBoardManager and clone the ZCU111 entry as shown in the attachment "clone.png".?Restart HDL Workflow Advisor (select not to restore previous run info if prompted), and pick your cloned version of the board as shown in the attachment "HDLWA.png" .

Summary: AUTOSAR mappings for blocks within referenced subsystem do not persist when model is reopened
Description: For an AUTOSAR software component model configured for the Classic Platform, the AUTOSAR mappings for Rate Transition, Simulink Function, or Function Caller blocks that are located within a referenced subsystem do not persist when the model is closed and reopened.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when opening MATLAB System block
Description: Opening the Block Parameters dialog box for a MATLAB System block might crash MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calls to graphical functions or MATLAB functions without return values within atomic subcharts might produce incorrect results
Description: Calls to graphical functions or MATLAB functions might produce incorrect results when all of the following conditions are true:? The called function is a graphical or MATLAB function within an atomic subchart. The called function does not return any values.? The function is called from another graphical or MATLAB function in the parent of the atomic subchart.?  ?This issue impacts both simulation results and the code generated from Stateflow charts.?
Workaround: Add an output argument to the graphical or MATLAB function definition.?

Summary: OptimizationExpressions with multiple power or mpower operators can return incorrect results
Description: When creating an OptimizationExpression  in the Problem-Based workflow, using multiple successive power  or mpower  operators can return an incorrect result. For example: x = optimvar('x');  p = x^2;  expr = p + p^2   yields: expr =?  ? Nonlinear OptimizationExpression  ? ? (x.^4 + x.^4)   The correct expression is (x.^2 + x.^4) .
Workaround: Avoid writing successive power or mpower operations on the same expression.For the example above, instead write:expr = p + x^4;orp = x^2;q = x^4;expr = p + q;

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Customized step function prototype?with custom storage class on Root-level Outport might generate incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated when all the following conditions are met:Model has customized step function prototype. Step function is configured to have return argument. Root-level Outport corresponding to this return argument has custom storage class.  The generated code might have uninitialized return value in the step function.

Summary: ARXML export from an AUTOSAR adaptive model generates incorrect tag,IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE, for Enumeration data types and references insideDataTypeMaps
Description: Whenexporting ARXML from an AUTOSAR adaptive model, theAUTOSAR classic tag, IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE , incorrectly generates for Enumeration data types and references insideDataTypeMaps. The correct tag that should be generated is the AUTOSAR adaptivetag, STD-CPP-IMPLEMENTATION-DATA-TYPE .
Workaround: Manuallyedit the exported ARXML to fix the incorrect tags.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with Stateflow chart
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a Stateflow chart. The Stateflow chart has an exported function defined inside it. The exported function uses an input or output variable from a signal which is not defined in the arguments of the exported function. The exported function is reused in another Stateflow chart?in the same model.? In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the?Optimize block operation order in the generated code? parameter is set to?Improved code execution speed.
Workaround: In Configuration Parameters dialog box, set?Optimize block operation order in the generated code??to?None.

Summary: Simulink Requirements exported ReqIF file has wrong attribute definition references
Description: The ReqIF file has incorrect attribute definition references which cause an incorrect mapping of the data when the ReqIF file is exported from Simulink Requirements and imported in a third-party requirements management tool. In exported ReqIF file, the <ATTRIBUTE-VALUE> elements within the <SPECIFICATION> elements are referencing the <ATTRIBUTE-DEFINITION> elements of a <SPEC-OBJECT-TYPE>. According to the specification, the value element should reference the definition in the <SPECIFICATION-TYPE>.

Summary: XCP-based external mode fails for binaries with debug symbols for empty compilation units
Description: XCP based external mode uses debug symbols to compute the addresses of global variables.Some compilers (including ARM Compiler 6.12 and Clang 3.8) generate DWARF debug information for empty compilation units. When parsing such debug information, the DWARF parser used by external mode may fail, and the following error may be displayed on the command line: std::exception
Workaround: If an empty source file is being compiled and linked, a fix would be to either remove it from the build or insert a dummy global variable.

Summary: Windows user names with non-English characters can cause issues when opening some MATLAB features
Description: MATLAB installed on a Windows system with a user name containing non-English characters might run into issues and be unable to open the Add-Ons Explorer, Add-Ons Manager, App Designer, and others.MATLAB stores preferences in the user's home folder on Windows, for example, C:\Users\username \AppData\Roaming\MathWorks\MATLAB\R2020a . When the username  is composed of non-English characters, MATLAB runs into issues while creating and modifying files that are needed during startup from the folder where preferences are stored.
Workaround: To avoid these issues:When installing MATLAB, do not use a username that contains non-English characters.?Set the environment variable MATLAB_PREFDIR to a preferences folder that does not have any non-English characters in the path.

Summary: Requirements links lost in round trip workflow when exporting with ReqIF
Description: When a requirement set is imported from a third party tool using a ReqIF file format, any links added in Simulink Requirements will fail to export unless a link of the same type existed in the original ReqIF file. For example, if you import a requirements document from Jama, and some items had "Related to" links between them, and then you add more "Related to" links in a Simulink Requirements session and export the updated Requirement Set to a ReqIF file, the added links will export successfully. However, if the original import did not have any "Related to" links, the newly added links will not be present in the exported content. This Import > Modify > Export workflow using ReqIF is referred to as a round trip workflow.

Summary: The Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) block may not calculate the same NTU factor when the two-phase fluid flow is reversed.
Description: When the NTU factor is small, the factor calculated by the Condenser Evaporator (2P-MA) block does not have the same value when the two-phase fluid flows from port B1  to port A1  as from port A1  to port B1 .
Workaround: Ensure that the two-phase fluid always flows from port A1 to port?B1.

Summary: Internal assertion during HDL code generation for Matrix Multiply block with a row vector output and sharing or streaming optimization
Description: When the DUT has a Matrix Multiply block with row vector output inside the hierarchy and sharing or streaming optimizations are applied, it leads to an internal assertion during HDL code generation. ?CGIR assertion 'In mightyCast: Assertion failed: MatrixType::isa(aDstType)' failed in 'b:\ matlab\ src\ cgir_support\ dom_core\ analysis\ range\ rangevaluefactory. cpp:1675'?

Summary: Subsystem Reference block's port properties are lost on reloading the model or if the contents of the Subysystem Reference block are refreshed
Description: Port properties (such as tag, description, signal info etc.) specified for a Subsystem Reference block are lost if you either reload the model or refresh the Subsystem Reference block's contents. A refresh happens if you modify and save the contents of the block. These properties are also lost for a Subsystem block when it is converted to a Subsystem Reference block.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b Update 1, R2021a

Summary: ARXML import for AUTOSARadaptive software component errors out when an ApplicationDataType is mapped to anAUTOSAR Adaptive Platform type
Description: When importing ARXML that contains an ApplicationDataType mapped to an AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform type, import errors out with a message similar to the following: Error using Simulink. metamodel. arplatform. ArxmlImporter/ readAssertion failed: Invalid property Type for element of type Simulink. metamodel. types. FixedPoint in file / tmp/somepath/mMaxShortNames_autosar_adaptive/ mMaxShortNames_interface. arxml line 275 at arxml/ ArxmlReadTransformer4p0. cpp:13512?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Tunable parameters in non-inlined S-function might lead to incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code might be generated if all these conditions are true:?Model uses a non-inlined S-function with parameters. Model configuration parameter Default parameter behavior  is set to Tunable . Model parameters are default mapped to a structured storage class.

Summary: Using VISA-related functionality on macOS Catalina 10.15.x might crash MATLAB
Description: MATLAB might crash on macOS Catalina 10.15.x if the Instrument Control Toolbox Support Package for National Instruments VISA and ICP Interfaces is installed. The crash can occur when using any of following VISA-related functions, apps, and Simulink blocks: visa , instrhwinfo , tmtool , Query Instrument  block, and?To Instrument  block.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the following steps to re-install NI drivers to your macOS Catalina 10.15.x machine.?1. If NI-VISA 19.0 is already installed, uninstall it.2. Download and install the following NI drivers in the given order only:a.?NI-488.2 ver 19.5 for macOSb.?NI-VISA ver 19.0 for macOS?3. Restart your machine, as directed by the NI installer.?

Summary: Interactions in the Live Editor become sluggish when displaying a large number of variables
Description: The Live Editor becomes sluggish over time if one or more live scripts are open and displaying a large number of variables. For example, interactions such as rerunning a live script take longer after some time.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing Data Store Memory and MATLAB System blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Data Store Memory block and a MATLAB System block. The read and writes to the Data Store Memory block occur inside the MATLAB System block.

Summary: Code generation error for AUTOSAR model that uses AUTOSAR.Parameter in Simulink.Variant object
Description: When an AUTOSAR model uses a Simulink.Variant  object, for which the condition references an AUTOSAR.Parameter ?object,? generating code might result in the following error: The call to autosar_make_rtw_hook,?during the after_tlc hook generated the following error:    Unrecognized function or variable 'Rte_SysCon_<SystemConstantName>'.

Summary: The Dependency Analyzer is stuck when opened or running an analysis
Description: If a project exists in the root of the current file system, the Dependency Analyzer is stuck in the following cases: When you try to open the Dependency Analyzer, MATLAB stays busy and the application does not show. The Dependency Analyzer is already open. You try to run an analysis, MATLAB stays busy and the analysis is stuck at the "Analyzing: Finishing analysis" stage.
Workaround: To continue the loading process, in the Command Window, press ctrl+c (or control+c on Mac OS X). The Dependency Analyzer will start working again. Some of the decoration may not be correct.To resolve the issue without upgrading, delete the project from the root directory or move it to a different location. Alternatively, on Windows, create a network map for a folder that contains the file or project you want to analyze. Make sure it does not contain a project in the top level root. Run the analysis from inside the newly created network drive.

Summary: Unexpected error message in the 4-Way Directional Valve (IL) block
Description: In the 4-Way Directional Valve (IL) block, when the?Area characteristics  parameter is setto?Identical for all flow paths  and the?Orificeparameterization  parameter is set to?Linear ¨C Area vs. spool travel, right-clicking on the block and selecting Fluids > Plot valvecharacteristics  erroneously generates the following message: Reference to non-existent field 'smoothing_factor_prompt.'
Workaround: To work around this issue, make either of these settings:Set the Area characteristics parameter to?Different for each flowpath, orSet the?Orifice parameterization parameter to?Tabulateddata ¨C Area vs. spool travel or Tabulated data ¨C Volumetric flow ratevs. spool travel and pressure drop.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results for models containing model reference hierarchy using?Simulink.LookupTableObject?as model argument with inconsistent LUT object struct order settings between parent and child models
Description: Incorrect simulation results for a model with these settings: Top-level model contains: Certain setting for?LUT object struct order for explicit value specification  and?LUT object struct order for even spacing specification  parameters (either  Size,Breakpoints,Table  or  Size,Table,Breakpoints )  A? Model reference block with these characteristics: Refers to a model containing an n-D Lookup Table block with these configurations Different setting from parent model in either?LUT object struct order for explicit value specification? or?LUT object struct order for even spacing specification ?configuration parameters. n-D Lookup Table block contains a Simulink.LookupTableObject  with these characteristics Data Specification ?parameter set to?Lookup table object .  Lookup table object configured to be passed as an argument to the Model reference block.      This is an invalid combination and will cause errors.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulation results may be incorrect in the?Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator mode for models with Assignment blocks inside a For Iterator subsystem
Description: Simulation?might produce incorrect results when a model meets the following conditions:Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator simulation mode is chosen. An Assignment block is inside a For Iterator subsystem.? The Assignment block has Y0  port enabled. The propagated sample time for the Assignment block is [inf 0] (that is, a constant sample time).

Summary: Variable change of IC block?parameter ignored for referenced model running in accelerator mode with fast restart
Description: For a referenced model simulated in accelerator mode with fast restart, Simulink incorrectly ignores a change to the Initial value  parameter of the IC block between simulations.

Summary: Unexpected error in the 3-Way Directional Valve (IL) block and Cartridge Valve Insert (IL) block
Description: The following blocks might erroneously generate the message: "Each column of Volumetric flow rate table, q(ds,dp) must be arranged in ascending or descending order." In the 3-Way DirectionalValve (IL) block, this might occur when the?Area characteristics  parameter is setto?Identical for all flow paths , and the?Orificeparameterization  parameter is set to?Tabulated data ¨C Volumetric flowrate vs. spool travel and pressure drop . In the Cartridge Valve Insert (IL) block, this might occur when the?Valve seat specification ?parameter is set to Custom ?and the?Orifice parameterization? parameter is set to?Tabulated data ¨C Volumetric flow rate vs. spool travel and pressure drop .
Workaround: To work around this issue, do one of the following:?Set the?Pressure drop vector, dp?parameter so that all vector elements are equal to or greater than 0 when the?Orifice parameterization parameter is?Tabulated data - Volumetric flow rate vs. control member position and pressure drop.Set the?Orifice parameterization parameter to?Linear¨C Area vs. spool travel or Tabulated data ¨C Area vs. spool travel.?For the 3-Way Directional Valve (IL) block, set the?Area characteristics?parameter to?Different for each flow path.

Summary: macOS users might receive a warning for GPU support in a future MATLAB release, without explicitly attempting to use a GPU
Description: macOS users might receive the following warning message without explicitly working on a GPU:? Support for GPU acceleration with Parallel Computing Toolbox on macOS will be removed in a future MATLAB release.  For example, users of the Deep Learning Toolbox might receive the warning message when calling trainNetwork   without specifying the? 'ExecutionEnvironment'  option or when setting it to 'auto' .?
Workaround: If you do not intend to use a GPU, you can ignore the warning message. To suppress the warning message, execute the following command:warning off parallel:gpu:device:MacOSGPUSupportToBeRemoved;?If you are using the trainNetwork function from Deep Learning Toolbox and you do not intend to use a GPU, you can set the?'ExecutionEnvironment'?option to 'cpu' to avoid the warning.

Summary: Subsystem code reuse regression in R2019a update 5 and R2019b for subsystems containing different units
Description: If two subsystems have these characteristics: They are identical with respect to their interface and contents. The only difference is the 'Unit' property of the signals feeding the subsystems. The subsystems do not contain any Unit Conversion blocks along the signal path from the input signal in (2).  The functions for the two subsystems might not be not reused on the generated code.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Linking errors when using code generation with Computer Vision Toolbox OpenCV functions for non-MATLAB targets
Description: Computer Vision Toolbox OpenCV functions do not recognizeproduct hardware types for code generation. This error results in the softwareusing MATLAB instead of non-MATLAB as thetarget, which produces library linking errors. Specifically, the generated code errors out during the build? process with library dependencies.
Workaround: To avoid the linking errors due to MATLAB built OpenCV library files, follow these steps:Locate the makefile in the generated code archive,? (<function name>_rtw.mk).Identify the variable that links the OpenCV binaries,? (LIBS = $(MATLAB_ROOT)/bin/glnxa64/libopencv_calib3d.so.4.2 ...) .Modify the variable that? links the OpenCV binaries built on the target (Eg: LIBS = `pkg-config --libs opencv` or LIBS = /usr/local/lib), with the location of the Opencv libs for the correct target.Build using the modified makefile on the target.?

Summary: Error during compilation of C++ file: stl_tree.h, line 2142: error: no instance of constructor
Description: When verifying a C++ application using option -compiler gnu7.x  or option -cpp-version cpp14 , the following error can appear during the Polyspace compilation phase: .../stl_tree.h, line 2142: error: no instance of constructor "std::pair<_T1, _T2>::pair [with _T1=std::_Rb_tree_node_base *, _T2=std::_Rb_tree_node_base *]" matches the argument list...

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing a Selector block that connects to a Unit Delay block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: The model contains a Selector block that directly connects to the input of a Unit Delay block. The input to the Selector block branches to another block aside from the Selector block.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect initial value for block output inside reusable subsystem
Description: The initial value for block output in a reusable subsystem may be incorrectly computed under all these conditions:?The model configuration parameter?Buffer for reusable subsystems  is selected. The reusable subsystem's inport is multi-rate (For example, a Mux block combines a Inf sample time signal and a discrete sample time signal feeds into the subsystem's inport).
