Summary: Spectrum Analyzer block and dsp.SpectrumAnalyzer show wrong samples per update if overlap percentage is not 0%
Description: Spectrum Analyzer block and dsp.SpectrumAnalyzer show wrong samples per update if both these conditions are true: Overlap % is not 0 Spectrum estimation method is 'Filter bank'?

Summary: Changes in Connection Port block?or Simscape Bus block connections across subsystem boundaries might be ignored when made between simulation runs
Description: After simulating a model containing Simscape blocks inside of a subsystem, if you disconnect Connection Port blocks and reconnect them to different blocks within the subsystem, the change in connections might be ignored on subsequent runs. Similarly, changing Simscape Bus connections by modifying Simscape Bus port identifiers when the Simscape Bus line crosses a subsystem boundary can also be ignored on subsequent runs.?
Workaround: After changing the Connection Port of Subsystem Bus connections, save the model and?close it. Then reopen the model to have Simscape recognize the changes in connectivity.

Summary: Slow comparison and merge for Simulink models with a large number of signal line changes.
Description: When comparing and merging Simulink models that have thousands of signal line changes, the comparison can take a long time to complete.

Summary: Incorrect results when using sbiofit with nlinfit and proportional?or combined error model
Description: sbiofit  returns incorrect results when?using nlinfit ?as the estimation function and proportional  or combined  as the error model.?nlinfit ?returns residuals that are weighted, but?sbiofit ?does not correctly account for this weighting.

Summary: Invalid simulation output in failed test case result
Description: If a test case fails due to an error in model simulation, then the simulation output from the previous model simulation might appear in the test case results section.

Summary: Model update hangs at "Configuring subsystem output ports"
Description: If a model has a large virtual bus with an uninterrupted feedback loop, updating the model might hang at "Configuring subsystem output ports" and the memory usage might increase when the model is compiling.
Workaround: Interrupt the feedback loop with a Unit Delay block or convert the large virtual bus into several smaller buses.

Summary: Rapid accelerator simulation fails on Windows with generic error
Description: A rapid accelerator simulation might fail on Windows with the following error message if you have previous MATLAB version DLLs on your system path: Error occurred while simulating the model model_name in rapid accelerator mode. Rerun the simulation in normal mode to diagnose this error.
Workaround: Clean up your system path of any MATLAB-related items before installing R2020a or invoke the attached winraccelworkaround.p each time you start MATLAB, such as in a startup.m file.

Summary: Estimated motor parameters are inaccurate if the binary files are manually downloaded to the target hardware?
Description: Motor Control Blockset provides binary files (*.out) that you can use as part of an alternate workflow for motor parameter estimation. If you manually download and run these files on the target hardware, and then perform motor parameter estimation, the estimated parameters may be inaccurate.?

Summary: Discover Clusters dialog doesn't open in MATLAB Online Server
Description: In MATLAB Online Server, the Discover Clusters dialog does not open when you select Parallel  > Discover Clusters... . The Discover Clusters dialog also does not open when you select Discover Clusters  from the Cluster Profile Manager dialog.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB Function block that performs comparisons with constant value in LHS generates incorrect HDL code with?MATLAB Datapath architecture
Description: The generated code for a Simulink model is incorrect if the following conditions are met: The model contains a MATLAB function block with Architecture  set to MATLAB Datapath  The MATLAB code contains constant expressions with the constant value on the left hand side of the relational operator. This occurs for the relational operators <, <=, >,  and >= .  For example, the following expression generates incorrect code: uint8(1) >= x ?
Workaround: The constant expression can appear on the right hand side of a comparison operation. For example:x <= uint8(1)

Summary: sldvisactive may incorrectly return false when translating a model with model blocks
Description: sldvisactive  may incorrectly return false  when Simulink Design Verifier is run on a model that contains one or more model  blocks.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 6, R2020b

Summary: Xilinx System Generator and HDL Coder integration broken due to Vivado incompatible upgrades
Description: The workflow described in?this document ?is broken in R2019a release and generates this error: Error: Evaluation of elaborate function on class hdldefaults.XilinxSystemGeneratorSubsystem failed with the error message: Xilinx:Environment: Unknown path type identifier.?
Workaround: An updated version of this example is available in R2020a. To see the example, refer to?this document.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Motor currents measured using example host models might be inaccurate
Description: The example host models (mcb_*host*.slx ) in Motor Control Blockset contain various signals that you can use to measure current from the motor. These measured values might deviate by about 10% when compared with the actual values from the motor.?

Summary: AUTOSAR adaptive model fails to generate code when root inport message data type is an array of fixed-point data
Description: AUTOSAR adaptive model fails to generate code when the following conditions are true: The model contains at least one root inport. The message payload for at least one root inport is an n-dimension matrix of fixed-point data.   Generating code for such a model results in the following error: CGIR assertion 'In constructGetNewSamplesCallbackFcnBody: Assertion failed: msgDataVar' failed in 'rtwcg_utils/message_functions/MsgAccessFcnTxAdpt1903Schema.cpp:192'

Summary: AUTOSAR adaptive model fails to generate code when root inport message data type is an enumeration
Description: Code generation for an AUTOSAR adaptive model throws an error if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a root inport connected to a Message Receive block. The root inport is mapped to an event. The data type for the message payload is an enumeration.

Summary: On macOS and certain distributions of Linux such as CentOS, MATLAB Web App Server might stop rendering icons for the web apps on the apps home page
Description: When MATLAB Web App Server has been running for a while on macOS and certain distributions of Linux such as CentOS, the server might stop rendering icons for the web apps on the apps home page.?? On macOS and Linux systems, the server stores some temporary data in the file system's /tmp  folder. When that data gets cleaned up, you might notice the app icons on the apps home page missing. On macOS systems, ?you might notice this happening after 3 days, and on Linux distributions such as CentOS, ?you might notice it after 10 days. This issue is not present on Debain distributions of Linux.
Workaround: You might need to restart web apps services (mw-webapps-launcher-R2020a, mw-webapps-R2020a) either using webapps-stop & webapps-start commands or using operating system tools ("systemctl stop" on Linux and "launchctl unload" on macOS) to resolve the issue, or tweak how often /tmp directory is being cleaned up.

Summary: mldivide or mrdivide with parpool('threads') can generate inconsistent warnings for some matrices
Description: When running a parfor  loop on a parallel pool using the parpool('threads')  mode, a call to mldivide  from within this loop might not be thread-safe. Specifically, calling x = A\b  with a sparse real symmetric indefinite matrix A  might generate incorrect warnings about the condition number of A . The output?x  is still correct in this case. This bug also affects the mrdivide  function x = b/A .

Summary: SimBiology projects created with unconfigured math lines in releases before R2019b error when loaded in R2019b or later
Description: SimBiology projects created prior to R2019b may use math lines to create reference lines in plots. ?The SimBiology Model Analyzer  app, introduced in R2019b, converts these math lines into observables. ?Loading a project containing math lines with invalid configurations into the app in R2019b or later generates the following error: Unable to convert result object to new format for SimBiology Model Analyzer: Unrecognized function or variable 'expressionResults'.
Workaround: Open the project in R2019a, remove the unconfigured math lines and resave before opening in R2019b.

Summary: Automated Driving Toolbox examples for monoCamera and birdsEyeView classes do not display expected results when installed with Vehicle Dynamics Blockset.
Description: The results returned by these examples are incorrect when the Vehicle Dynamics Blockset is installed.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Reading an MDF channel containing only empty strings might generate the error:?Invalid argument
Description: When reading MDF data from a channel that contains only empty strings, the following error might occur: ? Invalid argument

Summary: Example "Deployed Application of Parameter Estimation" errors when trying to compile the model because compiler does not find the model.
Description: In the example "Deployed Application of Parameter Estimation," MATLAB throws an error when running mcc  on file?parameterEstimationSdoBattery_run.m ?because the compiler is unable to find the model sdoBattery.slx.
Workaround: Find the model sdoBattery.slx in the following directory:>> fullfile(matlabroot,'toolbox','sldo','sldodemos','estim','sdoBattery.slx')Copy the model into the directory where you are working with the deployment example, i.e. the same directory with parameterEstimationSdoBattery_run.m and then run the mcc command.

Summary: Packaging a custom toolbox fails to include additional software entries
Description: When you package a custom toolbox that contains additional software, the packaging might fail and produce this error:  File '<toolboxName>.mltbx' was not found  If the toolbox has already been packaged and the mltbx ?files exists, an additional packaging process completes without an error. However, when the toolbox is installed on another machine, the additional software is not installed.

Summary: Cannot set?Number of Newton's iterations or Extrapolation order parameters with odeN solver and implicit integration method
Description: An unexpected error occurs when trying to set the?Number of Newton's iterations or? ?Extrapolation order  parameters in the Solver pane of a model's Configuration Parameters when the following conditions are met: Solver  is set to odeN (Nonadaptive) Integration Method  is set to either ode14x (extrapolation)  or ode1be (Backward Euler)
Workaround: At the MATLAB command line, use set_param?to set the?NumberNewtonIterations?and?ExtrapolationOrder?parameter values.?You do not need to change the solver.orIn the top model's Configuration Parameters - Solver pane:??Set the?Solver?in the Solver selection section to?ode14x (extrapolation).in the Solver details section, set the desired values for the?Number of Newton's iterations?and?Extrapolation order?parameters.?In the Solver?selection section, change the Solver back to odeN (Nonadaptive).

Summary: When restoring a SerDes Designer App session from a saved .mat file, references to workspace variables for the CTLE GPZ or custom channel impulse responses are broken and cause the app to hang.
Description: When restoring a SerDes Designer App session from a saved .mat file, references to workspace variables for the CTLE GPZ or custom channel impulse responses are broken and cause the app to hang.

Summary: MATLAB might error out during code generation when the same identifier is used for a top model and a system within it
Description: When the same identifier is to be used for a variable in the code generated from a top model and another system within it such as a Stateflow Chart or reusable subsystem, MATLAB might error out during code generation with this message: ?CGIR assertion 'globalTailBinder->prevTop == 0'

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results for some simulation modes for models containing model reference hierarchy?with particular LUT object struct order settings using Simulink.LookupTableObject as model argument
Description: Incorrect simulation results for a model with these settings: Top-level model contains: Model configuration parameter LUT object struct order for explicit value specification  set to 'Size,Table,Breakpoints'  A? Model reference block with these characteristics: Refers to a model containing an n-D Lookup Table block with these configurations LUT object struct order for explicit value specification ?set to? 'Size,Table,Breakpoints'  n-D Lookup Table block contains a Simulink.LookupTableObject with these characteristics Data Specification  parameter set to Lookup table object  Lookup table object BreakpointsSpecification? parameter set to  Explict values  Lookup table object configured to be passed as an argument to the Model reference block      Incorrect simulation results might also occur when one of these is true for the model: The simulation mode  of the model is set to Normal ?and the simulation mode?of the Model reference block is set to Accelerator .    The simulation mode?of the model is set to Accelerator   and the simulation mode  of the Model reference block is set to Software-in-the-loop (SIL)
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 3, R2020b

Summary: MATLAB? might crash while generating code for a model containing a Bitwise operator block on a Mac platform
Description: MATLAB might crash while generating code on a Mac platform for a model that meets the following conditions:The model a contains Bitwise operator block. The model configuration parameter Operator to represent Bitwise and Logical operator blocks  is set to a value other than Logical operator .

Summary: MATLAB crashes when generating code for a model that receives a message with an Enumeration or bus data type
Description: MATLAB crashes when generating code for a model with these conditions:  The model has a root Inport connected to a Message Receive block. The Inport data type is either an Enumeration or bus. The model is not a referenced model.

Summary: Incorrect Function not called check when using compiler pragma?inline=never
Description: Polyspace can incorrectly show a function as uncalled when the pragma?inline=never ?is used. For instance, if you force non-inlining of the function myfunc() ?for optimization purposes as follows: ?_Pragma("inline=never") int myfunc(unsigned char uvar) { ... }  The Polyspace analysis ignores the function definition. The analysis: Calls a stub function instead of the actual function Places a Function not called  check on the body of?myfunc()
Workaround: For Polyspace analysis, do not use the non-inlining feature.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover Server, Polyspace Code Prover

Summary: Using smexportonshape function to export an Onshape assembly to Simscape Multibody returns a blank MATLAB web browser
Description: If you are using smexportonshape  function to export Onshape assembly to Simscape Multibody, it will return a blank MATLAB web browser.

Summary: niftiwrite function creates NIfTI files with incorrect geometry information??
Description: ? The niftiwrite  function produces a NIfTI file (.nii ) that contains incorrect geometry information when the TransformName   field within the struct produced by niftiinfo  is set to 'Qform' . The image data is misregistered when used with other medical imaging software. With the Qform transformation, niftiwrite  calculates the quaternion parameters b and d incorrectly.

Summary: AUTOSAR createEnumeration function does not group imported BITFIELD_TEXTTABLE elements by short label
Description: After importing BITFIELD_TEXTTABLE elements from ARXML, if the imported elements are ordered by short-label, calling the createEnumeration  function generates Simulink enumerations with incorrect elements.

Summary: FOC example that uses Simscape Electrical converter throws 'Sample time mismatch' error during simulation
Description: The mcb_ee_pmsm_foc ?example model provides three options for inverter selection. If you select either Simscape Electrical 3 phase converter ?or Simscape Electrical Modular Multilevel converter , the model throws a Sample time mismatch ?error during simulation. This model may also display incorrect results when simulated with above two options, due to other issues related to plant model configuration.  However, if you select the option that does not use Simscape Electrical converter (Motor Control Blockset average inverter ), this example works as expected.

Summary: Standalone code generated for fftn or ifftn?produces incorrect output when the input array X is two-dimensional and and the size vector sz?has length greater than two
Description: Standalone code (static library/dynamically linked library/SIL/PIL) generated for the?fftn ?or the? ifftn ?function produces incorrect output when the input arguments satisfy all of these conditions: The input array X  is two-dimensional The length of the size vector sz ?(that contains the lengths of transform dimensions) is three or greater than three ?The size vector sz ?has the pattern? [1 a b c...] ?where:  The second element  a  is any positive integer greater than  2  The subsequent elements ( b, c,...)  are any nonzero positive integers?    For example, define a function foo  that calls fftn:  function y = foo(x) %#codegeny = fftn(x,[1 3 4]);end Generate a SIL MEX function for foo  by running these commands: cfg = coder.config('lib');cfg.VerificationMode = "SIL";codegen foo -args {ones(5,5)} -config cfg Execute the generated SIL MEX function foo_sil  with the two-dimensional run-time input ones(5,5) : ysil = foo_sil(ones(5,5))?  Execute the original MATLAB function foo  with the same input: yml = foo(ones(5,5))?  Observe that the two outputs ysil  and yml  are different. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when?linearizing Interpolation Using Prelookup block with lookup table object
Description: MATLAB might crash when linearizing the Interpolation Using Prelookup  block when the following conditions are true: ?The TableSpecification  parameter is set to Lookup table object  ?The algorithm used for linearization is set to blockbyblock.  ?For example via MATLAB command: linOption = linearizeOptions('LinearizationAlgorithm','blockbyblock');   ?A linear approximation of an?Interpolation Using Prelookup? block in model?mdl ?is obtained. For example by executing MATLAB command: linearize   (mdl  ,blockpath ,linOption)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Blocks that use elapsed sample time (such as Discrete Time Integrator or Weighted Sample Time Math) might produce wrong answers if placed inside an aperiodic partition
Description: Blocks that use elapsed sample time (such as Discrete Time Integrator or Weighted Sample Time Math) might behave incorrectly under the following conditions: The block is inside a partitioned atomic subsystem The subsystem has the?Schedule as ? parameter set to?? Aperiodic partition ?  Simulink might produce incorrect numerical results in Normal, Accelerator, and Rapid Accelerator simulation?modes as well as in generated code.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when opening a file with an extremely long expression.
Description: An extremely long expression is composed of hundreds of sub-expressions. These expressions are usually from machine-generated code. MATLAB might crash when you either: Use the editor to open a file that contains an extremely long?expression. Open a directory that contains a file with an extremely long?expression.

Summary: Requirements Editor might become frozen after deleting multiple objects
Description: Requirements Editor might become frozen after requirements or links are deleted with multi-selection with the following error message in a pop up dialog:  Dot index is not supported for variables of this type.   ?The error occurs when the following conditions are met: Loaded requirements or links has comments Multiple objects selected in the requirements browser are deleted or objects are deleted by API operation The objects being deleted with comments were never displayed in the Property Inspector.
Workaround: For deleting requirements or links, use single selection and delete them one by one using the requirements browser.Workaround for saving data?If the error happens, unsaved data can be saved by the following script:reqSet = slreq.find('type','ReqSet','Dirty',true);arrayfun(@(x) save(x), reqSet);linkSet = slreq.find('type','LinkSet','Dirty',true);arrayfun(@(x) save(x), linkSet);The following command will clear requirements and links from memory and close the frozen requirements editor:slreq.clear?

Summary: MATLAB renders the project root incorrectly and throws NullPointerException when creating a project from a Simulink Model Manifest
Description: When you create a project from a Simulink Model Manifest, the New Project dialog renders the project root incorrectly and throws a NullPointerException in the Command Window.
Workaround: The Model Manifest Report automatically attempts creating a project using the project root reported in Files to include section. If that is acceptable, you can safely ignore the issue. The project creation proceeds successfully.If a different folder is required, create a project from a folder: In MATLAB, on the Home tab, select New > Project > From Folder and specify the right folder. The project includes all files in the folder, not only the ones listed in the manifest report.

Summary: writeall on TransformedDatastore throws an error when the underlying datastore is a CombinedDatastore
Description: Calling the writeall  function on a TransformedDatastore  can generate an error if the underlying datastore is a CombinedDatastore  with multiple underlying datastores.? For example: imds = imageDatastore(repmat("peppers.png", 10, 1)); cds = combine(imds, imds); tds = cds.transform(@(img) img{1});tds.writeall("outputimages2", "OutputFormat", "png")Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values. Error in matlab.io.datastore.FileWritable/writeSerial (line 31)         if cachedFilename == "" || ~currentFileIndexComparator(dsCopy, numFilesRead) Error in matlab.io.datastore.FileWritable/writeParallel (line 41)             writeSerial(ds, location, compressStr, nvStruct); Error in matlab.io.datastore.TransformedDatastore/writeall (line 379)                writeParallel(ds, location, files, nvStruct);

Summary: Unable to compile a Stateflow chart (.sfx) file using MATLAB Compiler
Description: When you compile a Stateflow chart (.sfx) file using the mcc command or the apps in MATLAB Compiler, your compilation will fail with an error message.

Summary: Simulink PLC coder throws a typecast assertion during code generation
Description: Simulink PLC coder does not support multiword datatypes during code generation.? During code generation, PLC coder may throw the following assertion:? CGIR assertion 'In assertingCast: Failed to cast CG::Type (PKN2CG4TypeE) from |unsigned| to |N2CG11PointerTypeE|'  The assertion indicates the presence of multiword datatypes. For example, the Sqrt block may generate multiword related datatypes.
Workaround: For the Sqrt block, under Data Types > Intermediate Results , select "Inherit from output".

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data mismatch between Simulink and HDL simulation for partly serial architecture of Discrete FIR Filter HDL Optimized block
Description: A data mismatch between Simulink and HDL simulation can happen for partly serial architecture of Discrete FIR Filter HDL Optimized block and for the dsp.HDLFIRFilter  System object in either of these cases: When casting the coefficients to the specified data type produces different values depending on the Saturate on integer overflow  parameter. ?When the coefficients are only partly symmetric/anti-symmetric.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Design having RAM block under masked subsystems with HDL optimizations applied generates unusable generated model
Description: When the design has RAM block present under a masked subsystem and parameters of RAM block such as address width or data width are initialized from the mask initialization pane of the masked subsystem,?HDL Coder generates unusable generated model when HDL optimizations are applied inside the masked subsystem.

Summary: Default Critical Reynolds number used in the Hydraulics (Isothermal) Liquid 4-Way Directional Valve
Description: In the Hydraulics (Isothermal) Liquid 4-Way Directional Valve block, when Area characteristics? is set to?Different for each flow path ?and Laminar transition specification ?is set to?Reynolds number , the default Critical Reynolds number  value of 12 is applied to pathways P-B, A-T, and B-T, while the Critical Reynolds number  value specified in the block dialog box is applied to pathway P-A.
Workaround: Build the valve from its components, as shown in the block?Structural Component Diagram.

Summary: Model Transformer tool generates an error while refactoring a model to eliminate Data Store Memory blocks
Description: The Model Transformer tool generates an error saying Unexpected Exception caught,  when the following conditions are met: Model refactoring tries to eliminate Data Store Memory blocks. The model has a subsystem reading from a Data Store Memory block that?has been written to outside the subsystem.

Summary: MATLAB crashes in Test Extension workflow when the configured parameters result in dead logic
Description: Simulink Design Verifier supports extension of testcases when the parameters in the model are configured for analysis.?However, MATLAB crashes when the configured parameters result in dead logic, with the following crash log:?Abnormal termination:  Access violation    Snippet from the Stack Trace (from fault):  [ 0] 0x0000000147913ed7 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll+05127895 CG::BoolValue::BoolValue+00000039  [ 1] 0x000000014792995a C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll+05216602 CG::Value::createDirectReferenceValueRef+00002138  [ 2] 0x00000001479289c3 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll+05212611 CG::Value::createDirectReferenceValueCRef+00000051  [ 3] 0x0000000147921366 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\cg_ir.dll+05182310 CG::StaticMatrixValueCG::elementReference+00000198  [ 4] 0x00000001d30f7744 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00620356 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00198932  [ 5] 0x00000001d30f6e17 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00618007 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00196583  [ 6] 0x00000001d30f72ad C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00619181 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00197757  [ 7] 0x00000001d30f75c5 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00619973 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00198549  [ 8] 0x00000001d30fe9fa C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00649722 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00228298  [ 9] 0x00000001d30ff5e4 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00652772 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00231348  [ 10] 0x00000001d30c9a60 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+00432736 DvTransform::fcnTransformer+00011312  [ 11] 0x00000001d333d2e3 C:\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\sldv.dll+03003107 DV::StructExplosionCb::updateInterface+00451491

Summary: Update diagram might crash for model reference hierarchy in accelerator mode using data dictionaries and enumerated types
Description: Update diagram might crash for model reference hierarchy running in accelerator mode if all the following conditions are satisfied: Enumerated types are defined in a Simulink data dictionary or as Simulink classes. For example, Enum.sldd  or Enum.m . Design data in a data dictionary refers to these enumerated types. Design data could be defined in: Enum.sldd . A data dictionary referring to Enum.sldd . For example, Param.sldd .  One or more submodels in a model reference hierarchy: Simulate in accelerator mode. Use design data defined in Enum.sldd  or Param.sldd .  You perform an update diagram on the top model with the submodels closed.
Workaround: Keep submodels that meet the crash criteria open during update diagram on the top model.

Summary: Header file name for enumeration data types is not validated against the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform standard
Description: The AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform standard prescribes that header file names for data types conform to the following pattern: impl_type_<symbol>.h. The AUTOSAR Blockset does not enforce this pattern for header file names for enumeration data types.?
Workaround: Write a Model Advisor check to find enumeration data types used in the model and verify the header file naming convention to match the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform standard.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Block reduction might result in incorrect simulation results for a model containing a Signal Conversion block
Description: The simulation results might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:? A Signal Conversion block is inside an enabled subsystem and it connects directly to a Root Outport block. The input signal for the Signal Conversion block has a rate of infinity.  The block reduction optimization could reduce the Signal Conversion block resulting in a constant, incorrect initial value from the enabled subsystem in simulation.
Workaround: Exclude the signal conversion block from the reduction by selecting the option Exclude this block from block reduction optimization.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect cuboid rotation angle returned by the bboxwarp function
Description: The bboxwarp  function returns incorrect rotation angles for cuboids. The function incorrectly returns counter-clockwise rotations (with respect to the positive direction of the rotation axis), for transforms with positive rotation angles. The correct rotation should be clockwise with respect to the positive direction of the rotation axis.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, you can negate the rotation angle returned by bboxwarp.?

Summary: Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block only loads scenes in the Unreal Engine??project?/Game/Maps folder
Description: In the Simulation 3D Scene Configuration block, if you set Scene source  to Unreal Executable ,? the block only loads scenes that are in the?Unreal Engine??project?/Game/Maps ?folder. If you set Scene  to a scene path that is not in /Game/Maps , the block does not load the scene.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for a model containing directly connected Bus Creator blocks
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains two Bus Creator blocks and two Bus Selector blocks. The outputs of the Bus Creator blocks are of the same type. One Bus Selector block chooses an element from the first Bus Creator block and inputs it to the second Bus Creator block. The other Bus Selector block chooses an element from the second Bus Creator block. The index of this element is higher than the index of the element chosen by the first Bus Selector block.  In the generated code, the same local variable gets incorrectly reused for both Bus Creator block outputs causing an incorrect value for an element of the second Bus Creator block output. Here is an example of the incorrect code pattern:? Bus1.a=1 Bus1.b=2 Bus1.c=3?  Bus1.a=5??Bus1.b=20??Bus1.c=Bus1.b?  In the incorrect code, the three elements of Bus1  are overwritten, so that the third element gets an incorrect value of 20 .?

Summary: MATLAB Datapath architecture for MATLAB Function blocks incorrectly throws error about unsupported functions using complex integer?types
Description: When using complex integer types in a MATLAB Function block that has HDL architecture set to?MATLAB Datapath,  HDL code generation might ? throw confusing error messages about unsupported functions when they may actually be supported. For instance, an error can be seen if a zeros  function is used with a 'like ' declaration of a complex integer variable:v = zeros(1, 1, 'like', var); ?
Workaround: Change the integer type to an equivalent fi type.

Summary: Incorrect indenting and keyword matching when using?end?to indicate last array index
Description: If you use the?end ?keyword to indicate the last index of an array within a block of code, indenting within the block of code is incorrect. In addition, MATLAB indicates incorrect language keyword pairs within the block of code. For example, this code produces incorrect indenting and keyword matching:? if 1 ? ? x = 1:10;? disp(x(1:end));end?

Summary: In batch mode, MATLAB incorrectly opens a dialog box.
Description: MATLAB can hang by incorrectly opening a modal dialog box in batch mode.  When you open MATLAB in batch mode, modal dialog boxes should not be allowed. However, when you close a model in the Simulink Editor (in batch mode), the "Save model before closing?" dialog box appears. This dialog box waits for the user to click Yes, No or Cancel.

Summary: Simulink PLC Coder does not support the Simulink.LookupTable, Simulink.Breakpoint, and Simulink.DualScaledParameter objects for code generation
Description: The Simulink.Lookuptable , Simulink.Breakpoint , and Simulink.DualScaled  parameter objects use the coderInfo  property for tunability purposes, that might result in the generated code containing variables linked to these objects having no initial values.? For this reason, Simulink PLC coder does not support these objects for code generation.?

Summary: Fixed-Point Designer > Matrix Operations?Library blocks throw confusing errors for MATLAB Function blocks during HDL code generation with floating-point types
Description: When generating HDL code for Fixed-Point Designer library blocks that perform QR decomposition, various failure messages might be seen around MATLAB Function blocks when the block is configured to use floating-point types. Floating point is not supported for these blocks using HDL code generation.

Summary: Error message with Function-Call Subsystem added as slice component using addSliceComponent
Description: Adding a Function-Call Subsystem as a slice component using  addSliceComponent? API throws an error which says  Component is a function call and cannot be sliced
Products: Requirements Toolbox, Simulink Coverage, Simulink Check

Summary: An error occurs when a Simulink Subsystem Harness contains a block and subsystem with identical names
Description: When a Simulink Subsystem Harness contains a block and subsystem with identical names  a n error occurs in the post-simulation process   during coverage measurement or accumulation  by the Test Manager.  ?  ? MATLAB stack trace:> In cvi.TopModelCov.updateModelHandles>update_susbsys_handles (line 54)? In cvi.TopModelCov.updateModelHandles (line 34)? In SlCov.CoverageAPI.loadCoverage (line 139)? In cvload? In stm.internal.Coverage.loadSizeOne? In stm.internal.Coverage.add? In stm.internal.Coverage.addAndReturnFile???: stm.internal.Coverage.loadSizeOne

Summary: Baseline test with no signals selected in an iteration might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If you run a baseline test and one of its iterations does not have any signals selected for comparison, MATLAB might crash.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you simulate a model with dashboard blocks in external mode on a Simulink Real-Time target
Description: MATLAB crashes when you simulate a Simulink model in external mode on a Simulink Real-Time target if the model includes dashboard blocks that are connected to signals or parameters that have been deleted?or cannot be found. The crash produces this stack trace: [  0] 0x00007ff8517f5373 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\sl_hmi.dll+01069939 sl_hmi::binding::getBoundPortIndicesForElement+00000211? [  1] 0x00007ff8518bc2f3 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2020a\bin\win64\sl_hmi.dll+01884915 sl_hmi::WidgetRegistrationInfo::operator=+00200579?

Summary: SIL simulation with Microsoft Visual C++ compiler option /TP produces compiler error
Description: The??Microsoft Visual C++ compiler option /TP  specifies that its filename argument is a C source file, even if the filename does not have a .c  extension. If you run a software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulation and use the compiler option /TP , the simulation produces a compiler error.

Summary: Saving a model with Subsystem Reference having Simulink Function block fails
Description: A model with Subsystem Reference containing Simulink Function block may fail save operation with the below error message:  An error occured during a callback while saving "C:\localPath\Subsystem.slx" The previously version of this file (if any) has not been changed.  All the below conditions must be true for unsuccessful save of the edited top model:  The Subsystem Reference is edited in the context of top model  The Subsystem Reference have a Simulink Function block inside a Stateflow chart
Workaround: Directly modify and save the Subsystem file?

Summary: Induction Machine Squirrel Cage and Induction Machine Wound Rotor blocks do not include the?magnetic flux due to the leakage inductance in the saturation characteristic
Description: If saturation is enabled for the Induction Machine Squirrel Cage or Induction Machine Wound Rotor blocks, the characteristic does not include the?magnetic flux due to the leakage inductance. When saturation is enabled, the saturated voltage is not calculated correctly. The saturation characteristic obtained from simulation is different to the saturation data provided in the block parameterization.

Summary: Dataflow execution domains?might produce incorrect results when?AdvancedOptControl parameter is set to -SLCI
Description: Normal mode simulation of dataflow execution domains might produce incorrect results when the AdvancedOptControl  parameter is set to -SLCI .
Workaround: Remove -SLCI from the AdvancedOptControl parameter.? For example, at the command-line enter:>> set_param(bdroot, 'AdvancedOptControl', '');

Summary: Using file enum (derived from Simulink.IntEnumType) generates error on code generation
Description: During code generation, ara-generator is expected to generate implementation header files for all the data types used in the model. Since enum data type is not handled in ara-generator, this results in build failure.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Output variables not updated for sub-function block calls related to initialization
Description: Output variables are usually updated in SS_STEP/SS_UPDATE  case statements. In R2017a, Simulink PLC Coder stopped updating the output variables for function block calls related to initialization. For example, for the function block call: i0_IfActionSubsystem(ssMethodType := SS_INITIALIZE,  In1 := 1. 0);  b := i0_IfActionSubsystem. Out1; ? is not generated based on the assumption that?there will be corresponding SS_UPDATE/SS_OUTPUT call . Updating the outputs for function block calls related to initialization requires the caller variables to be updated as well, leading to special cases where generating?b := i0_IfActionSubsystem. Out1  might cause wrong answers.?

Summary: State-space parameter extraction fails in Extract Equations task of Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor though Check switched linear task might pass for unsupported Simscape elements
Description: In Simscape to HDL Workflow Advisor, a Simscape model containing the following unsupported blocks will incorrectly pass in the?Check switched linear  task:? Events Delays  Enabled Runtime Parameters Mode Charts  However, the Extract Equations  step will fail with the following error message: ? ? Unable to extract state-space parameters from model.

Summary: Storage class for data objects in the model workspace not honored for models with AUTOSAR target
Description: For models configured to use the system target file autosar.tlc , if you specify a storage class for a data object in the model workspace directly instead of by using the AUTOSAR Code Mappings Editor, the storage class specification is silently ignored by the code generator.??
Workaround: For data objects in the model workspace, specify storage classes by using the AUTOSAR Code Mappings Editor.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated C code for divisions with floor or ceil rounding modes might be incorrect
Description: When all the following conditions are true, the generated C code for divisions might return incorrect results: There is a signed division by constant or signed modulo by constant The division rounds to 'Floor'  or 'Ceil'  (rounding is not specified on modulo)? The generated code contains functions with names that are a variant of the following (where the number following the? s ?is? 8 ,? 16 ,? 32 , or? 64 ) :? div_nde_s32_ceiling ? div_nde_s32_floor  Inside the above function definitions, the following code sequence exists:? (boolean_T)(numerator % denominator)?

Summary: Embedded Coder fails to generate correct code from a Simulink Code Inspector compatible model if it defines instance parameters
Description: When a model that is configured to be compatible with Simulink Code Inspector defines instance parameters, Embedded Coder generates code that fails to compile successfully.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model?containing a bus data type across a reusable atomic subsystem
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:?? A bus data type is specified on the input and output signals of a reusable atomic subsystem. In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, the?Eliminate superfluous local variables (Expression folding) ?parameter is selected.?  In the generated code, one or more temporary local variables might be incorrectly eliminated.
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, clear the?Eliminate superfluous local variables (Expression folding)?parameter.

Summary: Error message generated for reals in Stateflow?Chart does not show location of unsupported code when Native Floating Point is used
Description: HDL Coder generates an error in presence of unsupported reals used in Stateflow?charts. However, the error message does not show the location of unsupported floating-point code. This can make fixing the error difficult especially for large models.

Summary: Some model hierarchies might throw an incorrect error when simulated
Description: Simulating a model hierarchy returns an incorrect error when all of the following conditions apply: The model hierarchy has at least three levels of referenced models. Referenced models have the configuration setting Propagate all signal labels out of the model ?selected. In a mid-level model, an output signal of a Model block has a label. The output signal passes through a Goto block and a From block then connects to an output port.  The error is: Computed interface information must match saved interface information for referenced model '<model name>'.?  ?Update diagram and resave the model '<model name>'.   For model hierarchies that meet the conditions, updating the diagram and saving the model does not fix the error.
Workaround: Identify any labeled output signals of a Model block that pass through a Goto block and a From block then connect to an output port.For each of these signals, right-click the signal line that connects to the output port, then select Properties. In the dialog box, select Show propagated signals.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Changing value of global variable used in non-tunable block parameter does not trigger rebuild of model reference code
Description: In a referenced model, if a variable in the base workspace or a data dictionary is used in a non-tunable block parameter (for example, Sample time ), changing the value of the variable does not automatically cause the code for the referenced model to be regenerated. This affects accelerated simulation code and production code.
Workaround: Use one of these alternatives:Define the variable as a Simulink.Parameter object with non-Auto storage class.>> MyVariable = Simulink.Parameter(MyVariable);>> MyVariable.CoderInfo.StorageClass = 'ExportedGlobal';Simulate referenced models in normal mode instead of accelerator mode.Delete the generated files to force them to be regenerated.

Summary: On machines with AMD processors, MATLAB can sometimes crash during exit.
Description: When running MATLAB on a machine with an AMD processor, MATLAB runs without any issue. However, during exit, MATLAB can sometimes crash.
Workaround: Set the environment variable MATLAB_DISABLE_CPU_TYPE to any value before launching MATLAB.

Summary: HDL Coder generates ambiguous error for design having bus element port as input to model reference
Description: When a design has a? Model block that references another model, and has bus element port as input, HDL Coder generates ambiguous error as:   'Undefined function 'Type' for input arguments of type 'handle. handle''.
Workaround: Use bus port instead of bus element port as input to the Model Reference block for successful code generation.

Summary: Model workspace variables cannot be tuned using?SimulationInput?with Fast Restart
Description: In Fast Restart simulation mode, model workspace variables do not assume the values specified with SimulationInput , causing unexpected simulation results. This happens when you pass?SimulationInput  to the initial run of model in Fast Restart mode and pass the parameter values as SimulationInput  variables from the model workspace.?
Workaround: Avoid passing SimulationInput to the initial run of model in Fast Restart mode. Instead, run the first simulation using the variables specified in the model workspace. For subsequent runs?in Fast Restart mode, you can use?SimulationInput?to specify a value for model workspace variables.?

Summary: App containing tab group does not resize properly?when AutoResizeChildren?property is set to 'on'
Description: If an app contains one or more tab groups and the AutoResizeChildren  property is set to 'on', then resizing the app can result in an unexpected layout.?For example, some components might be larger or smaller than expected, or some components might be clipped. The first resize might work as expected, but subsequent resizes might result in an unexpected layout.
Workaround: Set the Resize?property of the Figure?object to 'off' so that the app does not resize.If you are creating the app in AppDesigner:Select the uifigureIn the Inspector, go to the Position section and uncheck ResizeIf you are creating the app programmatically outside AppDesigner:f.Resize = 'off';where f is the handle to the Figure object.

Summary: systemIdentification app is not supported on MATLAB Online and should not launch
Description: systemIdentification app can be launched on MATLAB Online but using it to manage data or identify models throws errors.

Summary: Generated code may produce functions whose names conflict with C/C++ standard library I/O functions like fclose
Description: This issue affects MATLAB Function Blocks and MATLAB System Blocks in Simulink, and Stateflow charts. The generated code may produce functions whose names conflict with C I/O function names like fclose . When this happens the generated code fails to build with a C compiler error like: ?vrReadFrame.c:49:13: error: conflicting types for ˇ®fcloseˇŻ??static void fclose(real_T fileID);?? ? ? ? ? ? ?^~~~~~  In file included from vrReadFrame.h:47:0,?? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?from vrReadFrame.c:21:?/usr/include/stdio.h:239:12: note: previous declaration of ˇ®fcloseˇŻ was here??extern int fclose (FILE *__stream);?
Workaround: Add the conflicting function names to the model configuration parameter Reserved names(ReservedNameArray).

Summary: Bullet points not imported correctly from DOORS 9
Description: When performing direct import of requirements?from a DOORS module, and when Object Text for some items has bullet points, and the RichText option is enabled, Simulink Requirements fails to display bullet points. Instead, the Description area in the UI displays "Bullet point" text.? When examined using command-line API, the Description value contains image tags like this:

Summary: A model with string data with non-Auto custom storage class can generate code that does not compile
Description: String data with any of the following custom storage classes might generate code that does not compile: Volatile  ExportToFile  ImportFromFile  FileScope  Localizable  Struct  Reusable      The noncompilable code contains an incomplete character literal, such as the following: MyTestStruct.A[7] = '  For R2018b, only Update 5 and later are affected by this issue. For R2019a, only Update 5 and later are affected by this issue.?
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: Links to imported DOORS Next items not listed in browser popup window on DOORS Next side
Description: The Simulink Requirements ?widget in the IBM DOORS Next (previously known as IBM Rational DOORS Next Generation, sometimes referred to as DNG) interface allows users to Query Links from SL  (Simulink), but the displayed table of links only includes direct links from Simulink to DNG items, and does not include links from Simulink objects to imported DNG items in Simulink Requirements sets.

Summary: The change in enabled status of Proof Objective is not considered while rerunning property proving analysis
Description: Simulink Design Verifier fails to consider the change in enabled  status of a Proof Objective  in following scenario:  1. RebuildModelRepresentation  option is set to IfChangeIsDetected   2. Simulink Design Verifier generates replacement model during compatibility check  3. R ebuilding of model representation for Property proving analysis is triggered by : Changing  enabled   status of any of the Proof Objective  blocks without making any changes to model or Design Verifier options.
Workaround: Modify value of RebuildModelRepresentation option to Always

Summary: Clicking links from?serialport?read?warning messages returns an error
Description: Clicking the?serialport Read Warnings  ? links in the read  and readline  warning ?message for the?serialport ?interface ?might return the following error: Error using helpUtils.csh.helpviewMLFactory/checkForMapFile (line 32)  Specified map file does not exist  Error in helpview (line 213)  ? ? help_path = factory.checkForMapFile(mapfilename, topic_id);

Summary: Reaction expression in SimBiology models are not updated to use compartment-qualified species names after copying a compartment into the model
Description: Reactions referencing species by names in a one-compartment SimBiology model are not updated to use species names that are qualified by their compartment name. As a result, the resolution of the species in the reaction might be ambiguous.
Workaround: Update references to species names in reactions to names that are qualified with their compartment names. For example:radiodecayModel = sbmlimport('radiodecay');   decayReaction =  radiodecayModel.Reaction;m = sbiomodel('source model');comp = addcompartment(m, 'another compartment');addspecies(comp, 'x');copyobj(comp, radiodecayModel); disp(decayReaction.Reaction); % update references to species names to use names that are qualified with their compartment namedecayReaction.Reaction = 'unnamed.x -> unnamed.z';

Summary: MATLAB might crash while starting up on MAC
Description: MATLAB might occasionally crash while starting up on a MAC. The stack trace might include the the following line:   chromium embedded framework:base::CommandLine::InitFromArgv

Summary: Executing command-line scripts results in a "Unable to read the server info file" error
Description: When your system restarts and you execute command-line scripts for MATLAB Web App Server, you get the following error message: "Unable to read the server info file" . Despite the error message, the server remains operational and the web apps home page continues to be accessible.? The issue occurs due to corruption of the the file server.info  located in UserStorageRoot .
Workaround: To resolve this issue, restart the following services associated with MATLAB Web App Server using operating system commands:mw-webapps-launcher-R2020amw-webapps-R2020aWindows(Admin) Command Shell: SC STOP <service_name>Linux(sudo) Terminal: systemctl stop <service_name>?macOS(sudo) Terminal:?launchctl unload /Library/LaunchDaemons/com.mathworks.<service_name>.plist?After that you should be able to use webapps-start command to start MATLAB Web App Server again.?

Summary: Incorrect warning with Check use of default variants(mathworks.maab.na_0036) for?Label?variant control mode
Description: Model Advisor MAAB check for  na_0036 : Default variant  reports  No Default variant  for Variant Subsystem using Label  variant control mode.?  This warning message is incorrect as default variant is required only for Variant Subsystems using Expression  variant control mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Runtime error not reported when switch expression is non-finite valued
Description: When generating code for a program containing a switch statement, MATLAB Coder requires that the value of the switch expression is representable by the C type int . This is enforced through runtime checks in the generated code. When generating code for a MATLAB program containing: A double or single precision switch expression The expression is multiplied by a literal 0  The code generator might omit the necessary runtime check on the switch expression. This can lead to incorrect runtime behavior when the switch expression is non-finite valued. A simple example can be seen below in example_2172393 function y = example_2172393(x)   switch(x*0)      case 0         y = 11;      case 1         y = 22;      case 2         y = 33;      otherwise         y = 44     endend Generating code for this function with a double input argument and executing with a value of nan  or inf  should result in a runtime error.? >> codegen example_2172393 -args {0}>> example_2172393_mex(inf)Expected a value representable in the C type 'int'.  Found -nan instead.Error in example_2172393 (line 2)   switch(x*0) When this issue occurs, a runtime error is not thrown. Instead the generated code behaves as if the switch expression is valued as 0 >> example_2172393_mex(inf)ans =     11? See also Bug Report ID 2204299 ?

Summary: Code generated from model configured for the AUTOSAR Adaptive Platform does not honor function interface configuration settings for Function Caller blocks
Description: Codegenerated from model configured for an AUTOSAR adaptive system target does not honorthe Configure C/C++ Function Interface dialog box settings for Function Callerblocks. The generated code remains the same and does not reflect changes to the settings.

Summary: Memory leak in'inpaintCoherent' function
Description: When you call the inpaintCoherent function from within a loop, the MATLAB memory usage keeps increasing. The leaked memory is not released after the execution of the program.

Summary: The Simulink Design Verifier generated harness model does not simulate with design model having Out Bus Elements of heterogeneous types
Description: If the design model has Out Bus Elements  of the same bus but, with mixed data types, Design verifier generated harness model will throw errors on simulation. You may notice a sample error message as below:? Input port cannot accept a virtual bus of mixed data types. 'Input Port 1' of 'model_harness/Out1' expects a signal with unique data type. However, it is driven by a virtual bus with elements of differing data types. ?One solution is to convert the virtual bus to a nonvirtual bus by placing a Signal Conversion block prior to the input port, with its 'Output' parameter set to 'Nonvirtual bus'. ?Alternatively, if this is an input port of a function-call subsystem, consider checking the 'Latch input for feedback signals of function-call subsystem outputs' option. This message is related to a hidden Outport block.

Summary: Requirements Editor becomes slow when opening requirement sets with large number of incoming links
Description: When loading a Simulink Requirements requirement set with a large number of incoming links, especially spread over a large number of link set (.slmx) files, the Requirements Editor might become slow to respond and refresh.

Summary: Name conflict between RS232 blocks and some DSP filters causes a compile error when building a model containing both
Description: The RS232 drivers for the legacy serial ports use an identifier for the Interrupt Identification Register (IIR) that conflicts with a variable name for some Infinite Impulse Response (IIR) filters. ?If both of these are used in the same model, there is a compile error when the model is built for the target.

Summary: Starting a standalone application on macOS systems by double-clicking does not generate error messages
Description: When you start a standalone application created using MATLAB Compiler on macOS systems by double-clicking the app, any potential errors are not displayed in pop-up dialog boxes.

Summary: matlabFunction with enabled optimization is very slow
Description: Using matlabFunction on some huge input with optimization enabled, which is the default, can have the effect that the code generation is very slow.? It can happen that it takes hours or days for generating the MATLAB code.
Workaround: The problem will only occur if the optimization is turned on, which is the default. To avoid the problem, turn the optimization off by specifying the name-value pair 'Optimize', false.

Summary: System Design Description report generation fails when including links in linked libraries and dictionaries
Description: When a Simulink model is using a Data Dictionary, and the "Include links in linked libraries and dictionaries" ?preference is?enabled for the Requirements Report, the System Design Description report generation might fail. This error message is displayed when generating the report: A simulation of block diagram <DIAGRAM NAME>  is currently running. Consider using the cached search method option.

Summary: The Signal Labeler app's Import from Workspace?option allows importing signals with time information after importing labeledSignalSet objects in samples
Description: In?Signal Labeler  app, if you import a labeledSignalSet  object in samples using the?Import from Workspace ?option, you can then also import a signal with time information. The app should disable that capability in the import dialog box.

Summary: Error during integration of AUTOSAR Adaptive artifacts from an existing model into another one
Description: The following error is thrown when you set Instance Identifier  for a port in AUTOSAR Dictionary.  "Error occurred when evaluating apply callback"?  This issue only arises when a model for which the mapping was already created is integrated to a new model for which the mapping was not created.

Summary: AUTOSAR Component Quick Start for adaptive models does not honor specified component package
Description: When creating an adaptive component configuration, AUTOSAR Component Quick Start does not honor the Component package  path setting. The component is placed in the default package.
Workaround: Move the component to the desired package manually using the AUTOSAR Dictionary or the autosar.api.getAUTOSARProperties function.

Summary: MATLAB crash during connection phase of Simulink external mode simulation
Description: If your model contains a bus signal with variable-size elements and the signal is used in a conditional execution context, for example, in an Enabled Subsystem block, then MATLAB mightcrash during the connection phase of a Simulink external mode simulation.

Summary: Possible crash or error message 'Unexpected exception caught' when requesting static code metrics?
Description: If you select the model configuration parameter Generate static code metrics ?and your model misses an include file, you might encounter the following when building the model: Windows: Crash Linux or Mac: Message about exception in the Diagnostic Viewer saying:??Unexpected exception (basic_string::_M_construct null not valid) caught   You might see this error, for instance, if a custom source file in the model #include  i s a non-existent header file. Custom source files are specified using the configuration parameter Source file .

Summary: Using add_block?with subsystem references in libraries might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when you save your model if you previously used?add_block ?to add a subsystem reference?from a library and the subsystem contains a Stateflow block. For example: add_block(subsystemReferenceContainingSFFromLibrary,someDestination)   MATLAB might also crash if you use add_block? with a normal subsystem containing a Stateflow block and you set 'CopyOption ' to 'nolink ': add_block(subsystemConstainingSFFromLibrary,destination,'CopyOption','nolink')

Summary: HDL code generation error for Matrix Multiply block using DotProductStrategy other than fully parallel?with floating-point types
Description: HDL code generation for a Simulink model throws an error if the following conditions are met:? The model contains a Product block configured to perform matrix multiplication. That is, the Multiplication  block parameter is set to Matrix(*) . The block has the HDL block property DotProductStrategy  set to a value other than fully parallel . There are three or more inputs to the block that are floating-point signals. The Floating Point IP Library  is set to Native Floating Point.

Summary: MATLAB Datapath architecture of MATLAB Function block generates internal error when coder pragma is used as an input to a function
Description: Internal errors about unknown type or logical conditions might be generated during HDL code generation for MATLAB Datapath  architecture of the MATLAB Function block ? if pragmas are used as inputs to functions. An example is shown below. y = max( coder.const(2), x); ?
Workaround: Assign the pragma argument to an intermediate variable and pass that into the function instead.

Summary: Simulink models might fail to open
Description: A Simulink model might sometimes fail to open with an error similar to the following:    Error using open (line 146), File 'modelName.slx' does not contain a valid Simulink model in SLX format. Could not open source package. When this problem occurs, the size of the SLX seems normal and it can be opened in a Zip utility such as 7-Zip, however is cannot be opened in Simulink. This problem can occur with any of the newer MATLAB or Simulink file formats, but it is most likely to occur in larger files such as SLX, SLXC, MLTBX, CTF, and MLAPPINSTALL files.

Summary: Crash when calling codegen on coder.const of disp of coder.PrimitiveType
Description: Calling codegen  on a function which calls coder.const  on the disp  method of a coder.PrimitiveType  object, can cause MATLAB to crash. For example, given the function:  function foo     x = coder.const(feval("coder.typeof", 1));     coder.const(@disp, x);  end Running the following command causes MATLAB to crash:   codegen foo

Summary: Incorrect results for the Relational operator block when the block input is a nonscalar complex signal
Description: Incorrect objective status is reported by Simulink Design Verifier during the test-generation analysis when the objective belongs to the Relational operator  block whose input is a nonscalar signal of complex type.

Summary: MATLAB Production Server main process might crash under heavy load when the server has HTTPS enabled and?--num-threads?is set to greater than one
Description: The MATLAB Production Server main process might crash under heavy load when all of the following are true: The server has HTTPS (SSL/TLS) support enabled. The server configuration property  --num-threads  is set to a value greater than one.
Workaround: To work around this issue, implement one of the following:Disable HTTPS (SSL/TLS) support in MATLAB Production Server's main_config file, bind to local addresses only by setting the --http property to localhost:PORT, and implement SSL/TLS endpoint termination using a reverse proxy such as NGINX.Set --num-threads to 1.?If this setting is insufficient to support the throughput requirements of your MATLAB Production Server workload, then create and start multiple server instances, and use a reverse proxy load balancer such as NGINX or HAproxy.

Summary: Custom messages do not show up in rosmsg list when the message definition contains two different headers
Description: If you have?twodifferent headers in the same custom message, such as:? Headermsg_header  Headerimage_header??  The custom message does not show up when calling?rosmsg list . The parser for reading the message definition expects the message header to be named 'header ' .
Workaround: As a workaround, use std_msgs/Header instead of Header:std_msgs/Header msg_headerstd_msgs/Header image_header?
Products: ROS Toolbox, Navigation Toolbox, Robotics System Toolbox

Summary: In the SoC Blockset Packet-Based ADS-B Transceiver example, running the soc_ADSB model in Accelerator mode generates an error on Windows
Description: In the Packet-Based ADS-B Transceiver example, running the soc_ADSB model in Accelerator mode generates the following error on Windows systems. "The specified module could not be found"  Accelerator mode simulation uses compiled DLLs in the AD9361 Transmitter and Receiver Blocks for Windows. When the model starts running in Accelerator mode, the simulation looks for the DLLs and throws the error message when the DLLs are not found. Normal mode simulation on Windows systems is not impacted by this issue.
Workaround: Please add the path for the AD9361 DLLs to the Windows environment path. The following commands extract the required paths for the DLLs and associated LIBs from the installed MATLAB.>> fullfile(matlabshared.supportpkg.getSupportPackageRoot, 'toolbox', 'shared', 'sdr', 'sdrplug', 'sdrpluginbase', 'host', 'derived', 'bin', 'win64')>> fullfile(matlabshared.supportpkg.getSupportPackageRoot, 'toolbox', 'shared', 'sdr', 'sdrplug', 'sdrpluginbase', 'host', 'derived', 'lib', 'win64')?

Summary: Incorrect error message with sldvlogsignals when its?first argument?refers to a model with bus element port blocks
Description: sldvlogsignals  can take two arguments, where the first argument can be a 'model block' or a 'harness model' and the second is testcase index.? You may encounter an error message?Dot indexing is not supported  when the first argument in the above scenario happens to be a model block and that model block has In Bus Element  port blocks.

Summary: JSON data returned in response to a RESTful client that requests no data
Description: MATLAB Production Server incorrectly returns exactly one JSON element in the response from the server when both the following conditions occur: To execute a MATLAB function deployed to the server, the client uses RESTful API and JSON, and sets the request parameter?nargout ?to 0. The MATLAB function deployed to the server returns at least one data element.

Summary: SIL/PIL simulation fails if model contains Reset Function block and model step function uses function prototype control
Description: If a model contains a Reset Function block and you provide function prototype control that uses a return argument, a software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation produces a compiler error for the generated file, xil_interface.c . The message indicates that the generated function accesses a return argument that does not exist.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Uninitialized variable used when loop control variable passed to a function as an in-place argument
Description: For MATLAB code that meets the following conditions:? ?A for loop with the number of iterations below the specified LoopUnrollThreshold  value  The loop control variable is passed to a function as an in-place argument (that is, a = foo(a))   MATLAB Coder might incorrectly generate code for the unrolled loop using an uninitialized variable for the in-place argument corresponding to the loop control variable. This can lead to unpredictable behavior at runtime as the function called in the loop reads an uninitialized variable. For example,? function example_2166816()    for k = uint8(2):uint8(3)k = foo(k);    endendfunction io = foo(io)    coder.inline('never');    global out;    out = io;end Produces the following code which reads an uninitialized variable k void example_2166816(void){  unsigned char k;  if (!isInitialized_example_2166816) {    example_2166816_initialize();  }  foo(&k);  foo(&k);} The correct code can be seen below:? void example_2166816(void){  unsigned char k;  if (!isInitialized_example_2166816) {    example_2166816_initialize();  }  k = 2U;  foo(&k);  k = 3U;  foo(&k);}

Summary: Code generation may fail when combining?datetime arrays created by the NaT function with other datetime functionality supported for code generation
Description: Calling codegen  on a function may fail when both of these conditions are met: The function uses datetime  values created by the NaT  function The function also uses other supported datetime  functionality for code generation  When code generation fails in this manner, MATLAB? displays the error: ??? The left-hand side has been constrained to be non-complex, but the right-hand side is complex. Tocorrect this problem, make the right-hand side real using the function REAL, or change the initialassignment to the left-hand side variable to be a complex value using the COMPLEX function. For example, calling codegen  on the following function results in the above error. function x = exampleFun()   x = [NaT NaT NaT];   x(2) = datetime(2015,1,1,10,0,0);end
Workaround: Instead of creating NaT values with the NaT function, use the datetime constructor with NaN inputs.% Create a 1 by 3 NaT array via the datetime constructor?x = datetime(nan(1,3),0,0);

Summary: Creating a model from a template using new_system?does not execute?PreLoadFcn
Description: When creating a model from a template using the?new_system ?function,? the model PreLoadFcn  does not execute. new_system('mymodel','FromTemplate','mytemplate.sltx')
Workaround: To create a template for a model and execute the model PreLoadFcn, use?Simulink.createFromTemplate instead:Simulink.createFromTemplate('mytemplate.sltx','Name','mymodel')

Summary: Model simulation is slow or hangs if certain UI components are open during simulation
Description: Simulating models with Model Data Editor or Model Explorer open, causes increased simulation time and for large models may cause the model to hang at the termination step of sim.
Workaround: Ensure that Model Explorer and Model Data Editor are closed before simulating the model.

Summary: JMAAB check for?jc_0602?displays an incorrect warning for Outports connected to Bus Selector signals
Description: Model Advisor JMAAB check for  jc_0602 :?Consistency in model element names ?reports the warning? The following model elements are not consistent with the connected signal name?for Outports  connected to signals from Bus Selector  block. This warning message is incorrect as? the signal name from Bus Selector block is resolved as "<signalName>" and the check includes "<", ">" symbols in the signal name for comparison with the Outport block names.

Summary: Unable to view confusion matrix for completed trials in Experiment Manager
Description: When trying to view the confusion matrix for a completed trial, the following is displayed in Experiment Manager:?? No data is available to generate the confusion matrix   Also, the following warning is shown in the MATLAB command window when running the experiment:? Warning: Variable 'trainingData' was not saved. For variables larger than 2GB use MAT-file version 7.3 or later   An alternative symptom of this problem is that the Results   directory in the project is unexpectedly large (up to 2GB per trial).? ?

Summary: uisetcolor dialog box appears behind UI figure?windows
Description: When uisetcolor  is executed from an App Designer or uifigure -based callback, the uisetcolor  dialog box appears behind the existing UI figure window. This issue appears only on a Windows platform.

Summary: Unexpected error when specifying a cross-section with very short edges in the General Flexible Beam block
Description: The following error may unexpectedly occur when the cross-section specification in the General Flexible Beam block includes some very short edges: The sectional properties computation is ill-conditioned for the specified cross-section. This might be due to overlapping or very close edges. Resolve this issue in order to simulate the model.   In particular, this can happen when the first and last vertices in any boundary of the specified cross-section are repeated. Due to the floating-point representation of numbers, the coordinates of these vertices may not be exactly equal, which leads to an extremely short edge between them. This particular issue exists in the sm_sailboat  example.

Summary: Detections from the Simulation 3D Probabilistic?Radar block are offset by one timestep.
Description: The detections output by the Simulation 3D Probabilistic Radar block are offset by one timestep forward. All other blocks that communicate with the Unreal Engine do not have this offset.

Summary: Testcases are not extended when the configured parameters are of fixed-point type
Description: In Test Extension workflow, if the parameters are of fixed-point type, the provided test cases are not extended and the corresponding objectives are analyzed again.

Summary: Unable to filter datetime arrays in live script tables
Description: In a live script table that contains datetime  arrays, when you expand the table view dropdown to filter by dates, you cannot select a date using the date picker. You also cannot enter a date into the date text field.
Workaround: When filtering tables with datetimes in them, the filtering can be done by dragging the sliders in the plot to limit the datetime values.

Summary: Error when generating HDL code for Product block set to matrix multiply using serial multiply-accumulate?DotProductStrategy
Description: If a Product block is configured to perform a matrix multiplication where the row-times-column operation is a scalar operation (i.e. 2x1 * 1x2 = 2x2 ), and the DotProductStrategy  in the HDL Block Properties dialog box is set to serial multiply-accumulate , an assertion can occur: Assertion failed:  b:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\hardwaremodepostprocessor.cpp:101:latency
Workaround: Use the default DotProductStrategy, that is,?fully parallel.

Summary: MATLAB crash for AUTOSAR model if AUTOSAR mapping deletion affects library blocks
Description: An AUTOSAR model might crash MATLAB if all of these conditions exist: The model includes blocks that are defined in a library. The Simulink to AUTOSAR mapping of the model was deleted. The deleted AUTOSAR mapping included mapped signals associated with the library blocks.  The actions that can crash MATLAB include updating the block library, saving the model, or loading the model.
Workaround: Deleting the library-based blocks from the model and adding new block instances might resolve the issue.

Summary: HERE HD Live Map features do not support HERE Marketplace service
Description: HERE Technologies has migrated data catalogs for HERE HD Live Map from the legacy DataStore service to the Marketplace service. If you migrated to the Marketplace service for HD Live Map, using your credentials for the HD Live Map features (hereHDLMReader, importing maps into driving scenarios) results in the following error: "Invalid App ID and App Code combination."

Summary: For Each Subsystem generates assertion during HDL code generation if Partition Dimension is greater than 2
Description: For Each Subsystem generates this assertion during HDL code generation if?Partition Dimension ?is greater than?2:  Assertion failed:b:\ matlab\ src\ cgir_hdl\ hdl_emitters\ vhdlpiremitter. cpp:2356:ConcatComp: input is too big?  This assertion occurs when the?Partition Dimension? block parameter specified for the For Each block exceeds the number of dimensions of the input signal. To partition a row vector, specify the?Partition Dimension ?as?2 . To partition a column vector, specify the?Partition Dimension ?as?1. However, HDL code generation does not support multidimensional arrays and?  Partition Dimension?  > 2? specified for the block.

Summary: Setting?Value or?ValueDisplayFormat?of NumericEditField or Spinner might result in the edit field erroneously being populated with NaN
Description: If the combination of?Value ?or?ValueDisplayFormat ?properties results in text made entirely of spaces, the edit field of NumericEditField or Spinner UI components might erroneously become populated with NaN. For example:? >> n = uieditfield('numeric', 'Value', ?0, ?'ValueDisplayFormat', ?'%11. d')  In addition, when the?Editable ?property is configured to be 'off', that configuration might be ignored and the edit field can be manually edited.  The app user can recover by entering a new value into the edit field.?
Workaround: Using the default ValueDisplayFormat (i.e.'%11.4g') is recommended.Changing the Limits or ValueDisplayFormat properties of the component to avoid combinations that are exclusively made of spaces will help avoid this issue. You can test this by using sprintf. For example:>> value = 0;>> valueDisplayFormat = '%11.4g';>> sprintf(valueDisplayFormat,value)ans =??? '????????? 0'

Summary: Import or Update from Microsoft Word fails with an error popup
Description: When importing requirements from Microsoft Word, or when attempting to update a Requirement Set that was previously imported from Microsoft Word, the process may fail with an error popup:  Error getting data from Word: RPC call rejected by the callee.   This malfunction might be more frequent on machines using Italian locale settings.
Workaround: * When performing original import, do not enable the "RichText" option, and import as plain text.* When updating an existing RichText import, minimize the document before you click the "Update" button.? If the document window pops-up to the foreground at the start of the update process, minimize it again immediately.

Summary: Simulation error when C Caller block calls a function which has a struct containing single element array field
Description: Simulink throws an error when you run a model with a C Caller block for which all of the following conditions are true:? The C function uses struct type arguments or has a return value of struct type The struct type contains a field with a single element array  The simulation error is, "Error(s) encountered while building simulation target MEX-file for model."
Workaround: Change the single element array field into a scalar.?

Summary: Observer port moved to new signal shows link to original signal
