Description: If you link an observer port to a signal in an Observer model, save and reopen the model, and then, move that observer to another signal, the signal highlighting does not fully update. Although the observer port is linked to the new signal, the original signal still shows as also being connected to the observer port.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating HDL code for models configured for IP Core Generation workflow that introduce blocks with unused ports and have critical path estimation enabled
Description: Sometimes, generating HDL code for models configured for?IP Core Generation ?workflow that has?Generate high-level timing critical path report ?enabled.might cause a MATLAB crash when the model introduces certain blocks with unused ports.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generation from System objects that contain nested handle objects might produce incorrect C/C+ code
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect C/C++ code if the source MATLAB code uses multiple System objects and satisfies all of the following statements:Each system object is assigned to a persistent variable? There is a non-persistent variable that is selectively assigned from each of these persistent variables Each system object contains another system object or a handle class as a property? These properties are assigned in the setupImpl   method of the corresponding outer System objects  In such situation, after the?setupImpl ?method of the outer System object is called, the generated code might read wrong memory when accessing the properties of the inner handle object.? This example shows the specific MATLAB code pattern that causes this issue. In this code snippet, each system object is first assigned to a persistent variable. Then, depending on the value of the loop index?i , either object might end up being referred to by the variable a . Also, ?step  is only called on a . persistent p q if isempty(p)    p = MySystemObject;end if isempty(q)    q = MySystemObject;end result = {1}; for i = 1:x    if  i == 2        a = p;    else        a = q;    end    % Next line executes MySystemObject.setupImpl which allocates another object and assigns it to a.property    step(a, i);    % Next line accesses a property of the nested object    result = a.property.property;end This issue affects MATLAB Coder and MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink.

Summary: HDL code generation may error out with an internal assertion when the Serializer1D block is configured with bus inputs
Description: HDL code generation errors out when the Serializer1D block is configured with bus inputs that have similar data types. The following error message is generated:?? Error: Evaluation of elaborate function on class hdldefaults.Serializer1D failed with the error message: pir_core:pirudd:assertionFailed: Assertion failed: <matlabroot>\src\cgir_hdl\dom_pir_core\comppirnative.cpp:591:inSize % getRatio() == 0?
Workaround: Consider replacing Bus-creator block with a Mux block if possible.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for nested handle objects might produce incorrect answer
Description: Code generation from MATLAB might sometimes produce incorrect C/C++ code if all of these conditions are true: The source MATLAB code uses handle classes or System objects? The constructor of a handle class (or the?setupImpl ?method of a System object) creates an instance of another handle class and immediately assigns it to a property. For example, the constructor or the?setupImpl ?method has a line of code similar to this: obj.prop = OtherHandleClass();  The lifetime of the inner object is longer than the lifetime of the outer object. An example of a code pattern that can cause this behavior is: The source MATLAB code returns the inner object (obj.prop  in the above example) to a caller function, while the outer object (obj ) itself is not returned. So, obj  ends its lifetime, but?obj.prop  still lives in the caller.?   This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: If, by design, you need to return a child object from a function where the parent object ends its lifetime, consider changing the code so that the parent object is returned, and the child object is then accessed using obj.property notation in the caller function.

Summary: Check boxes in GUIDE Menu Editor do not show checked state.
Description: When viewing the properties of a menu in the GUIDE Menu Editor, the following check boxes always show unchecked regardless of their true state: Separator above this item  Check mark this item  Enable this item   Selecting the check box toggles the state but always shows it as unchecked.
Workaround: In the Menu Editor, select a menu. Then click on the?More Properties...?button to view or edit the menu properties using the property inspector.

Summary: Error when using MATLAB Compiler with standalone charts
Description: When compiling an executable that involves a standalone Stateflow chart, MATLAB Compiler throws an error.?  In R2019b, the runtime error says:   Cannot get debuggable line numbers from <path to chart> . This is not a valid operation for this file type.    In R2020a, the compile error says:   Failed to build CTF file: <path to CTF file> . Details: fl:filesystem:PathNotFound

Summary: hdlset_param function for specifying synthesis tool is case sensitive
Description: When tool name is set using the  hdlset_param function with different tool name, the Workflow Advisor does not display the tasks in Step 4 after HDL code generation. For example:  >> hdlset_param(bdroot,'SynthesisTool','Altera Quartus II'); does not set the synthesis tool to Intel Quartus Prime Standard. To set the tool correctly, use:??  >> hdlset_param(bdroot,'SynthesisTool','Altera QUARTUS II'); ?

Summary: The pcdownsample function causes build errors during code generation
Description: The pcdownsample  function causes build errors when performing code generation for MATLAB Coder supported hardware interfaces.

Summary: Missing source files or compiler options in Polyspace project when?polyspace-configure?fails to read compiler options file
Description: In some situations, the?polyspace-configure ?command?fails to open the cached version of a compiler options file and falls back to the version of the options file in the file system. In these situations, if the options file was modified during the build, polyspace-configure  will only use the last version of the options file, leading to missing source files or compiler options in the generated project.
Workaround: If possible, do not edit the compiler options file during the compilation. Reusing the same file name should be also avoided.

Summary: Compatibility check may fail for models containing Subsystem Reference blocks
Description: For a model containing?Subsystem Reference? blocks, Simulink Design Verifier may fail during the preprocessing phase of compatibility check with the following message: Checking Compatibility Failed. ? Simulink Design Verifier failed due to errors.  Additionally, the following error message may get reported in the Diagnostic Viewer window:Subsystem reference block <Block Path> has unapplied changes.  You may also encounter the above error message when you execute sldvblockreplacement ?on the model ?

Summary: Inaccurate time in MATLAB Profiler for the first function call recorded
Description: In the MATLAB? Profiler, the reported time for the first function call is greater than it should be. This problem only affects the first function listed in the Profiler window. It does not affect any subsequent calls or their recorded times.

Summary: Compatibility check results in unclear error message for a model with Initialize-Reset-Terminate (IRT) Subsystem inside a Model Reference
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check throws an error when a model has an Initialize-Reset-Terminate  (IRT) Subsystem inside a Model Reference  block. The error does not show any specific reason for the incompatibility.Sample error message: Index exceeds the number of array elements(2)

Summary: Compiling MATLAB Data API code with VS2019 Community Edition with Conformance Mode enabled generates an error with the message Error C2760 syntax error: unexpected token 'identifier', expected 'expression'
Description: In Microsoft Visual Studio 2019 Community Edition, the Conformance Mode option is set to /permissive-  (enabled) by default.? This causes the compiler to generate errors not generated by older versions of VS, or by other versions of Visual Studio 2019. In particular, it generates an error when a typedef  statement contains a dependent type name followed by an asterisk and parameter name, and the sequence is not preceded by the keyword typename . There are several such instances in the file ArrayFactory.hpp .
Workaround: You can use any one of these possible workarounds: Use mex or mbuild instead of Visual Studio to buildapplications. Under the Visual Studio project properties, under C/C++ -> Language, set Conformance Mode to No. Use a version of Visual Studio other than Visual Studio 2019 Community Edition.Edit extern/include/MatlabDataArray/ArrayFactory.hpp to insert the keyword typename before the dependent type names impl::ArrayFactoryImpl and impl::ArrayImpl in each of the four lines of source code for which the compiler generates errors.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b

Summary: Unable to export live script with more than 30 figures to PDF
Description: Exporting a live script to PDF fails if the live script has more than 30 figures. The following error will be thrown in MATLAB command window : [ERROR] FOUserAgent - Image not found.?

Summary: Inconsistent return type for bus port properties CompiledPortDesignMin and CompiledPortDesignMax
Description: When you use get_param ?to access the CompiledPortDesignMin? or CompiledPortDesignMax  properties of a Simulink bus port, the return type can be inconsistent. The get_param  function returns a scalar value instead of the expected MATLAB struct when either of the following conditions are true: All bus elements have identical min/max literal values.  The first bus element has min/max value set to []  and all other bus elements have identical min/max literal values.  If each bus element has?[]?specified as its min/max value, the result is empty.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generated when using y=f(y) style MATLAB function in a Simulink function inside a Stateflow chart
Description: Generated code using VAR_IN_OUT could compute an incorrect result when using a y=f(y) style MATLAB function block with identical input and output variables inside a Simulink function, within a Stateflow chart.??
Workaround: The y=f(y) style MATLAB function block with identical input and output variable?should be used directly in Stateflow chart instead of being inside Simulink Function in Stateflow chart.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for CODESYS target when using models with Shift Arithmetic blocks
Description: Behaviour of Shift Arithmetic  instructions on target CODESYS  is different from that in Simulink. There are two arguments to shift functions,(1) in : The number to be shifted, and(2) n :? The number of bits it should be shifted by. In Simulink, if n  is greater than word length of in , the answer will be zero. However, for such a case in CODESYS , the IDE will shift in  by n? MOD word_length(in )" bits?CODESYS SHL Operator  . This could lead to incorrect code generation.
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 1, R2020b

Summary: gcbp returns the wrong path in charts contained in an Atomic Subsystem
Description: If you navigate inside a Stateflow chart that is parented by an Atomic Subsystem, the editor's explorer bar will correctly show the path. However, the command?gcbp ?will return an incorrect result. For example, for a chart block with this path: ? ? ?TopLevelModel/TopLevelChart/SimulinkFunction/Atomic Subsystem/InnerChart gcbp ?returns a Simulink.BlockPath  object pointing to this path: ? ? ?TopLevelModel/TopLevelChart
Workaround: You can use the attached function to get the path to a chart inside an atomic subsystem, or more generally for any block. To use it, first select the block in?the Simulink Editor, then call the function.function path = get_current_block_path()? ? path = [];? ? sfObject = sfgco;? ? if ~isempty(sfObject)? ? ? ? ? ? ? ??? ? ? ? switch class(sfObject)? ? ? ? ? ? case 'Stateflow.Chart'? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? path = sfObject.Path;? ? ? ? ? ? otherwise? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? path = sfObject.Chart.Path;? ? ? ? end? ? end? ? if isempty(path)? ? ? ? currentBlock = gcbp;? ? ? ? if ~isempty(currentBlock)? ? ? ? ? ? path = currentBlock.convertToCell{1};? ? ? ? end? ? endend

Summary: packNGo fails to package files extracted from protected model
Description: When you use the?packNGo ?function to package files extracted from a protected model, the function might fail to create a zip file. This might happen when all of the following are true: The protected model contains custom files registered by an rtwmakecfg  function. The custom files are not in the current working folder. You use the packNGo  function to package files extracted from the protected model.
Workaround: Copy custom files to the working folder.

Summary: Analysis may terminate with an?Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end?error message.
Description: Analysis might terminate without producing the results. When you run the analysis with a user interface, it displays the following error message:Internal error in Simulink Design Verifier back end.  When you run the analysis in the Test Generation  mode, the error message displayed at the MATLAB Command Window is: Test generation produced errors  Stack trace observed from R2018a: src/data_graph/graph_rat_feasible.c:6508 err=data_graph_err_internal  src/data_graph/graph_rat_feasible.c:6508 err=data_graph_err_internal  src/tempo/tempo_safe_induction.c:1333 tempo_error=tempo_err_unknown  When this issue is hit, the stack trace will contain graph_rat_feasible.c  with a number between 6400 and 6600, or err=data_graph_err_verify_model.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 8, R2020a Update 7, R2020b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results with Variant Source blocks connected to duplicate inports referenced from top model
Description: The simulation or code generation of the top model might give wrong results because of incorrect propagated variant conditions.This issue might happen when all the below conditions are met:  A top model containing Model block refers to another model The referenced model contains duplicate inports directly connected to Variant Source blocks

Summary: External constraints are not recognized on arguments passed by reference to stubbed functions
Description: When you specify external constraints (DRS) on arguments passed by reference to a stubbed function, a Polyspace Code Prover analysis does not take into account the?Init Allocated ?constraint. For example, even if you specify that a function does not write to an argument passed by reference (set Init Allocated  to NONE ), the verification continues to assume that the function potentially writes to that argument. After the function call, source code tool-tips display full-range values on variables passed by reference, indicating that the function might have written any value within the range allowed by the variable data type.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In a model containing blocks from the SoC Blockset?and asynchronous sample time, the sorted order might be incorrect
Description: Simulink might produce an incorrect sorted order for a model that meets all of the following conditions: The model contains blocks from the SoC Blockset The?Signal logging  option is selected in the model configuration set Signals using asynchronous sample time are configured for logging  As a result, Simulink might produce incorrect results in Normal, Accelerator, and Rapid Accelerator simulation?modes as well as in generated code.

Summary: Build error for AUTOSAR model with bus inports and Concatenate block
Description: An AUTOSAR model build fails when all of the following conditions are true:The model uses bus ports as root inports. A bus inport connects to both an outport and a Concatenate block.  Building the model generates a code generation assertion.

Summary: Incorrect code generation for models configured for concurrent execution when the subsystems mapped to tasks contain inline variant blocks
Description: Code generated from models configured for concurrent execution might be incorrect when the following conditions are true: The model contains an atomic subsystem that contain inline variant blocks, such as Variant Source or Variant Sink blocks In the Code Generation  pane of the configuration parameters, the System target file  is set to ert.tlc  In the variant block parameter dialog, the Analyze all choices during update diagram and generate preprocessor conditionals   option is selected. This means that the Variant activation time  parameter for the Variant Source and Variant Sink blocks is set to code compile .
Workaround: Set the Variant activation time parameter for the Variant Source and Variant Sink blocks to update diagram or update diagram analyze all choices.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect overflow in Simulink.LookupTable and Simulink.Breakpoint objects when?number of breakpoints exceed the limit for derived Tunable Size data type
Description: Incorrect simulation results are generated for models that contain an n-D Lookup Table or Prelookup block with these settings: Model contains an n-D Lookup Table block: In the n-D lookup table block, Data Specification  is set to Lookup table object  Name  field is set to the name of an existing or newly added  Simulink.LookupTable object   In the Simulink.LookupTable object,   The Support tunable size  property is set Number of breakpoints exceeds the limit for the derived tunable size data type   Model contains a Prelookup block In the Prelookup block, Breakpoints data Specification  parameter is set to Breakpoint object  Name  field is set to the name of an existing or newly added  Simulink.Breakpoint object   In the Simulink.Breakpoint object ,  The Support tunable size  property is set Number of breakpoints exceeds the limit for the derived tunable size data type
Fixed In: R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 2, R2020b

Summary: Generating multicore code from a model configured for concurrent execution might produce a TLC error
Description: Generating multicore code from model configured for concurrent execution might produce a TLC error when both of the following conditions are met: The model is explicitly partitioned. The model contains atomic subsystems that contain non-inlined S-function blocks.
Workaround: Place the non-inlined S-function block inside a Model block, and reference the Model block in the model configured for concurrent execution.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating HDL code for large models that contain Switch blocks with unconnected output ports
Description: In some cases, HDL Code generation from large models containing Switch blocks with unconnected output ports might lead to a MATLAB crash. Following shows the stack trace: [? 1] 0x00007ff94d40c0c6 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+09814214 pir::Ast::setStr+00610934  [? 2] 0x00007ff94d40b4f4 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+09811188 pir::Ast::setStr+00607908  [? 3] 0x00007ff94d5fd9d3 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+11852243 pir::CgirToPirConversion::setPipelineVars+00163859  [? 4] 0x00007ff94d45a212 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+10134034 pir::OptimizeArrayIncDecOneIndexing::scope+00290130  [? 5] 0x00007ff94d457f66 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+10125158 pir::OptimizeArrayIncDecOneIndexing::scope+00281254  [? 6] 0x00007ff94d455fb7 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+10117047 pir::OptimizeArrayIncDecOneIndexing::scope+00273143  [? 7] 0x00007ff94d44bc93 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+10075283 pir::OptimizeArrayIncDecOneIndexing::scope+00231379  [? 8] 0x00007ff94d44b927 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+10074407 pir::OptimizeArrayIncDecOneIndexing::scope+00230503  [? 9] 0x00007ff94d44c19c C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+10076572 pir::OptimizeArrayIncDecOneIndexing::scope+00232668  [ 10] 0x00007ff94ce31f08 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\bin\win64\cgir_hdl.dll+03677960 pir::Ctx::doSerialization+00000040
Workaround: Run the attached P-coded file. The syntax for running the P-coded function is as follows:?>>?fixUnusedPortDeleter('<modelName>');

Summary: Update diagram fails for referenced model when anonymous structure type matches multiple bus types
Description: In a Model block, if the instance-specific value of a model argument has an anonymous structure type, an update diagram reports an error when there are multiple bus types that match that anonymous structure type.
Workaround: Instead of anonymous struct value, specify Simulink.Parameter with a structure value and same bus type as corresponding instance parameter.

Summary: Multiple runs of a model that uses Simscape Electrical Specialized Power Systems blocks might produce slightly different results
Description: Models that use certain Specialized Power Systems blocks inside the Simscape Electrical library might produce slightly different simulation results between the first run and all the subsequent runs.

Summary: The goertzel function throws an error when running in a MATLAB Compiler compiled application
Description: The?goertzel ?function throws an error when running in a MATLAB Compiler compiled application. The error will be similar to Unable to resolve the name coder.internal.constNonSingletonDim  but might refer to a different coder.internal  function.

Summary: ismembertol?with?the 'ByRows' option?might fail to identify matching rows when the second input contains NaNs.
Description: ismembertol  takes two input data sets, A  and B , and identifies which elements of A  are also elements of B  (within a certain tolerance). However, if the 'ByRows'  option is specified and B  has a NaN ?value in the first column, then?ismembertol ?might fail to identify some matching rows. For example: >> A = [20 8; 20 10];>> B = [20 10; 0 0; NaN NaN];>> ismembertol(A,B,'ByRows',true)ans =  2ˇÁ1 logical array   0   0 The correct answer for this example is [false; true] , since the second row of A  matches the first row of B .
Workaround: Remove NaNs from the second input argument of ismembertol using rmmissing:>> ismembertol(A,rmmissing(B),'ByRows',true)

Summary: MATLAB crashes while modifying or running code in the Live Editor
Description: When modifying or running code in the Live Editor, MATLAB might crash with a report that mentions the?mwddux_impl.dll ?file.

Summary: On macOS and Linux, MATLAB might hang during the execution of the system or copyfile command
Description: If the system runs out of memory during the execution of the system  or copyfile  command, or if you manually terminate the matlab_helper  subprocess by sending a SIGKILL  signal, then the MATLAB Command Window might hang.
Workaround: To recover from this condition, kill the matlab_helper process, then restart MATLAB.

Summary: No units appear in the unit library of the SimBiology app
Description: If the user-defined unit library contains a unit with an invalid composition, the SimBiology app does not show any units in the unit library.
Workaround: Remove all invalid units from the SimBiology unit library before opening the SimBiology app.

Summary: Initial values of RAMs created during HDL code generation of MATLAB Function blocks with MATLAB Datapath architecture have lower precision
Description: During HDL code generation, the RAMs created from persistent variables inside MATLAB Function blocks that have MATLAB Datapath  as the HDL architecture? have less precise initial values in the generated code than the original persistent variable value.?

Summary: Code generator places code for asynchronously triggered atomic subsystem in wrong location
Description: For an atomic subsystem that is triggered asynchronously, the code generator places the code for that subsystem in a location that prevents the code from being called as expected if these conditions exist: The subsystem that is triggered asynchronously contains another asynchronous function initiator and an atomic subsystem. The atomic subsystem contains at least one block with the sample time set to inf .
Workaround: Use the attached script?to detect subsystems that meet conditions that cause this error. Do one of the following: For the atomic subsystem that is inside the subsystem that is triggered asynchronously, clear the subsystem block parameter Treat as atomic unit.If the atomic subsystem contains a block with the sample time value set to?inf, change the sample time setting to -1. Check for blocks that exist under a mask (for example, Compare To Zero block). For these blocks, if necessary, unlock the library and links. Then, look under the mask to change the sample time value.

Summary: polyspace-configure could not open temporary options file when using Renesas SH
Description: When using some compilers such as Renesas SH, which uses a specific convention for caching files, polyspace-configure  fails to open temporary options files with the following message:? ERROR: Could not open options file generatedOption   The options file is cached correctly but polyspace-configure  fails to open the cached version.
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Calling MATLAB instrument driver (MDD) functions that have no output arguments might error
Description: For MathWorks-authored MATLAB instrument drivers (MDD), an error might occur when calling functions that have no output arguments. These functions complete successfully on the instrument side, but throw a MATLAB error when calling the invoke  function with?icdevice ?or when using the driver functions in the Test & Measurement Tool ?app. For example, the following code results in an error.     interfaceObj = serial('COM1');    deviceObj = icdevice('agilent_33120a.mdd',interfaceObj);    connect(deviceObj);    groupObj = get(deviceObj,'System');    invoke(groupObj,'beep');        Error using icgroup/invoke (line 105)    Unable to perform assignment because the indices on the left side are not compatible with the size of the right side.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can use try-catch and ignore the error, since the function completes successfully on the instrument side. For example:    try       invoke(groupObj,'beep')    catch       % ignore error    end

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model that uses row-major array layout and complex types containing fixed-point data types might generate incorrect results
Description: The generated code might be incorrect when compared to simulation results under all these conditions:? Model configuration parameter Array layout  is set to Row-major . Model contains complex types with fixed-point data types.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Potential validation model mismatch for model with redundant logic in presence of streaming and clock rate pipelining
Description: The validation model generated with HDL code for a Simulink model might mismatch if the following conditions are met: The model is large with multiple hierarchies and contains unconnected ports and signals ending in terminator blocks. The clock-rate pipelining optimization is enabled on the model with an Oversampling factor  greater than one. The streaming optimization is requested on blocks that are inside feedback loops.
Workaround: Run the attached P-coded file. The syntax for running the P-coded function is as follows:>>?fixUnusedPortDeleter('<modelName>');?

Summary: Reusing Simulink cache file errors out when no replacement model is generated for a custom block replacement rule
Description: Reusing Simulink cache file for Simulink Design Verifieranalysis results in an error  Operands to the || and && operatorsmust be convertible to logical scalar values  when all the followingconditions are true:  The SLDV option BlockReplacement  is set to on.  A custom block replacement rule is specified aspart of the option BlockReplacementRulesList .  Analyzing the model for the first time throws awarning as follows:? None of the active block replacement ruleswere available for replacing blocks in the model ˇ®ModelNameˇŻ. Analysis will beusing the original model 'ModelName'.  The option RebuildModelRepresentation  is set to IfChangeIsDetected  and SLDV analysis is performed again.
Workaround: Disable block replacements by setting BlockReplacement?option to off, whenever the warning:?None of the active block replacement rules were available for replacing blocks in the model 'ModelName'. Analysis will be using the original model 'ModelName'.?is shown.

Summary: Corrupted Word document when template image name or alternative text contains ampersand (&) or open angle bracket (<)
Description: If you add an image file to a Word template, and the file name contains an ampersand (& ) or open angle bracket (< ), for example, salt&pepper.png , reports that are generated using the template file are corrupted. Reports also are corrupted if an image's alternative text contains an ampersand or open angle bracket. Word produces the following error message when attempting to open the corrupted document:? Word experienced an error trying to open the file. Try these suggestions.  * Check the file permissions for the document or drive.?  * Make sure there is sufficient free memory and disk space?  * Open the file with the Text Recovery converter.?
Workaround: Remove any ampersands or open angle brackets from image alternative text. In Word, you can edit the alternative text of an image by right-clicking the image and selecting "Edit Alt Text...".Remove any images that were added to the template from files with names containing an ampersand or open angle bracket. Remove the problematic characters from the file names and add the images back to the report.

Summary: Requirements imported from IBM DOORS Next missing "Updated on" Revision information
Description: Using Simulink Requirement IBM DOORS Next (also known as DNG or IBM DOORS Next Generation) direct import, you can either import whole DNG module or query a project. Requirements imported this way are missing "Updated on" Revision information. In the Requirements Editor, you will see "N/A". This makes Links involving these requirements flagged for change issue immediately.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model with Unit Delay block inside a For Each Subsystem block
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions:The model contains a For Each subsystem block. There is a Unit Delay block inside the For Each subsystem block. The Initial condition  parameter of the Unit?delay block is set to non-zero value.

Summary: Error when Bus Creator block specifies one input and the signal name
Description: You receive an error when applying settings for a Bus Creator block when?Number of inputs  is set to 1  and Require input signal names to match signals above ?is selected. The equivalent programmatic command is: set_param(gcb,'Inputs','signal1')  The error looks like: Error evaluating parameter 'Inputs' in 'untitled/Bus Creator'  Caused by:  ? ? Unrecognized function or variable 'signal1'.
Workaround: Set the?Number of inputs to?1, select?Require input signal names to match signals above, and set Rename selected signal?to the signal name specified as a character vector. For example, 'signal1'.The equivalent programmatic command is:set_param(gcb,'Inputs','''signal1''')

Summary: Stateflow chart inside rate grouped Simulink Function might lead to assertion during code generation
Description: Assertion might occur during code generation if all these conditions are true:? Model contains rate grouped Simulink Function. Stateflow chart exists inside the Simulink Function. Stateflow chart has a function-call output event. Function packaging  of the Stateflow chart is set to 'Auto ' or 'Inline '.
Workaround: Set Function packaging of the Stateflow chart to 'Reusable function' or 'Nonreusable function'.

Summary: MATLAB warns about the Connector not running when using UITools functions in MATLAB startup.m file
Description: If you use UITools functions, such as listdlg  or questdlg , in the MATLAB startup.m ?or matlabrc.m ?file, MATLAB displays a warning message: Warning: MATLAB Connector is not running.
Workaround: Add the following line of code before the call to UITools functions in the MATLAB startup.m or matlabrc.m?file:connector.ensureServiceOn;

Summary: HDL code generated from models containing Bus Element ports may have incorrect syntax with Traceability Style set to Line Level?
Description: When Traceability Style  is set to Line Level  in? HDL Code Generation  > Report pane  of Configuration Parameters dialog, models with Bus Element Ports may generate HDL Code containing texts like?@[tracestart]  and @[traceend] . This leads to compilation error during synthesis.
Workaround: Set Traceability Style?is set to?Comment Based?or use bus selector block instead of bus element ports.

Summary: Streaming and sharing on same resource fails when clock-rate pipelining is enabled
Description: When generating HDL code for a model with clock-rate pipelining enabled, if a StreamingFactor  and SharingFactor  greater than 1  is specified on the same resource, streaming optimization is successful but sharing optimization fails.
Workaround: To perform Streaming and Sharing on the same resource, disable Clock Rate Pipelining.

Summary: Compatibility check fails for models with erroneous block specific copy action callbacks
Description: If you have a model with callbacks that prevent model copy, then Simulink Design Verifier analysis fails with the below message: Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: Checking compatibility failed  The error message can be noticed after executing any of the below commands  sldvrun  sldvcompat  sldvblockreplacement   Please note that no error messages are seen in the Simulink Diagnostic viewer.

Summary: Delays while editing Simulink model with variant and Simscape blocks
Description: Models that contain both variant and Simscape blocks might experience a delay when moving and connecting signals, ports, and blocks.

Summary: Internal assertion during HDL code generation for RAM mapping of double-typed persistent variables using MATLAB Function block in Native Floating Point mode
Description: If persistent variables are declared with double types, RAM mapping using the MATLAB Function block in?Native Floating Point  mode?generates? an assertion. Assertion failed: b:\matlab\src\cgir_hdl\hdl_emitters\piremitter.cpp:2967:Call failed to slResolve block mRAMDoubleIV/Subsystem/Single Port RAM System for value 1:256  An issue in RAM initial value support exists when the value is non-zero and double.
Workaround: Change the initial value of the persistent variable to single precision and manually cast the value to double.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Masked For Each Subsystem when used as a top-level DUT generates incorrect HDL code
Description: A masked For Each Subsystem when used as a top-level DUT generates incorrect HDL code.
Workaround: Consider placing For Each?subsystem inside?a different subsystem before generating code.

Summary: Warning for S-Function Builder version when the number of inputs or outputs is 0
Description: When the number of inputs or outputs of the S-Function Builder?is 0, Simulink displays the following warning: ?Invalid S-Function Builder version found.

Summary: Configuration Parameters dialog shows empty search results
Description: After you simulate a model that uses Simscape blocks, in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the search bar shows empty results.

Summary: The faster R-CNN detect?function returns an error when using grayscale images
Description: Calling the? detect? function on the fasterRCNNObjectDetector  detector object, which was trained using grayscale inputs, returns an error.?  For example, the following code generates an error, when the input image,?I , is a grayscale image and the detector, fasterRCNN , is configured for grayscale inputs:? [bboxes,scores,labels] = detect(fasterRCNN,I);  Index exceeds the number of array elements (2).  Error in fasterRCNNObjectDetector/detect (line 557)    updateNetworkIfRequired(this,params.ImageSize(1:3),params);?

Summary: Error when generating VHDL code for models that contain signals ending in terminators
Description: VHDL code generation for models that contain signals ending in terminators might lead to the following error: Internal error detected in code generation data. One possible cause is that the design has no output ports. This is not supported.  The issue does not occur when generating Verilog code from the model.?
Workaround: Run the attached P-coded file. The syntax for running the P-coded function is as follows:>>?fixUnusedPortDeleter('<modelName>');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: In a standalone chart in MATLAB,?every?executes the chart only once if used on an inner transition of a state
Description: If the absolute-time?temporal logic expression?every(n,sec)   is used?in?a standalonechart?as a trigger?on an inner transition of a state, the chart will be executed only once, when the state has been active for n  seconds. The chart will not be executed for subsequent instances of the every   operator after 2n sec, 3n sec, 4n sec, etc.

Summary: Persistent global variable used within a Parallel for-Loops(parfor) present in a MATLAB Function block or MATLAB?System block may result in code that does not compile
Description: The generated code may not compile if the following conditions are met:A persistent global variable is used within a Parallel for-Loops(parfor) present in a MATLAB function block or MATLAB system block. In the Configuration Parameter dialog box, the Generate parallel for loops  parameter is enabled.?

Summary: MATLAB crashes when running the generated MEX for a second time if your design has persistent deep learning objects
Description: For some versions of the GCC compiler on the Linux platform,? the generated MEX fails to unload if your entry-point design file has calls to gpucoder.sort .? If you make changes to the design function and regenerate MEX,? running the newer MEX may still continue to use older MEX, producing unexpected results.?The YOLOv2 and SSD object detectors from Computer Vision Toolbox internally use gpucoder.sort . A design using these object detectors may crash MATLAB if the MEX is run more than once. Using the clear mex ?command may also not resolve the issue.
Workaround: Restart MATLAB.

Summary: HDL Code generation errors out for a model?with n-D Lookup Table block?indicating incompatible extrapolation method
Description: The n-D Lookup Table block can be configured with an extrapolation method if the interpolation method is not set to Flat ?or Nearest . If the extrapolation method is changed to a value other than Clip , for example Cubic spline , and then the interpolation method is changed back to Flat , HDL Code generation might throw the following error. Block 'gm_model/Subsystem/n-D Lookup Table' supports 'Cubic spline' as its 'Extrapolation method' only if its 'Interpolation method'? parameter specifies 'Cubic spline'.
Workaround: The issue can be avoided if the LUT block in the original model is configured with "Clip" extrapolation using a set_param call.>> set_param(<lut_block_path>, 'ExtrapMethod', 'Clip')

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you call a Simulink function that has a run-time error from a Stateflow chart
Description: If you call a Simulink function external to a Stateflow chart and there is a run-time error in that Simulink function, MATLAB might crash instead of reporting an error to the diagnostic viewer. You might see a crash report with the symptoms:? [? 0] 0x00000000fcbfd71b bin\win64\sf_runtime.dll+00579355 StateflowJITEngineInterface::StateflowJITEngineInterface+00003579[? 1] 0x00007ffcd777c1e0 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\bin\win64\VCRUNTIME140.dll+00049632 _C_specific_handler+00000560[? 2] 0x00007ffcd7772a6a?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\bin\win64\VCRUNTIME140.dll+00010858 _TypeMatch+00000714[? 3] 0x00007ffcd7771dc6?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\bin\win64\VCRUNTIME140.dll+00007622 is_exception_typeof+00000838[? 4] 0x00007ffcd777b950?C:\Program Files\MATLAB\bin\win64\VCRUNTIME140.dll+00047440 _CxxFrameHandler3+00000144

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might produce incorrect values of MATLAB objects when using coder.OutputType for outputs that are or contain MATLAB objects
Description: Suppose that you generate code for multiple MATLAB entry-point functions. When all the following conditions are true, the generated code for the first entry-point function might notassign an initial value to certain properties and leave them undefined: You use coder.OutputType ?to specify the output type of one entry-point function as the input type foranother entry-point function. This type?is a MATLABclass or contains a MATLAB class. The firstentry-point function does not assign a value to some property of this class. Another entry-point function that is called later on assigns a value to that property. The MATLAB class definition assigns an initial value to this property.
Workaround: When using?coder.OutputType?for outputs that are or contain MATLAB objects, make sure the first entry-point function assigns values to all properties of these objects.

Summary: Simulink blocks with non-ASCII white space in their name may cause errors when running scripts or editing Simulink models
Description: All white space is supposed to be treated equally in block names. However, using non-ASCII white space in a block name may cause a failure to find that block when searching for it using alternative (non-exact) white space. As a result, Simulink returns the error "Invalid Simulink object name " or a broken link to the block in the Diagnostic Viewer.
Workaround: Rename blocks to replace non-ASCII whitespace with space, tab, or CR (Carriage Return).

Summary: Signal hierarchy for buses cannot be accessed from within the InitFcn?callback
Description: An attempt to access signal hierarchy using the?get_param ?function and the?SignalHierarchy ?parameter during the?InitFcn ?callback?phase?unexpectedly returns an empty structure.

Summary: Incorrect Streaming and Sharing report when both optimizations enabled
Description: When both sharing and streaming optimizations are enabled, the Optimization Report might display that streaming or sharing failed when it was successful on the model.  For example, this issue might occur when you have:  Vector inputs to Add and Product blocks at the same level of hierarchy inside a subsystem Both StreamingFactor  and SharingFactor  HDL block properties specified on the blocks

Summary: MATLAB becomes unresponsive when several figures are open and drawnow executes
Description: MATLAB might become unresponsive when drawnow  executes after you create several figures using the uifigure  function, or after you modify those figures. The likelihood of experiencing this issue increases with the number of figures you create.

Summary: Code generation errors out for tunable parameters having fixed-point data type
Description: Code generation throws an error for tunable parameters of type Simulink.Parameter  that have fixed-point data types even when the data type matches the corresponding value's data type.? For example, you may see the below error message: Invalid data type. ?The 'mParameterWithStorageClass/ Subsystem_ModelDefault/ Gain' block that consumes tunable parameter K_ModelDefault expects real32_T datatype. ?However the parameter values are of type real_T. ?Use a typed expression, ?such as single(21. 1) to set the value property of the parameter.?

Summary: Content extends beyond figure edges when using exportgraphics or copygraphics
Description: When you call the exportgraphics  or copygraphics  function with the ContentType  set to either 'auto'  (the default value) or 'vector' , text or other content might extend beyond the edges of the figure in the saved file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using coder.nullcopy to initialize a primitive array directly contained inside a cell array might cause incorrect code generation
Description: The code generator can produce incorrect code if all of the following statements are true: Your MATLAB code creates a cell array Each element of the cell array is itself an array of the same primitive type At least one of the primitive arrays is initialized by indexing directly into the cell array and using coder.nullcopy   For example, suppose that each element of the cell array myCell  is an array of type double . The only code pattern that can cause this issue looks like this:? myCell{1,1} = coder.nullcopy(zeros(3)); This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks? in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: When initializing a cell array of primitive arrays, do not use coder.nullcopy.

Summary: Polyspace annotation not correctly applied when syntax incomplete or severity field missing
Description: Polyspace annotation syntax that is incomplete or missing the severity field can lead to wrong Status and Comments in the Polyspace annotation. For example: This incomplete Polyspace annotation is missing ]  after the severity setting: polyspace *:* [To fix:Medium "my comments"  This syntax is missing the severity setting: polyspace *:* [To fix] "my comments"
Workaround: To avoid this issue use the complete annotation syntax, including Severity, of the form:  polyspace Family:Result_name [Status:Severity] "Additional Notes"For example:  polyspace *:* [To fix:Medium] "my comments"
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Under certain conditions, arrays greater than 14KB but smaller than 16KB take longer to clear from the workspace than normal on Microsoft Windows platforms.
Description: Under certain conditions, arrays greater than 14KB but smaller than 16KB take longer to clear from the workspace than normal on Microsoft Windows platforms.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Calling a function with the same input types from within and outside a parfor, with a low stack size limit, might result in a mismatch between MATLAB and generated MEX code outputs
Description: When using a function with the same input types from within and outside a parfor, ?you might see answer mismatches between MATLAB and generated MEX code when all of the following conditions are true: The code configuration parameter StackUsageMax  is enabled. The code includes a stack allocated local variable which exceeds the StackUsageMax  parameter size. The same function call from within and outside a parfor  loop with the same input types.
Workaround: Convert the parfor loop containing the shared function call to a for?loop.

Summary: HDL implementation model with validation logic can generate assertions during simulation for default Validation logic tolerance setting
Description: In the?Generate implementation model ?step of the Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor, the default Validation logic tolerance ?is set to?1e-12 . This tolerance value is too low, and can cause the HDL implementation model to generate assertions during simulation.
Workaround: Use a Validation logic tolerance appropriate for the implementation model such that it produces the desired functionality while allowing for a small numerical inaccuracy between the Simscape solver and the implementation model.For double precision, a value that is 1e-10 smaller than the largest signal being validated is recommended. For single precision, a value that? is 1e-5 smaller than the largest signal being validated is recommended.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for loops that iterate beyond flintmax?of index variable precision type and appear to assign all elements of an array or cell array might produce incorrect results
Description: The code generator optimizes assignments to a matrix or a cell array if all of these conditions are true: The matrix or the cell array is followed by a loop.? The loop contains assignments to all elements of the matrix or the cell array. The elements of the matrix or the cell array are not read between the initial assignment and the loop.?  In such cases, the code generator optimizes out the initial assignment statement and removes it from the generated code. For example, for this MATLAB code, the assignment x = ones(1,10) ?is optimized out and not included in the generated code: x = ones(1,10)  % this is optimized out %%  ... no uses of x between its definition above%      and the loop below that appears to write to%      every element of x ...% for i = 1:10    x(i) = i;end If the bounds of the loop exceed the largest consecutive integer that can be represented in the index variable's precision type, the removal of the initial assignment statement can cause incorrect results.? For example, the following program shows a loop that appears to write to all elements of the matrix. However, because flintmax('single')  is 16777216 , out(16777217) ?is not written by the loop.? But the code generator removes the assignment out = zeros(1, 16777218,'like',single(1)) ?from the generated code . As a result, the generated code might use a different value for out(16777217) ?than MATLAB. function out = fcnout = zeros(1, 16777218,'like',single(1)); %%  ... no uses of 'out' between its definition above%      and the loop below that appears to write to%      every element of 'out' ...% for i = single(1):single(16777218)    out(i) = i;endend This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: In your MATLAB code, avoid using loops whose upper bounds exceed the flintmax of the index variable precision type.

Summary: Range Collection Mode as Derived ranges in Fixed-point Tool fails for export function models
Description: Range collection in Fixed-point Tool with Range Collection Mode ?selected as Derived ranges  fails for Export-function models. The diagnostic viewer shows the error:?Ranges could not be derived for the model.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019a Update 9, R2019b Update 7, R2020a Update 5, R2020b

Summary: When starting a MATLAB process on Windows via certain external interfaces, the MATLAB process may stall or hang.
Description: When using MATLAB C++, Java, or Python engine APIs to start a MATLAB process, the process might stall or hang. The stall or hang might appear as a timeout while trying to find graphics devices during MATLAB graphics initialization.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 7, R2021a Update 6, R2021b Update 2, R2022a

Summary: User authentication fails with MATLAB Parallel Server on some Linux systems such as RHEL7
Description: MATLAB Parallel Server fails to authenticate users on security level 3 on some Linux systems such as RHEL7 due to problems integrating with PAM.  MATLAB Parallel Server reads /etc/pam.d/common-auth for PAM configuration, but this file is not necessarily the only place that PAM configuration might exist on Linux systems.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, work with your system administrator to produce an /etc/pam.d/common-auth file. If you have an existing /etc/pam.d/password-auth file, then you can use this as the basis.Alternatively, you can create a symbolic link /etc/pam.d/common-auth to /etc/pam.d/password-auth using the command:$: ln -s /etc/pam.d/password-auth /etc/pam.d/common-authWARNING: There is a danger that other processes might attempt to use this new common-auth link. This link might lead to unexpected behavior. Potentially, the user might be locked out of their machine.

Summary: Polyspace analysis stops with error: declaration is incompatible with "void OSEK_polyspace_ActivateTask(OSEK_polyspace_task_type)"
Description: When you analyze your C application using the option?-autosar-multitasking , Polyspace might stop during the compilation phase with an error message similar to the following: Verifying MyFile.c (Y/XX)pathto\Os.h, line xxxx: error: identifier "OSEK_task_polyspace_TaskType" is undefined|  StatusType OSEK_polyspace_ActivateTask(OSEK_task_polyspace_TaskType TaskID);|                                         ^
Workaround: Try configuring your Polyspace multitasking analysis manually. See?Configuring Polyspace Multitasking Analysis Manually.
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: Rapid Accelerator mode does not work on systems running CentOS 6.1
Description: On CentOS 6.1, the Rapid Accelerator build process will fail while linking and will report dozens of error messages similar to the following one: undefined reference to `std::out_of_range::out_of_range(char const*)@GLIBCXX_3.4.21?

Summary: Bus type with string elements cannot be exported
Description: A Simulink bus type can be exported to a custom header file in code generation when its DataScope   property is set to Exported . However, if the bus type has a string element, the bus type cannot be exported. Instead, an error like the following displays: "Error occurred when generating shared type: Exported type, ?BusObject, ?depends on a non-exported type, ?SLStringType. ?Either set DataScope of BusObject to 'Auto' or set DataScope of SLStringType to 'Exported'. "  Neither of the suggestions in the message works.?

Summary: Data store logging using Data Store Memory block and Discrete-Event System block might produce incorrect results
Description: Data store logging using a Data Store Memory block might produce incorrectresults when both of the following conditions are met: ?A custom MATLAB Discrete-Event System block exchanges data with a Data Store Memory block. The MATLAB Discrete-EventSystem block is configured to simulate through generated code

Summary: Dependency Analyzer reports a dependency on Simulink when using some functions in Optimization Toolbox
Description: When a MATLAB file calls certain functions in Optimization Toolbox, for example?lsqcurvefit ?or fmincon , the Dependency Analyzer reports that your file requires that Simulink be installed.

Summary: Collecting model metrics produces an error with Diagnostic Event Manager caller blocks
Description: Collecting model metrics with Diagnostic Event Manager blocks such as DiagnosticInfoCaller, DiagnosticMonitorCaller, and DiagnosticEventAvailableCaller might produce the following metrics calculation error: Error during compilation of Simulink model 'autosar_swc_expfcns'.Caused by:Error due to multiple causes.A definition for the function 'ExampleGet_GetEventStatus' called by Function Caller block 'autosar_swc_expfcns Runnable2_subsystem DiagnosticInfoCaller',?could not be found.
Products: Simulink Check, Simulink Coverage, Requirements Toolbox

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results are possible for a model containing multiple?Reusable?custom storage class with a?branched root Inport
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a model that meets the following conditions: A pair of root-level Inport and Outport signals contain the same?Reusable ?custom storage class specification. The root Inport signal is branched and feeds into an atomic subsystem. The Function Packaging  parameter in the Block Parameters of a subsystem is set to Auto  or Reusable function . The subsystem is reused in the generated code.
Fixed In: R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: MATLAB crashes while using fixed-point instrumentation to collect simulation min-max ranges if existing slprj folder is not read/write accessible
Description: MATLAB might crash when simulating a model that has been instrumented for fixed-point min-max range collection.The crash occurs when the slprj folder used for holding instrumentation artifacts has already been created, but during the current simulation, read or write permissions for that folder are not available.  A common scenario for this to occur involves two different users. One user with write permissions configured the model to collect fixed-point min-max range collection and did an initial instrumented simulation. The initial simulation created the slprj folder. Later, a second user attempts another instrumented simulation in the same directory location. If the second user does not have read/write permissions for the slprj folder, then MATLAB may crash.

Summary: Compatibility check may fail for models containing Data Store Memory blocks
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails? with the message  Checking Compatibility Failed ? when all the following conditions are met: The model contains?Data Store Memory blocks  The model contains blocks having?a constant sample time and the?BlockType ?equal to?S-Function  A replacement model gets generated   Additionally,? when these conditions are met,?you will encounter the following error?while executing? sldvblockreplacement :  $PRODUCT$ has detected an S-Function with a constant sample time. $PRODUCT$ does not support S-Functions with this configuration.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Model arguments with a non-Auto storage class might lose tunability in referenced models in accelerator mode
Description: Simulink does not honor changes to the value of model arguments, during both rebuilds and simulation, when all of the following conditions are true: The model argument has a non-Auto  storage class. The model argument is not pushed all the way up to the top model in the hierarchy. The referenced models?providing values for the model arguments use a simulation mode other than Normal .

Summary: Function hints in Command Window and Editor sometimes do not disappear
Description: In the desktop Command Window and Editor (for .m files), function hints sometimes do not disappear. The function hints persist even if you press Esc   or minimize the window. This issue most frequently affects Linux machines.
Workaround: Restart MATLAB to clear the function hint popup from the screen. Alternatively, unzip the attached file. Then execute the P-file which clears any function hint popups.

Summary: Signal selection changes in bus selector blocks might occur when reordering or deleting outputs of Stateflow charts or MATLAB Function blocks
Description: When the output port of a Stateflow chart or a MATLAB function block is deleted or reordered, bus selector blocks which are connected to these outputs might change their signal selection. For example, consider a Stateflow chart which has two outputs "out1" and "out2". These outputs are connected to a bus creator block which combines them into a single bus. There is a bus selector block connected to the output of this bus creator which then extracts the signal "out1" from this bus. If the port numbers of the outputs in the chart are changed, then the signal selection of the bus selector block might change from "out1" to "out2".?

Summary: Sample time colors overwrite the block foreground colors
Description: If a model has Sample Time coloring on, the sample time colors overwrite the block foreground colors when the model is saved.
Workaround: Select all blocks and set the foreground color to black.Turn off the sample time colors display in one of the following ways and re-save the modelIn the Simulink model window, on the?Debug?tab, deselect?Information Overlays?>?Colors.- or -From the MATLAB terminal, type: set_param(model, 'SampleTimeColors', 'off');

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink ignores the runtime value of a model argument when used as a Model Workspace data object's value
Description: For a model workspace data object, if you specify value with an expression that includes a model argument, definition value of the model argument is used during simulation. Simulink does not use the model argument's updated value from parent model during simulation.
Fixed In: R2018b Update 8, R2019b Update 6, R2020a Update 4, R2020b, R2021a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated lookup table code using coder.approximation produces less accurate results in some cases
Description: When using coder.approximation  during fixed-point conversion of MATLAB code, generated lookup table code produces less accurate results for generated lookup tables that use 1D interpolation. Similar inaccuracy exists in the Fixed-Point Converter and MATLAB Coder apps when you use lookup table replacements in Function Replacements  tab during conversion of MATLAB code to fixed point. In the Fixed-Point Converter and MATLAB Coder apps, the "Flat" interpolation method is not affected. Other interpolation methods are affected.

Summary: readMessages function fails on rosbag files
Description: After opening and parsing the log file from the rosbag  function, when you try to read messages from the loaded bag file with default readmessages  function, you might encounter the following error on some particular rosbag  files: Error using robotics.ros.BagSelection/deserializeMessages (line 641)Java exception occurred:java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException: Invalid combined index of 1182942899, maximum is 336592104at org.jboss.netty.buffer.SlicedChannelBuffer.(SlicedChannelBuffer.java:46)at org.jboss.netty.buffer.HeapChannelBuffer.slice(HeapChannelBuffer.java:200)at org.jboss.netty.buffer.AbstractChannelBuffer.readSlice(AbstractChannelBuffer.java:323)at org.ros.internal.message.field.PrimitiveFieldType$14.deserialize(PrimitiveFieldType.java:599)at org.ros.internal.message.field.PrimitiveFieldType$14.deserialize(PrimitiveFieldType.java:565)at org.ros.internal.message.field.ValueField.deserialize(ValueField.java:68)at org.ros.internal.message.DefaultMessageDeserializer.deserialize(DefaultMessageDeserializer.java:45)at org.ros.internal.message.field.MessageFieldType.deserialize(MessageFieldType.java:111)at org.ros.internal.message.field.MessageFieldType.deserialize(MessageFieldType.java:38)at org.ros.internal.message.field.ListField.deserialize(ListField.java:86)at org.ros.internal.message.DefaultMessageDeserializer.deserialize(DefaultMessageDeserializer.java:45)Error in robotics.ros.BagSelection/readMessages (line 278)                msgs = obj.deserializeMessages(obj.MessageList, rows);
Workaround: As a workaround, consider using the struct DataFormat with the readMessages function:bagselect = rosbag('ex_multiple_topics.bag');msgStructs = readMessages(bagselect,'DataFormat','struct');
Products: ROS Toolbox, Robotics System Toolbox, Navigation Toolbox

Summary: Calling delaunayn or griddatan on high-dimensional data may crash MATLAB(R)
Description: MATLAB may crash when the delaunayn  or griddatan  functions are called on a matrix with a large number of columns (greater than 20). The cause of the crash is that MATLAB runs out of memory. The stack trace of the crash will typically look like this: [  0] 0x00000000ee09ac2c C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\libmwqhull.dll+00175148 qh_new_qhull+00001820[  1] 0x00000000ee0a063a C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\libmwqhull.dll+00198202 qh_fprintf+00004026[  2] 0x00000000ee079b78 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\libmwqhull.dll+00039800 qh_nearcoplanar+00000984[  3] 0x00000000ee083003 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\libmwqhull.dll+00077827 qh_qhull+00018723[  4] 0x00000000ee07bbeb C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\libmwqhull.dll+00048107 qh_addpoint+00000699[  5] 0x00000000ee07c1ca C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\libmwqhull.dll+00049610 qh_addpoint+00002202 [  6] 0x00000000ee07e715 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2019b\bin\win64\libmwqhull.dll+00059157 qh_qhull+00000053

Summary: OPC UA node with large list of children might not display all child nodes
Description: If an OPC UA node contains a large number of child nodes, OPC Toolbox might not retrieve or display all child nodes. Only a partial list of nodes is returned. The exact number of nodes depends on the server configuration.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when using a DBC-file that contains inconsistencies
Description: When trying to open a DBC-file that contains inconsistencies, MATLAB might crash without generating any crash log. The impacted workflows include:? Calling the function canDatabase  with such a DBC-file. Specifying such a DBC-file to use with the following blocks: CAN Pack, CAN Unpack, CAN FD Pack, CAN FD Unpack, J1939 Network Configuration.
Workaround: If accessing a DBC-file causes MATLAB to crash, you can use Vector CANdb++ to perform a consistency check for the file in question. Fixing the reported inconsistencies should prevent the crash.
Fixed In: R2020a Update 8, R2020b Update 8, R2021a Update 7, R2021b Update 3, R2022a, R2022b

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect dead logic reported for multiport switch having constant array as control input
Description: Simulink Design Verifier reports incorrect dead logic when model has multiport switch with constant array/vector as control input.Example: Consider a multiport switch with 4 inputs. For such a multiport switch if the control input is connected to [1 2 ] i.e a constant array/vector, then Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports truncated input(2) value as unreachable .

Summary: Run-time error "j1939Receive: PGN Registration Error: 17" on target computer for model that uses xpcJ1939lib?blocks out of order
Description: A run-time error "j1939Receive: PGN Registration Error: 17 " occurs on the target computer for models that execute xpcJ1939lib  blocks out of the intended order. The block priorities for these blocks are: J1939 'Protocol Stack' priority -2 'Controller Application' priority -1 'Receive Message' priority 0   The blocks need to execute in that order (-2, -1, then 0). Block priorities only serve as a suggestion and do not guarantee the relative execution order.?If the blocks are placed at different levels in the model (for example, 'J1939 Receive Message' is placed in a virtual subsystem), the blocks can get assigned an unintended execution order and then throw this error at run-time.?
Workaround: Note:? We recommend trying the newer J1939 blocks (>> slrtj1939lib)To continue using xpcJ1939lib, try placing the blocks at the same level in the model to resolve the issue. Otherwise, check the execution order of the blocks and try to achieve a modeling pattern that yields the desired order:View the execution order of the blocks (They must have the order listed below):Right click in the Simulink Canvas > Other Displays > Blocks > Sorted Execution OrderExecute First:J1939 Protocol Stack?Execute Next:?J1939 Controller Application & J1939 Database (CANdb) Setup?Execute Last:?Other xpcJ1939lib blocks?

Summary: Initializing function-scoped static variables in custom C++ code causes target computer crash
Description: When attempting to execute a real-time application, initialization of function-scoped static variables in custom C++ code might cause the target computer to crash. This issue is caused by a problem in the C++ runtime library that can lead to incorrect initialization of a function-scope static variable in custom C++ code.
Workaround: Change the scope of the static variable from function scope to global/file scope in the C++ code.

Summary: Loading rosbag with custom messages results in error for missing message headers
Description: When loading a rosbag into MATLAB, if your rosbag contains custom messages, you might encounter the following error: No definition for Header Message def. Header header
Workaround: As a workaround, consider either of the following options:Create all custom messages in the rosbag using MATLAB as shown in the following page, then reload the rosbag. ROS Custom Message SupportUse the Python script below that cleans up rosbags, then reload the rosbag:https://github.com/gavanderhoorn/rosbag_fixer?
Products: Robotics System Toolbox, Navigation Toolbox, ROS Toolbox

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: ROS Call Service block stops sending service requests when creating a service client repeatedly.
Description: If you repeatedly create a service client, a Java memory issue can eventually lead to the following error message in MATLAB: Failed to create a /test_service_call service client.?  If you are using the ROS Call Service block, it stops sending service request.?
Workaround: As a workaround, consider shutting down MATLAB ROS network to manually release all Java threads with the following command:?>> rosshutdown?
Products: ROS Toolbox, Robotics System Toolbox, Navigation Toolbox

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: A disabled mask parameter on a linked block with mask-on-mask evaluates to model workspace value if it references to model argument
Description: A disabled mask parameter is incorrectly evaluated to model workspace value ,if all of the following conditions are true: Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator simulation mode is chosen The referenced child model contains a linked library block The linked library block in the child model has a mask directly on another mask The mask parameter of the linked block references a model argument The parent model specifies the model argument values  The disabled mask parameter evaluates to base workspace value for model with Normal simulation mode Also please refer to the Bug Report?1687932 ?for more information about this behavior.

Summary: Operation using wrapped values from a previous orange overflow is green even if tooltip indicates a possible second overflow
Description: If you use the value?warn-with-wrap-around ?for the options -signed-integer-overflows  and -unsigned-integer-overflows , you expect that following a possible overflow (orange check), the verification wraps the overflowing values and continues. If a second overflow occurs because of the wrapped values , you expect to see this overflow through another orange check.  However, for possible overflows that have the same root cause, only the first instance is highlighted with an orange check. The subsequent instances are green even though source-code tooltips indicate possible overflows. You cannot see all instances of overflows from wrapped values that come from the same root cause. Example of situation:? int input();void getEven(int* copy1, int* copy2) {    int var;    var = input();    *copy1 = var * 2; //First overflow: orange    *copy2 = var * 2; //Second overflow: green (yet tooltips show possible overflow)}
Products: Polyspace Code Prover, Polyspace Code Prover Server

Summary: Streaming Simscape data to disk slows down simulation
Description: There is a performance regression in model simulation time when Simscape data is streamed to disk, compared to R2016b. Simulation times are comparable with those in R2016a.
Workaround: To improve simulation time, turn off streaming Simscape data to disk:1. On the MATLAB? Toolstrip, click Preferences.2. In the left pane of the Preferences dialog box, select Simscape, then clear the Stream data to temporary disk directory check box.

Summary: ncinfo crashes MATLAB? when accessing NetCDF files from older OPeNDAP THREDDS servers
Description: On Windows? systems, calling ncinfo  on NetCDF files accessed through older OPeNDAP THREDDS servers can cause memory leaks and segmentation violations in MATLAB?.
